restaurants.name: Rich Table
restaurants.address: 199 Gough St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Jenny
reviews.text:
We went for a birthday dinner on Feb 3 and it was our first time here. The food was great and our servers were attentive and friendly. However, there was no acknowledgement that we were celebrating a birthday. In hindsight, this reservation detail may have been missed during check in.

We still had a good dinner. We particularly liked the Porcini doughnuts & Raclette and the chicken liver mousse with duck fat madeleines. We enjoyed the rice and shine milk wash cocktail. We tried the dry aged ribeye and the tonnarelli sea urchin Cacio e Pepe. Both entrees were very filling and delicious for our party of 2.

reviews.author: Denise C
reviews.text: Everything we ordered was delicious and perfectly cooked, flavored, and plated. I didn’t love the liver mousse toast because the liver flavor was muted, but my partner enjoyed it. Service was excellent. Pretty crowded though and space is too jam packed.

reviews.author: CANDELA MONGE
reviews.text: We had an excellent dinner during the last night of our honeymoon. The service was perfect and did everything to make the night even more special. The food was delicious and surprising, every bite was an experience and we had lots of fun. Thank you for the great time! The sardin chips and porcini donuts were delicious and very creative! The toro toas was my favourite and the burrata was also very fun! We had the sea oursin pasta that was very good as well but couldn’t really feel the sea oursin flavour. The wine recommendations were also great. For desert we had the panna cotta that was very rich and yummy!

reviews.author: D MP
reviews.text:
We came here for the first time and ordered the tasting menu and two drinks each. The drinks reflected how skilled the bartender on duty is, I believe she was the one who designed the cocktail menu. The tomato martini is hands down the best drink my partner and I have ever tried, I ordered it, but we ultimately shared it because he couldn't stop sneaking sips.
The tasting menu was nothing short of incredible. It wasn't pretentious, but it displayed a great understanding of balance, flavor and technique. We left feeling full and basically floated to our second location of the night, still riding the high from the sensational dining experience.
Aside from the above, what stood out the most to me at this restaurant was the service. The staff was attentive, engaging and so thoughtful in all their details.
Hats off to the entire staff, we cannot wait to celebrate more moments at Rich Table!

reviews.author: Marsha M
reviews.text: Such a great experience. The uniqueness of the ingredient combinations and the elegance with which things are prepared is quite remarkable. I started with the sardine chip which is brilliant. The sardine is so intense in its fresh, fishy flavor and combined with the whipped horseradish it's such a great foil for the intense flavors. I also had the beef dumpling which I was disappointed with. It lacked seasoning and even though there's a lot of chilies they had no heat. The chicken liver was surprising and delightful. It's quite sweet with the quince and almond and candied pecans and seems to compensate for the sweetness with an excess of flake salt. However, it does work and I would have it again. The uni pasta was just a delight, ao creamy. The shape of the pasta does enhance the experience. Thea amuse bouche was also delightful. And I had the mocktail milk washed cocktail. It had a clean yet. Creamy mouth feel with subtle notes of pickling which perfectly paired with dinner. Really delicious.

restaurants.description: Californian fare from local ingredients, served in a salvaged-barn-wood space with an open kitchen.

restaurants.name: DACHA Kitchen & Bar
restaurants.address: 1085 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Pasha Gleyzer
reviews.text: I liked the experience overall.  Appetizers: borsch, chicken pate, pickled vegetables and eggplant were delicious.  Garlic pampushka should be more garlicky in my opinion, but that's debatable.  For the main courses, the feeling was mixed.  I liked my Pozharky Cutlet but not the garnish.  It was sweet.  Goulash meat was very tender, but also sweet.  And then biff stroganoff was not what I expected at all.  Meet looked like a kebab that was boiled.  Honey cake and Pavlova were excellent.  Olesya - our server was grace itself.  I would think that they should smooth out some of the kinks, but very nice place.  I will try kebabs next time.

reviews.author: Angela Chetina (Psychology and sexology)
reviews.text:
A cozy and vibrant gem in San Francisco

Dacha is one of those rare places in San Francisco where you feel genuinely welcome from the moment you step in. The atmosphere is warm and intimate — casual enough to relax, yet elegant enough to feel special. Whether you’re coming in for a full meal or just a drink, there’s a quiet sense of hospitality that makes you want to stay longer.

The food is always beautifully plated — you can tell there’s thought and care behind every dish. Their interpretation of classic Eastern European flavors feels fresh, bright, and deeply satisfying. The Mimosa Salad, with its light layers and a surprising touch of crisp apple, was a standout. Every ingredient tasted fresh and seasonal, and the balance of flavors was spot on.

The house-made infusions are another highlight. I brought home their passionfruit nastoyka, which has become one of my favorite after-dinner sips. And every time I come back, there’s something new — a creative cocktail, a seasonal dish, or a thoughtful little twist on a familiar flavor.

The service here is consistently excellent. The staff are friendly, patient, and clearly passionate about what they do. It feels less like a restaurant and more like being a guest in someone’s (stylish) home.

Dacha is a place where food, comfort, and creativity come together — a spot I always look forward to returning to.

reviews.author: Kolya Yastrebenetsky
reviews.text:
The place is not an authentic Eastern European as one might have expected based on the description. It has a cosmopolitan vibe with some Russian items on the menu. The food was ok, not great. I liked the pirozhki (tried the mushroom one and it was surprisingly interesting), but didn't like the goulash main dish. The goulash was dry, bland and overdone. It was more of a braised meat on mash of squash and sweet potato than anything close to Hungarian goulash.
The chicken liver appetizer was nice but very small dish for a price.
Czech pilsner canned beer was $14, which is way too much for this drink.
Overall nice atmosphere, not very authentic and pricey to my opinion.

reviews.author: Yuliya Kazlova
reviews.text: If you want to try the best syrniki in the city, you have to visit Dacha. They never disappoint. We love the cozy atmosphere, delicious food, and the warm, welcoming service.

reviews.author: Nika Airikainen
reviews.text:
The best place in town for dining, enjoying a cocktail, having a romantic night out, or hosting an event.

Big thanks to Dacha’s founders, management, and entire team for giving us an amazing opportunity to host our networking event there.

We were truly showered with support, hospitality, care, and warmth.

Suki and Katya — the founders of Dacha — pour their heart and soul into this place. Every corner speaks to their personal touch, creativity, and passion for bringing the best experience to the market.

And we were able to feel those vibes throughout our entire event.

On top of that, the cuisine is a masterpiece. The honey cake and the Nalivka are absolute must-tries.

restaurants.name: Bottega
restaurants.address: 1132 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Lucia Hinton
reviews.text: Our waiter, Giovanni, was attentive and kind throughout the entire experience. We started with Brussels sprouts and bruschetta, which were excellent. For the main courses, we ordered gnocchi with Bolognese sauce, gnocchi with pesto, and fettuccine with vodka sauce. Everything was very flavorful, beautifully prepared, and the staff was extremely friendly and welcoming.

reviews.author: Aqua-Sun Romero
reviews.text:
Bottega's food is authentic and most of the staff is Italian, they are very hospitable and knowledgeable about the menu.  If I lived here, it would come at least once a week. Eggplant Parmesan is the best I have ever tried statewide. Flavor profile of the dish was the perfect melody savory and umuni, creamy and tangy.
Restuarant has quality ingredients and willing to accommodate vegetarians.

reviews.author: Meg Nagesha
reviews.text:
Bottega is one of my all-time favorite spots in San Francisco. Every time I land, this is the first meal I have to get.

The truffle mushroom ravioli is unreal—easily one of the best pasta dishes I’ve ever had. Their tiramisu is amazing too (when they haven’t sold out, which happens often on weekends). This time, I tried the limoncello mascarpone cheesecake, and it completely blew my mind. I hate lemons, and this dessert somehow made me fall in love with them.

Great appetizers, drinks to die for, and a super cute, aesthetic space. Bottega never disappoints. ✨

reviews.author: Moorissa Tjokro
reviews.text: Their mushroom truffle pizza is a must-try! The mozzarella and truffle was magic on first bite. I added arugula and anchovy in mine which made it even more bomb. Love their “build your own” pizza and pasta concept and the open seating made me feel like being in Europe.

reviews.author: Brandon Miller
reviews.text:
Granting this a 5-star review here with one caveat, the waiters could be a little more friendly.

We've been twice now. We are from the PNW but we frequent SFCA. We like the food and the vibe of the city... Bottega is no exception.

Our first time, we came in for espresso martinis. Lord, those were delish! The bartender was so friendly, engaging, joked with us and had a legitimate good time. We really appreciated him.

Came back the next night for dinner. It was cool outside but we took an outdoor table. Our waiter took drink orders. While we waited for the drinks, the waiter for the table next to us passed by... we asked for the outdoor heater to be turned on (we were extremely polite). He literally huffed at us and reluctantly turned it on. We thanked him and he ignored the gratitude. Nice looking guy, 20s, tats, crisp white shirt, looked professional but certainly didn't act like it.

Food was excellent. All four of us got a different pasta that we thoroughly enjoyed. Drinks were strong and on-point. Wait was a little long but that can be overlooked when considering the quality of food.

Service was great, unfortunately our wonderful waiter was shaded by his coworker who had a bit of attitude.

After nearly 25 years in customer service and patient care... let me recommend one thing... don't let your teammates ruin your patron's (potentially) perfect experience. You may not be our waiter but your actions are observed.

restaurants.description: Classic Italian fare including pizza, pasta, and meat dishes are offered at this modest restaurant.

restaurants.name: The Progress
restaurants.address: 1525 Fillmore St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Leah
reviews.text:
We celebrated Chinese New Year here last night, and it turned out to be an incredibly enjoyable and satisfying experience.

We reserved the first seating at 5:30 PM and arrived around 5:20. Even before opening, there was already a line forming outside — a clear sign of how popular this place is. The energy started building before we even walked in.

Inside, the space feels warm and lively. Tables are fairly close together, but the atmosphere is vibrant rather than cramped. It’s relaxed, energetic, and full of movement — equally fitting for a lively dinner with friends or family, or a cozy date night with a couple of cocktails.

The menu is à la carte rather than a tasting format, which gave us the flexibility to explore a wider range of dishes.

We ordered:
•	Crispy Tofu
•	Seaweed Noodles
•	Potato Cloud
•	Artichoke Toast
•	Signature Duck Rice

The Crispy Tofu and Seaweed Noodles stood out immediately. Both carried bold Asian influences, and as Asians ourselves, we felt the flavors were exceptionally well executed. The balance and depth were impressive — easily highlights of the night.

The Artichoke Toast and Potato Cloud were also excellent. The toast had great chew and structure, with layered, well-seasoned toppings. The Potato Cloud was silky and airy, elevated by the contrast of caviar and a crunchy potato base — beautiful textural play.

Interestingly, the Signature Duck Rice felt slightly more understated. It was by no means bad — the portion was generous and well-prepared — but compared to the other dishes, it didn’t quite reach the same “wow” level. Perhaps our expectations were simply higher given its reputation.

Service was fantastic. A special thank you to our server Brett — genuinely kind, attentive, and knowledgeable. He walked us through the menu thoughtfully and recommended two cocktails for me: One Punch Man and Liquid Swords — both refreshing, clean, and pairing beautifully with the meal.

For dessert, we brought our own celebratory cake and enjoyed it alongside a peanut milk drink, which made for a sweet and fitting end to the evening.

Overall, The Progress delivered across atmosphere, service, and food. It’s a Michelin one-star that feels vibrant rather than formal — a place you can relax, celebrate, and truly enjoy yourself.

Highly recommended for date nights, group dinners, or any special occasion where you want both quality and energy.

reviews.author: Kyla W
reviews.text:
I really want to love The Progress (and state bird) because the vibes are so good, the service is great, and some of the dishes are legendary, but it was my second time for dinner there last night, and 4 or so visits to state bird next door, and I see too much of a pattern to want to really come back to either ever again.

How can these dishes be SO all over the place but coming out of the same team? What is going on?! I see other reviewers here posting similar things so I know I’m not alone.

On the one hand you have perfectly balanced, creative, generously portioned dishes like the duck or the seaweed pasta, and on the other you have dishes with flavors or textures that do not come together at all and are even bordering inedible. The “lamb” dish last night was actually chopped up sausages that you had to roll up in big greasy tortillas, with large chucks of pumpkin, raw half-tomatillos and then garnish with fresh oranges and some kind of mole-like sauce. Who wants to this like craft project in the middle of a nice meal out? Dripping and making a mess and then taking a bite and it’s just a full raw tomatillo? LOL

Or some dishes are super unbalanced (borderline gross flavor combinations), way too salty to be edible, or portions that are comedic. The bok Choy is HALF OF ONE bok choy cut into quarters for like $20—what???— We would have had to order like 4 of this dish for the table to share a reasonable portion per person!!

BUT then you have the duck— perfectly cooked like something from a dream, and incredible rice with nuts that is a huge crunchy and squishy and just wonderful platter. Or the pasta dishes which were delicately balanced (Bonito with the brown butter and seaweed was like a flavor bomb! Amazing!!!). You are left thinking about your memory of these dishes later they are so good.

The dessert isle flotant arrives looking like a science experiment (again— what are we doing here with textures and plating?!) but is saved by the incredible sensory mix of everything but the sorbet (which should probably be dropped from this dish because it is too dense and intense compared to the textures and flavors of the other two things, and increase the portion of the meringue which melted into the sauce in a few mins…). Why is this happening?

Anyway, this review is way too long. In the end, dining here and exploring the menu and dishes (or at state bird)—as you want to do because the descriptions and ingredients are exciting!— becomes like gambling, and then when you look back on the experience/meal as a whole you are just left mostly with feelings of confusion. It’s like a flavor, texture (and emotional) roller coaster… and not in a good way.

Since I’m often the one to coordinate dinners out, I don’t think I can bring people to either of these places again for anything but cocktails (which are actually all amazing!!! And the somm was super nice!) because they will think I am loco. Please do something to fix this, chef. You are better than this, I know you are.

PS: shout out to the Glassy baby candles. Incredible organization !!

reviews.author: Jamie Li
reviews.text: Made a last minute reservation for a random weekday, and got seating in an adorable booth by the bar! It was the perfect spot to catch up with a friend and enjoy some food and drink. Cocktails were phenomenal, and every dish we had came out really well presented and tasty. For my drink, I got the one punch man. I am a huge fan of milk-washed cocktails and if you are too, you should not miss out on this one. We got the scallop and seaweed noodles which I would definitely get again.

reviews.author: Farrah Palmer
reviews.text: My husband and I were visiting the city and decided to go here for our one and only date night without the baby. We were beyond happy with our decision. The food was phenomenal! Portions were perfect for a party of 2. Our absolute favorite was the pork chop. It was to die for. We wanted to pick up the bone and eat off the rest of the meat. A server told us they are a no judgment zone and do the same. Thank you for a wonderful evening!

reviews.author: Jorge
reviews.text:
Had dinner here last night.
The server coursed well all dishes, starting with caviar potato cloud, then riverdog farm pig head (kind of a salad), after we tried the marinated artichoke toast (top notch), then chanterelle mushroom & yuba stir fry (Asian style). For main we shared the famous bbq liberty farms half duck (generous portion and nice flavor, wish just that the skin were crispy). And for desert floating island (an explosion of 5 different flavors).
Drinks we ordered Bird’s Eye View (mix of sour and sweet together) and The Upside Down (mocktail with a very different structure of flavor).
Generally I would say we had an ok experience, considering they have 1 star Michelin, was expecting a little more.
Ps.: they have a really nice environment and also an open kitchen. It was kind of funny to see one of the cooks hiding under the kitchen island to eat some food after 10pm, think just they should do that in a separated area not in front of guests in a Michelin Restaurant (my table was facing that view).

restaurants.description: New American restaurant from the State Bird Provisions team serving inventive, family-style meals.

restaurants.name: Aziza
restaurants.address: 5800 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: bartika dutta
reviews.text: We went to this place on the evening of Valentine’s Day, and it took the staff a little while to get us seated – hence the four stars. But from then on (a huge thank you to Abigail), the evening became entirely about taste. The Rosebud was so vibrant and refreshing. The trio of spreads arrived like a flavor overture, the lebni and the smoked trout roe being delightfully briny and indulgent. The salmon with beets and kumquats was subtle. The grilled whole branzino struck that perfect balance between soft and crisp, its charmoula earthy with just the right kick. The kefta brochette carried a mild, aromatic warmth that lingered pleasantly. And the delicately spiced harissa butter elevated the soft and deliciously dense cornbread into something memorable. Whatever mild annoyance I felt while waiting for our table simply evaporated. The staff are open to explaining dishes before you order, and they’re genuinely lovely while doing so. I don’t usually comment on restrooms 😄, but this one deserves a mention. It’s more like a dimly lit lounge, with live plants, a vintage gilded mirror , and plenty of space to paise and admire the aesthetic. In true San Francisco fashion – a tiny wait, big flavors, and a night that felt deliciously worth the reservation scramble.

reviews.author: Yikna Wu
reviews.text: Came here for a casual brunch with the family. We ordered the two lattes, Burrata, French toasts, shrimp, and eggs Benedict. The Burrata was exceptional, except it only came with two small pieces of sour dough… I could use double the amount of bread. Everything else was good. No complaints.

reviews.author: Theresa
reviews.text: Exceptional is every way! Every dish we ordered was phenomenal, truly. Not only  beautiful presentation, but unique and delicious flavors. Great vibe, friendly staff, and very good mocktail and wine list too. Best dishes included the cauliflower (wow!!) and the cornbread (melt in your mouth good!!)!! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Ru
reviews.text: Excellent delicious flavorful food! We had the octopus, cauliflower, lamb and corn bread - 10/10!!! Also super kind staff! Only thing to note: it is very loud. If you’re looking for a quiet place to dine, I’m not sure this would be the place.

reviews.author: Christopher Chung
reviews.text:
High-quality, high-end Moroccan cuisine, with modern California touches that deconstruct but stay true to the core flavors and ingredients of the North African palette.

Heritage Chicken, with preserved lemon and olives, is arguably one of the best roast chickens in the city (a tall order!) — perfectly succulent, juicy and ultra-clean tasting. A symphony for the eyes and nose.

Grilled Broccolini and Humus with Pita Bread are also great accompaniments.

restaurants.description: Innovative Moroccan cooking with Californian touches & creative cocktails in a colorful setting.

restaurants.name: Dalida Restaurant
restaurants.address: 101 Montgomery St Suite 100, San Francisco, CA 94129, USA

reviews.author: Grace Nicolas -Kim
reviews.text:
Oh my. We came here for brunch and we were pretty impressed by overall experiences including the quality of foods, service and architecture of the place.  I am not sure if this is Turkish or Greek and I am supposed to be cautious about this bc I can’t offend either one of them but regardless all of the dishes we had were delectable.

We came here for brunch and ordered Turkish flatbread, Octobus and Sujuk and baklava . I don’t think I had better baklava . It was so dreamy and melting inside of my mouth. It’s not the traditional baklava but it had perfect mixture of sweetness, texture with tanginess from raspberry.  We really loved Octopus and Sujuk as the spices with Sujuk were so unique and flavorful. My daughter loved flatbread .

I recommend also getting pistachio coffee if you love pistachio and coffee.

We will definitely come back for diner !

reviews.author: Ashia Bibi
reviews.text: A marvelous meal with a remarkable ambiance. I started off with oysters which were thankfully up to my expectations. The tamarind based cod entree was super smooth and silky in texture and taste and I found myself unable to stop. It was that good however the baklava with raspberry sauce felt like a culinary joke. If my dessert experience was up-to par I would’ve given it full stars. Either way I will still come back for another seasonal meal beside I love the bay! And trust me, when I say today was an absolutely beautiful day with Golden Gate Bridge my view and a delicious meal fascinating my soul to write, I am filled with gratitude and bliss.

reviews.author: Akshay Bhagat
reviews.text:
Dalida is elevated Mediterranean food and a stunning view from the outdoor patio onto presidio's tunnel tops park.

I ate at the bar and got the breaking bread which includes humus, muhammara, labneh and other assortments with a pit bread baked with zaatar. A great dish for the table.

Also got the tapsi kebab that came with shoestring potatoes and egg. This was delicious and meaty, and with the runny egg yolk was 🔥. The potatoes were crisp and the kebab was like a juicy, mildly seasoned chaapli kebab.

For desert I had the chocolate hazel nut tart. Not super on brand like kunefe but this was stunning. It came with some chocolate crisps and anise ice cream. I loved this!

To finish I got the ottoman coffee which was a good Turkish coffee.

The server was super helpful and made sure we didn't over order. I think I will go back for brunch.

reviews.author: Katy
reviews.text:
We had a couple hiccups during our visit here with our order, but I’ve worked in retail and food before and I understand how things can get confusing and mistakes can be made despite your best efforts. That being said, I’d confirm just to make sure they got it right when ordering.
Dalida has a great atmosphere and the location is bar none. The Presidio backdrop is honestly fantastic. The inside is gorgeous and the vibe is amazing as well.
The food is solidly good. It’s not the best I’ve ever had, but it’s a 3.75-4 star for me. The fries however are a 5 and we did enjoy the lamb dumplings. The Aleppo Chicken was okay—36$ I was expecting my mind to be blown—but I feel like I could have cooked it at home too. The Kalispera drink was a 10/10 for me. The Ottoman Coffee was fun as well. It was a good experience overall. Not sure if I’d go out of my way to go back, but not a waste of a dinner either. 3.75/5.

reviews.author: Justin Wong
reviews.text: I'm raving about the octopus dish here. The sauce was so tasty and the presentation was so beautiful! We also really loved the lamb chops which very tender. And make sure to get the pita bread and mezze platter too! Service was friendly and on point. Everything was really great!

restaurants.name: Lokma
restaurants.address: 1801 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Elma C
reviews.text:
A story about how one of my favorite spices (cinnamon) ruined brunch on a Sunday.

I love cinnamon. In pastries, in coffee, in savory chili.i just did not expect it to hijack a savory Turkish ratatouille and dominate my entire plate.

The Good:
• For a walk-in only, a short 10 minute wait was very much appreciated
•The eggs were beautifully executed - soft, fluffy, properly folded, tender inside without being runny or rubbery. You can tell the kitchen knows what they’re doing.
• Tzatziki on eggs? Unexpected and absolutely delicious. Cool, tangy, creamy against warm eggs - amazing!
• The Arnold Palmer was perfectly balanced (not too sweet, not too tea-heavy). Clean layers, refreshing, 5/5.
• Cold water served immediately upon arrival (and properly cold). It’s such a simple touch, but always appreciated.
• Ambiance is top notch - warm, inviting, great brunch energy.
• Attentive staff throughout.

The Not-So-Good:
• The Turkish ratatouille was overwhelmingly cinnamon-forward. That was the only dominant note I could taste, and it overpowered the dish to the point where I struggled to finish it. Interestingly, the server initially mentioned they don’t add cinnamon but after checking with the kitchen, confirmed that cinnamon is indeed added. It was just far too much.
• No side plate. With oranges, sauce, and potatoes sharing space, there was nowhere to rest utensils or keep citrus from soaking into the stew. Small detail, but it matters.
• No salt and pepper on the table, so there was no way to tweak seasoning.

The frustrating part is that the technical cooking - especially the eggs - was excellent. But when one spice dominates and a few small service details are overlooked, it changes the entire experience.

Cinnamon, I still love you.

reviews.author: Lana Ellison
reviews.text: Been at Lokma 3 times and its the most memorable times, delicious authentic food and great service along with intimate atmosphere. Menu always have something new to try and is always a hit! Really enjoining the interior of not a big space but it’s very functional!

reviews.author: Muhammad Atif
reviews.text:
Authentic Turkish Flavors with a Cozy Vibe!
Lokma is an amazing spot in San Francisco for authentic Turkish and Mediterranean food. The ambience is warm, modern, and inviting — perfect for brunch or dinner. The dishes are beautifully presented and full of flavor — from the juicy kebabs to the freshly baked bread and delicious mezes. The service is attentive and friendly, and everything tastes fresh and homemade. Their Turkish coffee and desserts are a must-try! A perfect blend of great food, great service, and great atmosphere — highly recommended!

reviews.author: Nicholas Pun
reviews.text:
I've never seen so many headless turkeys in my life. Bless that one server that is singlehandedly carrying the entire restaurant.  She must be able to deadlift 1000 lbs without breaking a sweat. I've never had it so difficult to grab a waiter's attention. No one is working????

Service aside, food is comforting and simple. Nothing wild. Probably not worth the price tag.

Overall, my experience that day was horrible and I'm never coming back.

reviews.author: Yassi Sadri
reviews.text: Such a cute place. The food is mediocre. Started with a high note on appetizers, the kofta was on the dry side but it had all the right spices, the zucchini dolmas tasted delicious. The grilled chicken was also dry, the mousaka was rather bland and had not enough beef. But overall the food was gorgeously plated, the place was cute and the service was great.

restaurants.description: Mediterranean brunch items & a few Californian classics served in a petite, homey atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Scoma's Restaurant
restaurants.address: 1965 Al Scoma Way, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Joshua Michael
reviews.text:
Scoma’s Restaurant absolutely lives up to its reputation. From the moment you walk in, it feels like a true San Francisco classic warm, welcoming, and effortlessly charming. It’s the kind of place that reminds you why certain restaurants stand the test of time.

The food was outstanding. Everything tasted incredibly fresh, especially the seafood, which was prepared perfectly and full of flavor. You can tell quality and care go into every dish, and nothing felt rushed or overlooked. This is seafood done right simple, elevated, and memorable.

What truly set Scoma’s apart, though, was the service. Hands down, it was the best service I’ve experienced since moving to San Francisco. Attentive without being intrusive, knowledgeable, and genuinely kind it made the entire dining experience feel special and relaxed.

The atmosphere adds to the magic. Classic yet comfortable, lively but never overwhelming, it’s an ideal spot whether you’re celebrating something special or just enjoying a great meal out. Everything flows seamlessly, which is a testament to how well this place is run.

I’ll definitely be back, and it’s a place I’ll happily recommend to anyone looking for an unforgettable dining experience in San Francisco.

reviews.author: Katie
reviews.text: This restaurant is PHENOMENAL. Came here for date night with my boyfriend and it was all around such an enjoyable experience. Make sure to get a reservation beforehand, as they can get pretty busy. The food here was amazing, it was all so flavorful and just delicious. My boyfriend got a steak dish and he said the cut of meat was really high quality and that it was cooked to perfection. I got the clam linguine and it was also amazing. Finally the service here is just amazing, all the waiters are extremely kind and respectful. I will be coming back once I am back in SF!

reviews.author: Sonam M
reviews.text: If I could give this place a 10/5 I would. Greatest seafood experience in San Francisco and we will definitely come again whenever we visit next. The pier to plate is so worth it. We had the mixed seafood grill - the cod was PERFECT. Just an fyi, if you like your salmon more on the well done side, mention that. The next meal we tried was the Lazy Man’s cioppino and we got it spicy - amazing! That would be my go to next time. The service is fantastic, they take care of everything and check in frequently. Reasonably priced for seafood and location is prime. Def recommend!

reviews.author: Jason Fair (Nomadic Nerd Project)
reviews.text: This was such a highlight of our trip to San Francisco. Every fish was superb and the service was impeccable. Each dish was well executed. The cioppino was the best. I loved the pacific snapper and mushroom spaetzel. The riff on the Rockefeller oysters was genius, and they had such great clam chowder. Everyone working was attentive and the atmosphere was great. This is definitely the seafood place to go to.

reviews.author: Angie Hughes
reviews.text: This is an excellent, off-the-beaten-path restaurant serving fresh fish & seafood!! We grabbed an early dinner (being from a different time zone) and it wasn’t too busy. Our server, Heather, made our visit personal and memorable in the best way possible. We were early enough to catch the end of the lunch specials. While they were “lunch” specials, the portions were very generous! Truffle fries were our appetizers - BEST TRUFFLE FRIES EVER! Our group had a mix of starters, Caesar salad, beet salad, and  clam chowder, all were superb. The Red Snapper, ordered by all, was the best fish any of us have had! A lemon tart finished off the meal. It was a perfect ending. FIND THIS PLACE ON THE DOCK!! You’ll be sorry if you don’t.

restaurants.description: Spacious waterfront restaurant serving seafood pulled straight off the boat.

restaurants.name: Pearl 6101
restaurants.address: 6101 California St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Maria
reviews.text:
I had an absolutely wonderful dining experience at The Pearl 6101 in San Francisco. Every dish was thoughtfully prepared and beautifully presented. The Japanese sweet potatoes were an unexpected standout, perfectly roasted with a caramelized edge that made them both comforting and elegant. The kampachi crudo was bright and fresh, with a delicate balance of flavors that let the fish shine.

The scallops were seared to perfection, tender and buttery, while the lamb pasta was rich, deeply flavorful, and one of the most satisfying pasta dishes I’ve had in a long time. You can tell the kitchen puts real care and creativity into every plate.

What made the evening even more special was the exceptional service. Our waiter was incredibly kind, attentive, and knowledgeable, the kind of service that makes you feel genuinely taken care of without ever being intrusive.

From start to finish, The Pearl delivered a warm, refined, and memorable dining experience. I can’t wait to come back and try more of the menu!

reviews.author: Hayden Sampson
reviews.text:
Had a great dinner at Pearl 6101! We grabbed the chips & dip while waiting for our table — super good and tasty, definitely worth it. Then we had the Japanese sweet potato as an appetizer, which was insanely good.

For mains, we tried the Handkerchief and Crescenza Caramelle pastas — both were delicious, and you can tell the pasta is homemade. The whole vibe of the place is really cozy and fun. Make sure to get a reservation in advance as this place is always busy!

reviews.author: Sophie H
reviews.text: Everything we had here was fantastic! We tried the gemelli and bucatini pastas and they both exceeded our expectations. We also got a chicory Caesar to share which was great as well. The cocktail and wine we selected complemented our meals well and were very pleasant. The standout for me was the pistachio tiramisu as a dessert lover! The mix of textures was just fantastic. We came here post-proposal to celebrate, and the atmosphere was absolutely perfect.

reviews.author: Pat Gleason
reviews.text: Some of the most flavorful and well balanced food I have had in a long time. Outstanding flavors, perfect portion sizes, and elevated while still being approachable.

reviews.author: Jane Khoury
reviews.text:
Pearl 6101 hit all the marks. Creative dishes, shareable or not, fun drinks, charming dining room, and attentive service.
Definitely a redo

restaurants.description: Small restaurant & bar offering Mediterranean-inspired pastas & seafood, plus specialty cocktails.

restaurants.name: Sisterita
restaurants.address: 669 Commercial St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA

reviews.author: Anjera C
reviews.text: What a neat place! The decor and food is great inside. There was one party that seemed to enjoy it so much they came back the next day! They were quite popular on a Sunday morning when the rest of the city was quiet. I ordered the Secret cocktail and the Voila French Toast. We also ordered the Emerald which I heard was good and a latte. The Emerald eggs benedict seemed to be poached well and the green sauce it came with was beautiful. While the food took some time, the Secret cocktail was quite a good sneaky cocktail to sip on. It was pretty sweet and mostly tasted of lychee, but I didn’t mind it. It was slightly one-noted but still good and beautiful overall. The Voila French Toast was really great. I didn’t know French Toast bread could be shaped like an oval, and it was so soft inside. The caramelized bananas were great with the candied walnuts if that is your thing. I personally didn’t enjoy the walnuts as much, but the textures are fun, and the cream of vanilla goes really well with the French toast as a drizzled topping or dip. I’d love to come back to try their other items!

reviews.author: Simone Alexis Sonnier-Jang
reviews.text:
I was late to my reservation due to a BART delay and they graciously moved my time for me. When I arrived, I had my bag and my backpack for my mini vacation. They didn't treat me like an inconvenience at all. Some restaurants are very lacking in their customer service, and they would have given me subpar service. The waiters were attentive and the food was great, except for one small piece of egg shell. I have done that before while cooking. And I was coming in with 40 minutes to spare for their operational hours, so, they were probably rushed. I totally understand. I also understand that when I get eggshell in my food it is because I am not professionally trained. The atmosphere was very pleasant, so was the decor. The matcha latte and tiger stripe latte were both delicious, and every bite of the florentine eggs benedict, except that last bite, was delicious. I was kinda disappointed, but everything in life can't be perfect. Solid 4 stars!

P.S. Be careful with the potatoes they're lava inside. My mouth is burned. Let them sit for like 5 minutes plus before eating them. They may look OK to eat, but no, they're literally lava.

I looked it up and restaurants are supposed to tell you the food is hot, especially if it isn't visible. I came to San Francisco for a 3 day feast, with reservations at various restaurants around the city, and this 1st restaurant ruined it for me. It's day 2 and I'm settling for anything on the menu that isn't hot at my second reservation this morning. Kinda upset about it. I'll have to visit again, but I will avoid Sisterita at all costs. Even if it were free, I don't appreciate negligence.

reviews.author: Gaelle Eustache
reviews.text: Almost gave this place 5 stars. Seems to be pretty popular, stopped in without a reservation and there was quite a wait. It was about 11am. Service was fast, maybe too fast but I understand due to the line out the door. All 4 of our plates tasted great & were fresh. We decided to go back the next day and unfortunately we were disappointed. Stopped in earlier the next day around 820. Service was again fast, but this time our food was cold and not as fresh as the day before. The steak was chewy & flavorless & the French fries were oily. Kind of wish we didn’t return and kept the satisfaction from the first day

reviews.author: Virginia Huffman
reviews.text:
Super cute Café with friendly service.

We sat at the counter because the place was already packed, and that was our only option. Glad we did as we were served by the lovely April.

The coffee was amazing. So many other restaurants have incredibly strong and really bitter coffee and this was perfectly smooth and balanced. There are  lots of options for trying creative drinks and cocktails.

For breakfast I enjoyed the avocado toast with egg and tomatoes. My husband ordered the french onion soup which he gave thumbs up.

Will definitely return.

reviews.author: Yumile andrea Leguizamon tibata
reviews.text: The place is charming, the atmosphere and the staff's attention are wonderful. The cocktails and drinks are very well prepared and the food is exquisite. We spent a week in Chicago and went there for breakfast every day. The hostess is unique and attends to every detail. I recommend it 100%.

restaurants.name: Sweet Maple
restaurants.address: 2101 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Mary Maldonado
reviews.text:
This morning I visited Sweet Maple for brunch with high expectations.

I ordered the New York steak medium rare, but unfortunately it was served medium. The presentation was good, but when it comes to steak, temperature is everything, and I specifically did not order medium. When the server came to remove the plate, my dog barked unexpectedly, which startled him and caused coffee to spill onto the table. It was an awkward moment, but the staff handled it professionally and remained attentive throughout.

The overall service was good and friendly, and the waiting time was pretty standard for a brunch spot. The décor is simple and basic, nothing particularly memorable, but comfortable enough.

I also ordered a Nutella crepe, and here's where I was truly disappointed. It wasn't Nutella.. it tasted like regular chocolate spread. And as someone who considers herself a Nutella connoisseur, I can tell the difference immediately. If you advertise Nutella, it should be Nutella.

Overall, it was a decent experience with some details that could definitely be improved. I would give it another chance, hoping next time the steak temperature and the "Nutella" live up to expectations.

reviews.author: Melissa Eisner
reviews.text: Well worth the wait. Came on a busy weekend and there was a line … ended up waiting about 30 minutes for a table so not too bad. They make a good cup of coffee, and the service is pretty fast. I ordered the eggs with tots, and upgraded to the millionaires bacon, which was fabulous. Totally lived up to the hype. The omelette was also great, loved the multi color home potatoes. Will definitely come back. Yum!!!

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text: Delicious food and friendly service! The line may look long but moves pretty quickly, especially if you’re willing to sit outside in cooler weather. The Bay Bottom Omelet is one of my favorites and comes with a generous serving of crab. Their bread is AMAZING. The fried french toast is good but tastes like dessert/fair food, so be prepared! The millionaire bacon is melt-in-your-mouth, sweet, and spicy. It’s worth trying at least once. The food is always quality!

reviews.author: Marty Ignacio
reviews.text: We stumbled on this looking for a breakfast spot. We walked in and was seated before 8 am. I got the omurice dish and it was very tasty. We tried the bacon and was seasoned to perfection. We would definitely come back again!

reviews.author: Kevin Drost
reviews.text:
Sweet Maple is definitely a brunch spot worth the hype! I ordered their Big Hip— their famous deep-fried French toast— and wow, it did not disappoint. Crispy on the outside, soft and warm on the inside, and paired perfectly with pure Vermont maple syrup, butter, and powdered sugar. Absolute perfection.

But the real star of the show? The Millionaire Bacon. Oh. My. God. Hands down the best bacon I’ve ever had in my life— rich, slightly spicy, sweet, and just insanely flavorful. Even if you don’t order a full meal, you HAVE to at least get a side of this bacon. It’s a must-try and totally worth the trip just for that alone!

restaurants.description: Local spot for homemade morning/lunch fare like signature brown-sugar-glazed “millionaire’s bacon.”

restaurants.name: Seven Hills
restaurants.address: 1896 Hyde St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: bartika dutta
reviews.text: This restaurant deserves five stars–and then some. Between the dim, intimate, lighting, attentive service, and truly fabulous food, there was nothing more we could have asked for. The complimentary bread arrived warm and pillowy soft. The autumn breeze mocktail was a beautiful study in balance, with ginger delicately offset by pomegranate. The agnolotti was playful and smart; when I punctured the cheerfully green pillows of pasta, the chicken filling revealed itself – creamy, inviting, and every bit as delicious as it looked. The market fish was earthy and impeccably cooked, fresh and silky with a gorgeous char. Its tender texture was perfectly balanced by the comforting starchiness of the rice. The squid ink lobster pasta was bold, unexpected, and deeply satisfying. The affogato seemed to evaporate on the tongue, leaving behind nothing but pure flavor. Our server, Kim Thi , was animated and graceful, adding warmth and ease to an already exceptional evening. This is a place that deserves to be lavishly praised!

reviews.author: John Hanley
reviews.text:
In writing this review, my only disappointment is that I can’t give them six stars! From the moment we walked in, we were welcomed as if we were old family friends from our hostess. She gently showed us into a waiting area and showed us the previews of the menus. Less than three minutes later on a very busy Saturday night,we were seated at a very comfortable table.

Once we are seated, our wait person Dereck brought us still and sparkling water. we ordered a bottle of bubbles to start our dinner adventure.

After reviewing the menu with us and explaining the specials, we moved onto the first course. To in our party shared the meatballs, which they raved about… My wife had the prosciutto salad, which was a very generous portion… And I had the raw fish crudo of amberjack from Hawaii. Marvelous!

Our main courses were an assortment of their housemaid pastas…one pappardelle, one tagliatelle, one maccheroncelli and The squid ink pasta with lobster special. These were prepared and presented with perfection. Dereck did a wonderful job in guiding us through our dining experience.

We did score a couple of delicious desserts, one of which being a chocolate budino and the other was the bread pudding.. I had a hard time sharing the budino..It was so good.

Thanks to Alexis For stopping by and greeting our table.

Comfortable atmosphere, attentive service, some of the best pasta we’ve had this side of Italy… It’s hard to beat this. Our family was thrilled and couldn’t stop talking about it.Thanks again to all the staff, and we look forward to coming back to sample more items of your menu.

reviews.author: Tomya
reviews.text: My friend and I made a reservation for here, it was mid. Our waiter was nice. They informed me that I wasn’t able to modify my meal. I tried their food anyway. The presentation of the food was boring and the pasta my friend had was watery. Their lasagna has no unique flavor. How can you tell me I’m not able to modify my meal and your meal still lacks quality and flavor? If you like bland food with low vibrational energy this is the place for you. When you walk in the door you should leave an impression on your guest. This place needs to do better. How do you have an Italian restaurant with no Caesar salad?

reviews.author: Viktoriia
reviews.text: Had an amazing dinner at this Italian restaurant! Everything tasted incredibly fresh — you can really tell they use high-quality ingredients. We especially loved the prosciutto with melon and the duck liver pâté — both were absolutely perfect. The atmosphere was cozy yet elegant, and our server Derek was great — super friendly and attentive. A truly memorable dining experience. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Matthew Holzer
reviews.text: We came to Sevenhills to celebrate a family member’s birthday and left absolutely blown away. The staff were genuinely kind and made a sweet point of acknowledging the birthday — it meant so much. And the food? Honestly some of the best Italian I’ve ever had. Every bite was incredible. We can’t wait to come back.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

restaurants.description: Lively Italian eatery serving farm-to-table Roman fare with an inventive twist in a candlelit space.

restaurants.name: Collina
restaurants.address: 1550 Hyde St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text:
One of the best meals I’ve had the pleasure of enjoying. Everything was incredibly delicious and the service was perfect. Alexis sent us over to the wine bar across the street while we waited for a table, which was a great move! Our server was attentive but not overbearing and gave great recommendations. She was a delight.

The famous lasagnette had great flavor and the noodles were cooked perfectly. The wagyu beef cheeks were incredibly tender and paired beautifully with the parsnips and potatoes. The butterscotch budino was delightfully sweet and salty. Everything was delicious. We cannot wait to come back. Thank you for a wonderful experience!

Collina is very small inside so make a reservation, get there early, or plan to wait. It will be worth it!

reviews.author: Alexander Hammonds
reviews.text:
The lasagna is unique, one of my favorite Italian dishes ever. Instead of the usual heavy layers, this version is refined with perfectly balanced flavors and texture.

The service was warm and thoughtful.

reviews.author: Clay Stanley
reviews.text:
Very cute stop off the SF cable car. Make a reservation far in advance. I’m a local but love to act like a tourist in my own city. If you can time it right, I would recommend hopping on the Hyde street cable car and riding it directly to the restaurant!

Every single item on the food is spectacular. The raviolo al uovo is so amazing - loved every bite. Same with the lasagnette. Tiramisu is fire.

reviews.author: Barry Hashimoto
reviews.text: Classic Nob Hill -ish bistro with tasteful and dark interiors. Moderate noise, solid menu. Good for any type of dinner. Reservations are necessary, with just four seats at the bar, which sits directly across from the door and is very drafty/chilly. Not suitable for large groups unless you book the entire restaurant.

reviews.author: Liz Jones
reviews.text: Such a cute restaurant. Perfect for date night or small group (4-6 max). Food and service were excellent. Can’t wait to come back and try more dishes. The lasagnette is a must get. Scallops were so fresh and a nice lighter dish for the table. The pork Milanese was also excellent and ginormous. It doesn’t come with sauce though, so Next time I’d get the giant ravioli and the pork Milanese and dip the pork in the egg yolk. No bad dishes here. This crushed.

restaurants.name: Boa's CVC
restaurants.address: 3951 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Joan Falleiro
reviews.text: Such a beautiful gem of a place! Cute decor and vibe! Love the server Brooklyn - she was sweet. Had the pizza which was delish with the full of flavor meatballs. Good portions. Will definitely visit again and try the eggplant sandwich. Lambrusco was on point! The CVC threw me off. Now I understand why this place got high reviews.

reviews.author: jade
reviews.text:
Easily one of the best meals ive had in sf which is saying a lot because i rarely RARELY have a bad/mediocre bite in this city
This is just top tier for me
The bread is INSANEEE crispy good flavour thin but with just enough chew; the perfect sandwich bread ever
We got the margarita with chicken  the tomatoes are sooo fresh the sauce is delicate and also bursting with flavour
The fruit toast is a must: the honey is mild so it sweetens the ricotta and fruit but its still kind of complex because its not just sugary, but its not over powering honey flavour. Ive never had anything like it
Loved the taglio salad it was punchy and vingary with crunchy lettuces
NA moscato is really nice, sips exactly like a mild white wine
Ordered an Arnold Palmer with their iced tea and mint lemonade 😍😍
Already plotting on when to come back.
Amazing ingredients prepared to perfection!!!

reviews.author: Carol Pan
reviews.text: We came here after going to a special exhibit at the Legion of Honor, what a treat!  I loved the decor with dark paint and gold/brass lamps in this warm cafe'.  I had the Green Dream with grilled Zucchini, whipped ricotta with lemon zest,  pesto and pistachio, mmmmmmm... Flavors dancing on my tongue!  The service was friendly and quick.   This will become a regular lunch spot when going to the Legion of Honor.

reviews.author: David Huey
reviews.text: Boa CVC in SF has a self-service setup that works well. Housemade bread is good, pizza is tasty and more flatbreadish, and salads are fresh. The casual vibe and friendly staff make it easy to enjoy a quick, satisfying meal.

reviews.author: Alicia DiGiammarino
reviews.text: 41st and Balboa isn’t exactly a destination in SF but I am glad I made a special trip to check out CVC. I loved the bandiera chicken pesto sandwich and was glad the server talked me into the chocolate chip banana bread. They did something to the butter to make it taste so good. Maybe lemon?

restaurants.name: Lily
restaurants.address: 225 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Lizzy Masotta
reviews.text: I feel honored to have sat at the counter to watch the chef in a true flow state. Every single thing on the menu had a unique approach and was done artfully and masterfully. You can’t go wrong ordering. Cocktails were creative and well done. Dessert (hot chocolate torte) absolutely blew my mind. This place has a Michelin Star in its future.

reviews.author: Ingrid Carolina Ramón Parra
reviews.text: Gorgeous food, fresh, delicate, and soft atmosphere. The chef is so sweet he greets you and checks in on you during dinner. We enjoyed everything we tried, especially the fried dishes which are so light and crispy. Fried fish and the shaved ice were my faves from the night. Not an every day spot but perfect for when a nice dinner is in order. Coming back again for sure.

reviews.author: Hannah Arcilla
reviews.text:
Everything was amazing. You could close your eyes and point to anything on the menu and it would be good.

The restaurant was beautifully put together and had a fun and lively atmosphere. Super clean too.

The service was prompt and attentive. Really excellent overall.

We got the shaking beef salad, egg rolls, pork belly rice paper rolls, garlic shrimp noodles, and mussels shared family style. We finished it off with banana bread pudding and rainbow shave ice. 10/10 every dish was amazing.

reviews.author: Ersyla Nellajoy
reviews.text:
So understand, I have fallen into the very fortunate position of being a guest of a woman who has a list of the 100 best restaurants in San Francisco.
After some impromptu searching. The venue of the evening we landed on was lilies, and I am grateful, first of all, I am gluten-free, their Vietnamese menu is almost a custom fit.
deep fried catfish with a GF batter! (or wheat free.)delicious! I Don't run into that, ever.
I don't think there's enough room for me to praise everything that I put in my mouth, it was all so fresh and delicious. covered in fresh aromatic herbs. I've never had a pho soup so delicious;From the precious rice noodles to the luscious broth to the exquisite, thin sliced wagyu beef. if I could, I would take a stack of those shrimp crackers.
I am thinking about the cooks and the chefs that put this menu together and keep putting out this fine, Excellent food day after day after day. I feel gr8full

reviews.author: Tung Ho
reviews.text:
The menu at Lily restaurant is a true culinary masterpiece.
Each dish I tried was expertly crafted with fresh, high-quality ingredients that burst with flavor. The attention to detail in every plate was evident, and I appreciated the chef's creativity in creating unique and delicious flavor combinations.
The wine list was extensive, offering a wide selection to pair with the meal.
The waitstaff was knowledgeable and passionate about the cuisine, making excellent recommendations and ensuring that every need was met.
The service was prompt yet attentive, allowing me to fully enjoy my dining experience without feeling rushed.
If you're looking for a truly exceptional dining experience in San Francisco, Lilly restaurant is an absolute must-visit.

restaurants.description: Easygoing restaurant offering classic Vietnamese dishes, including sandwiches, soups & spring rolls.

restaurants.name: Octavia
restaurants.address: 1701 Octavia St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Phil Crest
reviews.text: I visited on New Year’s Eve and they were running a set menu with plenty of courses! Delicious food and great service. Although it was a rainy night, we sat outdoors in the covered area and it was still perfect. Very warm with overhead heaters, completely sheltered, and the candles made everything cosier. No wind or breeze as it’s fully sealed + curtains by the entrance. Thank you, Octavia! Happy New Year!

reviews.author: Max Sugerman
reviews.text: I grew up in San Francisco and live in NYC. I wanted to show my wife some California cuisine while we were in town and I came across Octavia.  We did the tasting menu and it was marvelous. Fresh, local ingredients with stunning flavor combinations. We enjoyed every dish thoroughly

reviews.author: Abril Ruhstaller
reviews.text: We went for dinner to celebrate our anniversary and had an amazing time! We started with a fresh salad, followed by a delicious pasta with pork and a perfectly cooked fish as our main courses. What really stood out to us was the sourdough bread super elastic, warm, and with that perfect tangy flavor, absolutely incredible. To make the night even more special, they surprised us with a dessert on the house for our anniversary. The service was excellent from start to finish. We’ll definitely be coming back!

reviews.author: Caroline Martin-Frost
reviews.text:
I never write reviews, but I had to come back for to say this. Best meal I’ve had in a LONG time. 10/10. Everything was amazing. The service, the wine. We had two appetizers, each a glass, each a main and shared a dessert. About $130 a person. Shocked how how well the cost was with that.

I got my meal to go and it still tasted just bad good the next day.

We got the bread with the butter, the blue cheese salad, I got the wagyu and him the cod, chardonnay wine, and the galette to share.

reviews.author: Rovik Robert
reviews.text: Did the tasting menu with wine pairing and thought it was actually incredible value, especially with hits like the soft shell crab and the lamb risotto. Fish dish was also very good. Service was friendly and conversational!

restaurants.description: Elegant New American dishes served in a polished, casual corner restaurant decked in neutral tones.

restaurants.name: CHEF XIONG KITCHEN 川香園鉄木真
restaurants.address: 2428 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

photos.attribution: CHEF XIONG KITCHEN 川香園鐵木真

reviews.author: Ms. San Francisco Real Estate Janice Lee
reviews.text:
Chef Xiong Kitchen is a fantastic spot that really delivers on flavor and convenience. The atmosphere is no-frills and casual, it feels like a genuine, family-run eatery, which adds to its charm. The service was quick and efficient, perfect for a late-night craving.

The food itself is what really shines. The scallion pancakes were crispy and delicious, a perfect appetizer. But the real stars of the show were the main dishes. The spicy wontons were mouthwateringly good, with a perfect balance of heat and flavor. The large bowl of beef noodle soup was incredibly rich and hearty, loaded with tender beef, fresh bok choy, and plenty of cilantro. It tasted incredibly authentic, reminding me of the food I've had in China.

it's not a place for a fancy dinner, but for an authentic, delicious, and convenient meal, especially since they're open late, it's a solid choice. It's a lifesaver when you need a great meal long after other places have closed.

reviews.author: Katie Leung
reviews.text:
I had dinner with my family at Chef Xiong Kitchen yesterday and we ordered several dishes: green peppercorn stewed fish, stir-fried potatoes, pig feet and chicken skewers, cold spicy noodles, beef noodle soup, and stir-fried green beans. Everything was absolutely delicious, especially the cold spicy noodles, which were a standout.

The portion sizes were very generous and offered great value for the price. Each dish was packed with flavor and had its own unique flair. The service was impeccable; the staff were attentive and made sure we had everything we needed to fully enjoy our meal.

I’ll definitely be coming back, whether for dine-in or takeout! I will also be telling friends and family to try this place out!

reviews.author: Bei
reviews.text: This is our go to place for Sichuan food. We had some special local dishes that we typically don’t find in the Bay Area, 魔芋烧鸡，乐山甜皮鸭. Super tasty. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Yi Yan
reviews.text: Stopped by after a hiking day, and felt like I hit the jackpot when it comes to flavor and value. Homemade legit Sichuan pickles, handmade dumplings and handmade fresh sticky rice cake! They even have Bing Fen (they call it boba jelly on the menu)! All the dishes are excellent and right amount of spicy. Service is excellent and attentive. Will be back again for sure!

reviews.author: Xinyu Li
reviews.text: We stumbled upon this place randomly during our trip and it was a hidden gem! Really authentic Chinese food and friendly service! I will be coming back!!

restaurants.name: Fog Harbor Fish House
restaurants.address: 39 Pier, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: L Hubert
reviews.text: What a wonderful dinner we had at Fog Harbor!  We were celebrating my friend's retirement and Shawn our server was great and joined in to make it a really fun evening!  The scallop & crab risotto (with grilled shrimp added) and crab gnocchi were both delicious!  Best seafood we've had in San Francisco!   Laughter, great customer service and wonderful food made our evening so fun!  And the dessert was fabulous too!!

reviews.author: Lina White
reviews.text:
Fog Harbor Fish House was absolutely amazing! The restaurant itself is beautiful and the view or the harbor and ocean with the sunset is incredible it makes the whole dining experience feel really special.

The food was fantastic. The oysters were perfect! super fresh and full of flavor. Everything we had was delicious and you can really tell they care about quality.

Our waiter, Andres, made the experience even better. He was excellent, professional, friendly, quick, and totally on point the whole time. He made sure everything was perfect and we felt really well taken care of even offered to take our pictures!

If you’re looking for great seafood with an incredible view and top-notch service, I highly recommend Fog Harbor Fish House. I’ll definitely be coming Back next time im in SF!

reviews.author: Junaed ABDULLAH
reviews.text:
Great convenient location on pier 39. This is a popular destination so expect wait time at the restaurant during peak times. We got lucky and was seated for early dinner around 5 pm right away.

Our table was all the way in the back on the interior side by the kitchen... Felt stuffy, but that's the trade off for getting seating right away.

Food prices were reasonably compared to Miami, our base. Famous clam chowder in sourdough bread bowl at $13 is a bargain.. Not to be missed... Delicious!

Shellfish platter for 1 had greatly prepared fresh seafood. Heavy on the butter but great tasting noodle.

Kid plates $11---what a bargain.... Great tasting too.. Dessert was spot on as well.

reviews.author: Nath Nath
reviews.text:
The food is great. Served quickly.

I love the cioppino and my baby’s clapping for their clam chowder!

Our server Pedro is so sweet & attentive. But everyone there are friendly.

Kids friendly.
They have a high chair & gave us a large paper bib, wet tissues, without being asked.

Clean restrooms.

reviews.author: Mark Sowa
reviews.text: I really wish I could give this restaurant 5 stars. The food was delicious, and the atmosphere was very upscale, though quite loud. However, the service was absolutely terrible. First of all, our waiter, Deniss (who never told us his name to begin with) never brought any bread and butter. Okay, small thing. Excusable. But then he then forgot an extra soup bowl I requested so I could share the chowder with my wife. I asked the guy who brought our food (a different guy) for a bowl. It never came. I finally asked Deniss a second time for a bowl and finally received it in between his socializing with the table next to us. After that, he came over again to socialize with the people at the table next to us. While doing so, he actually sat down on the edge of our table with us back to us while we were eating! Really?! He was totally oblivious to what he was doing and stayed there for a good minute or so. His squirming and gesturing shook the whole table. Of all the years of eating at restaurants of any quality, I've never had that happen.  And to make matters worse, his socializing was very loud. For that matter the restaurant was loud in general. I'm not sure what they could do to fix that part. Lastly, toward the end of our meal when Deniss came to see if we wanted anything else, I said no, and he immediately handed me the bill. He had already prepared it! He didn't even ask if we wanted dessert. It felt as is we were being invited to leave. Terrible, terrible service. So, 5 for the truly great food, 1 (zero if I could) for the absolutely horrible service, which equals an average of 3. Sorry Fog Harbor. I really wish I could give you better, but you have some server problems to fix.

restaurants.description: Pier 39 seafood institution with fresh fish & scenic views of the bay & Golden Gate Bridge.

restaurants.name: Iggy's Place
restaurants.address: 3519 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Marc Milrod
reviews.text:
Stopped in on a whim, glad we did. Though, their Reuben is Corned Beef vs. Pastrami, I asked for a Hot Corned Beef sandwich with mustard and a Chicken Matzo soup (cup). The cups are generous, as are the sandwiches. A half should do just fine.
Didn't have time to stick around, or we'd work through this menu, top to bottom.  A smallish shop, pleasant staff equally at ease in Ukrainian, English or Spanish. Great value, great flavors.

reviews.author: Jennifer Corbell
reviews.text: Stopped by when my usual post-vet visit spot was closed and wow! What a find! Super friendly staff to start who welcomed me in from the street. I ordered the 1/2 matzo ball soup 1/2 turkey sandwich and was blown away. Amazing depth of flavor in the broth with nice pieces of chicken and a delectable matzo ball. The sandwich was to die for. Large half portion with yummy Rize Up bread with perfect texture and crunchy sesame seeds. The turkey on the sandwich was sliced in house (I asked) and was just the right thickness to really come through in the sandwich. The bites were perfect with just the right amount of balsamic in the roasted onions, the sweetness complimented by the spicy arugula. Stop by; you won’t be disappointed!

reviews.author: Ann Imal
reviews.text:
Delicious sandwiches and salads near Golden Gate Park! A great place for a quick stop to dine-in, or to go for a picnic.

I got the Cobb salad (half portion), chicken schnitzel sandwich, and matzo ball soup. Everything was flavorful. Service was wonderful, too. The owner is super down-to-earth, sweet and friendly!

reviews.author: Ken Wong
reviews.text:
June 2025:
This place is amazing! I ordered the chicken matzo ball soup, the sardine sandwich and the vegetarian sandwich.

Everything was just super delicious. The chicken matzo ball soup had a lot of flavor. Much better than some of the well known Jewish delis. The sardine sandwich had a nice combination of fish and herbs. Good bread for a sandwich is very important. Iggy's definitely used good bread.

My mom had the veggie sandwich and she really liked it.

Service was friendly and attentive. Will definitely come back again for more delicious sandwiches and soup!

reviews.author: Chloe Doan
reviews.text: Great neighborhood addition! Came by for dinner and the ambiance inside was casual but cozy/cute. Great portion sizes, and they have an AWESOME matzo ball soup. The meatball sandwich and the schnitzel dish were great; really thought out and put together.

restaurants.name: Prubechu
restaurants.address: 2224 Mission St #A, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Amaury S
reviews.text:
Food from Guam? San Francisco really does have it all. This was my first time trying this cuisine and I was blown away. I did not know what to expect, but I got exactly what I needed: delicious dishes, great portions, and unconventional flavors that somehow worked perfectly together.

Sadly, I do not remember the names of most of the dishes, but I do remember the tinaktak. The handmade egg noodles had plenty of broth and the most incredible coconut aroma and flavor. It was next level. I could have had several bowls of that. The fried chicken was also 10/10.

On my way to the bathroom I caught a glimpse of the patio and the vibes were immaculate. I honestly wish I had eaten out there, especially since they had live music.

Such an insane spot. If I lived in SF, I would be there all the time. The servers were also very attentive.

reviews.author: Sandra Govea
reviews.text: This Chamorro Restaurant in The Mission is superb and delicious! The staff and chefs are so welcoming. My mija (daughter) and I shared CHICKEN KELAGUEN & COCONUT TITIYAS, BEEF & POTATO and TINAKTAK with homemade egg noodles. We savored each bite, I was in Guam food Heaven. I will definitely be returning with my hubby who served 2yrs at USMC Base Camp Blaz, Guam, USA.

reviews.author: Gladys Soriano
reviews.text:
I hate to say this and I very rarely feel this way about a restaurant, in fact I don’t even remember the last time I felt this way, but this was by far one of the worst dining experiences I have had. I will preface this by saying that Prubechu was at the top of my list of restaurants to try while visiting SF. I heard great things about it and was very excited to come here with my husband for our anniversary. Unfortunately, that excitement was met by the biggest disappointment. Here’s why:
After checking out the menu, our server explained to us that the fiesta table (tasting menu) is where the “chef brings out the best of the food” in 10 courses. It’s $96 per person for this option. We compared the regular menu items, which by the way already seemed unreasonably expensive ($25 for 5 chicken wings, $32 for a potato dish, $10 for pickles, etc), and we decided YOLO let’s do the feast. We were excited to go all out (it was our anniversary) and to try their cuisine. First course was a creamy potato soup. Very mediocre, and I was concerned at the decision we just made to spend $200 on dinner with this being the first course. Then a “Caesar salad”. Idk in what world that was a Caesar salad. Not that I care that it wasn’t a Caesar salad, but why are we calling it that? It was large chunks of tomatoes, very large pieces of frisée, corn, and a creamy dressing. Caesar? Also it was very lacking in flavor. Then they brought us a cheesy focaccia with super crispy edges (this was the best thing we ate there but how is that even Guamanian?) Then they brought one small shrimp tostada. For the two of us. It had an avocado cream base and cold shrimp on top. Very basic, kinda bland. At this point we asked the server for hot sauce, he brought us a (tiny) condiment cup of hot sauce and told us to use very little because it’s super spicy. He wasn’t lying, the entire dinner we consumed probably half of a teaspoon of the hot sauce (this is relevant for later). After that they brought us two tacos with cold meat and grated coconut. Again, not flavorful, and cold. Then the entrees: short rib and chicken wings. The short rib was surprisingly tough, we had a hard time cutting it. And the chicken wings had a nice crispiness to them but extremely overhyped. By now, we are absolutely shocked at what we had been served for the price they were charging. But I figured dessert may just fix all of this. Wrong. A scoop of vanilla ice cream with a little nut crumble topping sprinkled on top. That’s when I really felt scammed. $96 per person and you’re serving us a scoop of vanilla ice cream for dessert? That felt extremely lazy and disrespectful. And then get this - we get the bill and they charged us FOUR DOLLARS for the hot sauce. WHAT?!! We couldn’t believe it. We already felt the tasting menu was way too expensive for what they served, and then they can’t even give us a complimentary spoonful of hot sauce?? I have never felt more scammed in my life by a restaurant. My husband and I go out often and don’t mind spending money on nice meals, but this was beyond disappointing and the biggest waste of money. I genuinely have no idea how people are leaving such great reviews. I truly wish we felt that way because this was no cheap dinner, we only got 9 of the 10 courses for some reason, portions were small, horrible value, and just not good food. The only good thing at the restaurant was the service, the guys who served us were nice.

reviews.author: Matt Kobulnick
reviews.text: Highly highly highly recommend the tasting menu. It’s a type of cuisine you don’t often get a chance to try. You might as well get to experience the full range of it. And man did it deliver. Absolutely loved it and will be back

reviews.author: Francesca
reviews.text: Prubechu has a really great vibe and the staff was super friendly and welcoming. The chicken wings and coconut braised cassava were my favorites and the shrimp and grits where also delicious.

restaurants.description: Modern Chamorro fare from Guam served family-style in spacious digs with natural elements.

restaurants.name: House of Nanking
restaurants.address: 919 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Sadia Haq
reviews.text:
House of Nanking absolutely lived up to the hype. We ordered the mobu fish, five-spice fish, rice soup, tofu bao bun, cold sesame noodles, and apple prawn—and every single dish was exceptional. The flavors were an explosion in the best possible way: bold, layered, and perfectly balanced.

The rice soup was hands down the standout—number one. I honestly wasn’t expecting it to be that incredible, but it was unbelievably delicious and comforting, with so much depth. Second place goes to the cold sesame noodles, rich, nutty, and perfectly balanced. Third was the mobu fish, followed by the five-spice fish, and then the apple prawn, which rounded everything out beautifully.

The blend of flavors across all the dishes was exquisite—nothing felt one-note or overdone. The staff were very kind and efficient, and while everything is fast-paced and there’s often a line, they move people through quickly and smoothly.

Truly one of those meals where you leave thinking about it long after—absolutely worth it.

reviews.author: Artemis
reviews.text:
Don't be surprised if there's a line/wait to get in.

The food is quite good, very enjoyable.

The service was a bit rough. We asked for things we didn't get. There was a wine on the menu, but not available (they don't have many, so I'd of preferred if it was lined off the menu, or we were told beforehand it wasn't available).  I wasn't given a menu to choose another drink and the staff were pushy in suggesting 1 particular drink.  The staff yell questions at you. It isn't a good feeling. It's tough getting staff attention.

I get "hustle"/ lunch rush environments, but this had elements of  too little staff, too much work. As a patron, it felt stressful.

Even though the food is great, it's not a place to chill and have a  relaxing meal.  We were here Valentine's Day afternoon.

We had the Eggplant and Sweet Potato (great eye appeal and tasted delicious), Apple Chicken (it is not basic, it's comfort food), Sizzling Beef, delicious;  Chili Oil Pork Wontons and Pork Confit Eggrolls got us started.

I do like the business signage. Visible inside and out there are mentions of the business being seen in Matrix 4 and pictures with Keanu Reeves.

Seating is "family style". While we had our own table, we were sandwiches between a party of 2 to the left and a party of 3 to our right. You'll be elbow close to a stranger, if that's not for you, you can decide accordingly.
I DID see at least one table on its own.

You go up to the counter to pay. Check in with the line, because it intersects with the bathroom line, so you don't wait for one thing, when you want the other.

reviews.author: Avery Sliwak
reviews.text: I went here for lunch.. the food is definitely not traditional but it grew on me. I've honestly never had anything like this before, seriously. And everyone loved it. Some dishes weren't quite for me but if you come here, there will definitely be a dish for you.

reviews.author: Dwayne “Cisco kid” Ohelo
reviews.text:
House of Nanking is a true San Francisco classic. The food was excellent—full of bold, comforting flavors that clearly come from decades of experience in the kitchen. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared and well balanced. Blosome tea and deep fried pork rib was excellent and the fried rice!

The service was great as well: attentive, friendly, and efficient without feeling rushed. Even though the restaurant itself isn’t very large, it more than makes up for it with its rich history and character. You can really feel the legacy of this place the moment you walk in.

Overall, House of Nanking delivers outstanding food, warm service, and a timeless atmosphere that makes it well worth the visit.

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text:
Amazing American-style Chinese food! The honey crispy beef was insane. The cubed beef was so tender and rich- it paired perfectly with the acidity of the garlic bok choy. Absolutely delicious, though the portion of meat was a bit small in comparison to the veggies. The crispy pork belly bun was incredible, too. I’d love to do the chef’s recommendations one day but it felt like overkill for a single diner on a Wednesday morning.

I went right when they opened at 11am but they were still setting things up. I came back around 11:30 and was seated immediately. There were only a few other customers so I wasn’t rushed at all. Go during the week day if you can! It’s worth it!

restaurants.description: Known to have lines out the door, this long-standing spot serves Chinese chow in cozy quarters.

restaurants.name: Hong Kong Clay Pot Restaurant
restaurants.address: 960 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Anirban Misra
