Also really enjoyed the octopus and the chicken confit enchiladas.

The inside is stunning - decorations are very interesting and lively.

reviews.author: The Reviewer
reviews.text: Their sauces and dressings are good. Fried avocado tacos are great with the habanero aioli. Young coconut appetizer was good if you like sour lemony sauce. Sweet Potato would have been better if it were baked or maybe bbqed - the dressing is good. Cauliflower tacos were good. Chiles Rellenos was okay - it felt too oily.

reviews.author: Syamantak Roy
reviews.text: This is the second time we visited and definitely loved it like the first time. The ambiance is great, food excellent and the servers warm and welcoming. The tacos are very fresh and tasty. The enchiladas well prepared and heat salsa options to go along with it. The churros were perfect with the chocolate dip. Usually very busy so made a reservation before heading there. Definitely recommend the place for a cozy dinner.

reviews.author: Marcos Ciarrocchi
reviews.text:
This is the second time we come and unfortunately we were rushed out in slightly over 1hr.

We had a reservation at 7:30pm on a Friday night, got seated at 7:40pm and by 8:40pm we were out.

The server didn’t even offer dessert or other drinks. The check was on the table as soon as we finished our plates and they checked every 2min if we had put our card. The whole service dynamic felt off.

Ambiance is fun, margaritas were great.
Fish tacos were very good, the mole enchiladas lacked flavor.

reviews.author: Katie Laskowska
reviews.text: The fish tacos are incredible. If you’re a fish taco lover and have had them from all over like me, absolutely get these. I’m so picky and these were fantastic.  The chicken tinga quesadilla tasted good but was so disappointing small. And the beer and drink pricing is high. $9 for a can of beer and $6 for a Mexican coke. Cocktails were all $18. This place gets busy so try to reserve ahead of time!

restaurants.description: Knickknacks decorate the colorful walls of this venue known for inventive Mexican small plates.

restaurants.name: El Rey Taquiza Artesanal
restaurants.address: 2491 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Dharmy
reviews.text:
visited for Lunch big dinning area and staff very welcoming, the atmosphere is great with classic 80's pop playing at time of our visit
good menu selection i went with the lamb Tacos and draft beer, the food arrived fresh prepared well presented. the lamb was fall apart with salsa full of freshness and flavour, the dipping gravy was perfect just enhanced the lamb flavour.

good Gluten free option easy to order with little fuss

this is a great value perfect casual dinning spot

reviews.author: Jayasimha Nuggehalli
reviews.text: Visited here for dinner. Ordered chicken taco and veg quesadillas. Service was quick. Food was very tasty. Price was reasonable.

reviews.author: Ted Eicher
reviews.text:
One of the most generic restaurants  I've ever eaten in. The service is just plain horrendous. The server taking my order was just as neutral as can be, no greeting or anything. She purposely added more items to my order to increase the overall bill. Example being I ordered chips and salsa, but she brought chips, salsa and guacamole. The guacamole is separate and a additional $6. If you don't indicate that you don't want handmade tortillas for your tacos they will do it automatically and you'll be charged a dollar extra for each taco. Very dishonest business practices going on here. The server forgot to bring my glass of water three times, place was practically empty at the time. The carnitas tacos and el pastor taco had no flavor whatsoever. I could taste the tortilla, onions, cilantro and salsa. The meat was completely bland. The chips were good, the salsa and guacamole were mid. This place is decorated to match the food, generic and bland. I'm a really good tipper, but I left no tip to this shady server playing games with the bill.
Between the dishonest billing and awful food best to stay away from here.

reviews.author: Christina Michaud
reviews.text: Literally the best. I just tried every taco I could get my hands on around the bay area and this was the best. I am praying I never forget this place. Amazing. 100 Stars lol. I wish I lived here. Worth it. The salsa was sooo good. I order the vampiro. It came stacked. Delicious. I am like wow rn. Why was it empty. There should be a line around the corner for these tacos. Beautiful inside. Beautiful music. I'm sad I am leaving but I am so full off 2 tacos. If I ate 1 more right now I would just be stuffing myself. I didn't get a drink but they have bar. I need to bring all my friends here some time soon. This place deserves its own parking lot.

reviews.author: Sarah Gross
reviews.text: Great service, very kind staff. Had a couple Tacos Estilo Tijuana with chicken. Very good, especially with the handmade corn tortillas. Highly recommend!

restaurants.name: Taqueria Pancho Villa
restaurants.address: 3071 16th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Gina Davis
reviews.text:
Never give this place a tip, the menu prices keep going up and they will not give you the extra food that you pay for. Extra meat; you still have the same amount of meat you would in the regular.
The food is good but the service is very shaky, you have to watch closely or you will get got/ jipped/ ripped off ‼️ so as you can see in the photo I paid for extra meat it's on the receipt but they did not give me extra meat this place is really shady
and it depends on what time of day you go weather you wait 10 or 25 minutes for your food

reviews.author: Shakarri Haynes
reviews.text: We came to San Francisco for the Lowrider Parade in the Mission District. We came here to eat lunch before heading back home to San Jose. Their food is always fresh and delicious.

reviews.author: Derrick Liang
reviews.text: the bbq pork spoke to me and it delivered, great portion. the charred jalapeño was nice too combine. perfect texture balance. nice solidity and oiliness mixing with tortilla, and full of cheese. just great. then the guitar guy came in was a bonus

reviews.author: Marie Norman
reviews.text:
I paid for extra meat but as you can see in the photo they did not give me extra meat this place is shady watch them very closely
The food is usually good, the service can be really crappy, and the food also depends on if they make your order as you requested. I've been coming here since I was a young child I'm now in my mid 30s. I'm disappointed today every bite has a whole bunch of fat and tendons; so I'm literally chewing my burrito and spitting out what's supposed to be all the meat. And this is the whole first half of my burrito. I had it cut in half and wrapped separately. Only to get to the end of my burrito and find that there's no meat

reviews.author: Parin Kothari
reviews.text: Roomy and spacious Mexican eatery with “not bad” vegetarian options supplemented by a full salsa bar. The tofu tacos is good in quantity but the tofu is too soft. The staff is cheery and the place is clean.

restaurants.description: Humble counter-serve eatery with ample seating serving burritos, tacos & tortas, plus agua frescas.

restaurants.name: el Mil Amores
restaurants.address: 2780 21st St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Anirudh Sudarshan
reviews.text:
One of the best plates of food I've had in a while.

We tried the cdmx bowl and the chilaquiles. We also ordered the tres leches pancakes.

The savory dishes were 10/10. The pancakes were good but we felt they were short on the tres leches syrup

The only complaint I have is 3 tables were empty for a while but it took the staff a long time to seat us.

reviews.author: American Angler
reviews.text:
In my quest for the best enchiladas in SF I found myself at the el Mil Amores Mexican Restaurant.  el Mil Amores is an unassuming little place tucked into an area I rarely visit, but great things can come in small packages.  So, let me just say this...The Enchilada was Amazingly Delicious, the flavors melded together  as if God himself was in the kitchen!
5 stars and worthy of a two hour drive!  As a matter of fact...they were so good, and after I had eaten, I got an order to go for the next day!  ☆☆☆☆☆

reviews.author: My Tenderloin Kitchen
reviews.text:
We went for breakfast at El Mil Amores and it was such a wonderful experience. The atmosphere was bright and welcoming, and the staff made us feel right at home.

My favorite part of the meal was the café de olla. It was sweet, spiced, and comforting, exactly how it should be. I also loved the CDMX Plate, their breakfast sampler that included house chilaquiles, my choice of meat, scrambled eggs, home potatoes, and the most delicious concha French toast. Every bite tasted fresh and full of flavor, and the portions were generous without feeling heavy.

It is the kind of place where you want to linger over your coffee and look forward to coming back to try more of the menu.

reviews.author: Jubal Rodriguez
reviews.text: Came here on a Tuesday ~10:30am and waited around 15 mins. The food was so delicious. We ordered the Cdmx plate and tres leches pancakes. The cafe de olla reminded me of abuelita's coffee. The place is very clean and the staff is attentive and kind.

reviews.author: lori sartori
reviews.text: The food is crazy great! There is almost always a wait so be prepared but it is well worth it. If you get there closer to when they close they may be out of things. Even after an long wait, no one ever complains. They do not have a liquor license yet but who's cares?

restaurants.name: La Cumbre Taqueria SF
restaurants.address: 515 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: David Murillo
reviews.text: This place was the first burrito I had when visiting San Francisco and wow I definitely did not make the wrong decision in coming here. This was the best burrito I have ever had and while I haven't been to all the popular burrito spots, this has been my favorite thus far on my trip. The food is all very fresh and they grill the meats right in front of you. They also have three options of salsas that are all great. Also, while it may not be the usual order, I also got the fajitas and the steak was delicious. Not only that, this place has a history to it, which I always love to see when I go to a place. I would highly recommend this to anyone in the San Francisco area!

reviews.author: C Wang
reviews.text: Most massive $8 dollar super taco I ever had in the entire state of California, very cute spot  with a water fountain , has plenty of parking and a restroom

reviews.author: Martin Marquez
reviews.text: Food and customer service was excellent.. Good portions on food. I had a regular carne asada quesadilla. Almost too much food but I managed to chomp it up...  this place needs a little updating; light fixtures were old and they need new menus..

reviews.author: Jax Lucier
reviews.text: I was told this was the place to go for the most authentic mission burrito experience, and they were right. I wound up getting a burrito over two pounds. The thing was massive and exactly what I needed after a long day. This place was on Man Vs. Food and a few other shows, and I can see why. The service was fast, and the food was good. What more could you ask for?

reviews.author: Auggie Aagii
reviews.text:
I'ld say dont come here eat food not really good i can recommend not this place. Meat is chewy dry cold. not fresh food at all
RUde people work over there. rice was smelly.

restaurants.description: Unassuming Mission standby wrapping huge burritos & other Mexican classics with late hours.

restaurants.name: El Metate
restaurants.address: 2406 Bryant St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

photos.attribution: ブラウチガム ベルト

reviews.author: Tee Ponsukcharoen
reviews.text:
There is so much to say about this place but here are some highlights:
- The decor of this place is beautiful. Down to earth yet thoughtful and colorful.
- The chips are so creative. The puffy ones are amazing with beautiful color. I usually discard most of chips but I need to hunt down for them this time.
- So much variety of salsa and sauce. It is my first time having a warm salsa as well. It is incredible! It is such a service mind to provide all accommodations for all tastebuds.
- The food itself is big and delicious. My quesadilla is packed with carnitas. I wish it can be a bit more crispy but it is also fulfilling. My friend got a seafood plate and it can feed 2 people. All dishes we got are under 20 dollars, which is a rare steal in SF.
In short, this is my new go-to spot for Mexican food in my beloved San Francisco!

reviews.author: Arthur Bouffard
reviews.text: Banging quesadillas with huge portions. A great selection of sauces and toppings too. The staff is super sweet and they have plenty of options. The seating and overall atmosphere is great and I would totally recommend stopping by for lunch

reviews.author: Jonny Chin
reviews.text: Been here a few times and I really enjoy their daily specials. This most recent time I got their chicharron burrito special and it was delicious. It was so good that I forgot to let my phone eat first. I absolutely loved the variety of texture in each bite. Also, their salsa bar is both convenient and has quite a selection. Seating is easy to find too! Definitely recommend this neighborhood staple!

reviews.author: Raymond Ngu
reviews.text: The meat selection was limited, no lengua. I got the burrito with al pastor. The rice was undercooked and the meat didn’t taste like al pastor. On the positive side the chips were pretty good.

reviews.author: Geo
reviews.text: Hate to report that the food no longer tastes the same. Both the enchilada sauce and salsas were watery and missing salt/spice. They didn’t have that authentic, rich Mexican flavor. Food did not taste fresh at all.  When food is missing something, I typically use salsa to improve the taste but it was just too sad. I remember this place being my go to years ago. Unfortunately, no longer a gem.

restaurants.description: Upbeat outfit with a colorful vibe offering burritos, fish tacos & other Mexican classics.

restaurants.name: Taqueria El Farolito
restaurants.address: 2950 24th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Manny Ellison
reviews.text: Yesssssssss the Mission is alive and well. I’m from the mission and getting burritos as a kid was like a weekly ritual. Burritos and tacos are a part of my childhood. The mission and taquerias are iconic and I love this place. Totally mix of Latinos, younger folks, and everything in between. I got a chicken burrito, regular with black beans. My wife got the carne asada quesadilla. Their sauces are hella good. The green salsa is spicy and good. We just had water there but they have a huge selection of different beverages. My daughter goes her on the regular. Support a taqueria today! 🌯🌮❤️

reviews.author: Albert Vital
reviews.text:
I’ve heard only good things about Taqueria El Farolito and boy did they deliver! Nothing fancy about this place just straight up real good authentic Mexican food! Serving is huge and tasted soooo good and service friendly and quick. Flan is amazing as well. It’s located in the Mission area where there’s lots of Mexican restaurants but I think this resto is one of San Francisco’s best Mexican food ever served! Will come back again for sure! 😋👍🏼
(Street parking only guys)

reviews.author: Sooraj Nair
reviews.text: The burritos and tacos were absolutely delicious! The portions were huge, and the prices were super affordable.

reviews.author: Waller Marcel
reviews.text: Taquerias El Farolito is a must‑visit for anyone craving authentic Mexican flavors in San Francisco. I tried the carne asada tacos, al pastor burrito, and a side of chips with guacamole, and everything was deliciously fresh and flavorful. The meats are well-seasoned, the tortillas soft and warm, and the salsas give just the right amount of heat. The staff were friendly and efficient, keeping things moving even when the restaurant was busy, which made the experience smooth and enjoyable. The casual, lively atmosphere adds to the charm, and prices are very reasonable given the generous portions. Parking nearby is manageable, and the restaurant feels welcoming for families and solo diners alike. A true local favorite worth every bite.

reviews.author: Yolanda Morrissette
reviews.text: My coworker introduced me to this place a few days ago and I had the steak and shrimp burrito. It was one of the best burritos I’ve ever had and the chips are one of the best chips I have ever had not too salty. The flavor was totally on point.

restaurants.description: Basic, counter-serve joint serving Mexican street fare like tacos & burritos until late at night.

restaurants.name: La Corneta Taqueria
restaurants.address: 2731 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Dominique Ashe
reviews.text: The crew who work here are always fast and adapt your order however you want. I’ve gone here since I was in high school and the food is just as delicious! There’s usually a soccer game (sometimes baseball) playing on there numerous flatscreens but they don’t take away from a pleasant conversation while dining. I would choose this taqueria over any other in SF!

reviews.author: Rhett Burden
reviews.text: Stopped by La Corneta today and I’m so glad I did! The place has that authentic neighborhood taqueria vibe, nothing fancy, just good honest Mexican food done right. I went with the super carne asada quesadilla and wow, this thing was massive! They definitely don’t skimp on the filling here. The carne asada was perfectly seasoned and tender, with just the right amount of char. The chips and salsa were a nice touch too. The green salsa had a good kick to it, and the red one was more smoky and mild. The location on 24th Street puts you right in the heart of the Mission, so it’s perfect for grabbing a bite while exploring the neighborhood. Will definitely be back to try their burritos next time!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

reviews.author: Yueyi
reviews.text: The portion is great and the taste is awesome. I ordered 4 tacos but couldn’t finish them. Very authentic :)

reviews.author: Joshua Brathwaite
reviews.text: Great place with great food! Outstanding selection of food on the menu and regular street tacos comes with beans which a lot of taquerias do not usually offer! Place was clean with a separate bar and tv screens playing soccer or baseball! Nice heathy portions at a great value, worth every penny! Place is spacious with food always hot and delicious!

reviews.author: Mat Lit
reviews.text:
One of the better carne asadas in the city. Burritos are always very hot. Green sauce is very creamy and tart and the red sauce has an addictive umami, spicy taste.

The restaurant itself has a great vibe—like you’re eating in a plaza. A solid choice for burritos. Tacos are interesting because they include beans.

restaurants.description: Large portions of burritos, tostadas, carne asada & more served up in a bright, no-frills space.

restaurants.name: Gallardo's
restaurants.address: 3248 18th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Rae Marie
reviews.text:
This has been the best food ever
We never have trouble getting a table here if there's a wait it's normally around 20mins max
Every thing on the menu is delicious
Priced right for this economy and the serving is great
We come here about every few months or so. The wait staff is so very friendly and helpful
Eating outside is great especially on the sunny days

reviews.author: Vismaey
reviews.text:
Stopped by this place on a Saturday afternoon. They have a nice streetside patio, calm but lively, perfect for a relaxed meal. The staff were all friendly and attentive, and the manager or maybe the owner was personally checking in with every table, which you don’t see often. Really solid customer service all around.

I went with the spinach omelette, a great option if you’re skipping meat. The portion was generous, and the warm tortillas on the side were easily some of the best I’ve had. You could tell they were freshly made.

It’s tucked away on a quiet, safe street with bike parking nearby, which worked out well since I rode over. Prices were also very reasonable for the quality and service. Overall, great food, good energy, and people who genuinely care about what they do. Definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: E T
reviews.text:
Food: 3.5 - 4 stars(need to try other things)

This place is basic but good. Came for breakfast, we had the huevos divorciados and mexicanos. Tortillas were thick and handmade!  Salsa was tasty.

I will be back to try more things :)

reviews.author: Johnny Valdez
reviews.text: I am sitting in this restaurant and am completely awestruck. This food is breathtaking, I am from San Antonio, Texas. I grew up around this style of food. “But” they are hands down the best I have tasted. Every bite is a blessing from these men. They are very kind and welcoming. Me and my wife came early when they were nearly setting up, but still welcomed us in. It was very fast service but not rushed as in quality. The quality of service and food is very good. I know they will be around for a very long time, due to the quality all the way around. If you are reading this and have not came in then you should. And if you have, you need to come again.

reviews.author: Jp. Perez
reviews.text: No disrespect to anyone I gave this place a couple tries before I decided to write a review. i come from a traditional mexican background. I'm very familiar with traditional recipes.When it comes to mexican food. I understand some of these dishes may be altered to taylor more, to the American palate. With that said the only thing I can honestly recommend from this place. Are there soups. I tried their enchiladas.I try their fajitas and the dishes overwhelming plates full of glop. Overbearing sauce with tons of cheese and sour cream, and i'm not sure if the sauces are actually made from scratch or our ofna can for the enchiladas. I had the same waitress twice. It looked like she was upset, not very friendly at all. but it is what it is. My review is based on the food, but service should matter as well. I can see this place is very well established, and I'm sure the local people enjoy it for what it is. So to summarize for someone who doesn't live in San francisco would not recommend for anybody to drive out here and I am not coming back.

restaurants.description: Laid-back, family-operated eatery providing homestyle Mexican dishes, plus sidewalk seating.

restaurants.name: La Palma Mexicatessen
restaurants.address: 2884 24th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Georgio Atana
reviews.text:
Ordered super burito, paid for extra meat,  got almost  no meat in the burito, but a lot of beans.. very small buritoin a first place, . plus left $3.50tip,
Very disappointed,  and the small bag of chips was dry old chips. Only the salsa was worth.

reviews.author: Miae Aramori
reviews.text: Amazing pupusas, chicharon and horchata.  There are even tables outside so you can dine al fresco.

reviews.author: Mark K
reviews.text: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ = Solid (1=Avoid, 3=Standard, 5=Can't Miss). There's no seating here, just pickup. But in my search for good Mexican food in the city, La Palma takes the top spot for fast, casual. The spicy refried beans here are a must if you are getting a burrito and the orange sauce is good! Prices are also fairly reasonable given the quantity, but I would stick to the burritos here (tacos and super tacos were difficult to eat). Street parking only for La Palma.

reviews.author: ari salomon
reviews.text:
If you want masa to make your own tortillas you can order at the deli counter by the pound. It’s not sitting in the display cases. And then you pick it up at one counter and then you can pay at the third counter.

There is the “simple” kind or the tamale kind that has lard in it. You can make your own tamale with the simple kind and add oil or butter.

This is the authentic deal!

reviews.author: Ted Eicher
reviews.text:
Got the super burrito with carnitas, black beans, rice, cheese, guacamole, pico de gallo. Cost was $16.50. Too much rice/beans in the entire burrito. Not enough cheese. The ingredients tasted fresh, except for the carnitas. The carnitas was very dry, tough to chew and very bland(see photo).
Really disappointing. If the carnitas was fresh and flavorful, with less rice/beans, this would've been a great burrito. For $16.50 a burrito needs to taste great, not the case here. Based on the lack of quality on the carnitas, and too much rice/beans, I will not be back.

restaurants.description: Open since 1953 & selling to-go Mexican foods, including housemade tortillas, plus Latin groceries.

restaurants.name: Papalote Mexican Grill VALENCIA
restaurants.address: 3409 24th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Aakash Hegde
reviews.text:
There are a lot of options for vegetarians here!
I had the marinated tofu burrito bowl with guac and it was pretty good! I would recommend!
They also give a bag of nachos with a dipping sauce that was phenomenal.

reviews.author: John Brooks
reviews.text:
Really Disappointing.
We've been fans of Papalote for years. This is the first order in several months and perhaps it was a bad night. Whatever, there were problems. Overall the food was meh. My memory is everything being flavorful. My Chile Verde burrito lacked flavor and frankly tasted watery. No chile flavor at all.

The other order was Carne Asada Tacos. I added Rice and Beans for which I was charged $4.75 each. I was expecting side orders of each. No, apparently that was for the included rice and beans - see the photo.

All in all it was the kind of visit that has totally put me off the place going forward. Try the food truck across the street instead.

reviews.author: Joshua Brathwaite
reviews.text: I tried a super breakfast burrito huevos a la mexicana which was delicious and full of flavor. My burrito was very filling and the ingredients tasted very fresh. I like the different breakfast burrito options that they have which will have me returning to try out more. The young lady was super polite and service here was overall great. They have inside seating which can seam a little dark inside but they do have an outside patio deck which is great to enjoy the sunny weather in the Mission.

reviews.author: Jessica Irwin
reviews.text:
I found this place because I was looking for a vegan burrito and I was not disappointed! I tried the soyrizo.

The burrito was huge and so tasty and filling. The salsa was outstanding and I want to bathe in it. The staff and owner were so friendly and welcoming.

In the future, I’ll try the shrimp burrito because I’ve heard good things.

There’s an outdoor seating area and plenty of tables inside.

reviews.author: E T
reviews.text:
Unreal how bad their uber delivery quality is.

I ordered one of those “uber one” chicken burrito buy 1 get 1 deals from here, it was a bean and rice burrito with the SADDEST amount of chicken ever… can’t believe they let this go out the door…

If you have to cut corners like that to offer a “deal”, then just don’t offer the deal?!

I would have gladly paid full price for a real chicken burrito instead of the bait n switch!

EDIT:
Per owners comment on the protein, i’ve been reading other reviews, skimping on the protein  is a common theme, so no, not just me, but thanks for the reply.

restaurants.description: This no-frills taqueria stuffs its burritos with veggies, famed salsa & other fat-free ingredients.

restaurants.name: Taqueria Guadalajara
restaurants.address: 4798 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94112, USA

reviews.author: Alex Snazell-Holding
reviews.text:
Would never trade this place for anything else. I hope they never change anything about what they do, because they serve the BEST mexican ever.
Rivals mexican in the mission.
I usually get a tomato based  super burrito, with carne Asda and pinto beans. Absolutely amazing. They give you free tortilla chips with your meal, and they also have extras you can take for free e.g. Lime wedges, salsa, jalapeños...

reviews.author: Obie Tric
reviews.text:
When it comes to finding one of the best burritos in town, Taqueria Guadalajara is a go-to favorite. My companion and I frequent this location often, and it never disappoints.

The Food

We ordered the chicken burritos, and as always, they were excellent. The portions are generous, the flavors bold, and each bite is loaded with tender, well-seasoned chicken. The green salsa is a standout, vibrant, flavorful, and the perfect complement to the burrito. Plus, every order comes with chips and access to the salad bar, which offers plenty of fresh options to customize your meal.

Service & Atmosphere

The staff is consistently friendly, welcoming, and quick. Whether it’s a busy lunch rush or a quieter evening, they always show up with great attitudes and efficient service. It’s the kind of place where you feel like a regular, even if it’s your first visit.

The atmosphere is simple and straightforward, nothing extravagant, but the colorful murals on the walls add a nice artistic touch that reflects the restaurant’s cultural roots. It’s a casual, no-frills spot where the focus is on good food and good vibes.

Final Thoughts

If you’re looking for consistently great burritos, friendly service, and a laid-back environment, Taqueria Guadalajara is a must-visit. The food is always fresh, flavorful, and satisfying, making it one of the best spots in town for authentic Mexican fare. Highly recommend!

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.author: Charles Reynolds
reviews.text:
If you’re hungry and your friends and family are too, make sure to check out Taqueria Guadalajara for some fantastic cultural food!

The burritos are delicious. My companion and I ordered the Regular and Super Shrimp Burritos, along with chips and guacamole. The main difference between the regular and super burrito is the addition of sour cream and extra salsa in the super one. The large shrimp are grilled just enough before being wrapped up in the tasty burrito. You can choose from a variety of tortillas—flour, tomato, spinach, or wheat—and fill it with rice, beans, cheese, and your choice of hot sauce or pico de gallo. I’d recommend the flour tortilla.

The guacamole side dish is huge, packed with diced tomatoes and onions, and the chips are pretty tasty too.

Taqueria Guadalajara came highly recommended to me, and now I’m passing that recommendation on. If you’re in the mood for a good burrito, definitely give it a try!

reviews.author: Shawn S.
reviews.text: It was good for a quick bite for lunch. I ordered a super burrito with all the trimmings. But if you have multiple orders and add or omit different ingredients to the order, you may come up short and become unsatisfied on what you spent $16 and some change on.

reviews.author: Paul Harrison
reviews.text:
This is the best Mexican in San Francisco. Completely authentic. Full of flavour. Unassuming in its presentation but full of genuine flavours.

I highly recommend.

restaurants.description: Counter-serve Mexican joint offering burritos, tacos & quesadillas amid murals of Mexico.

restaurants.name: The Italian Homemade Company
restaurants.address: 716 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Espie Vergara
reviews.text:
table of 3 - easy access to seating and service. v lively yet chill at the same time. server was great, fast and clean, loved her accent.

food was excellent, my friend had the gnocchi with pasticciata, 2nd friend had veggie ravioli w butter and sage, and I had the fettuccine Alfredo w salsa parmegiano.

the fettuccine was immaculate, creamy, cheesy, rich, and buttery. it was probably one of the better fettuccine's I've ever had. the gnocchi as my friend describes it is very pillowy and rich, melts in mouth. the ravioli as my other friends describes is earthy, creamy, and delicate.

overall great experience, fantastic food.

reviews.author: Viktoria Weizel
reviews.text:
We came here in the late afternoon and were surprised to find that both the carbonara and the gnocchi were already sold out — two of the dishes we were most looking forward to. It felt a bit odd that some of the most popular items weren’t available so early in the day. Maybe the kitchen wasn’t fully prepped yet, but it was still disappointing.

The restaurant has “Homemade” in its name, so naturally you expect something truly special — fresh, well-crafted pasta and a homemade feel. But the experience didn’t quite live up to that expectation. It felt more like a small chain (there is at least one more location), and while the food wasn’t bad, it didn’t match what you’d imagine from a place emphasizing “homemade” in the branding.

We ordered the lasagna and the penne with pesto. The penne portion was very small — surprisingly small — and absolutely not justified for the price. The lasagna, on the other hand, was a very generous portion and tasted good. The pesto pasta was also tasty, but nothing exceptional.

I ordered a Spritz, but it wasn’t an Aperol Spritz. It tasted good, but it seemed like it was made with lemonade or something similar, so it wasn’t quite what you would expect.

The staff seemed very busy and focused on getting things prepared, probably because it was still early in the shift. They weren’t unfriendly, just quite occupied and not very attentive.

Overall, a mixed experience: the food was okay, but the limited menu availability, uneven portions, and the gap between the “homemade” promise and the actual quality make it hard to rate higher.

reviews.author: Linda Maestre
reviews.text: A small unassuming restaurant with outdoor seating, limited seating inside.  Homemade pasta and sauces, yum!  It was cold outside, so we opted to eat inside.  Lunch was served quickly, the lasagna was a huge!  We enjoyed the food and the friendly banter of those working in the kitchen.  My daughter accidentally knocked her drink over, much to her embarrassment.  They were quick to take care of the spill and we're quite charming while taking care of it.  We would eat here again.

reviews.author: Sona Tigranyan
reviews.text: The food is fast and delicious, and the prices are really good for homemade pasta. The sauces were tasty. I did not love the oven baked tomato salad, it was just okay, but the pasta for that price and quality is definitely worth it. There is limited seating inside, but outside there are plenty of shared tables.

reviews.author: Millie the Cat
reviews.text:
I have been here a few times before. The food (and the vibe!) is always good and the staff is always nice. They make their pasta from scratch next door, so it tastes fresh and tasty. The gnocchi are just chewy enough.

HOWEVER, I personally think it’s a bit pricey for the portion you’d get. That’s why I don’t come here too often. But it’s definitely a must try in North Beach!

restaurants.description: Italian deli & market stocking housemade pasta, imported cheese, charcuterie & more in homey digs.

restaurants.name: North Beach Restaurant
restaurants.address: 1512 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Zach Weinstein
reviews.text:
Nice experience on a Tuesday night. Ordered fritto misto, lasagna, tiramisu. I drank Powers, neat. They provide bread with cheap olive oil and balsamic, complimentary.

I asked for a table away from the live music but they put us right in front of it, so we had to ask to move. The table we moved to wasn't much quieter, but had more romantic lighting so we settled down there. Great portion size on the fritto misto.

Server, Nino, was good - a little anxious towards the end, he was trying to check out - but he gets a pass. The pasta is fresh, though missing an element of structure. Tiramisu was disappointing, again lacking structure and missing depth of flavor.

A decent place for a romantic dinner, though maybe not for a critic, or to bring a group to enjoy the atmosphere. The music and lighting complemented the vibe.

reviews.author: Vera Maslova
reviews.text: North Beach Restaurant is truly the best spot in the neighborhood. I eat there all the time. Their dishes are incredible — the tortellini, the seafood pasta, the lasagna, the prosciutto with burrata — everything is outstanding. The service is exceptional. Francesco is the best host, always so attentive, and the whole team makes you feel genuinely cared for. They put so much love into every dish. I really, really recommend this place.

reviews.author: Aaronette King (Chef AK)
reviews.text: Marcello was one of the best servers I've had in a while. Attentive but not over bearing. Knowledgeable but let me feel like I knew a little too. Food was outstanding. Creme Brulee wasn't set but the flavor was there. Really good. Our new SF date spot. Definitely worth the drive from the town.

reviews.author: Holly En
reviews.text: Delicious, well prepared Italian with house made pasta and fresh ingredients. We enjoyed falanghina wine, caprese, tortellini, pappardelle and tiramisu. Staff was attentive, courteous, helpful and professional. Waiter was able to suggest a fantastic wine to accompany dinner. Sourdough bread service provided with excellent olive oil and balsamic. Hopefully, they will continue to provide their services for many years to come. Well worth the cost.

reviews.author: Caris Sandino
reviews.text:
The food was delicious and service was excellent. The establishment is clean and ambience is nice (great for groups or family dinners).

It was my first time here and I will definitely be back soon.

Thank you so much for such delightful and thoughtful dishes. My palate appreciates it! Shout out to the cooks in the kitchen, and to Apo, for his wine recommendations. I highly recommend this place.

PS
They have great vegetarian options,
great wine and the most delicious pistachio baklava I've ever had in my life! TYSM!!!!

restaurants.description: Traditional Tuscan fare & housemade vino served in an upscale setting by old-school waiters.

restaurants.name: Sodini's Trattoria
restaurants.address: 510 Green St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Lori Adams
reviews.text: We had amazing Service here.  My spaghetti and meatballs was good. As as the salad.  People at my table, had the Jeannine and clamps that they said was excellent.    Our server went out of her way to make us feel comfortable, even though we had a two-year-old at the table.   They have a lovely red wine in carafe’s for a good price.  Not a bad place to check out. They have been around for years.

reviews.author: Hilda Liu
reviews.text: I came here because a friend recommended me their gnocchi. Oh my gosh, it was so good. We also got the seafood pasta. The portion was very big for both plates. The server were very friendly and serve as well. They don’t serve any dessert and we luckily brought our own cake. They provide a fork to us. The environment is full of special decorations from the owner, and I love that they put a wedding picture of the owner in the restaurant. I will definitely come back again and try more food.

reviews.author: Rue
reviews.text:
Sodini’s  boasts a charming old-school Italian ambiance that initially sets a pleasant tone for the evening. However, my experience was mixed, and I didn’t quite receive the dining experience I had anticipated.

I asked for a dish recommendation, and based on the waiter’s suggestion, I ordered “the most popular dish.” Unfortunately, it turned out to be just okay, lacking the flavor and excitement I had hoped for. To make matters worse, the waiter never returned to inquire how the dish was.

The bread served at the start of the meal was also disappointing—cold and hard, lacking the warmth and freshness one expects at an Italian restaurant.

As we were leaving, an awkward moment occurred when the bartender yelled at my husband, saying, “We’re closed.” My husband replied, “We just ate here; we’re leaving. I’m waiting for my wife who’s using the bathroom.” This interaction felt unprofessional and added to our dissatisfaction.

Another notable aspect was the open-concept kitchen, which allowed us to see the kitchen staff. I noticed them casually sipping from wine bottles, which detracted from the overall dining experience.

When it came time to settle the bill, I requested an itemized receipt. Unfortunately, what I received was nearly illegible, resembling chicken scratch rather than a clear breakdown of charges. To add to the confusion, I noticed an unexpected charge on my bill. When I inquired about it, the staff explained that the credit card machine had added a percentage, which felt a bit off.

Overall, while the atmosphere was enjoyable, the service, food quality, and professionalism did not live up to the expectations set by the setting. I would recommend considering other options if you're seeking a truly satisfying Italian dining experience, as this place feels more like a tourist trap than a genuine Italian restaurant.

reviews.author: Tim Hilton
reviews.text:
It's a great cozy Italian atmoshere but with limited seating. We arrived about 6 pm on a weekday, without reservation, and there was ample seating. By the time we finished (7-ish) there were people waiting for seats.
The food was great and prices very reasonable. I recommend the house wine if you don't want authentic Italian wines. A very cozy atmosphere. Of course parking can be a pain (street parking in little Italy of SF) but if you don't know this ahead of time, parking generally sucks anywhere in San Francisco.

reviews.author: Duane Steinhorst
reviews.text:
Straight out of central casting! Old school and absolutely un apologetically old school family Italian food!
These beautiful people have been making Italian food the old school way for over a hundred years!
Get the lasagna, get the house salad..go home happy.
The lasagna was rich in cheese, the marinara rich and the ricotta they I used is spectacular.
My wife had the veal parm and it was beautiful.
Trust Alex when she makes a recommendation! She’s served in most of the restaurants in the area before landing at SodinoI’s and knows the menu well!

restaurants.description: Hefty portions of Italian basics served up in a throwback space filled with Sinatra memorabilia.

restaurants.name: Bocconcino
restaurants.address: 516 Green St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Mena
reviews.text: 10/10 experience! Had no reservation but we arrived at opening time and we were grateful to get a table on a Saturday night. Food was incredibly authentic and I was impressed as a self proclaimed Italian food connoisseur. Pasta was very fresh, sauce was so well balanced, and tiramisu like grandmas. One of my top 5 favorite restaurants in the city

reviews.author: Artemis Huntsman
reviews.text: This is a nice Italian restaurant, small intimate. I liked that there were no TVs but the music was so loud. My friend said their potatoes were over salted but recommended the calamari! I liked the chicken parm and fettuccine. About $200 for 4 people

reviews.author: Melody
reviews.text: Best Italian food, best gnocchi, I have ever had. Limoncello sliver and Gorgonzola gnocchi with prosciutto added. Homemade, authentic, so fresh. So good I wiped the sauce from the plate with extra bread. Wonderful atmosphere and warm staff. The prices are completely fair. Highly recommend. I really enjoyed my experience.

reviews.author: Rodrigo Rojas
reviews.text: My girlfriend and I were visiting San Francisco and decided to explore Little Italy and found this restaurant and decided to try it, best decision we could have taken. The restaurant is small, but welcoming. It is nicely decorated, with soft music playing. The staff and owner welcomed us with welcome arms and were both friendly and helpful. Our server was attentive and friendly, both the waiter and the owner were attentive if we needed anything. They have a big wine selection, and all of them are great. The food was amazing, really tasty and enjoyable, perfect amount as well. The dessert was lovely as well and the coffee was on point too. If you come to San Francisco, please come by, it feels like a neighborhood restaurant and the vibes are amazing, you feel like home. 10/10.

reviews.author: Andrew Tang
reviews.text:
During my first visit, the pasta, vegetables, grilled fish, and steak at Bocconcino were of decent quality, but pricey. However, the quality really dropped during my second visit, with poor quality pasta sauce, small portions, and bizarre tasting ravioli. The rice dish was small and not good tasting at all. We all have our ups and downs, but I leave a two star review for now.

The interiors of the restaurant are high quality works of craftsmanship.

restaurants.name: Fior d'Italia
restaurants.address: 2237 Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: H T
reviews.text:
We would definitely come back. Good food, massive portions, very fair prices. Make a reservation.

The pasta dishes seem to be more regional than just Napolentean/Roman what I normally see in American Italian restaurants. The noodles were good even the next day. Get what you like, they were all done well.

Hard to tell but very huge basket of calamari. Best for 5 or more people to share! I also liked their free bread and mushroom main, though we got it as an appetizer.

Service wasn't slow for us like some reviews mention. Quaint ambiance with some live piano.

reviews.author: Ed Summerfield
reviews.text:
Overall, unprofessional and never again. Maybe it was one good but these people seemed all over the place.

Atmosphere
--------------------
Cold
-Wear Thermals, the long pants, thermal socks or wooll socks, and thick leather shoes
No woven
Wear a jacket.

Host
--------
Wasn't there to seat us so we had to seat ourselves

Waiter
-----------
Came once in awhile. Other waiters had to help.
He held the credit card.. I had to get up and walk around to the register to look for it.

The bartender had to help look for the waiter.  He eventually came back to us.

He made us feel as though he took the card to make a copy somewhere so I'll check the purchases. He then said we can't take any bread home because he's not allowed where on Columbus street, every restaurant gives bread to take home.

EVALUATE
EXCELLENT
Antipasti Caldi - Hot Appetizers
-------------------------------
Calamari in Cesta

Vitello - Veal
--------------
Piccata al Limone e Capperi

OK
Insalate e Piatti Freddi - Salad and Cold Entrees
----------------
Insalata Lattuga Romana- blue cheese is pungent. NOT worth the price.

reviews.author: Michelle Brady
reviews.text:
We had an incredible dining experience at Fior d’Italia, America’s oldest Italian restaurant, and it truly lives up to its legacy.

If you’re searching for authentic Italian cuisine, fresh pasta, and a memorable dining atmosphere, this is the place to go!

We started with the Bruschetta, which was outstanding—perfectly toasted bread topped with ripe tomatoes, fresh basil, and high-quality olive oil that tasted straight from Italy. For our main course, the Tagliatelle was the star of the night. The pasta was fresh, handmade, and cooked to perfection, with a rich and flavorful sauce that made every bite exceptional. We ended with an Affogato, and it was the perfect finish—creamy gelato “drowned” in bold espresso. Simple, classic, and delicious.

The ambiance is warm, charming, and full of history. A live singer took requests and performed Italian classics (including “That’s Amore”), which made the entire evening feel magical—like being transported to a trattoria in Italy.

Service was fantastic from start to finish—friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about the menu and wine pairings.

If you’re looking for authentic Italian food, fresh pasta, terrific service, and a unique historical dining experience, you must visit Fior d’Italia. Perfect for date night, celebrations, or anyone who loves classic Italian done right!

reviews.author: Jason Fair (Nomadic Nerd Project)
reviews.text: This is such a great restaurant. The atmosphere has that old school Italian vibe with white tablecloths, nicely dressed waiters, and impeccable service. The desserts we had were spot on. They have homemade pastas for a really good price as well. We were just enjoying a late night cocktail & dessert this time.

reviews.author: mahboobeh mahmoodi
reviews.text:
Good food, but dishonest billing practices and poor communication ruined what could have been a great experience.

We visited this restaurant for lunch after seeing an appetizer photo on Google Maps that looked amazing. When we asked the waitress which menu item it was, she told us that dish was no longer available. We asked which appetizer was similar, and she showed a few options. We chose the Burrata con Pomodori Basilico e Olio and placed our main course orders.

A few minutes later, she kindly returned and said the chef would make something like the photo we had seen. We were impressed by their willingness to accommodate us — it felt like great service.

The food was good, but when we got the bill, there were two extra appetizers added, totaling about $70. When we questioned it, the waitress insisted we had ordered three appetizers and started arguing instead of calmly clarifying. She claimed we should have known the items weren’t complimentary. After a frustrating exchange, she apologized only to close out the payment.

Later, we noticed that other reviewers have had similar experiences — the restaurant sometimes offers to make something “off-menu” without mentioning the price, then charges unexpectedly high amounts.

If you visit, make sure prices are confirmed before accepting any off-menu offers and double-check your bill.

restaurants.description: Landmark trattoria with roots in the 19th century, serving classic Italian fare, cocktails & wine.

restaurants.name: Cotogna
restaurants.address: 490 Pacific Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Manik Kumar
reviews.text:
Had lunch at Cotogna and ordered gelato for dessert. For the main course, we had the Agnolotti del plin and the tortilla with sunshine and rosemary—both were amazing and full of flavor.

The ambience was great and the restaurant felt kid-friendly, which was a plus. However, the service was not satisfactory for the price point. Everything took a long time, from placing the order to receiving the food, and even getting the check took about 10 minutes.

If you have ample time and don’t mind waiting, the food and atmosphere are excellent—but be prepared for slow service.

reviews.author: Maria
reviews.text:
Cotogna is an absolute gem in San Francisco’s dining scene, a perfect balance of warmth, sophistication, and unforgettable flavor. Every dish we tried was a masterpiece, both in presentation and taste.

The prosciutto puff pastry was the perfect start, light, flaky, and beautifully balanced with the savory richness of the prosciutto. Then came the raviolo, which was pure heaven on a plate, delicate pasta filled with creamy perfection and that luscious egg yolk center that makes every bite feel indulgent.

The agnolotti pasta was another standout, with perfectly al dente pasta and a filling so flavorful and comforting it almost defies description. And the lamb pasta, wow. The depth of flavor, the tenderness of the lamb, and the subtle herbs made it one of the most memorable pasta dishes I’ve ever had.

Cotogna doesn’t just serve food, it creates an experience. The service was warm and professional, the atmosphere cozy yet refined, and every bite reminded us why this restaurant is so beloved.

If you love Italian cuisine done with heart, precision, and creativity, Cotogna is a must-visit.

reviews.author: Eric Broockman
reviews.text: What an amazing place! Three of us had a beautiful lunch. We’ve traveled extensively in Italy and wanted to visit while vacationing.  Great Choice!  Service knew the menu, was helpful, friendly and professional and attentive. We had two different unique salads and both were delicious. I enjoyed my decadent ravioli / ricotta w brown butter and egg yolk center. I very much enjoyed my panna cotta with hazel nuts also. You owe it to yourselves to try Cotogna. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Debbie Kwok
reviews.text:
Did not plan to fine here and had no reservation. But Cotogna was able to accommodate a table of 1 outside seating. Ordered the 2 antipasti dishes: Bluefin Tuna Crudo, Sformata. Both were excellent. The bluefin crudo was fresh had perfectly balanced in taste & texture. Sformato was a tasty, light, savory flan. Then the meal ended with a delightful Cannoli, which was a perfect combination of the crispy pastry shell and the no-so-sweet creamy filling with chocoate bits and nuts sprinkled on the ends. They are made to order, and i was informed that the cannoli is not an everyday offering on the menu. So if you see it on the menu, order it!
Service was impeccable. Friendly and helpful with great suggestions. Highly recommend this restaurant.

reviews.author: Q Chow
reviews.text:
Wow, this place surprised me. The ambiance, the service and the food were amazing!

We ordered the squid appetizer, the raviolo (giant ravioli), and the agnolotti. Both the squid and the agnolotti had amazing flavor and almost tasted like it was Asian inspired. My wife loved the raviolo, she really like the sweet and saltyness of it.

I can't wait to come back here!

restaurants.description: Popular eatery serving spit-roasted and grilled meat & fish, wood-fired pizza and house-made pasta.

restaurants.name: Mona Lisa Restaurant
restaurants.address: 414 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Miu Takeshi
reviews.text:
Came here for dinner and it wasn’t too crowded — we got seated right away.
The tables are a bit close together and seating feels slightly tight, but nothing uncomfortable. The place was clean overall.
We ordered two pastas, a pizza, and two glasses of wine and were definitely full by the end.
The pasta sauce is house-made and actually really good — you can tell some care went into it.
We didn’t order an expensive wine, so the red wine was pretty standard.
Overall a solid spot. Would come back again and would recommend it to friends.

reviews.author: Ed Summerfield
reviews.text:
Complimentary
---------------
Bread is excellent but needs butter. Because I don't like the Olive paste or the oil and vinegar.

Waiting for food and service is a long time. So this is great for a conversation place.

But if you're by yourself, it's not good unless there's nobody in the restaurant.

64° or less outside, bring a jacket As it's cold inside

EXCELENT
Chianti wine- smooth

Zuppa E Insalata
-----------------
Insalata Alla Cesare-Caesar Salad.

Homemade Pasta Con Carne
---------------------------
Spaghetti Polpette- meatballs are perfect

GOOD
Sparkling water is Pellegrino.

Zuppa E Insalata
-----------------
Insalata Della Casa- ask for no olives. They put too many in unless you're an olive fan

reviews.author: ASHOUSTON
reviews.text: Stopped in here for dinner in December 2025 (right before New Years). It was recommended. I was visiting SFO and figured why not. The location is relatively easy to find or get to especially if using Uber (or Waymo). Reservations recommended, but walk-ins are possible as I did see a few who did. The restaurant is definitely all about the Italian cuisine and ambiance. Potraits and renditions of Mona Lisa drape the walls. Lots of themed Italian art pieces and decorations. The service was good. The waitstaff attentive and timely. The food was equally good however, it was 'expected'. I mean the options are regular dishes you find in just about any Italian restaurant. It was well made and cooked and very tasty. Almost homemade like. Will try it out again when in the area.

reviews.author: Yassi Sadri
reviews.text: Amazing authentic Italian food. I will start by saying the service was absolutely perfect. The waiter didn’t let our glasses to be one sip empty, everyone was addressed as Bella with great attitude. I had the privilege to meet the owner and he is awesome. We tried fried calamari, burrata Fresca (which by the way gets imported every week from Italy), the crab arancini (out of this world delicious), and many other menu entree which were all amazing. Highly recommend this place.

reviews.author: Bill O
reviews.text:
My wife and I had been going to the original Mona Lisa at 353 Columbus for several years now.  It closed, unbeknownst to us when we made our reservation for Valentines Day, in October 2025.  The owners, who also owned/own Mona Lisa Mare E Monti renamed that restaurant Mona Lisa.  The food is still great.  Highly recommend the Capesante.  The service for us was a little slow for a restaurant in the US.  We just pretended we were in Italy where it would have been just about right.

The pricing I selected was just for food. Our bill was higher because we got wine, a must at an Italian restaurant IMO, too

restaurants.description: Renaissance-inspired decor sets the scene for homemade pastas, pizzas & other Italian specialties.

restaurants.name: Piccolo Forno
restaurants.address: 725 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Stephanie
reviews.text:
After our trip to Alcatraz and an afternoon of shopping at the Wharf, we worked up quite an appetite and decided to try Piccolo Forno. The restaurant is small and cozy, and as many have mentioned, there was a bit of a wait since we arrived later in the evening, but we didn’t mind at all. The incredible aromas coming from the kitchen made it worth it.

Our table ordered a great variety of dishes including pizza col salame, lasagna classica, ravioli di zucca, pappardelle al ragù, and ravioli di aragosta. For dessert, my friend enjoyed the cannolo siciliano. Overall, the food was delicious, comforting, and clearly made with care.

Service was friendly overall. There was a small hiccup when I was initially served the wrong dish. When I mentioned it, there was some confusion as I was told it was what I had ordered. However, my group did hear my order, and as a vegetarian, the dish I received wasn’t something I could eat. That said, this didn’t negatively affect our experience. The rest of the staff were very accommodating, and the owner handled the situation
wonderfully, which we truly appreciated.

I don’t enjoy confrontation and never want to make an issue, but I felt the way the situation was resolved spoke volumes about their professionalism and care for guests. We left full, happy, and satisfied and would absolutely return.

reviews.author: Selena Dsouza
reviews.text: There’s a reason this place has such amazing ratings — it’s hands down one of the best Italian spots I’ve been to! The portions are HUGE (come hungry), and the owners are incredibly warm and welcoming — they truly treat you like family. Such a wholesome, feel-good experience. Definitely coming back for more!!

reviews.author: bartika dutta
reviews.text: A cosy restaurant serving genuinely delicious Italian fare. The meal began with complimentary bread and olive oil. The burrata arrived like a cloud - creamy, soft, and beautifully balanced with briny olives and sweet cherry tomatoes. The lobster stuffing of ravioli di aragosta was perfectly minced, and luxurious. The spaghetti alla carbonara was silky, and lifted by generous chunks of guanciale. We ended with an affogato that delivered the perfect finale - bold, sweet and beautifully comforting. The pastas did take a while to reach our table, but knowing everything was freshly prepared made the wait feel entirely worth it. The staff were warm, cheerful, and genuinely welcoming throughout our meal. This is one spot I’ll happily return to, no hesitation.

reviews.author: Alison Ferro
reviews.text: I was lucky to grab an outside table before the lunch rush fully hit. The restaurant quickly got busy but felt I still received good service. Food was delicious and staff was responsive. Would definitely stop by again when I’m in SF.

reviews.author: Roman Vysochin
reviews.text: This restaurant instantly became one of my favorite restaurants in San Francisco! The food is amazing and the atmosphere is just like you’re somewhere in Italy. These guys serve you with the real Italian passion and the last but not least you MUST try their Tiramisu, it’s from a different planet!

restaurants.description: Casual neighborhood trattoria with Italian classics & espresso in colorful, contemporary quarters.

restaurants.name: Capo's by Tony Gemignani
restaurants.address: 641 Vallejo St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Hilda Garza
reviews.text: The portions are good for two persons. We ordered a César Salad with anchovies, a baked reguetoni Genovese, a vanilla ice cream and a tiramisu and everything resulted 70 USD. I loved the service, the ambience, the food and price made this place one of my favorite restaurants in Vancouver. We arrived after 6:30 pm so couldn't order anything from the  happy hour menu. I loved this reslafy

reviews.author: Jae Jun
reviews.text: Solid spot if you are in the mood for pizza but don’t want to wait in line for Tony’s. We had a few apps and the award winning Detroit style pizza with vodka sauce, chicken and broccolini. Not my style of pizza but the crust was amazing and it was still enjoyable.

reviews.author: Casey Casas
reviews.text: We found this place from searching for Chicago style pizza & we’re so glad! Everything was absolutely delicious and the service was amazing! The outdoor seating was really comfortable (with some options under a roof). We want to come back & try the Detroit-style pizza!

reviews.author: Syd Grant
reviews.text:
So many great reviews and we wanted a great Italian dinner for our last night in SF. Thankfully I got reservations online because this place gets busy.

When we walked in it felt like we were transformed back to old Chicago. The plush leather seats, dark atmosphere and mobsters pictures on the walls.

The staff were awesome! Our server was “N” but can’t remember her name. Spectacular 💫
She was helpful and so friendly. We decided on a couple glasses of wine (very tasty too) and the Caesar salad. When it came out my eyes bulged out. It’s HUGE!!! Omg it was scrumptious with fresh Parmesan and the anchovies on top. LOVED IT!

She helped us pick the Old Chicago (think cracker crust). The deep dishes were way too much for us to finish. It didn’t take long to come out and it was simply amazing as well. Loved every single bite.

You have to stop here for the food, atmosphere and service!

reviews.author: dalyn preston
reviews.text: Sensational deep dish pizza, great service and an amazing atmosphere. I’m only visiting for the weekend and I went back the next day. Best pizza

restaurants.description: Speakeasy-like Italian restaurant & bar specializing in Chicago-style pies & handcrafted cocktails.

restaurants.name: Tommaso's
restaurants.address: 1042 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Allison Bauser
reviews.text: I stumbled upon this restaurant while staying in the area. I'm originally from NY and have a high standard for Italian food. The service was amazing, warm and inviting waitstaff as well as the hostess. It's a very cozy family styled restaurant which makes you feel right at home. The food felt homemade and definitely was satisfying with large portions. My tummy was happy and full. While we were sitting the owner came and checked in on our table. She was very modest in her accomplishment of taking over the restaurant and was happy to share that she locally sourced and also seasonally sourced some of the ingredients shown on the menu. I had an amazing experience, meeting the owner was so warm and inviting and the food was to die for. Highly recommend to visit this place!

reviews.author: Ryan
reviews.text:
Ordered pasta for takeout. After I brought the food home, I noticed that the Parmesan that came with my order was moldy. Very obviously too, so it took some negligence for this to end up with my order. And I doubt this is the only moldy cup of Parmesan in the restaurant.

I don’t know if they just don’t care about their takeout orders or if it extends to their dining room too, but sending out moldy food is unacceptable.

If you have a takeout order, check your food for mold before you leave the restaurant.

reviews.author: S A
reviews.text: Per the recommendation of a friend, I came without reservation to San Tommaso. I waited very little for a table on a clearly very busy night. My server Tomás was incredibly kind, competent, and made suggestions that were perfect for what I was wanting. It was truly a lovely dining experience. Not to mention he was managing many tables at the same time- very impressive. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone! Gracias al porteño Tómas :)

reviews.author: Tristan Thomas
reviews.text: Tommaso’s stands out among some classic North Beach restaurants and has loads of charm, delicious food and wine, and it feels like you arrived home. I was greeted by the staff with a welcoming vibe like, “ah, there you are… we’ve been waiting for you all day.” As you step down into the space you see the quaint tables and snugs, with white table clothes, laid out beautifully. In the back corner, a gorgeous oak-burning brick oven rests in the same spot since the day it was imported from Italy, in 1935. You may want to come to Tommaso’s for the pizza, but I recommend also ordering the Gnocchi! They are little tasty edible clouds, pillowy and impossibly soft. Not heavy. The warmth and intimacy of the restaurant accompanied my first gnocchi bite, made me pause and tell my guest, “this is right where I want to be.” I hope you enjoy Tommaso’s as much as we did.

reviews.author: Lynn R
reviews.text: My wife amd I had dinner before a show. No big sign out front. A nice little family restaurant with a short flight of stairs behind the front door. The service was very good and friendly. The food was very good. The owner stopped by to visit. Everyone is very nice. A good ambience.

restaurants.description: Family-run institution (since 1935) firing up thin-crust pizzas in its original wood-burning oven.

restaurants.name: Cilentano Pasta Bar
restaurants.address: 579 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Alison M C
reviews.text: Incredible pasta dishes--fresh and light and lovely. I had the truffle gnocchi, the homemade sausage rigatoni and the mushroom agnolotti. Next time I'd skip salad and desserts and just order double the amount of pasta. It's that good. Owner is a friendly, funny guy. Bill came out exactly right. Can't emphasize enough how delicious the pasta is. I spent a ton including a bottle of wine last night and I'm seriously considering going back tonight to try more of the menu.

reviews.author: J H
reviews.text:
We came to Il Cilentano Pasta Bar four years ago and had such a great experience that we had to come back — and it’s still just as amazing! My daughter loves their gnocchi, it’s perfectly soft and full of flavor. The pizza, fresh bread, and appetizers were all delicious too.

The restaurant has such a cozy, authentic Italian feel, and the staff are friendly and welcoming. Definitely one of our favorite spots in San Francisco for fresh, homemade pasta! 🇮🇹🍝

reviews.author: Sally
reviews.text: We both got the spaghetti carbonara. It was very weird and neither of us liked the sauce. It was not egg flavored at all it was just too much pepper. Bacon did taste really fresh but would never come back here so not worth it.

reviews.author: Alex Marzouca
reviews.text: Just a lovely place to get dinner. It’s all home made and santo is a lovely person. This is my go to spot for pasta in San Francisco. Could not recommend it enough. Fair prices and amazing dessert.

reviews.author: Matty Easton
reviews.text:
Il cilentano has one glistening trait that helps it stand out against just about anywhere else in SF: it’s one of the few spots open late while still offering table service. Paired with some solid Italian pasta, and it makes for the perfect late-night eat.

The interior is clean, albeit not particularly inviting; we sat next to the window to people watch the people walking by, which was romantic except for the harsh overhead lighting.

Notwithstanding, the food was all solid. We enjoyed the fettuccine bolognese but the stand-out was definitely the spicy rigatoni paestum. The menu had some comically aggressive warnings on the backside, which I couldn’t help but imagined were provoked by an owner or manager having enough of customer bullshit.

Indeed, it turned out our waiter was the owner, and he had a surprisingly charming no-nonsense vibe underneath his Italian accent. When asking for wine recommendations, he almost incredulously responded, “Everything on the wine list is worth getting, I’m the one that chose the menu.” It made us laugh, and the unfiltered confidence came off endearing. It turns out he was right — we DID enjoy the wines we ordered.

I’ve bookmarked this as a place to return to next time I’m hungry, it’s late, and I feel like something more wholesome than bar food.

restaurants.description: Floor-to-ceiling windows line this cozy Italian venue that dishes up pizzas, pastas & desserts.

restaurants.name: Tony's Pizza Napoletana
restaurants.address: 1570 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Sukhpreet
reviews.text: Tony’s Pizza Napoletana in San Francisco is a must-stop for any pizza lover. The menu has tons of styles to choose from, and every slice tastes fresh, authentic, and made with real care. The crust is perfectly cooked — crispy on the outside and soft inside — and the ingredients are high quality and flavorful. The atmosphere feels lively but still comfortable, and the staff is friendly and knowledgeable when helping you pick a pizza. Whether you’re a classic margherita fan or want to try something unique, Tony’s always delivers an amazing pizza experience. 🍕

reviews.author: Aryan
reviews.text:
Delicious, authentic, romantic, and delicious!!
Went for dinner around 7:30 pm on a Thursday with my girlfriend and there was no wait for a table outside. The patio Christmas decor was so pretty and the ambiance of the place was so warm and welcoming.
We couldn’t decide between the New Yorker, the Burratina, and the Porto; all award winning pizzas.
The burratina is a thick crust pizza on the focaccia so we didn’t get that since it would’ve been a lot of food for us. We ordered the Porto. The crust was so thin and had the perfect balance between fluffy and soft. The ndjua was delicious on it but this pizza is more on the umami side of flavors. We wish we got one of the classic pizzas, but definitely coming back to try it out.
We also got the Pasta Genovesa, which we lovedddd. The pesto was super rich and every ingredient in the dish was perfectly balanced. Fresh sundried tomatoes, artichokes, pine nuts, goat cheese. Honestly would come back just for that.

We also got the Tirramisu for dessert! Absolutely must must get!

reviews.author: Stone Soup
reviews.text: When I say this was the best Margherita ive ever had. I'm not even exaggerating.  The sauce was so delicious.  We had to try the royal Margherita as well. It came with a fresh basil plant and oil to put on. It's not as big as the Margherita but packs a punch. We can't wait to come back to the bay and eat here again. I highly recommend this place. Service was good even though busy. Prices were reasonable as well. Especially when its so delicious.  Thank you for such a great meal.

reviews.author: Sylvia Sauber
reviews.text: First time trying the hyped up Tony’s and overall delicious authentic pizza. Fabulous cocktails to start. We got the New Yorker and Porto which are Neapolitan style crust. Also got Detroit style which was way too much bread in my opinion—my brother perhaps should’ve ordered toppings on it. Service was a bit slow but to be expected at a crowded, popular spot. Will return to try salads & other Neapolitan pizzas!

reviews.author: M15 Taka
reviews.text:
Man, I miss SF. The best city in the world. Walking through North Beach brought back so many memories and is even more magical that what I remember. A lot of San Francisco's charm is the array of restaurants and the neighborhoods they are in.

Just look at the picture...

Tony's pizza does not need my review, obviously. They have a firmly planted existence here and they are wholeheartedly deserving of all the praise and stellar reviews on their legendary pizza.

My review is for all the professional servers. They were very attentive and efficient. Casual observation showed they all worked together seamlessly and enmeshed into the vibe of this buzzing room belonging to the most pleasing pizza.

Much props to the Tony's team for an excellent experience!

restaurants.description: Bustling Italian eatery with varied pizza options from coal-fired to Roman-style, plus beer on tap.

restaurants.name: Sotto Mare
restaurants.address: 552 Green St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: T
reviews.text: Had a very warm welcome by staff and was seated in the downstairs banquet area. Atmosphere is lively and the food was so fresh. We started out with the crab salad and it was super refreshing. Crab cioppino was what we came for and each ingredient was so fresh and cooked perfectly. We got the halibut from the daily fish special and it flaked perfectly. The risotto was surprising -- so fresh and packed with flavor! We had an amazing time and will definitely be back!

reviews.author: Sy W
reviews.text: Such amazing Italian food!! Ordered a half dozen of east + west oysters. I personally prefer east cuz it’s sweater. Absolutely favorite is the seafood risotto, so much seafood and fresh. Same with their signature seafood soup(?) forgot the actual name of the dish. But it’s served with so much fresh seafood. Crab, shell, squid, mussel. Seasoning is absolutely spot on. Not too heavy or creamy. Such delicious food for amazing price! Def a 10/10!! Server was so nice. Just be aware they have added a 4% service fee. I didn’t take it out because I feel they deserve that and to better survive in sf as such an amazing Italian restaurant.

reviews.author: Richard H
reviews.text: Located in the heart of Little Italy, North Beach San Francisco.  This is my favorite place for classic cioppino, fresh oysters and marinated Dungeness crab!  They offer raw oysters either on the half shell or as shooters, west coast or east coast species with a spicy red sauce.  Be sure to ask for their fresh horseradish.   For starters, we also love their bay shrimp Louie salad, which is big enough to share with 2-3 people.  I also recommend their marinaded, cold whole Dungeness crab, which comes in an olive oil, lemon garlic sauce.  Be sure to order their marinaded”Best Damn Cioppino” , which is served in a large bowl:  Dungeness crab, mussels, clams, squid, and scallops.

reviews.author: Beullah Escalante
reviews.text: Such a local gem! Our good friend took us here for my partners and i's pre engagement lunch! We got the signature crab seafood soup and tye risotto and omg, was it heavenly! My partner also tried some oysters. You can tell the quality of the seafood in one bite! I highly recommend going there for small or moderate size groups but I think they have a banquet space in the basement.

reviews.author: Viktoria Weizel
reviews.text:
We came here to try the famous Cioppino, and it absolutely lived up to its reputation. The portion is very generous — officially for two people, but honestly perfect for sharing between three. The stew was incredibly flavorful: a rich, garlicky tomato broth packed with crab and seafood. Truly delicious and a must-try.

We had the oysters as an appetizer. They were okay — fresh enough, but nothing special. Since there are so many great places in the city to eat amazing oysters, I wouldn’t necessarily recommend ordering them here.

The service was friendly and attentive. We were seated downstairs in the cellar area, which is thematically decorated and actually quite charming. The restaurant was very busy upstairs, but still well-managed.

Overall, highly recommended — especially for the Cioppino, which is absolutely worth coming here for.

restaurants.description: Neighborhood spot with nautical decor and a menu of pasta, cioppino, and other Italian-style seafood.

restaurants.name: Ideale
restaurants.address: 1309 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Hilda Liu
reviews.text: We found this place randomly when we were scrolling around little Italy. There were plenty of wine choice and the owner knows a lot about wine and wine pairing. The environment feels very cozy. The seed were big and spacious. Although it's full of guest, me and my friends still had a nice time talking and eating. The pastas were great. Very special noodle and I think it's house made. I would definitely recommend to get the bucatini and I will return to try something else

reviews.author: Alberto Gonzalez
reviews.text:
Amazing more than wonderful… the real Italian food. The pizza was delicious, perfect Italian style and the pastas out of this world… we loved and I find it on Google Maps so I just was giving a shot and it was excellent, we are not local we was visited San Francisco from Miami; highly recommend this place !!!
Thank you so much guys, specially our sever Gino 🙌👌

reviews.author: J P
reviews.text: First time here and it was satisfying. Staff were attentive and friendly. The pasta was delicious, however the beef could’ve been better cooked. Wasn’t a fan of the dessert but would definitely go back and try other items off the menu.

reviews.author: Yun Choi
reviews.text: I really enjoyed my gluten free spaghetti.  It was cooked to perfection, unlike many places where they overcook it and only offer gf penne or rotini.  The server made an excellent wine recommendation.  My friends also enjoyed their gluten filled meals.

reviews.author: Yong Zheng Xin
reviews.text:
we had a reservation at 8pm and there was no one at the receptionist, and the hallway to wait was really crammed. we waited till 8:45pm (there were two waiters coming to get us but they simply acknowledged that we had reservations without attempts to get us seated.) at the end we just left the restaurant because even if we were to get a table, it is unlikely for our food to come before 9:30.

advice for the restaurant: if you don’t have the capacity to serve that many people, do not open reservations.

restaurants.description: Unpretentious trattoria presenting Roman specialties, homemade pasta & an extensive wine list.

restaurants.name: Firenze By Night
restaurants.address: 1429 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Nicole Lee
reviews.text:
8/10
a small intimate restaurant in north beach, perfect for a date night.

the bruschetta (9/10) was solid, and exactly what you'd expect from bruschetta lol. idk how you could mess it up, but it was good!

we got the linguini tuttimare (7/10). it lacked a little flavor, and the mussels and clams were fishy and tasted old :/ presentation was great though and it came out hot!

the penne mare e monti (9.5/10). for what it is, it was great. the shrimp was perfectly cooked and the sauce had the flavor that the linguini lacked.

apparently this place is known for their gnocchi so i'd want to come back and try that. i'd recommend firenze! very solid and very reasonable prices in north beach :)

tiramisu (9.5/10). whipped cream was fresh, so good. there was a strong coffee taste and bits of sugar. man, so delicious. my only critique is i wish the bread was softer. it tasted a bit stale
