reviews.author: Mathew Heart
reviews.text:
Tonight, we dined at Firenze By Night, a cozy Italian gem in San Francisco's North Beach neighborhood, and it was nothing short of spectacular during our Dec 2024 visit.
* Caesar Salad: Absolutely perfect! The greens were crisp, the croutons were perfectly crunchy, and the dressing was balanced with just the right tang and creaminess.
* Alfredo Pasta with Jumbo Prawns: Creamy, rich, and indulgent. The jumbo prawns were cooked to perfection, adding a fresh and luxurious touch to the dish.
* Fettuccine Pesto with Chicken: This was, without a doubt, the best pesto I've ever tasted. The vibrant basil flavor, paired with tender chicken and perfectly cooked fettuccine, made it unforgettable.
* Gnocchi: Light, pillowy, and coated in a delicious sauce--this award-winning dish lived up to its reputation.
* Bread with Olive Oil and Olive Paste: The olive oil itself was great, but the olive paste served with it was out of this world! It added an intense, savory richness that elevated the bread to another level.

The ambiance was warm and inviting, with a classic Italian charm, and the service was friendly and attentive throughout the evening. However, the place can get loud if there are too many large parties, which might affect the overall experience for some diners.

Overall, Firenze By Night delivered an exceptional dining experience. From the unforgettable pesto to the incredible olive paste, this is a must-visit spot for authentic Italian cuisine in San Francisco. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Jacobo Uztariz
reviews.text: I made a reservation after a friend recommended it to me, he told me this place was an incredible choice to go for after proposing to my gf. The service was phenomenal, Fabio was on point every time, and gave us a warm and comfortable and memorable experience. Nadia was amazing receiving us. This restaurant exceeded all of my expectations. Thank you truly, the food transported me to the Tuscany, flavors after flavors. 10/10

reviews.author: Vineet Sinha
reviews.text:
We made reservation reservations for Friday evening at 6:30 PM and we’re probably seated very friendly and welcoming staff

We got the two dishes below and a side of focaccia

Our server was attentive and friendly, and told us about the specials

Try to ask for a table away from the arterial thoroughfare so you don’t end up, brushing elbows against people passing you by

This place is right in the heart of Little Italy with a lot of fun things happening nearby

Food was excellent

reviews.author: Sushmita Vigraham
reviews.text:
Sooo, we went to have a nice Italian dinner because of the great reviews. The customer service was great—the staff were lovely and attentive!
1.	“Italian” orange soda – We asked for a non-alcoholic Italian drink, and they suggested this. Unfortunately, it turned out to be a poorly made mocktail. Still, we gave them the benefit of the doubt.
2.	Bread for the table (charged, of course) – Amazing! The bread was fresh and the pesto sauce was excellent.
3.	Grilled vegetables – Sadly, this was just a cold salad.
4.	White sauce vegetarian pasta – I requested this off-menu, and they agreed but charged it as a special. Unfortunately, it tasted like something made half-heartedly, just to get it out of the way. Definitely below average. [edit: look at the amount of butter/oil that was used in the white sauce pasta.]
5.	Tiramisu – I could actually taste the granulated sugar. I’ve had much better..

Well , just an extremely disappointing experience. Maybe it’s just me, but I won’t be going back or recommending this place. I personally find it overpriced for very mediocre food.

restaurants.description: Dinner-only ristorante serving traditional Italian pasta, fish & meat dishes with wine.

restaurants.name: Original Joe's
restaurants.address: 601 Union St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Lisa Wang
reviews.text: 10/10 meal and experience. At a super busy time (6:45 ish) we were seated immediately at the counter, which I’d highly recommend. Gives you an appreciation for how hardworking and skilled the cooks are. The food was immaculate. We got shrimp cocktail, the Prime Rib (rivals House of Prime Rib), and the spaghetti with meatballs. Everything was delicious. It’s loud but the ambiance is part of the experience. Such a cool, classic old school dining experience.

reviews.author: Manuel Hernandez
reviews.text: I would say that the restaurant lived up to expectations and had the best pizza I've had in San Francisco so far. I would suggest you order a cheese pizza base with pesto, goat cheese and cherry tomatoes, its chefs kiss. The restaurant also offers other delicious things and are flexible with customizations.

reviews.author: Taylor R
reviews.text:
My wife and I love Original Joe’s in North Beach! It’s an absolute staple in the neighborhood and one of the most consistent restaurants in San Francisco.

The Food:

• Chicken Parm: I always order this. It’s a massive, thin cut of chicken with a generous portion of cheese and delicious marinara. Get the ravioli as your side!

• Seafood: My wife usually rotates between the Salmon with lemon beurre blanc or the Dungeness Crab Louie (they don’t skimp on the lump crab meat).

• Dessert: Do not leave without trying the Butter Cake. It is incredibly rich, so definitely share it.

The Wine (Pro Tip):

The wine list here is solid, but many of the bottles are heavily marked up. We used the Sip Savvy app to scan the menu for the best value vs rating bottle aligned with our palate. It flagged the Austin Hope 1L bottle as the Best Buy—it was $98, which Sip Savvy showed was one of the lowest markups on the menu for that quality. It paired perfectly with the Italian food. If you’re a wine person, definitely try the app here. It takes the guesswork out of finding great bottles without overpaying!

reviews.author: Ms. San Francisco Real Estate Janice Lee
reviews.text: Original Joe's offers a truly classic San Francisco dining experience, the moment you step inside, the timeless atmosphere, complete with plush red booths and dark wood paneling, sets the perfect mood. The food quality is exceptional across the board: the grilled steak and rack of lamb were both cooked to perfection and beautifully presented, while the hearty Italian dishes, like the rich ravioli and comforting spaghetti with meat sauce, were satisfying classics. We started with delicious, crisp fried calamari and finished the night with a festive birthday dessert, capping off a meal where the service was consistently prompt, professional, and attentive. Original Joe's flawlessly delivers on high-quality ingredients, excellent service and a fantastic, old-school atmosphere.

reviews.author: Lana
reviews.text: Delicious old school North Beach restaurant with great service. The spicy rigatoni and grilled prawns were perfectly cooked and the sauce was delicious and mildly spicy. For dessert the warm butter cake with ice cream smelled amazing, with a hint of nostalgia. Take a look at the wall of family photos and celebrities. Reserve your table at least one month in advance, this place is always busy. There is a bar area for walk-ins, but people do get turned away on very busy nights.

restaurants.description: Neighborhood fixture serving traditional, old-school Italian entrees, steaks, and seafood.

restaurants.name: Trattoria Contadina
restaurants.address: 1800 Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: AJ F
reviews.text: This spot  offers delicious pasta and decent service. The classic Italian dishes, particularly the pasta, are a definite highlight.However, I would strongly advise you to skip the cocktails. I tried three different cocktails, and all were disappointingly similar in taste. Stick to the wines. Given its popularity, reservations are highly recommended to secure a table.

reviews.author: Ms. San Francisco Real Estate Janice Lee
reviews.text:
I had a great experience at this cozy Italian restaurant. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with a charmingly rustic feel, complete with a well-stocked bar and string lights that give it a homey, comfortable vibe. The service was friendly and attentive without being overbearing, and our waiter was helpful with the menu.

The real standout of the meal, and something you can tell from the photos, is the sheer size of the portions. They don't mess around! I ordered a pasta dish topped with a generous amount of fresh basil and what looked like burrata or a similar creamy cheese. My friend's spaghetti with a huge, impressive-looking meatball was also massive, and the clams in white wine sauce were abundant and delicious. You definitely get your money's worth here, and you'll likely have leftovers.

For dessert, we had a simple but delicious bowl of vanilla ice cream with chocolate syrup. It was the perfect way to cap off a very filling meal. I highly recommend giving this place a try.

reviews.author: Bay Area Reviews
reviews.text: Had a very yummy dinner here. The service was good, the food was excellent. I enjoyed all the dishes I ordered. The affogato at the end was a great way to end my night.

reviews.author: Nicholas Rebholtz
reviews.text: Second time visiting, great service and excellent food.  If you’re craving Italian in the city, I highly recommend Trattoria Contadina!  Made my mom’s birthday celebration very beautiful, even provided a complimentary dessert for her!

reviews.author: Wolf Fivousix
reviews.text: The first time I came here I had an amazing pasta and great service. Came back with a friend for a second time and had a lack-luster experience. The pastas were fine, but nothing close to what it was before. My mom does a better pasta. Their tiramisu was good and the bartender was extremely nice and accommodating to our drink requests. Big props to him! I wish the waiter could learn a thing or two from him.

restaurants.description: Cozy, unpretentious fixture for classic Italian fare such as pasta, meatballs & gnocchi.

restaurants.name: Tosca Cafe
restaurants.address: 242 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Susie S
reviews.text: I like the old school atmosphere of this place. The food was ok if you like that east coast comfort food type of Italian food, not Italian from Italy type of Italian food. The pasta was way overcooked. The food runner team needs to work on knowing table numbers and seat numbers. The incorrect food was delivered to the table.  Even when our own food was delivered, they kept interrupting conversation asking us if we have that dish and whose dish it is multiple times.

reviews.author: Alex Schultz
reviews.text:
Atmosphere is great. Food is decent. No salt and pepper at the table and food kind of bland.

Service was very disappointing and honestly ruined the experience. Our waiter was very inattentive. Took 10 minutes after being seated for him to even come the table. Never came to check how we were doing after we got our food. Never came to ask if we wanted another drink after finishing our first. No attention for 30 minutes and then we got a “You all good?” then we asked for the check. Stood over me watching to see what I’d tip. The support staff was great and brought water, cleaned our table between courses, etc. Really loved the atmosphere and seemed like the manager was going around to bigger parties and being very attentive but we did not get that level of service. Maybe jt was just our server but the service did not match the environment and price point. Probably will not go back because of this. Not worth the gamble to see who you’ll get.

reviews.author: Jon Kerwin
reviews.text: The place was easily 85 degrees tonight. Walked in and there was no host. We had reservations so we waited a bit for any host to be sat. Once sat by the scorching kitchen, we waited for 30 minutes to get our round of drinks and asked twice if we could order. The menus doubled as fans. The service was terrible. Apparently LeBron James was in the private room which meant we all suffered. They threw free drinks at us and comped most of our meal, which was appreciated. Quite a deal for the sweat and chaos. But will I dine there again? No.

reviews.author: Adam Lambert
reviews.text:
Vibes here are immaculate. Refined enough for the low light, small enough to feel the real buzz of the restaurant operating like a fine machine.

Margo, the big bartender, keeps an eye on the entire restaurant from the cocktail station. The care and attention to each patron is evident

100-year-old establishment right across from City Lights bookstore? Don't think twice. Go, and get whatever Margo recommends, and you're guaranteed to make a memory.

reviews.author: Annie M
reviews.text: Something weird is going on here. We went in. It reeks of cheap cleaning product. Overpowering. 630pm the place is empty. I see all the recent bad reviews but when I was planning my trip months ago things were good. The menu has changed. Its very small and they were out of several things. A table next to us was arguing with staff bc I guess the things they ordered were no longer available. They left after a salad. This is beginning of the night! I heard the waiter ask chef if they had pork (they didnt) then I couldn't hear what else they were saying they were out of. We got chicken parm and pasta. It was good. It wasnt what I came there for or worth the price. It was very different from my other visits here. I hope they get it together.

restaurants.description: Lively bar & eatery featuring Italian fare, cocktails & wine in a revamped, yet old-school setting.

restaurants.name: Acquolina
restaurants.address: 1600 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Аlisa Paludeti
reviews.text: It’s one of the spots in North beach you’ll always find a table without wait. Their food is decent but the service could be better (we had our appetizers coming together with the main course). Prices are average and can’t say anything special about the ambience. My recommendation on food would be lasagna.

reviews.author: Abbey
reviews.text: I had a great time at Acquolina. The food and the vibe were excellent. I had an outdoor seat, but it was kept warm by a heater. I highly recommend the burrata and the spaghetti ai frutti di mare. They are both incredibly fresh and flavorful, and worth every penny.

reviews.author: Sarah Gonzales
reviews.text: Best Italian food I've had. The experience to watch them make your dish in front of you was cool. The staff was nice, knowledgeable of the items..

reviews.author: Sarina Iwai
reviews.text:
Came here on thanksgiving day with a reservation since it seemed like it was one of the few places that was open that day.

We were seated outside but thankfully they had heating! We got the eggplant parm, the mozzarella pizza, and one of their pastas. I liked the eggplant dish but nothing surprising about the pizza- maybe it wasn’t up to par since it was super busy that day(?) but the pizza was super thin (a little too flimsy) and kind of wet which I wasn’t a fan of. The crust was fluffy and nice but I’ve had better pizza in the area.

reviews.author: Jennifer Ames Hoskins
reviews.text:
I HAD to review this place. To be honest, came here because there was a crazy long wait on the other spots in the area, but I am so glad I came!!

First of all, NO SEED OILS!! we had a pepperoni pizza, a cheese pizza, meatball appetizer, salad, and a perfectly cooked lamb chop, medium rare. Vegetable 🥕side dishes.

Start to finish, amazing. Service, drinks, food. Across the board. So yummy. We even ordered another pizza to take home! The sauce to cheese ratio was so perfect 🤤

I wasn’t prepared for this level of delicious so apologies for the lack of pics 😆

Two ten year olds, two adults very happy 🙏

From the kids: “ best food I have ever had, I will definitely come back!!”

restaurants.description: Neighborhood bistro serving pizza & other Italian eats in a relaxed space with some sidewalk seats.

restaurants.name: Luisa's Restaurant Wine Bar Since 1959
restaurants.address: 759 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Alberto Mota
reviews.text:
The best food, the best service and to top it all to have a Luisa give us a call. Nothing but the best Italian food in all San Francisco!!!

Joanna, words cant explain,  I fell in love with Luisa’s restaurant it was love at first sight, amazing food, delicious and the service is amazing. Luisa’s will always be a stop every time I  come out here for work and to top it off they appreciate their customers I was happy to speak with her and express how much i enjoy the place. 💛

reviews.author: George Nguyen
reviews.text: Stumbled across this restaurant late at night and I was surprised. The lasagna was sizzling and tasty. The garlic bread was crispy too. We also saw Luisa herself, who was a gentle and kind lady. The portions are a bit small so it will not leave you feeling too full.

reviews.author: John vach
reviews.text:
This was Italian cuisine at its most disciplined and articulate—arguably finer than what I’ve experienced in Italy itself.

Each dish demonstrated restraint and clarity: pasta with exacting texture, sauces stripped of excess yet rich in intent, ingredients allowed to speak without interference. Nothing relied on nostalgia or theatrics; everything was deliberate.

Service moved with quiet confidence, pacing the meal as a single, coherent composition rather than a sequence of courses.

This was not a reproduction of Italian cooking, but its distillation—tradition understood, refined, and expressed with uncommon precision. A symphony that plays to even the most discriminative pallet.  Don't miss the chance to eat the most wonderful and authentic Italian  gastronomic food on our wonderful planet. Magnificent!

reviews.author: Julia Chuang
reviews.text: We went pretty late at night on a weekday so it was empty. There's not a lot of tables so definitely call or make a reservation if you're going during prime dinner time. It's very quiet and great atmosphere. Friendly service. I loved their house red, the bruschetta, and the lobster ravioli in particular.

reviews.author: Cynthia Lopez
reviews.text: We liked the food here. I ordered the lobster ravioli with vodka sauce, a Caesar salad, and a glass of the house red wine (Cabernet Sauvignon). The service was helpful and attentive. The one thing I was not wild about was the atmosphere. When we walked in, the whole place smelled like disinfectant. Also, the decor was rundown, and loud 70s music blared from the speakers. We were seated next to a window so that was the one nice touch from an atmospheric perspective. We could people watch and eat at the same time.

restaurants.description: Cozy, long-running Italian eatery featuring seafood dishes, homemade pasta & Neapolitan-style pizza.

restaurants.name: Ryoko's Japanese Restaurant & Bar
restaurants.address: 619 Taylor St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Pooya Fayyaz
reviews.text:
Cool atmosphere and a unique vibe, but the sushi itself was pretty average. We ordered a variety, and the only one we really enjoyed was the soy wrap. The special hot sake with lemon sounded fun, but the flavor didn’t stand out either.

On the plus side, the service was friendly and attentive. Overall, not bad, but definitely not a top sushi pick in SF.

reviews.author: Kong Lee
reviews.text:
I've read that Royko's was a hole-in-the-wall Japanese bar with great vibes and sushi. Missing izakaya vibes in Japan, I had to stop by, and I did not regret it. Because Ryoko's was just that.

Small, cozy, dark, lively. Great food and drinks. The sushi was top notch. The beers were good. And I had a few other items on the menu like the Shoyu Ramen and Chicken Teriyaki. All pretty good.

I loved Ryoko's. The vibe was definitely reminiscent of izakayas in Japan. Good service. Id say come here for the vibes, the drinks, and the sushi! The sushi was more on the pricey side (around $12 - 15 for 2 nigiris). However, it was so good I couldnt stop ordering for more 😅.

reviews.author: Minny mm
reviews.text: Sorry, not great. I'm just here because it's near my hotel. I didn't expect much, and it was right. No taste. Sorry, chef, yes, just for drinks with small dishes is okay, but not for a main dish. Servers were nice though...

reviews.author: Daniel Boczarski
reviews.text: I've never seen a bouncer at a restaurant, ever. If you come here you will see one though, very strange. Very loud dance music playing making conversation difficult. Service was unorganized and not good. Food was fine, very average.

reviews.author: Jade May
reviews.text: One of the best, if not the best, sushi bars I've been to in San Francisco in the twenty years I've been staying here. Itamae-san Atsushi was very skilled and quick at making sushi, as well and friendly and charasmatic. I came back a second night with a friend and the service was just as good, if not better with the pre-existing rapport from the night prior. Even their security guard Vern was a delight to talk with. I highly recommend trying the o-toro and ika ki zuke.

restaurants.description: Compact, woodsy Japanese bar & restaurant offering a long sushi menu & draft beers.

restaurants.name: Taniku Izakaya
restaurants.address: 1035 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Victoria P.
reviews.text:
I finally made it to their happy hour, which has some great deals.

Their happy hour menu contains some of their ramen bowls off their main menu for $13. I got the garlic pork ramen, and they did not skimp on the pork. There were two thick pieces of soft pork belly that was coated with garlic and seared to perfection. And the meat melted in my mouth.
The bok choy was crunchy and its bitterness was tamed with the flavorful pork. The noodles had a good bite while the corn added some sweetness. And the marinated egg also added another flavor profile. Not sure what it was marinated in, but it had a nice tea flavor. And the yolk was perfectly jammy.

And then there's the broth: full of umami, pork, and garlic flavors. I have been incredibly stressed from two projects at work and the moment I took a sip of the broth, I immediately felt at ease. The ramen was so comforting, especially now that we're experiencing autumn weather in San Francisco. I tend to eat fast, but there was no rushing this. The world and my worries melted away with each bite.

I also got the amaebi shrimp hand roll, which came with a yuzu sauce. I actually found it to be a bit gingery, which was a nice surprise in flavor. It seemed the rice was also marinated in perhaps soy sauce for that umami note.  And the shrimp was plump. It was a good bite.

This is a popular spot with a small dining space, so a line can form pretty quickly outside as the night progresses.  But that ramen is worth the wait.

reviews.author: Sara
reviews.text: i’m honestly very surprised this isn’t on some “hidden gems” list because Taniku Izakaya is truly phenomenal. We’ve come back a couple times within about two months, and everything is so great. The small plates are where Taniku really shines — each one packs a powerful punch of flavor. this go around, we had the grilled Japanese yams, the pork belly and the Kobe beef skewers which are *chef’s kiss* 🤌🏼. We haven’t tried everything just yet, but we’ll keep coming back for more. (The pork belly skewers have been a repeat favorite!)

reviews.author: Sellers Family
reviews.text: Super delicious.  The ramen was good, but honestly the small plates were better.  My gluten free daughter really enjoyed the tofu and the scallops and the Brussel sprouts.  The chicken katsu mushrooms were also amazing. Would definitely return.  It is a small place though so ready to queue up outside.

reviews.author: Belle C. Sujitra
reviews.text:
It’s a small and cozy izakaya, perfect for dining alone or with a small group of 3–4 people. I ordered appetizers from the special menu. First up: Mentaiko Zucchini (so delicious! The very first bite was refreshing, followed by the rich umami saltiness of the mentaiko. A little dip in Karashi mustard made it even better—definitely a must-try! Highly recommended).

Next was Kuramaebi, tiger prawns deep-fried tempura style and topped with tartar sauce. After that, I ordered Ba-ra Kushiyaki and Kobe Kushiyaki (served 2 skewers each). I especially enjoyed the Kobe—it was grilled perfectly and went really well with Shichimi Togarashi. If it had the same green onion topping as the Ba-ra, I think it would have been absolutely perfect.😋

reviews.author: John
reviews.text:
I don’t often write reviews while in the restaurant but I couldn’t wait because this is simply one of THE BEST Izakaya restaurants I’ve ever visited. You can feel how much effort the owner has put in

FOOD - I strongly recommend their Yakitori. All of them are good. They were grilled perfectly and marinated just good so that you can still taste the freshness of the material. We also had Takoyaki, it’s also very tasty, you can tell that they were made fresh! I also recommend their Katsu curry rice, they are not the usual ones you’ll get, it’s very authentic, feels like the one in Japan. Lastly, try their Yuzu beer, it’s very refreshing!

SERVICE and ENVIRONMENT are all 100/100. The manager respect you and always ensure you get the thing you want. The environment is also very cute, decorated with many Japanese style decorations. From the Sonos speaker you can see how much efforts they put on these little details.

If you have a chance, come and taste it. It’s THE BEST!!!

restaurants.name: Izakaya mayumi
restaurants.address: 2221 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Teresita S
reviews.text: Reservations are recommended but we were able to get a table in 5-10 minutes by walking in. The restaurant is small so I wouldn't recommend it for a big group. There are a few tables as well as bar seating. The menu selection was solid and the food we got was pretty tasty too. Service was efficient and because the place is small, it was easy to get their attention if we needed it. Parking is hard but that is the Outer Richmond for you.

reviews.author: Ken B. Montgomery
reviews.text:
I really liked this place, very Japanese from decor to food options to presentation! One of best chirashi bowls I have had in my earthly pilgrimage. Definitely get the karaage appetizer, and the miso soup comes with more substance than most other places. Also green tea ice cream is a
very generous portion.

reviews.author: Sam Lee
reviews.text:
This is one of the best Izakaya restaurants in the Bay Area and the price is reasonable.  It offers many items not typically found in many other Izakaya places.  A friend and I ordered
- potato croquette
- grilled miso black cod
- takoyaki (deep fried octopus dough balls)
- agedashi tofu
- tori no karaage (fried chicken)
- takowasa (raw octopus mixed with wasabi sauce)

All were excellent. Our server claimed the restaurant has the best karaage in San Francisco. He might be right. It got lighter and more delicate coating than any karaage I had before.

reviews.author: Saburo Matsumoto
reviews.text: Hanabi (meaning firework in Japanese) roll was beautiful and delicious. But try agedashi tai (snapper) too. A hidden gem in SF. Oh, during happy hours their Dassai 45 (daiginjo sake) is a steal. Enjoy the smooth taste and elegant aroma.

reviews.author: Arthur Ho
reviews.text:
Authentic izakaya spot, every dish we had was delicate and high quality. The server was super attentive and courteous. Definitely coming back!

Karaage: juicy fried chicken, perfectly soft skin
Agedashi mochi
Negitoro Taru Taru: fatty tuna served in unique bamboo dish
Motsu nabe: beef intestine hot pot soup
Agedashi tai: fried red snapper

restaurants.description: A compact neighborhood hideaway with izakaya-style Japanese plates plus sake by the glass or bottle.

restaurants.name: Goemon Izakaya Bar & Karaoke Lounge
restaurants.address: 3129 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Christina Corona
reviews.text: So good! This new spot in the Richmond is developing its business, soo worth coming in. The drinks are creative and delicious. Food is always tasty. I wish I took a picture of the chicken karaage -highly recommend ☺️ they have rooms to rent for karaoke to bring your friends and outside seating

reviews.author: Kiana Daniels
reviews.text:
Our first time stopping by. We stopped by on a Monday for a late night bite. We were a party of 3, there was no wait and the staff was very kind. Kind service & relaxed environment. Jane was very sweet and helpful. They have karaoke Wed, Friday, & Saturdays @8:30. We ordered the Yuzu whiskey sour.  The cocktail was beautifully presented and tasted refreshing. Seaweed salad, agedashi tofu, kimchi fries, skewers, & shoshito peppers were all cleared form our plates. I would've loved to share more photos but was enjoying the moment. We are visiting family so we are only here for a short visit but we will definitely return to this establishment.

- The founder and chef from Tee Off bar & grill; Paul Groarke's returns for a popup every Wednesday. This is great for people that had the privilege to support him more than 10 years ago. We hope to enjoy this experience while they offer it.

THANK YOU JIAN!

My girls and I had an unforgettable night filled with laughter, singing, amazing food, and delicious drinks! This place went above and beyond our already high expectations, making it the perfect spot to celebrate my best friend's birthday. We sang our hearts out with karaoke and truly enjoyed every moment.

The food was absolutely fantastic--highly recommend the salmon, chicken, and steak skewers. Each one was grilled to perfection: juicy, fresh, and bursting with flavor! Kudos to Chef! And a huge shoutout to Jen, the incredible bartender, who crafted the most delicious signature cocktails for us. We loved them so much, we just had to keep ordering more!

From the lively atmosphere to the mouthwatering menu and top-notch drinks, this spot has it all. Can't wait to come back!

reviews.author: Rachel Ni
reviews.text:
I attended a friend's birthday party at Goemon Karaoke Bar and had an amazing time! The food was delicious, we had some grilled items and specials… the service was very attentive throughout the evening. The highlight of the night was definitely KJ John--he was super entertaining and kept the energy high all night long.

We all had such a great time singing and celebrating. BTW, the signature cocktails are bombs! ( shout out to bartender jen)  I highly recommend this spot to all karaoke enthusiasts. They also offer private karaoke rooms that you can rent by the hour, which made the experience even more fun and personalized. We're already planning to come back for another dinner party soon. Can't wait!

reviews.author: Jane Polak Scowcroft
reviews.text: Love this place. Feels like home. Welcoming, great drinks, awesome happy hour. Come quickly before it gets too popular!

restaurants.name: The Public Izakaya
restaurants.address: 700 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Nick Gloyd
reviews.text:
Great atmosphere with really great food!
We just tried this place for the first time and it did not disappoint! We had various ramens and they were all good. We also had the Flaming Toro roll, which was sublime. We are definitely coming back!

reviews.author: Gustavo Herrera
reviews.text:
I find the quality of their ingredients to be really high, I enjoy eating here and taking advantage of their happy hour, the food is really good.

This place has a great atmosphere, they really went all out with the interior design, its really fun and creative.

The service can sometimes be a bit distracted, because they can get very busy and ever since they had to close and reopen due to someone breaking their gas line to the building (not their fault) I can tell they are trying really hard to regain their footing since they are now a bit understaffed because of it

I think this place is a great addition to the neighborhood and hope they have success for a long time

reviews.author: Barney Jin
reviews.text: Not a good experience overall. The wooden spoon smelled like fresh from the soap water and its wet. Nabe was too salty, I suspect they put too much soy sauce in it. Not much service either. The only good ones are the skewers. Not worth the price definitely.

reviews.author: Dana
reviews.text: I want to share my impressions of visiting this establishment. I love Asian cuisine, and after seeing the rave reviews, I decided to go in. It was late evening, which was good, since the establishment was still open at that time. The cafe was completely full. We had to wait a long time for someone from the staff to approach us. Then I had to wait the same amount of time until they took my order. I'll say right away that the prices are high, the portions are small. The dishes looked so-so. I didn't like the soup, it's the first time I've seen Asian soup with American chips🤦🏼‍♀️ I've never seen such a mix. The rolls were made terribly, all different sizes, and fell apart immediately. tempura it was three shrimps. We never received the receipt, so we had to go to the cashier ourselves. The staff was disgusting. Our waiter had a dissatisfied face. I visited this cafe again, but the service was just as terrible. The cafe is not very clean, the shelves with decor are all dusty. I was left with a terrible impression after visiting this establishment.

reviews.author: Chris Eyo
reviews.text: Now THIS is about to be one of my go-tos on SF trips. A 10pm happy hour??? Hello!!??? Everything slapped. The wait was ~10min for 1 person which wasn’t too bad. The chicken and mushroom skewers were perfectly seasoned. Perfect! The albacore and hamachi nights were pretty standard. The spicy miso salmon kama was decent, a little fatty for my liking but still solid. The karaage and gyoza barely got a pic I chewed it up so fast. It was so good. I washed it all down with a Okinawa breeze cocktail inside of a pineapple. Finished off with the mango Sanyo. Chefs kiss. These prices are hard to match. I’ll be back.

restaurants.name: Zentarou Sushi and Sake Bar
restaurants.address: 1380 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Bobower E
reviews.text:
I had an amazing experience at Zentarou Sushi and Sake Bar! The omakase here is truly impressive, especially for the price. Every piece of fish was incredibly fresh, well-balanced, and thoughtfully prepared. You can really feel the chef’s passion—each course was explained with care, and the pacing of the meal was perfect.

The selection had a great mix of classic cuts and creative bites that made the whole experience memorable. The portions were just right, and I left feeling satisfied without being overly full. The ambiance is cozy and welcoming, and the staff were attentive, friendly, and made the experience even better.

For the quality you get, Zentarou offers one of the best and most affordable omakase experiences in the area. I’ll definitely be coming back and recommending it to anyone who loves sushi!

reviews.author: Joko Manullang
reviews.text: Zentarou has outstanding tableside service, even when the restaurant is busy. The staff is attentive and genuinely takes care of your needs. I really enjoyed the umeshu plum wine, and the omakase is a must-try—the fish is very high quality. The menu changes every season, which keeps things fresh and interesting. The cozy atmosphere makes the experience even better. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Ranxu Zhao
reviews.text: I went Zentarou with my friends for my husband’s birthday. The experience was the BEST I could ask for in the entire Bay Area. Bee was super friendly, nice, and professional. All the staff were welcoming and passionate. We tried some sakes. All of them were amazingly tasty!! My husband and I had an amazing time there! They also gave us a cake. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Rick Sam
reviews.text:
The food is seriously top-notch—super fresh, beautifully made, and perfectly balanced. Every bite feels thoughtful, like real care went into it. This is sushi done right, with zero shortcuts.

The drinks deserve their own shoutout. They’re creative, well thought out, and pair incredibly well with the food. Definitely not your average sushi bar cocktails or sake list.

What really makes the place special, though, is the people. Chef Zen’s passion and creativity shine through at the counter, and Beach is one of the friendliest, most attentive servers you’ll meet—welcoming, genuine, and always checking in without being overbearing.

Great food, fun and inventive drinks, and warm hospitality all around. This is the kind of place you leave already planning your next visit.

reviews.author: Illan Weiner
reviews.text:
This place is my second home. I come here quite often during lunch time and it always soothes my soul. The tranquil atmosphere paired with delectable fish, truly fresh and rare fish they import makes the experience so special.

Wonderful people. Even better food. Highly recommend if you like sushi. You literally can't go wrong with anything you get (I'd pass on the gyudon lol)

restaurants.name: Ebisu Restaurant
restaurants.address: 1283 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Bijan Shahriari
reviews.text:
This is by far my favourite restaurant in SF! The quality of the fish, the service, the flavour! Everything was top notch. If you can, try to be seated at the bar so you can talk directly to the chef… it adds to the experience!

Expect to wait 30min before getting seated. The restaurant is packed on Friday or Saturday nights!

reviews.author: Asia Perpetua
reviews.text: You can't call to make a reservation here. You'll need to walk in and either be seated immediately or write your name on the list. Whatever happens as long as you get to sit down and eat at this establishment is your good fortune. I had the mackerel dinner, my son had the salmon dinner, and my partner had all types of sushi. I could barely speak because my tastebuds were too busy having a party in my mouth. Definitely worth any wait!!!

reviews.author: Wing Mok
reviews.text:
First when I come in they put me at the darkest corner in the restaurant and I asked to move to another table.

Second, no one ask for what I want to drink after 5 minutes I sat down. (For some reason all the staff including the chef were focusing on one specific table may be VIP I guess)

Should have more options on Omakase, I don't mind to pay more.
But if I ordered Omakase with only regular Salmon, Tuna, Albacore, Ikura and MASAGO without even 1 piece of UNI or Scallop why would I order deluxe sushi or Omakase.

Ankimo, first time ever with no sauce and I had to ask for ponzu sauce on the side .

Conclusion, way overrated for their stars.

去之前期望太高👀
食完之後食物和服務水準比預期差太遠！😔😔😔

reviews.author: Hit List
reviews.text: Sorry for not visiting sooner. Top sushi nigiri with such delicious soft sushi rice. No wonder there was a line for a seat. I will definitely be hitting this place up more often. No joke.

reviews.author: SunshiineMoonRise
reviews.text: Date night! We tried this restaurant for the first time for dinner. They don't take reservations, so you sign up on a clipboard when you arrive. It's a small, cozy space, and we only had to wait 15 minutes on a Wednesday. Our server, Nori, was excellent. We ordered a few yummy appetizers, like seaweed salad, edamame, and shrimp tempura. The hand rolls were great, with fresh, crispy seaweed—there's nothing worse than old, chewy seaweed! We also loved the “chef's choice”, and my favorite was the Romeo/Juliet roll. The Aladdin sake was flavorful and unfiltered, just the way I like it. The team-based service was appreciated. Highly recommend, and we'll definitely be back!

restaurants.description: Long-standing Japanese eatery serving sushi & classic entrees in a cozy, streamlined setting.

restaurants.name: Ju-Ni
restaurants.address: 1335 Fulton St STE 101, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Kien Lam
reviews.text: My wife and I went as our Valentine's date night, expecting maybe a quiet-ish time at the bar and some polite banter perhaps with our chef and maybe the couples next to us. What we got instead quite by happy happenstance was a rollicking good time chatting with the super friendly couples next to us at the bar, while our chef JK obliged with friendly comments within our conversation while still preparing the delectable courses of the omakase. Of course, the happenstance of the chatty friendly couples sitting next to us can't be replicated, but our chef JK really rolled with it and knew how to adjust the service to the cadence of our conversation -- our lucky accidental group ended up being the last guests at the restaurant, at which time the chefs broke out some japanese sake for us on the house, and we proceeded to chat another 20 minutes before leaving with the distinct conclusion that this was unquestionably the best time we have ever had at an omakase and that we shall return!

reviews.author: Andrea Santos
reviews.text:
ju-ni focuses on the fish with some delicious nigiri pieces, but overall the ambiance & service was distracting and overpriced for the number of pieces served. overall, glad I tried it and loved the standout pieces, but wouldn’t recommend.

service: our chef wasn’t bad but the difference of service between our seating and the one next to us was night and day. ended up listening to the other chef more since he explained each dish with detail while ours mumbled his way through.

standout dishes: fatty tuna, red snapper, salmon roe with monkfish pate

reviews.author: Neal Mueller
reviews.text: Ju-Ni literally means “twelve” in Japanese, a nod to the original 12-seat counter and the tight, timed omakase format. Service is mostly nigiri, paced one piece at a time, with a lot of torch work at the counter.  ￼ Chef-owner Geoffrey Lee’s signature bite is the “ikura with ankimo”: salmon roe topped with shaved, deep-frozen ankimo (monkfish liver, often called “foie gras of the sea”). Lee has described it as “our dish at Ju-Ni,” and it’s a standout.  ￼

reviews.author: Mike, and sometimes Llewellyn.
reviews.text: 16-course omakase service in this tiny location. The fish was outstanding, half of the courses were interesting, memorable, and outstanding and only two courses were "just fine" but that's gonna come down to personal preferences. Limited drink options, and service was fine but nothing to write home about. Come for the fantastic fish and creative courses. Was pretty loud whole I was there for to a business dinner that was very boisterous, but seems like that's probably rare for this place.

reviews.author: Janet K
reviews.text:
Ju-Ni is a standout. ​Every component of the experience was flawless. The shari (sushi rice) was seasoned to perfection, providing the ideal foundation for the fish. The chef's application of sauces and glazes was subtle yet enhanced every bite without overpowering the star of the show: the fish.

​The quality of the neta (toppings) was exceptional, and for good reason -- I learned that everything, aside from the salmon, is imported directly from Japan just the day before service. You can truly taste the difference that level of freshness makes. From the delicate madai to uni, each piece was a testament to quality and skilled preparation.

It's been a long time since I've had high-end nigiri this satisfying in the Bay Area, especially post-pandemic. Highly recommended for any serious sushi lover.

restaurants.description: Contemporary, upscale bistro with clean lines &  counter seats serving elevated omakase sushi.

restaurants.name: Kuma on Valencia
restaurants.address: 198 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Tina Pak
reviews.text:
Always wanted to dine here. Stopped by the other night. Overall, a pleasant experience, but nothing that wowed me.

The food was good - but in a city like San Francisco with so many standout sushi spots, it didn’t quite rise to the top. I’ve had better sushi elsewhere in town, so it didn’t leave a lasting impression.

Service, on the other hand, was excellent. Friendly, attentive, and everything came out smoothly, no complaints there. The vibe was fine too: comfortable and low-key, but not particularly memorable or unique in a city full of more visually or atmospherically interesting places.

All in all, I enjoyed my meal and appreciated the hospitality, but it’s probably not a place I’d go back to. Solid, but not that special for a second visit (to me).

reviews.author: Illan Weiner
reviews.text:
Insane that they don't offer hon wasabi from the tube or stem as a takeout item.

The one time I was able to get them to say yes, it was a lie and I didn't get anything.

Despite the sushi tasting good, I'm genuinely not interested in ordering from here ever again so long as Sushi Shio exists.

Packaging is cute, but they gotta work on their accommodations. One of the sushi I ordered was really fishy, yet they did nothing to help.

reviews.author: Kim De Guzman
reviews.text: My favorite sushi spot in San Francisco! Been here several times and it never disappoints. The caliber of sushi they serve competes with higher end places for a more reasonable price point with a super comfortable, relaxed atmosphere. Love the local tofu, omakase nigiri tasting, Kuma roll, and any carafe of sake they recommend. Ryo and his team really puts in their best effort in all the food and service for customers. Highly recommend if you are looking for delicious sushi!

reviews.author: cantik
reviews.text:
Good atmosphere and location, but portion sizes don't match the prices.

The Chirashi Deluxe was very disappointing - despite being described as having "all the fixings of our regular chirashi PLUS extra delicacies like toro, uni and scallops" the portions were surprisingly small for the premium price.
The Sake-Masu Chirashi offered better value with clearly stated 12-piece serving.
The rice underneath the chirashi was served in a very small portion.
Ebi Tempura and Rainbow rolls were decent quality.
Service at the bar was quiet but adequate.
Overall: Nice vibe but overpriced. Would recommend checking portion expectations before ordering the pricier items.

reviews.author: Nicole Gurtler
reviews.text: Found this spot through Resy and it did not disappoint. The local tofu was incredible, and the salmon avocado roll was our favorite but we liked everything we tried!

restaurants.name: Akari Japanese Bistro
restaurants.address: 1155 Folsom St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Donny Pusawong
reviews.text:
I came here for a private event featuring a live bluefin tuna cutting, and it was my first time experiencing something so incredible. Watching a master sushi chef expertly carve an enormous, fresh tuna was truly mesmerizing—something I had never seen before except videos.

The bluefin was exceptionally fresh and delicious, with different cuts served sashimi-style as well as incorporated into rolls, allowing us to experience the variety of flavors and textures throughout the fish. In addition to the tuna, the restaurant also served other standout dishes, including rich and flavorful sukiyaki beef.

What really elevated the evening was the attentiveness and care of the staff—you could genuinely feel how invested they were in making sure every guest had an unforgettable experience. As a bonus, I even won the raffle and was able to take more tuna home!

An exceptional and truly special experience. I will absolutely be returning to dine here again!

reviews.author: Jenny Xu
reviews.text:
Came here for a dinner and tuna cutting and it was amazing. I didn't expect the tuna to be so large!

Good:
- Super cool explanation of how everything works and the chef adds some fun humor into it which makes it even more entertaining than the cutting already is.
- The buffet we had was so good, I loved the crepe cake dessert and grapes too, and there was shrimp, tuna, chicken, everything you could imagine
- The tuna was soooo fresh
- The staff were so kind and helpful!
- There was also a sake pairing and wine pairing which elevated the experience

Bad:
- Can be pricey!

Overall this was such a premium experience and so glad we got to experience it!

reviews.author: Colin Shi
reviews.text: This Japanese restaurant is a true gem in the city. Authenticity is unmatched anywhere else in the US. I’ve had their Makunouchi lunch sets, which are carefully crafted from the best ingredients for a balanced meal. I’ve also tried their sukiyaki with Washu beef, which is already melt in the mouth tender. Flavors are completely on point. You can tell the kitchen staff takes a lot of pride and care in preparation. Sure, it’s not cheap, but this is one of the few Japanese places that I feel is worth the price for authenticity.

reviews.author: Mirko Mantovani
reviews.text: Akari’s fixed seafood and meat menu is incredible. The fish is fresh, featuring Hokkaido scallops and uni directly from Japan. Pacing and portions were perfect. While the decor could be warmer and more curated, the food quality is amazing. A must-visit—we will definitely be back soon!

reviews.author: Bryan Wei
reviews.text: Amazing food and outstanding service! I even got to watch the maguro cutting show, which was seriously impressive. Highly recommend this spot if you're looking for authentic Japanese cuisine.

restaurants.name: Ozumo San Francisco
restaurants.address: 161 Steuart St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: Liz P.
reviews.text: The food at Ozumo was absolutely delicious. It’s a shame the Santana Row location near us went downhill, otherwise we’d still be dining there often. This time, we stopped in before a Giants game and enjoyed a sake tasting flight along with a dozen other dishes—everything was fantastic. The vibe was chill and, being a Wednesday night, the restaurant was pretty quiet. Our server was sweet and the runners were all on point. Thank you for a fabulous meal—definitely a million times better than ballpark food. We’ll need to come back more often!

reviews.author: Charley
reviews.text: Had really good food, very close and convenient to the hotel beside it. Service was a little hectic, but they made up for it. It is a bit on the pricey side. Has a big drink selection. The server was really nice. All the workers looked professional.

reviews.author: Andrew Michael
reviews.text:
Large open space with a very clean vibe.  Bar with some seating in front, open high-ceiling table-seating in back. The sushi was outstanding.  We had several different rolls - the rainbow roll was notably excellent.  The Mizuna Salad was great - fresh fruit, mixed greens and pepinos with a light dressing.  Their miso soup is subtle and perfect. The wagyu beef was melt-in-your-mouth delicious, though as others have commented, a bit pricey. Special mention to Army, who provided excellent service.

If you are looking for high-quality sushi in the crowded SF market, try Ozumo!!

reviews.author: Maya N
reviews.text:
The first time I went here months ago, I tried the spicy poke (“tuna poke” if you order via Toast), and loved it. I wanted to wait for a special occasion to reorder it, since it’s really expensive. Today I ordered online for pickup and was wildly disappointed upon receiving my order.

When I picked up my order, I was shocked to discover that this isn’t even the same dish.

There was NO radish, takuan, cucumber, scallion, pickled ginger, or crushed avocado at all!!! And on top of that, there were ingredients that aren’t even listed, like seaweed salad, white onions, and edamame.

Generally, I am a fan of their food (happy hour is great!), ambience, and customer service, but today I was really disappointed. :( Not to mention, the fish did not taste fresh. :((

I would have appreciated full transparency if the item is discontinued or ingredients were out.

reviews.author: Violet Byrne
reviews.text:
Ozumo Sushi at Santana Row absolutely exceeded every expectation. I came with my best friend to celebrate her 21st birthday, and from the moment we arrived, the customer service was outstanding. Everyone was incredibly friendly, welcoming, and genuinely excited to help us celebrate.

The staff was extremely attentive without ever being overwhelming—constantly checking in, clearing plates, and making sure we had everything we needed. Our table was never cluttered, and we never once felt forgotten. On top of that, we were treated to so many fun amenities that made the night feel extra special and celebratory.

The overall energy was warm, upbeat, and professional, which made the experience feel effortless and memorable. Between the exceptional service and the thoughtful attention to detail, it was truly an amazing night. I would absolutely recommend Ozumo for any special occasion—or honestly, just for a great dining experience in general.

restaurants.description: Creative sushi, robata grilled items & an extensive sake menu in a contemporary space.

restaurants.name: Roka Akor - San Francisco
restaurants.address: 801 Montgomery St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: mimi
reviews.text:
pleasant atmosphere, attentive servers that are easy to get the attention of, and good food. our reservation was for 8pm and the server kindly informed us that the kitchen closed at 9 so ordering any extra food before then was acceptable. we first ordered the sashimi platter, dry aged ribeye, wagyu tartare, fresh oysters, and bone marrow. around halfway through our meal we also ordered the corn, more oysters, and a bowl of rice. the staff were very accomodating when we asked for plain rice around 9pm, didnt expect them to accept that order but they managed to fulfill it.

one issue we had was how long it took for the corn, dry aged rib eye, and bone marrow to arrive to our table. the sashimi, tartare, and oysters were quick, but the other three took much longer and didnt arrive until the 44 minute mark of the hour. otherwise, amazing service.

the oysters and bone marrow were our favorites, everything else was okay. the wasabi was textured and different from every other place we have dined at on the east and qest coast! truly wonder how roka akor prepares the wasabi.

the corn was good but what they dont tell you is that its corn on the cob halved. it's a little messy and personally i think cutting them lengthwise into quarters or thinner would ensure for a less messy experience for the customer so that customers can still use their chopsticks for the corn on the cob.

reviews.author: James
reviews.text:
Very upscale lovely steak and seafood restaurant. The omakase offering is a variety of their signature and most sort after menu items. They are not small portions by any means, all meticulously crafted by the chef as a beautiful way to explore their menu.
Everything from A5+ Wagyu to lobster tail and a dessert selection. I won't tell too much as to give away the experience of being in the chef hands - but I can say they are good hands to be in. Thankyou for this special meal on our 20th wedding anniversary.

reviews.author: Michael Kawamoto
reviews.text: Contemporary, but excellent. I've been to dozens of omakases, but this one was super unique, easily in my top 3. Besides sashimi, nigiri, and the usual hot stuff, they had a huge lobster tail, wagyu, and an amazing dessert platter. I was so stuffed I had to take 20% of it home. Definitely recommend this unique experience.

reviews.author: Matt Perez
reviews.text:
Absolutely an amazing experience.  Andrew was our server and he was great. His knowledge and helpfulness was great and made our night that much better . A5 Wagyu , uni and uni on bluefin as well as spotted prawns with uni and caviar.  Bone marrow and all. Absolutely an amazing experience. Would recommend it to anyone .
I’ve never bought a Japanese A5 steak before . I finally did it and it melted in my mouth . Worth it especially as Christmas gift to myself .

reviews.author: Ja Flo
reviews.text:
Outstanding service and amazing modern robatayaki style japanese food. shouts out caesar on the grill - your charcoal grilled presentation was amazing and so tasty !
nate — sushi was amazing - fish temperature was on point, the shari was delectable
tanner - thank you for the hospitality, kindness and laughters
and lastly Jeff — our server , you were truly on point the whole night from beginning till end. Much appreciated team Roka .. ill definitely be back .

restaurants.description: Upscale Japanese restaurant & bar supplying steak, seafood & sushi in a contemporary setting.

restaurants.name: Rintaro
restaurants.address: 82 14th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Arrchana Pradeepan
reviews.text:
Great service. Great ambiance. Great food. Shared 8 courses between 2 people. Left full and very satisfied.

Hojicha panna cotta, and squid salad were the most unique in terms of flavor.

Dumpling were delicious and a beautify presentation.

Skewers smokey flavour was delicious.

Mochi kinchaku was warm and a lovely texture. Other dishes not mentioned were also delicious. Though the ones mentioned above were especially yummy.

We sat at the bar seating, which gave a great view of the chefs preparation.

reviews.author: Marija Vulfs
reviews.text: Rintaro is an experience. The chef’s father was a temple carpenter and built the restaurant interior with precision and incredible attention to detail. Maybe it was the clay on the walls from where I grew up or maybe it was the welcome as we were seated but I immediately felt at home.  The food is prepared with the utmost concentration and care. Sit at the bar if you can and watch the amazing process of preparation. My favorite? Beef tendon in broth with shiitake mushrooms. A wonderful and special meal.

reviews.author: Caroline Chow
reviews.text: So cozy and delicious. From the exterior to the delightful interior to the restrooms, this is a beautiful restaurant. Wonderful service and the food was all delicious. We loved the ume welcome drink. We tried the sashimi sampler, the gyoza, duck mizuna salad, trumpet mushroom and chicken thigh sansho yakitori, matsutake udon and passionfruit profiterole.  Our favorite was the mushroom yakitori. If we came back we'd try more yakitori and the whole fried fish. Tricia and our server were awesome. Excited to try recipes from the cookbook!

reviews.author: melissa manousos
reviews.text:
I was in town to visit friends and family. The friend I was staying with had been wanting to go here for years - before the flooding shut them down for a while - so she was super excited to get a reservation. The only one available was at 9:30 on a Tuesday night. But that worked perfectly for our schedule. It was very cold, even though we were seated inside. I can't imagine how cold the folks sitting outside were. But I bet it's lovely on a warm summer evening!
The food was excellent. We opted for the winter tasting menu. At $90 per person, I think it's a deal. It was a great way to taste some of their fan favorite items, and we got so much food. They even brought a bonus order of karaage because they didn't think the katsu was a big enough portion for 3 people! I do wish I had gotten the kake udon instead of the mabodofu don (rice bowl). But I wanted to taste the house-made tofu. I was not disappointed. It was just so filling! My friend got the udon. So I did get to taste that too, and it was delicious.
We also probably over-ordered because we wanted to try the gyoza too. They were great, but unnecessary with how much food we got from the set menu!

reviews.author: Carolyn Marie Santos
reviews.text: We got the Winter Set Menu we spent the extra $15 to get everything on thr set menu and it was worth it! Everything on the set menu was flavorful and will definitely come back. Since we were on our honeymoon we got complimentary Umai Fizz drink which was delicious, I had to order the full size. If in San Francisco this is a must go to place and getting the Winter Set Menu should be the first to try. Staff was amazing and explained every dish they served.

restaurants.description: Japanese izakaya serving traditional menus in a hip, buzzy space decked in exposed woods.

restaurants.name: HINODEYA Ramen & Bar Chestnut
restaurants.address: 3340 Steiner St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Kyler DuVall
reviews.text: An very nice place to eat, the back has a more open feel while the front has more of a Japanese bar kind of feel. We spent about $100 on two appetizers, sake, a beer, a soda, and two entrees so meh on that. But the food was delicious and the atmosphere was great too. We went on Christmas, surprisingly, and it was packed. The service was practical, we enjoyed ourselves and stayed for about an hour. We got the spicy chicken, house ramen, some calamari and gyogi. The bento boxes are actually pretty big and my wife's ramen was great too. I'd come here again.

reviews.author: E Lee
reviews.text: This location is huge with outdoor and indoor seating. The back room opens to a large dining room with plenty of group seating. Sparkling sake was good. There are a lot of peaceful decorations displayed around the restaurant. Good was okay. I thought the ramen noodles had good texture. The zen broth could have more yuzu. The pork katsu cutlet was pretty fatty. The chicken karage varied in size leaving the pieces cooked at varied levels. Overall a good place to come with a group.

reviews.author: LianeL
reviews.text: My partner and I came here for dinner based on the high reviews and love for ramen. The menu was extensive and pictures were helpful. But then the food came and it didn't live up to expectations. My partner is somewhat a connoisseur of unagi, but was very disappointed with his $33 unagi Don. The unagi was tough, chewy and lacked flavor. Despite adding some sweet chili sauce from my bento box, it still didn't help. I ordered the ramen and tempura box. Shrimp tempura was hot but it was basic like from the freezer. I ordered it with a spicy miso mini ramen but there was no spice and umami flavor was not there. Took a while to get the attention of the many servers to give us our check - even though it wasn't busy. Sadly it was an overpriced mid dinner.

reviews.author: Emily N
reviews.text: Great spicy miso ramen. The broth is very rich and the noodles had a good chew. Overall a decent price and a nice atmosphere. There’s indoor and outdoor seating. Very quick as well!

reviews.author: Katie Laskowska
reviews.text:
The bento boxes are amazing! I got the katsu bento with ramen. It had edamame, rice, spring roll, egg and even a shishito pepper. The ramen was rich in flavor. The katsu was the real star though. So insanely crispy and fresh, while cooked just right inside.
We also got the spicy miso ramen. It was full of flavor and delicious. It wasn’t nearly as spicy as we were expecting but that’s fine.
The green gyoza is also definitely worth trying.
I’d for sure come here again and can’t wait to try some of the other dishes.

restaurants.name: Amami Sushi Bistro Geary
restaurants.address: 5740 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Sheed Ranko
reviews.text:
I have the bento lunches here. They are filling and worth the money. The chicken katsu is tasty. They give you a good amount of wasabi and ginger to accompany any of your rolls.

I had only California rolls so far here and they have been good. The salad that came with the bento had a bit of moisture at the bottom of the bowl and could have used more dressing, but that is a lesser issue that I did not change my 5 star rating for the food.

The service was excellent. I overheard my server explain to another patron the differences in several pieces of sushi, patiently.

This place opened relatively recently and across the street from long time local Japanese spot Kitaro. I have not eaten at Kitaro as the standards fell off a cliff right before COVID. I hope this Amami place sticks around. I do enjoy their bento lunches.

reviews.author: David Huey
reviews.text: Tried out Amami recently and was pleasantly surprised! The sashimi was the clear standout—super fresh with thick, melt-in-your-mouth slices. We ordered one of the dinner combos, which was pretty solid, but honestly, the sashimi is what makes this place worth coming back to. Some of the newer or cooked dishes were just okay, but nothing disappointing. Service was friendly, and the vibe is laid-back and casual. Definitely coming back for more sashimi!

reviews.author: Katia Dasari
reviews.text: Very welcoming atmosphere and top-notch service! The menu has a great variety, the prices are very reasonable, and everything is super fresh. Our kids really enjoyed the gyozas and chicken karaage, and we loved the chef’s choice nigiri. We’ll definitely be back — so happy to have this place in our neighborhood!

reviews.author: J.j M
reviews.text: The omakase is on point! Writing this review has me wanting to go back again.  The specialty roll ... mouth watering.  My experience was absolutely phenomenal, not just because the food tasted so great but because the staff is incredible as well, friendly and charismatic. Great sushi, great staff, great ambiance ... this is the go-to spot alone or with friends!

reviews.author: SC
reviews.text:
Amami Sushi is a cool new spot that has a great, Japanese dining experience.
The simple, modern design is refreshing and inviting, creating a comfortable atmosphere. loved the by the window seating with the view on Geary. The service is incredibly attentive, with staff going out of their way to ensure a you get a good dining experience. The only downside was that a some menu items were missing during (no sake😕), but that didn’t detract from the overall meal. The Omakase is a must-try, showcasing an impressive selection of fresh, different variety of fish, making your taste buds explode. The “Melted in the Mouth” roll is also a standout - absolutely delicious and living up to its name with each bite. “Dragon” can be skipped though.
Highly recommend of you are looking for great sushi in a welcoming, simple environment!

restaurants.name: Chotto Matte San Francisco
restaurants.address: 50 O'Farrell St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Briana Villarreal
reviews.text:
Could imagine this place being a hit in the evening but for this time, I enjoyed a lunch on a beautiful clear day.
Starting with the lobby entrance you feel as if you’ve escaped the city. The restaurant displays a uniquely edgy and exotic vibe with much to appreciate. Would highly recommend sitting outside should weather permit.
Service was divine and made our experience smooth and comfortable.
Being that I went for lunch I opted for their Lunch Special which is a great deal. You are able to choose from 4 set menus with it being $40 per person. I went with the Land & Sea, all looked delicious.
We ended the meal with the phenomenal Matcha crepe layered cake. It was light, smooth and truly IRRESISTIBLE.
I did see previous reviews stating price is high but they do have many shareable dishes and I personally thought you get a great amount of food for their Lunch Special.

The next time you find yourself shopping in Union Square and looking for a good bite to eat, you know where to go!

reviews.author: Ann A
reviews.text:
Cozy and nice atmosphere with great service from start to finish.
The Black Cod Aji Miso is a must-order--the miso sauce pairs perfectly with the cod and is incredibly tasty. The Tentáculos de Pulpo has a strong spicy aroma, but when mixed with the purée it becomes a great combination, and the octopus is tender and well cooked. The O-Toro Aburi Sashimi is super fresh and melts in your mouth.
For the Spicy Tuna Hako Roll, the tenkasu tempura makes the rice a little hard, but the flavors are still good. Dessert was amazing--the cake is delicious, especially with the chocolate sauce and berry crumble.
Overall, a great dining experience. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Rebecca Dawson
reviews.text: At Chotto Matte everyone is having the time of their lives, and you are also having the time of your life. We went for our anniversary dinner and decided to go with both the Signature Sharing Menu and the Deluxe Sharing Menu. Everything was great and I loved the variety in all the dishes. Also, the service was incredible. All the servers were very friendly and considerate and it really added to the experience. The booth we were seated in was also so comfortable. Although you may hear the cheers of a birthday celebration at a nearby table, your own table will have a private and intimate atmosphere you can relax and enjoy your meal in.

reviews.author: Noosh z
reviews.text: My team and I came here to celebrate my birthday, and it was the perfect choice. This place is truly iconic,from the stunning atmosphere to the impeccable service. The food was outstanding, and every detail was handled with care. We had an amazing time and will definitely be returning!

reviews.author: jessicaavilene92
reviews.text: Spent one of my birthday celebrations here and WOW! Plenty aesthetic photo opportunity here! Then there was a DJ inside totally killing his set. The food was amazing. They did a sweet birthday celebration for me. Service was quick and the server was knowledgeable and nice. I would def come back again and again!

restaurants.name: Kabuto Restaurant
restaurants.address: 5121 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Jordan Crawford
reviews.text: I have come here over 100 times, and consistently Eric is innovating. After having lived in SF for 15+ years I have tried every sushi place from Robin to Ebisu to the best in Japan town. There’s no better place than this for sushi in San Francisco. You’ll never have to wait to get in, it’s priced incredibly reasonably (homemade wagyu dumpling is like $7), and everyone is so welcoming. If great food at a killer price is your metric, this place is unbeatable.

reviews.author: Jose Montano
reviews.text:
I can't recommend their omakase enough. Each and every piece/dish that we were served throughout the 18-course meal was such a delight. Beautiful presentation, great attention to detail and high quality ingredients. We were lucky enough to be served by Chef Eric, who gave us detailed explanations of the fish we were about to eat and how it was prepared. It was great chatting with him throughout the meal. Chef Eric and the rest of the Kabuto staff are very welcoming and friendly.

Can't wait to be back again!

reviews.author: Stacey
reviews.text: What a gem! The sushi is incredible and the prices are great. Creative, flavorful and fun! Very grateful to have found this wonderful sushi spot. Service is fast and friendly. Highly recommend ✨

reviews.author: Ronald Gowan
reviews.text:
You simply cannot beat the value at Kabuto. The price top quality ratio is insane as the fish is so fresh, beautifully/thoughtfully presented. It's definitely an authentic experience through and through.

It's a small mom and pop shop, so best to make reservations! They are so sweet and kind here.

reviews.author: Ella Bergquist
reviews.text:
Kabuto is the best sushi restaurant in San Francisco. Hands down a great hidden gem! I was recommended to go here and was so impressed with the complex flavors. I highly recommend the Hamachi Pear, Ceviche, and Spicy Tuna roll.

I’ll be thinking of these flavors for a long time!

restaurants.description: Japanese fish specialist for traditional & inventive sushi, plus omakase options.

restaurants.name: Wako Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.address: 211 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Clemens Wan
reviews.text: This was the best sushi Omakase I've had. Period. It was worth all the decisions Ive made to get me into that seat eating the freshest and meatiest pieces with perfect preparation. This will haunt me until I come back a year later. I can only tell time by the anchor to this experience. What time is it? Oh just 3 days since I had the best sushi of my life. Chef was super friendly and even showed us his knife collection. Come hungry. Approx 2 hours for the full course.

reviews.author: Lucid he
reviews.text:
We had such a wonderful time! The atmosphere was very friendly, and the chef was incredibly outgoing and engaging. He gave vivid, detailed explanations of each piece of sushi — including which part of the fish it came from and how it was prepared — which made the experience feel extra special and educational.

One unique touch was the in-house special drink designed to transition between the appetizer and the sushi course — such a thoughtful detail! Since it was our birthday, the restaurant even surprised us with a hand-drawn birthday card. Super cute and such a lovely gesture!

Constructive feedback:
1.	It would be great if the reservation time could be extended to 2.5 hours. Things felt a little rushed toward the end.
2.	It would also be nice to have a choice between water and hot tea as a default beverage option.

Overall, a fantastic experience — we’d definitely come back!

reviews.author: Michael Kawamoto
reviews.text: We had a lovely anniversary dinner here. The omakase was very well constructed with a balance of hot and cold dishes that complemented each other really well. The sushi was clearly meticulously made and each dish was a delight to enjoy. Highly recommend the omakase and hot sake pairing!

reviews.author: Emily G
reviews.text: My partner and I celebrated our 7-year anniversary at Wako and had an incredible experience - every bite we had was perfectly balanced and considered in the context of the meal. Everything was delicious but Chef Jin’s enthusiasm for the seasonal pike mackerel made me love it the most! Such obvious and deep respect for the food from everyone on staff, and the relaxed and cheerful vibe made it even better. We loved the staff and Chefs Jin and Ivan took care of us from the moment we stepped in the door - service was a true highlight. Friendly, funny, warm, and unpretentious, and everything you eat is explained super clearly and accessibly. Wako was the best meal I’ve had in several years, and we couldn’t have been happier to support a business run by such lovely people. Worth every penny. We’ll definitely be back!

reviews.author: Taylor Au
reviews.text: First time trying omakase, so I don't have anythin to compare this to... but overall we really enjoyed the experience. Each piece was fresh and delicious. Everyone was very friendly and knowledgeable. There was lots of variety and flavors. I did lots of research based on friends' recommendations and local SF articles. Wako ranked highly for quality, value, and experience. Would definitely recommend.

restaurants.description: Snug, rustic-chic omakase eatery presenting innovative sushi, Japanese small plates & sake.

restaurants.name: Izakaya Sozai
restaurants.address: 1500 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: ShadowMax
reviews.text:
First time walking it, it felt small but had character, you can tell this was a local's favorite. Only once have I seen this place not packed. Service was great, knowledgeable and kind. Chef was attentive to care to see if any guests had any food restrictions/allergies.

The food was amazing, not the run of the mill Japanese dishes you'd find typically. Many of the dishes I was unfamiliar with. Lotus chips was one of the groups favorites, served with tuna. French fries! Not your normal fry, Japanese yam imported from Japan, everyone loved it. Just thinking about it makes me want some. Ramen bowls were great, flavors were roasted and peppered which is quite unique. You can tell the Chef is not scared to experiment. Bone Marrowww, can't go wrong with that. So much more....Savory and Sweet.

Great experience, Great Staff, Great Food. Definitely should give it a try, I will be back soon.

reviews.author: Shelley Carr
reviews.text: What a hidden gem! I came here with some family and we ordered several things from across the menu, and at multiple points in the evening. Every single thing was beautifully presented, stunningly delicious, and we stuffed ourselves. They also have an exceptional collection of Japanese beers and sake that are all divine. Be aware this small place fills up every night of the week, so either plan ahead or get there early. I will absolutely be back the next time I’m in town. The agedashi tofu is the best I’ve ever had.

reviews.author: Andy Goebel
reviews.text: Love coming to Izakaya Sozai. We we fortunate enough to celebrate their 16 year anniversary and are happy to say we have been visiting them every one of those 16 years. The ramen is amazing. The Ankimo Pate is delicious. The staff is friendly and fun. Looking forward to many more visits.

reviews.author: Ivy N.
reviews.text: Now I have to say, for a small joint like Izakaya Sozai, they can sure serve up mini courses that will absolutely get your eyes wide open. Most of the dishes were very delicious and for a very decent price. Izakaya style, of course. My top favorite dishes were the Tako Wasabi, bacon wrapped enoki mushroom and mochi, and Ankimo pate. If you are a foodie like me and are adventurous, I recommend you come here to try it.

reviews.author: Betty L
reviews.text:
We arrived 20 minute prior to our reservation and the table was set and ready to receive us. We were seated within a few minutes and put in an order for a bottle of Sake. Our 2 other friends arrived in time when the bottle showed up at our table in an ice bin.

After a round of Sake to quench our throats, we were ready to place our food orders. We scanned the QR Code on the table. We not only orders foods off the menu but from the Chefs Menu posted on the wall.

This is a Tapas concept so hope other customers don't expect portions to be plentiful. It's all about the varieties, great place for groups and a wonderful selection of Sake offered.

Our party of 4 sat indoor, next to the entrance. The place was cozy and the staffs were really friendly.

I noticed there's a chefs table for 2, at the high counter. A party for 2 would be perfect for that spot. Maybe I'll request for that spot on a future visit.

restaurants.description: Ramen, yakitori & other traditional Japanese small plates in simple, casual surrounds.

restaurants.name: PABU Izakaya
restaurants.address: 101 California St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA

reviews.author: Allen Zhang
reviews.text:
Valentine's Day Dinner — A $175/Person Disappointment

At $175 per person, you'd expect near-Michelin-level quality in both food and service. What we got instead felt like a calculated cash grab dressed up as a special occasion dinner.

Atmosphere & Seating
The moment we walked in, it was clear the restaurant had reconfigured its layout specifically for Valentine's Day — cramming in as many 2-top tables as possible to maximize covers. The result? A packed, noisy dining room where couples are elbow-to-elbow with strangers. To make it worse, the seating was wildly inconsistent — some couples were seated in large booths meant for 6, while others (like us) were stuck at tiny two-tops with zero privacy. For a "romantic" Valentine's dinner, the atmosphere was anything but.

Food
Mediocre at best. The sashimi/nigiri plate was unremarkable, and the overall menu felt deliberately simple — likely to speed up table turnover. The steak was the highlight, with a creative sauce and solid mashed potatoes, but that's a low bar for this price point.

Drinks
We paid $40 for a glass of Moët & Chandon that arrived relatively flat and was not poured tableside — despite the table next to us receiving a full tableside pour for their champagne pairing. That level of inconsistency at this price is inexcusable.

Service
