This is where the experience truly fell apart. Our server checked on us exactly zero times throughout the entire meal. When our wine glasses were empty, we waited 20+ minutes just to flag someone down. The one request we made all night — asking if our wine could come out quickly so we could enjoy it with our steak — was met with our server rushing away and saying, "It'll come out when it comes out." On Valentine's Day. At $175 a head. Stunning.

To top it off, when a server accidentally dropped a plate nearby, a manager in a suit stood there, watched him clean it up, shook his head, and walked away — no apology to nearby guests, no offer to help. It was a perfect snapshot of the attitude permeating the entire staff.

The Bottom Line
This restaurant saw Valentine's Day as an opportunity: pack in as many tables as possible, charge premium prices, serve a stripped-down menu for fast turnover, and let service slide. The lack of hospitality wasn't accidental — it was the predictable result of a management team that prioritized profit over the experience they were selling. Save your money and your evening.

reviews.author: Kimberly Do
reviews.text:
I’ve been here twice now. The servers are friendly and polite, but service is noticeably slow, especially considering the price point. This is definitely on the expensive side.

We ordered à la carte and overall the food was good. Highlights included the tuna poke with tobiko, negi, and crisp wontons, and the house made duck gyoza with scallion, pickled ginger, and chili rayu. The butter lettuce and hearts of palm salad with radish, grapefruit, avocado, and carrot ginger dressing was fresh and well balanced.

The miso broiled black cod was good, though a bit sweet for my preference, likely from the kabocha squash. The triple seared American wagyu strip steak with fondant daikon and grilled maitake mushroom was nicely cooked and flavorful. We also had a good selection of sushi, including fatty bluefin tuna o toro, yellowtail hamachi, Michael’s negitoro, and the rainbow roll, all of which were fresh and enjoyable.

We had three cocktails and sake as well. The ambience is warm and relaxing, making it a nice place to sit and unwind. Overall, good food and atmosphere, but the slow service and high prices make it a place I’d visit occasionally rather than regularly.

reviews.author: Harry
reviews.text:
Had our team holiday lunch here. Service was exemplary, props to the team for that. As for the food... It was a bit of a mix. The nigiri was high quality and was probably my favorite. The karaage were a bit too dry, but I still enjoyed it. Flavor profile reminds me of Taiwanese popcorn chicken. As for my kastu, I was pretty disappointed. I've had better katsu for half the price. The meat was so tough too.

Overall great restaurant. Probably not one I'd go to on my own budget, but it's definitely the experience that makes things worthwhile!

reviews.author: Stelios Serghiou
reviews.text: Not recommended. Prices are in Nobu territory, but the food is barely average. The tuna carpaccio was unremarkable. The duck gyoza tasted indistinguishable from Trader Joe’s frozen gyoza. The miso cod was genuinely delicious, but the portion was comically small for the price. The Ken roll was good, but overly salty. The nigiri was the biggest disappointment. The kampachi in particular was among the worst I’ve had. They aim for a modern izakaya vibe, but the execution falls flat. The music is too loud, and the overall ambience feels forced rather than refined. Save your money and get far better sushi at Nakama, Eiji, or Okane. Very disappointed and won’t be returning.

reviews.author: Constance Shi
reviews.text:
Came here 4 years ago and loved it. Came back and… wow. What happened??
This turned into the worst dinner I’ve had all year—in a so-bad-we-could-only-laugh way.

Honestly, this place has probably survived this long with such high prices only because companies in the Financial District keep expensing fancy dinners. It’s far from a good izakaya.

Highlights of the chaos:
•	Restaurant 1/5 full on a Friday night, yet we were told to sit at the bar. On uncomfortable stools. For almost two hours.
•	It took nearly two hours to serve six dishes—the kind of timing you’d expect from a tiny 7-seat omakase where the chef performs every course right in front of you… except here, there was no performance, no chef interaction, and honestly, barely any food.
•	Sushi chefs in front of us had no idea we ordered sushi until they casually asked, “Did you guys order?”
•	Staff vanished constantly, and when I finally complained, the “manager” showed up with zero apologies. Not even a fake one.

We spent over $300 for 2 and left hungry, confused, and laughing hysterically at how surreal the whole night was.

The grand finale:
The “Wagyu on Hot Stone” that took nearly 2 hours to appear was… two tiny pieces of beef, gone in one bite each (though I have to admit it was one actually good dish, just very far from justifying its price and the crazy wait). Our waitress insisted we “needed rice” with it when we ordered it. We refused. They brought COLD rice that’s enough to feed 2 anyway as an “apology.”
Not sure what we were supposed to eat it with—our memories of the beef?

And when the bill came, the manager STILL didn’t apologize. Instead he asked—twice—
“You’ll come back, right?”
We didn’t answer. I mean, isn’t it obvious?

restaurants.description: A sleek space with a buzzy ambiance, this high-end spot offers classic Japanese cuisine in style.

restaurants.name: Early To Rise, Scratch Made Brunch
restaurants.address: 1801 McAllister St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

photos.attribution: Early To Rise, Scratch Made Brunch
Early to Rise - Scratch Made Brunch

reviews.author: Melinda Scholten
reviews.text: We had the most delicious and creative breakfast here…I can see why it’s so popular! Our server was amazing…helpful and personable. The coffee was so smooth. My French toast was a work of art. I have been back again with family and they loved it. 15/10 stars

reviews.author: Dušan
reviews.text:
Very busy and vibrant spot. Our server Brian was so nice and accommodating as well the host, that offered coffee while waiting for the table. What I really like is the simplicity of the menu, fresh ingredients and not overwhelming portions of the food.
Cherry 🍒 on the top was the chef Andrew 👨‍🍳 that approached each table ensured that each table is satisfied with the food, this place care and that's something we appreciate. We will be back indeed.  Thanks everyone once again,  especially lovely Chef Andrew!

reviews.author: Liz Cong
reviews.text:
Finally came here for brunch on a weekday with my husband. Parking was fairly easy and we were sat right away - the host asked if we had reservations- so I guess you can make reservations…..

Server was nice, explained that donuts were being made all day (two styles), so of course I had to have one (rolled in sugar) no picture because it was too delicious we ate it in 2-3 bites. Soft warm and sweet.

I ordered the shrimp and grits and he had the basic breakfast.
I also had a Bloody Mary and coffee.

Shrimp and grits had a generous amount of broth surrounding it-server said this was the chef’s favorite dish and reminiscent of ramen-good description and how I would characterize it as well.

It was a tad too seasoned for my taste-the bacon ends and broth added a lot of salt. I would have loved more grits as I think the saltiness would have been well contrasted with the delicious grits. The shrimp were abundant and cooked nicely. I loved the green onions and sliced chili peppers. I would order this again but only with a side of grits.

Husband’s food was good. Eggs over easy made perfectly and hash browns had a taste I couldn’t name but it was not like any hash browns I’d tasted before-excellent crispness, bacon perfect and arugula salad added a nice contrast to the meal.

Bloody Mary was delicious and you can add a strip of bacon if you wish.

Overall a great place for breakfast/brunch. I recommend if you’re in the neighborhood. I know it gets busy on the weekends so plan accordingly!

I’ll be back sometime to try the pancakes and French toast.

reviews.author: LisboaMac
reviews.text: Visited Christmas Eve and was quickly seated at the bar. The ambiance was great, open airy and bright with a buzz in the air from customer chatter and the busy servers hustling really well presented platters from the open kitchen. Noise was moderate, but conversation was easy. I started with a Bloody Mary + House smoked bacon which was really tasty. When my order arrived ( Shrimp & Grits ) one glance told me to get ready for something special and I was not disappointed! Just beautiful with a perfectly poached egg, 5 good sized shrimp on a bed of grits surrounded by a delicious sauce loaded with finely sliced peppers, mushrooms, onions and small bits of bacon. What I really enjoyed was the interaction of the various flavors like the soft egg yolk  oozing  onto the grits and sauce then taking a bite of shrimp and tasting the freshness explode in your mouth. I actually had to tell myself to slow down and savor this well created dish. A final observation on this and the other dishes I saw as they passed by was that the portions served are, by any standard generous, and a good value. Yes I will definitely return!

reviews.author: Pamela L
reviews.text: Located in a quiet neighborhood. Staff makes you feel welcome.  Great brunch food.  Parking is nil. The restaurant has lots of natural light which makes it welcoming in a pleasant atmosphere.  All food ordered were well plated, inviting and tasty.  Coffee and water were constantly refilled.

restaurants.description: Cozy corner venue with communal seats serving seasonal, reservations-required weekend brunch fare.

restaurants.name: Wooden Spoon
restaurants.address: 2172 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Picky Panther
reviews.text: Got big spoon, potatoes were the best. Granola french toast was unique. Fresh orange juice was delicious and refreshing. Would not recommend the huevos. Portion sizes are decent. There was a waiting line but it moved quick. Service was quick too. Bar seating is more than table seating but bar seating is super cramped. Open kitchen and bar, good atmosphere.

reviews.author: Amy Dahlberg
reviews.text: Came in without a reservation mid day on a weekend and was able to get a spot at the bar without a wait. The bartender/waiter was incredibly friendly and helpful. In fact, all the staff were almost joyful and seemed happy to be there, which made this experience so pleasant. I ordered the grilled cheese and tomato soup and my partner had the standard breakfast. We both got mimosas as well, which the bartender was quick to ask us if we wanted refills. The food was delicious and the atmosphere was very relaxed and made for a nice afternoon. All of the eclectic wooden spoons that decorate the interior add to a their vibe and brand. We absolutely will be coming back. I saw quite a few things on the menu I’d like to try.

reviews.author: Vash
reviews.text:
Really solid brunch spot with an upscale, clean vibe and a menu full of interesting options. We tried the Dr Birch muesli, veggie bowl, salmon scramble, and Swedish pancakes. The muesli stood out — hearty, not overly sweet, and packed with nuts and fruit. The salmon scramble was also really well done and felt satisfying without being heavy.

We went around noon on a Sunday, and it was packed, but the wait wasn’t bad if you’re fine with bar seating.

I’d definitely go back and work my way through more of the menu.

reviews.author: rob strechay
reviews.text: This place is a gem! Ian, our server, was fantastic and helpful as it was out first visit and everything looked awesome. We sat at the kitchen bar and could see all the dishes coming out. Everything looked awesome and was prepared with care. It's not cheap but the portions are generous, so bring an appetite. Coffee and cappuccino were fantastic. Great beers, even though didn't start there! Will visit again when in town.

reviews.author: William Heikkie Dean Mills
reviews.text:
Came out of curiosity, the menu was interesting. Lots of counter seats and a host stand that leads to tables. Divorced eggs is a childhood favorite, and who can resist a Hangtown Fry?
Attentive service, good prices, and very delicious!
Enjoy!

restaurants.description: Elevated brunch fare & cocktails served in a quaint, stylish space with marble counters & beadboard.

restaurants.name: Sisterita
restaurants.address: 669 Commercial St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA

reviews.author: Anjera C
reviews.text: What a neat place! The decor and food is great inside. There was one party that seemed to enjoy it so much they came back the next day! They were quite popular on a Sunday morning when the rest of the city was quiet. I ordered the Secret cocktail and the Voila French Toast. We also ordered the Emerald which I heard was good and a latte. The Emerald eggs benedict seemed to be poached well and the green sauce it came with was beautiful. While the food took some time, the Secret cocktail was quite a good sneaky cocktail to sip on. It was pretty sweet and mostly tasted of lychee, but I didn’t mind it. It was slightly one-noted but still good and beautiful overall. The Voila French Toast was really great. I didn’t know French Toast bread could be shaped like an oval, and it was so soft inside. The caramelized bananas were great with the candied walnuts if that is your thing. I personally didn’t enjoy the walnuts as much, but the textures are fun, and the cream of vanilla goes really well with the French toast as a drizzled topping or dip. I’d love to come back to try their other items!

reviews.author: Simone Alexis Sonnier-Jang
reviews.text:
I was late to my reservation due to a BART delay and they graciously moved my time for me. When I arrived, I had my bag and my backpack for my mini vacation. They didn't treat me like an inconvenience at all. Some restaurants are very lacking in their customer service, and they would have given me subpar service. The waiters were attentive and the food was great, except for one small piece of egg shell. I have done that before while cooking. And I was coming in with 40 minutes to spare for their operational hours, so, they were probably rushed. I totally understand. I also understand that when I get eggshell in my food it is because I am not professionally trained. The atmosphere was very pleasant, so was the decor. The matcha latte and tiger stripe latte were both delicious, and every bite of the florentine eggs benedict, except that last bite, was delicious. I was kinda disappointed, but everything in life can't be perfect. Solid 4 stars!

P.S. Be careful with the potatoes they're lava inside. My mouth is burned. Let them sit for like 5 minutes plus before eating them. They may look OK to eat, but no, they're literally lava.

I looked it up and restaurants are supposed to tell you the food is hot, especially if it isn't visible. I came to San Francisco for a 3 day feast, with reservations at various restaurants around the city, and this 1st restaurant ruined it for me. It's day 2 and I'm settling for anything on the menu that isn't hot at my second reservation this morning. Kinda upset about it. I'll have to visit again, but I will avoid Sisterita at all costs. Even if it were free, I don't appreciate negligence.

reviews.author: Gaelle Eustache
reviews.text: Almost gave this place 5 stars. Seems to be pretty popular, stopped in without a reservation and there was quite a wait. It was about 11am. Service was fast, maybe too fast but I understand due to the line out the door. All 4 of our plates tasted great & were fresh. We decided to go back the next day and unfortunately we were disappointed. Stopped in earlier the next day around 820. Service was again fast, but this time our food was cold and not as fresh as the day before. The steak was chewy & flavorless & the French fries were oily. Kind of wish we didn’t return and kept the satisfaction from the first day

reviews.author: Virginia Huffman
reviews.text:
Super cute Café with friendly service.

We sat at the counter because the place was already packed, and that was our only option. Glad we did as we were served by the lovely April.

The coffee was amazing. So many other restaurants have incredibly strong and really bitter coffee and this was perfectly smooth and balanced. There are  lots of options for trying creative drinks and cocktails.

For breakfast I enjoyed the avocado toast with egg and tomatoes. My husband ordered the french onion soup which he gave thumbs up.

Will definitely return.

reviews.author: Yumile andrea Leguizamon tibata
reviews.text: The place is charming, the atmosphere and the staff's attention are wonderful. The cocktails and drinks are very well prepared and the food is exquisite. We spent a week in Chicago and went there for breakfast every day. The hostess is unique and attends to every detail. I recommend it 100%.

restaurants.name: Sweet Maple
restaurants.address: 2101 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Mary Maldonado
reviews.text:
This morning I visited Sweet Maple for brunch with high expectations.

I ordered the New York steak medium rare, but unfortunately it was served medium. The presentation was good, but when it comes to steak, temperature is everything, and I specifically did not order medium. When the server came to remove the plate, my dog barked unexpectedly, which startled him and caused coffee to spill onto the table. It was an awkward moment, but the staff handled it professionally and remained attentive throughout.

The overall service was good and friendly, and the waiting time was pretty standard for a brunch spot. The décor is simple and basic, nothing particularly memorable, but comfortable enough.

I also ordered a Nutella crepe, and here's where I was truly disappointed. It wasn't Nutella.. it tasted like regular chocolate spread. And as someone who considers herself a Nutella connoisseur, I can tell the difference immediately. If you advertise Nutella, it should be Nutella.

Overall, it was a decent experience with some details that could definitely be improved. I would give it another chance, hoping next time the steak temperature and the "Nutella" live up to expectations.

reviews.author: Melissa Eisner
reviews.text: Well worth the wait. Came on a busy weekend and there was a line … ended up waiting about 30 minutes for a table so not too bad. They make a good cup of coffee, and the service is pretty fast. I ordered the eggs with tots, and upgraded to the millionaires bacon, which was fabulous. Totally lived up to the hype. The omelette was also great, loved the multi color home potatoes. Will definitely come back. Yum!!!

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text: Delicious food and friendly service! The line may look long but moves pretty quickly, especially if you’re willing to sit outside in cooler weather. The Bay Bottom Omelet is one of my favorites and comes with a generous serving of crab. Their bread is AMAZING. The fried french toast is good but tastes like dessert/fair food, so be prepared! The millionaire bacon is melt-in-your-mouth, sweet, and spicy. It’s worth trying at least once. The food is always quality!

reviews.author: Marty Ignacio
reviews.text: We stumbled on this looking for a breakfast spot. We walked in and was seated before 8 am. I got the omurice dish and it was very tasty. We tried the bacon and was seasoned to perfection. We would definitely come back again!

reviews.author: Kevin Drost
reviews.text:
Sweet Maple is definitely a brunch spot worth the hype! I ordered their Big Hip— their famous deep-fried French toast— and wow, it did not disappoint. Crispy on the outside, soft and warm on the inside, and paired perfectly with pure Vermont maple syrup, butter, and powdered sugar. Absolute perfection.

But the real star of the show? The Millionaire Bacon. Oh. My. God. Hands down the best bacon I’ve ever had in my life— rich, slightly spicy, sweet, and just insanely flavorful. Even if you don’t order a full meal, you HAVE to at least get a side of this bacon. It’s a must-try and totally worth the trip just for that alone!

restaurants.description: Local spot for homemade morning/lunch fare like signature brown-sugar-glazed “millionaire’s bacon.”

restaurants.name: Outerlands
restaurants.address: 4001 Judah St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Lauren Davidson
reviews.text: Outerlands is a lovely oasis of fresh baked bread and homemade soup in the Sunset District. I booked dinner reservations here for my birthday and was warmly greeted with a glass of sparkling rosé. My server was so friendly and genuine and took excellent care of me! The walls and ceiling are made from weathered wood and all the food is local and organic. Chef Landa prepares delicious, hearty, and attractive appetizers, meals and desserts. I ordered the beans and greens: bone broth, cannellini beans, kale, and grana padano cheese, served with pain au levain.. it was so good! I also enjoyed a hibiscus margarita, which was beautiful and refreshing, and took home a chocolate chip cookie for dessert. Yum! Highly recommend making a reservation, especially for brunch, as this is a very popular neighborhood restaurant and fills up fast everyday of the week.

reviews.author: American Angler
reviews.text:
Autopsy Joe and I were on our way out to Ocean Beach for a little Surf Pearch and Crab fishing and thought we'd snag a little breakfast before we got to the sand.  Well, it was dark, cold, and damp and Joe says we'll stop at the Outerlands breakfast restaurant because not only is the food amazingly delicious... but they're open!  Sometimes it's hard to find a really good breakfast restaurant that's open this early in the morning, but the Outerlands is a true morning miracle!
5 stars and worthy of a stop! ☆☆☆☆☆

reviews.author: Gabriela Calana
reviews.text: Cozy place perfect for drinks, small plates, or nice dinner. The Brussels sprouts and potato peak soup are delicious. The pear sorbet was very refreshing and tasteful. Lastly, the cocktails were great and well as their wine menu.

reviews.author: Sean Anderson
reviews.text: This place is super cozy! We were able to get a seat for 2 for dinner without a reservation. The place had just the right amount of people chatting it up. The service was quick, the servers seemed happy, and the food was impeccable. I will concede that it was $36 for a chicken dinner, and I thought that was a little pricey, but it was honestly a really good meal and was worth the price. We got some of their fresh baked bread w/ butter and that was delicious, too.

reviews.author: Angela Oliveira Pisco
reviews.text: We had the mortadela sandwich and the Dutch pancake, they’re both small. The sandwich had no sides, which I expected for the price. Coffee was fine, but not the best and no refills. No service, it’s find a table yourself, stand in line to order, grab your water and utensils.

restaurants.description: Cozy place with weathered-wood walls serving New American eats, housemade bread & a popular brunch.

restaurants.name: Peacock Pansy
restaurants.address: 392 Fulton St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Moyette Gordon
reviews.text: Group of 4. Ordered several dishes and everything was delicious. The customer service was great, especially considering how busy it was during Super Bowl weekend but the staff handled the crowd like pros! Would definitely recommend this place. Make reservations, especially on popular weekends!

reviews.author: Debra Johnson Chin
reviews.text:
This place has excellent food, a great place for brunch. The space is small and quiet crowded on the weekends. Place a reservation. There is outdoor seating which I would recommend on a nice day.
Vegan French toast is to die for.

reviews.author: Natasha P
reviews.text:
We stopped by for brunch at Peacock Pansy yesterday and must say, our experience was a 10/10.

The staff was so attentive and polite, despite the heavy foot traffic due to Valentine’s Day. The food was absolutely delicious and worth every penny.

If you’re coming on a holiday, make sure to make a reservation or arrive early like we did, as the crowd does pick up and you’ll have quite a wait.

reviews.author: Kristen Brady
reviews.text: Cute brunch spot with whimsy decor and tasty food but slow-ish service. I came on a weekday morning and waited a few minutes to be seated despite it not being busy (however it seemed they were lacking staff). The menu had many enticing options. I ended up ordering the tom yum crab omelet and a latte. My food arrived at a reasonable pace but I didn’t get my latte until 5-10 minutes after my food was served. The food itself was delicious. The omelette was packed with crab and the sauce had amazing flavour. The mushrooms were a great addition to the omelette. Potatoes were well seasoned and I honestly didn’t even have room for the toast. This place could be five stars with more staff

reviews.author: Jenny Wakeman
reviews.text: Great breakfast for vegans. Loved their coconut caramel coffee with oat milk as well. The French toast special was so Lovely...           Lovely service and a calm atmosphere. Thank you for the stay.

restaurants.name: Plain Jane
restaurants.address: 1000 Guerrero St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: nora cavett
reviews.text: First time at plain Jane and it was a wonderful experience. Drove all the way from Vacaville for breakfast, walking into the restaurant the atmosphere is calm and warm and friendly. Got greeted by staff right away and sat in under 1 min. Sat at the table for about 10 min before our orders got taken and then waited around at her 15-20 for our food. Bit longer than I would like to wait when the restaurant wasn’t busy but the food made it worth it. Food came out hot and perfect presentation was perfect. The shrimp and grits was nice and rich plenty of shrimp and a good sausage. The only thing that could have been better was for the grits to be seasoned a bit more. The bathroom was very clean and nicely maintained. 10/10 experience and worth the drive.

reviews.author: Elaine Kwong
reviews.text: This is hands-down my favorite breakfast spot in all of SF. Everything is consistently superb, from the ingredients to the execution. My go-to order is the Big Jane’s Plate. The scrambled eggs are perfectly fluffy and moist, and the potatoes are seasoned just right with that fresh-out-of-the-oven crunch. And the coconut rice pancakes? Unreal. So fluffy and nutty, easily the best pancakes I’ve ever had. Will be going back on a weekly basis!

reviews.author: Charlotte Clark
reviews.text:
Ordered a coffee (not the greatest) and went with the Monte Cristo sandwich with greens on the side, which was a great choice. The greens were very fresh and the balsamic was excellent. Whenever a MC is on a menu, I always order one. This place has by-far the best in The Bay.

My husband ordered a Bloody Mary that tasted very fresh, and the accoutrements were wonderful. He also enjoyed the duck hash with potatoes. One small note: the potatoes could benefit from a light herby finish to elevate the flavor, but overall the dish was enjoyable.

reviews.author: Abhi
reviews.text:
This spot absolutely delivers on a classic, satisfying breakfast and brunch. The value is spot-on for San Francisco; you get generous, well-prepared portions without the shocking big-city price tag.
I have to single out the stuffed French toast—it was incredible! Absolutely lived up to the hype and is a must-order.
Be prepared, especially on weekends, as it gets very busy. But honestly, the food is worth a little patience

reviews.author: Jay Biz
reviews.text: it’s so small in there that the people next to you are basically sitting at your table with you. No space at all.  I didn’t even take a picture of the food, that’s how over it I was. Half the potatoes were over-fried and trash. Seems like they expect you to season your own food around here too… I guess that’s a thing. But after I added some salt and pepper my oyster scramble was good. I ate one Pancake pf the 2. If you are not from California this tax is not a tip, it's to pay health insurance, So make sure

restaurants.description: Bustling eatery with quaint vintage dishes serving elevated breakfast & brunch dishes with mimosas.

restaurants.name: Eight AM Brunch
restaurants.address: 1323 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Michael Monteiro Fishing
reviews.text:
Just over a year ago, my family and I visited 8 AM Brunchbin for the first time, and we walked away talking about two things: how good the food was and how genuinely kind and attentive the customer service felt. That combination stuck with us so much that on this trip, we made it a point to come back—and ended up eating breakfast here two days in a row.

The food is exactly as we remembered: incredibly fresh, simply prepared, and absolutely bursting with flavor. The lox bagel was perfect, and the waffle might be one of the best I’ve ever had. We actually found ourselves debating how the cooks manage to make something so straightforward taste so exceptional.

The service is still top‑tier—friendly, patient, and welcoming in a way that makes you feel like they’re truly happy you’re there.

Highly recommended. This place has earned a permanent spot on our San Francisco must‑visit list.

reviews.author: Spencer Q
reviews.text:
Food: 3/5
Service: 3/5
Ambiance: 3/5
Portion to Price: 3/5
Overall: Standard fare.

The extended review...

Food:

Banana Nut Waffle - 3/5 - Taste was pretty standard fare.  Waffle was fairly crispy.

Service: 3/5 - Standard service.

Ambiance: 3/5 - Pretty standard.  They have both indoor and outdoor seating.

Portion to Price: 3/5 - Standard portion size.

reviews.author: CH NG
reviews.text: Great coffee. Friendly staff. The portion of the avocado toast 3 ways is good for 2 to share. Will come back !

reviews.author: Carter Denny
reviews.text: I stopped here around 10 AM on a Saturday. It was busy so we had to wait a few minutes for a table. The service here was great and very quick. I had the open faced bagel with avocado, egg and ham - it was a great combo. The fruit cup that came with it was very generous and fresh. I also had a crepe with peanut butter and banana. It was delicious. I would definitely recommend stopping here.

reviews.author: Walter Leviz
reviews.text: Eight AM is an absolute gem for breakfast in the heart of San Francisco’s tourist area. The food is beautifully plated and just as delicious as it looks — every dish feels fresh and made with care. Service is quick, and the whole team is super friendly, which really adds to the experience. It’s the kind of spot that makes you want to linger a little longer over your coffee and enjoy the vibe. For me, it was the perfect way to start the day, and I’d recommend it to anyone visiting the city. Definitely worth the stop and easily a 5-star breakfast spot.

restaurants.description: Compact, modern cafe serving American breakfast & lunch classics, plus coffee & smoothies.

restaurants.name: Board and Drink
restaurants.address: 1329 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Sandra Tassel
reviews.text: We are so happy we stumbled on this place today. Our omelette was large enough to share, full of tasty ingredients, cooked perfectly. In addition we enjoyed a side of chicken sausage and a lovely fruit salad. The staff/owners were delightful, service was ideal, food arrived quickly. Interior is simple clean and bright.

reviews.author: Jayasruthi V R
reviews.text:
The place is small with few tables but it’s cute. Ambience could have been more aesthetic and cozy.

Menu options are very good for brunch. Food is great. French toast was the best! Especially the whipped cream. The fruits were also very fresh. Potatoes could have been more crispier. Omelette was average. Tater tots were very well seasoned and crunchy.

Service was perfect. Everyone was polite and nice and it was fast service.

reviews.author: Michelle Barnes
reviews.text: Delicious brunch if you've got a sweet tooth! My friend and I arrived on a Sunday just past 1pm and were seated right away. We ordered the sweet board for 2 with brioche French toast, pancakes, and sausage. The board came with fresh strawberries, blueberries, blackberries and raspberries, eggs, and toppings of syrup, Nutella, and berry butter. We also ordered a side of tater tots as the board didn't come with any potatoes. The board was beautifully presented everything tasted delicious. My friend loved the Nutella and I was a big fan of the berry butter! We honestly didn't even need the tator tots as the board was quite filling. My Friend also ordered a strawberry matcha latte, which she said was quite good! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Shane J
reviews.text:
Great coffee with generous food portion sizes. Unfortunately, there were a couple of letdowns; the bread served with the omelette was very hard and overcooked, and the pancakes didn’t arrive with the choc chips as ordered. The café lacks a little atmosphere but is clearly very popular, so getting in early is recommended.
Note that an automatic 20% gratuity is added to the bill for groups of four or more.

reviews.author: Arisa Chuklin
reviews.text:
Great brunch spot for foodies and instagrammers! The loaded tater tots were the bomb - twas definitely our favorite dish, even after trying other stuff. The shakshuka was tasty, and the Espresso Martini is a must-try!

Portion was quite big for us. The wait wasn’t long and we went around Saturday noon!

restaurants.name: Fable
restaurants.address: 558 Castro St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Moorissa Tjokro
reviews.text: loved the atmosphere! food was rather decent and not much of my particular liking, but service was nice and we really enjoyed being in their patio! their garden was absolutely charming and beautiful.

reviews.author: Brandon H
reviews.text:
Fable’s biggest draw is definitely the backyard garden dining area — it’s spacious, charming, and a great setting for catching up with friends. That part lived up to the hype. Unfortunately, the food didn’t quite match the atmosphere.

I ordered the fried chicken sandwich and specifically asked the waiter whether it was chicken thigh or breast. He told me it was dark meat, but it was very clearly chicken breast, and on the dry side. I’ve had solid chicken breast sandwiches before, but this one missed the mark texture-wise. It was seasoned fairly well, but I strongly prefer fried chicken sandwiches made with thigh, so this was disappointing — especially after asking directly. I didn’t make a point to call it out at the table, but it’s a pet peeve when staff confidently give incorrect info. Beyond preference, accuracy matters, especially for dietary or allergy reasons.

The breakfast potatoes on the side were just okay — slightly undercooked and not crispy enough for my liking. My friends both ordered the double cheeseburger and described it as “fine” but nothing memorable. The patties were on the smaller side and noticeably smaller than the bun, which is always a miss in my book.

Overall, Fable is a decent option if you’re looking for a pleasant, inviting space to hang out and talk, but I wouldn’t come back specifically for the food. Good vibe, average execution.

reviews.author: Sacha O
reviews.text:
What a wonderful dinner we had! There weren’t many vegetarian options but there were enough for a fabulous dinner. We had the empanadas to start- filled with potatoes and drizzled with a yummy sauce- and a very lightly dressed small salad on the side was a nice surprise. They were Delicious. Then we went with a salad with orange slices and more. This was a very good sized salad and tasty too. Finally we shared the vegan lasagna. With fresh basil and a light tomato sauce, the flavors popped. The noodles on the bottom were firm and the vegan cheese did not over power. The fresh veggies in it were really good too. Splitting the 3 dishes was just about the right amount of food and every thing we had was wonderful.

The service was great as well. Everyone was friendly and nice, but also gave us our space. The great thing is that they didn’t bombard us with everything all at once. They timed it perfectly so we could relax and enjoy the meal.

We’ll be back soon I hope. I didn’t get to try the strawberry dessert and it sounded really good.

reviews.author: Matt N
reviews.text: Food was on par with very nice covered courtyard dining area. Tasty dishes made with quality, fresh ingredients. Menu was varied enough to cover our group’s dietary restrictions and open air dining made it possible for the immuno-compromised to dine out unmasked. Not an easy combination to find!

reviews.author: Daniel Johnson
reviews.text: This is one of those classic can’t go wrong with any choice type of places. Nothing is way better than something else, everything delivers equal quality and quantity. The prices are average for SF and the vibe is very cute. The garden space is nice and has heaters, because you know… SF. Service was friendly and attentive and we overall had a great experience.

restaurants.description: Casual spot serving modern American brunch & dinner in a storybook-inspired setting.

restaurants.name: Mymy
restaurants.address: 1500 California St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Richard Coney
reviews.text: There were 4 of us.  Came to about 35.00 per person.  Including a carafe of mimosas and a coffee.  Everyone loved their food.  I am the one star drop and let me perfectly honest.  I had classic Eggs Benedict . Thiers says right on the menu it has grilled tomatoes.  I never had it. I personally think the tomatoes made the muffin to wet. Watery wet not yoke or hollandaise.  So I'm sure if i remove it , they will get the extra star. Definitely worth the visit.  Our wait time was 30 minutes.  I will go back again!

reviews.author: Аlisa Paludeti
reviews.text: I love this small but mighty brunch spot on Nob Hill. The wait  is always there - expect 20-40 minutes in the line outside but you will be rewarded! Their main dish portions are huge and the food is great. I like to take miss Mymy with lemon ricotta pancakes, poached eggs and beacon. The service is friendly and nice despite the place being packed at all times. The prices are also fair for the amount and quality of food you get!

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text:
This was the best brunch I’ve found so far in SF. I got the Baja California Benedict and it blew me away! It came with a very generous portion of dungeness crab. The hollandaise complimented the crab meat beautifully but didn’t overpower it. The pile of shrimp on the side was a pleasant, delicious surprise! And the crispy potatoes were everything you could ask for in a crispy potato. Perfect for scooping up the last bits of yolk and sauce.

Incredible meal and great service. I had no wait as a single diner on a Friday morning. Thank you so much! I can’t wait to come back!

reviews.author: Neal Mueller
reviews.text: MyMy serves brunch with a level of precision that makes every dish feel engineered. The French toast is the standout: the salted butter is the key, and the bread has a texture close to bread pudding without collapsing. The burger is solid, but it’s better with the side salad instead of fries because the greens are fresher and pair well with the rest of the menu. The lobster eggs Benedict is best ordered with low mayo, which keeps the dish balanced, and the potato wedges are the strongest part of the plate. They’re cooked evenly, crisp on the outside, soft inside, and best with a little extra salt and Tapatía hot sauce.

reviews.author: HappyGoLucky
reviews.text:
Absolutely worth the wait! We spent almost 40 mins waiting in the line, but do not let that deter you.

The service was truly great. Despite the restaurant being completely packed, our server was attentive, friendly, and managed to check on us just often enough without hovering. She handled the high volume with incredible grace and a genuine smile.

​And the food? Oh my goodness! Everything we ordered was absolutely yummy! Fresh, perfectly cooked. The banana souffle pancakes were divine!

Will definitely come back :)

restaurants.description: Colorful cafe for breakfast & brunch fare like eggs Benedict, pancakes & French toast, plus mimosas.

restaurants.name: Four Chairs
restaurants.address: 3282 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Priscilla
reviews.text: Oh my! Oh my! So yummy! Perfect spot for breakfast and brunch. Menu is not extensive but has plenty of options you can’t go wrong. We had a sweet tooth so we tried their crème brûlée French toast and their mascarpone pancakes. Both were DELISH and not too sweet at all! Paired it with their iced tea volcano coffee. The place inside is neat with a cute atmosphere. Service was excellent. I’ll get their savory menu next time.

reviews.author: Lani X
reviews.text:
Food: 4.5 stars (rounded up to 5)
I had the Korean fried chicken bene.  Looks spicy from the photos but it's more tangy than spicy.  Chicken was tender and well sauced. Eggs were perfect and the Benedict sauce finished the dish nicely. Potatoes we extra crispy (as I requested) and were a perfect texture but they weren't very well seasoned. I had plenty of food on my plate so I mostly ignored the potatoes.

The matcha drinks were my friends so I can't comment on those but they sure were pretty!

Had a bite of my friend's chicken and waffles.  Again, chicken was cooked well and the waffle was light and crispy!

Service: My friend arrived a bit before opening and we showed just after 10.  They were able to seat us immediately, which was great because although it was Monday, it was also a holiday.  When we left, I noticed the line was much longer and ppl were also waiting outside.

Once inside, they took our order pretty quick and the food was delivered promptly.

Atmosphere: cute spot, tables were well spaced...

Will be back!

reviews.author: serenity
reviews.text:
First, FOOD WAS GREAT. I had the French toast crème Brulé and it was creamy not too sweet! My boyfriend had the chicken and waffles and he said it was good, chicken was good but little dry.

The coffee: extremely POWERFUL im a coffee girly but wow it was strong, good but strong so I added some creamer to help haha.
Seating: place slightly crowded but im assuming it’s because the food is good

reviews.author: Kanekoa Pawn White
reviews.text: The atmosphere is amazing. We are visiting from Hawaii and loved how this place felt warm and inviting. The food is very delicious! Would recommend the pancakes and the K-Special. The only downside is the staff. They may be short a person or two. We had to wait a good min for our check AND for our check to be processed.

reviews.author: Susie S
reviews.text: This place is actually worth the wait. It took about 40 minutes for us to be seated. The service was very efficient once we got our table. The coffee drinks were very tasty and the food was excellent. The service was courteous, friendly, and timely. We will be back for sure!

restaurants.name: Son and Garden
restaurants.address: 700 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Michelle Sanchez
reviews.text: We finally got to check off Son and Garden from our to-do list on our last day in San Francisco, and it was a fantastic choice! The food was delightful; I ordered the breakfast egg, which was served with fresh fruit, seasoned potatoes, and a delicious blueberry scone. I also enjoyed a refreshing glass of fresh-squeezed orange juice. While they offer a variety of cute alcoholic drinks, we opted to skip them since we were traveling. My husband ordered the chicken sandwich, which he thoroughly enjoyed, accompanied by crispy fries and an iced black coffee.The ambiance of the place is charming, with a lovely tea theme that adds to its appeal. It’s definitely a must-visit.

reviews.author: Spencer Q
reviews.text:
Food: 3/5
Service: 3/5
Ambiance: 4/5
Portion to Price: 3/5
Overall: An instagram worthy establishment.

The extended review...

Food:

Deep fried French toast - 3/5 - Definitely a specialty that most places do not have.  In ranks standard fare amongst others I've had.  Kudos for the plating work.

Service: 3/5 - Friendly staff.

Ambiance: 4/5 - Quite the nice interior and it's totally decked out.

Portion to Price: 3/5 - Pricing is a little on the steeper side but there are definitely many elements to the plate

reviews.author: Jasper Colville
reviews.text: Absolutely loved Son and Garden! The food is delicious , especially the Bibimbap and Royal English Breakfast . Great atmosphere , friendly stuff, and beautifully presentation. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Suthasinee A
reviews.text:
Son & Garden is such a beautiful and dreamy spot! The interior is filled with flowers and pastel colors, making it perfect for photos. The food is not just pretty but also delicious. I loved corn pop and sticky rice tiger prawns– both were flavorful and well-presented. Service was friendly and fast. Great place for dinner with friends or a special date.

Highly recommend if you’re looking for a cute and cozy restaurant in the city!

reviews.author: Val Fredericks
reviews.text: Really fun brunch or special event place. Food is delicious, cocktails are different and delightful, and the service is great. I would go back and recommend to anyone for a fun different meal.

restaurants.description: Pastels, plants & flowery wallpaper outfit this cozy destination that serves New American dishes.

restaurants.name: Plow
restaurants.address: 1299 18th St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Shawn Hermosillo
reviews.text:
Plow

If you’re over in the State Streets area of San Francisco and need a late breakfast around 9–10 AM, this is a fantastic place to stop.

I recently dropped my wife at the airport and swung by afterward — and it was exactly what I was hoping for. Busy enough on a weekday morning to know the food is great, but still running efficiently. That steady crowd is always a good sign.

The food absolutely delivers. Fresh ingredients, generous portions, and everything cooked just right. Their breakfast plates are hearty without feeling heavy, and the flavors are clean and well-balanced. You can tell they take pride in what they serve.

What stood out just as much as the food was the service. Even with a full dining room, the team kept things moving with a positive, welcoming energy. It’s the kind of place where the consistency and hospitality make you understand why people show up for a late weekday breakfast.

If you’re making an airport run from Santa Rosa like I was, this is a perfect stop on the way back. I’ll definitely be coming here again.

reviews.author: Lily Wu
reviews.text: Usually I don’t have any opinion at all on brunch places because the sameyness of the food and vibes tend to blend in together, but Plow does every element so well that it really left an impression and I cannot wait to go back. Everything was delicious (especially the  duck confit), but what really impressed me were the potatoes. Usually brunch potatoes end up being soggy and bland, but these were so, so, crispy. The buttermilk biscuit was soft and had a great flavor. Staff were nice. Love love love.

reviews.author: Manny
reviews.text:
We have been trying to come here for years, but we could never stick around for the hour-plus wait typical on weekends. We finally came on a Tuesday at 8 a.m. and were seated immediately. The interior is a well-lit, spacious, modern organic space.
​The staff was friendly and the food was delicious. We ordered the buttermilk biscuit and 'The Plow,' which comes with eggs, lemon ricotta pancakes, choice of meat (I had the pork sausage), and their famous Plow potatoes. The potatoes and pancakes were my highlights. The potatoes had the perfect balance of seasoning and crispiness, while the pancakes were fluffy and flavorful. Next time, I'll swap the sausage for bacon and try the French toast, which I hear is incredible.

reviews.author: Lisa J
reviews.text: We went at about noon on a weekday and had only a 10 minute wait.  Delicious food!  Loved the crispy potatoes and the hit of lemon in the pancakes was great.  The grilled cheese was fantastic, along with the huge salad.  I'm picky about coffee and their decaf was really good.  I was super impressed with the rapid service from everyone.  LOVE this place!

reviews.author: Tony d
reviews.text:
Absolutely obsessed with this place. The atmosphere, the staff, and the food are truly out of this world. There is a reason there is a wait every weekend.

The fried egg sandwich is unreal, and the prosciutto and gruyere toast is next level. Every dish feels thoughtful and perfectly executed. You can taste the quality in everything.

I have been in four out of the last five days and still cannot get enough. The team is warm, welcoming, and makes you want to keep coming back.

Hands down one of the best spots to eat in San Francisco.

restaurants.description: Popular brunch spot serving farm-to-table American comfort food in a cozy ambience.

restaurants.name: Lapisara Eatery
restaurants.address: 698 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Monica Ifland
reviews.text: First time visit as we were actually headed for a nearby establishment.  We walked by and saw the menu out front, the cheery interior and decided to give it a try.  We lucked out on a weekend (arrived early) and were seated without a wait.  The staff was warm and welcoming.  Food came fast.  The variety on the menu was impressive and I loved the Thai additions.  Other than my Thai sausage being a bit gristly, everything was great.  The flavor of the sausage added a nice variation on the regular breakfast fare found elsewhere.  The lattes were adorable and post worthy in themselves.  Agree with other reviewers that the ube latte is beyond cute, if sweet drinks are not for you, opt for a regular or matcha latte.  The proprietor (or Management?)came to our table to check in which is always a nice touch.  We are really happy with this serendipitous stop for breakfast.  Don’t think twice about it, just go.

reviews.author: Lauren Rodriguez
reviews.text: My husband and I came here on a Saturday morning around 10:30 and got a table right away. The staff was so friendly and the lattes and food were amazing. I can’t think of anything I would change about this place and I hope we get to try it again next time we visit San Francisco. 10/10. I had the Thai Panang omelette and it was like a cup of beef curry wrapped in egg. It was just the right amount of spicy and sweet. Reminded me of Khao soi which is my favorite Thai dish. The dressing on the salad was perfection and the potatoes were just a little crispy and so light and airy. My husband got the chicken and waffles and it was by far the best we’ve ever tried. The chicken was perfectly crispy and juicy. And the drinks are so cute and delicious. Don’t hesitate to try this place out.

reviews.author: david hargraves
reviews.text:
Cozy corner cafe offering excellent menu of traditional favorites BUT you come here for the Thai inspired menu items.   I had the Thai Panang omelette and was one of best omelettes I’ve ever eaten.  Flavorful. Colorful.  The beef was tender. The vegetables were crispy. The sauce was exquisite and the presentation was first rate.

I don’t have a critical thing to say about this dish.  If you like spicy and bursting flavors, it’s a must have.

reviews.author: Brandy Buttram
reviews.text:
Consistently really good. As a local, the brunch is one of my absolute favorites in the city. Think regular brunch items with a Thai influence. Must have items are the chicken & waffles, the panang omlette, and the tom yum burrito. Also, the ube latte is delicious and so cute.

The restaurant is family ran and the staff is incredibly nice and friendly. They also have bottomless mimosas.

reviews.author: Yoz M
reviews.text:
Great food, decent everything else.

Jan/26 update: The meals are still lovely, although I noticed they changed their menu and it no longer includes the pancake I had previously which I was sad about. Ended up going next door to Heist a few more times because of that.
One more note, this time there was an incense burning when I entered. This is far too strong scent for a restaurant, may be better not using it.

restaurants.description: Chic destination serving American classics for three meals a day, plus a few Thai dinner dishes.

restaurants.name: Doppio Coffee & Brunch
restaurants.address: 1551 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Subhan Ansari
reviews.text: I visited Doppio Coffee & Brunch today and had a great experience. They offer delicious vegetarian breakfast options, and the coffee was excellent — they use Lavazza beans, which made the coffee really smooth and flavorful. The café was very clean, calm, and relaxing, with beautiful Turkish music playing in the background. It’s a perfect place for chatting and spending time with friends. The staff were also very friendly and welcoming.

reviews.author: Shanna A
reviews.text:
I was on my way to an appointment near Gough and felt prompted to pause and find a quiet breakfast spot—and this place turned out to be such a blessing. From the moment I walked in, there was a calm, welcoming atmosphere, genuine hospitality, and care in every detail. The food was fresh, beautifully plated, and prepared with intention—you could feel it.

I’ve learned that God often speaks in the pauses, in the moments we don’t plan but are led into. This stop was one of those reminders. If you visit, take a moment to say hello to Firat and the crew, and treat yourself to a freshly squeezed orange juice, a scrumptious crepe (options in sweet or savory), a latte, or a lox bagel. Simple joys, meaningful moments, and a truly lovely start to the day.

Joy is always in the journey—especially when we slow down enough to notice.

— Joy in the Journey

reviews.author: Meltem O
reviews.text: It was clean and spacious. The staff was friendly and professional. My friend got a Soma bagel, and he said it was delicious. I think the exterior sign needs to be redesigned.  The interior decor was modern and the layout was nice. Most importantly for me, the restroom was clean.

reviews.author: Laura Montoya
reviews.text: New favorite breakfast spot. The tomato mozzarella toast is hands-down one of the best I’ve ever had. Fresh, perfectly balanced, and so satisfying. Paired it with a matcha and it was the perfect start to the day. Highly recommend

reviews.author: Gary Graf
reviews.text:
Spent a week in SF and right across from our daughter’s apartment we tried Doppio on our last morning in town. We should have come here everyday!!!
We drink half caf coffee and this was hands down the best cup we have ever had from a coffee shop. Bold and tasty. Not watery and lacking flavor like most.
The menu is huge so we struggled with what to order. Got the parfait which has Greek yogurt, fruit and granola. Very good.
The avocado toast with an extra egg was delicious.
Place is super clean and reasonable.
Definitely a great place!!

restaurants.name: The Grove Restaurant & Bar
restaurants.address: 690 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: PhillyNEFL
reviews.text:
Excellent vibe. Was here during SB weekend so it was busy.
You place your order first and then find seating. Plenty of space and you have the option of inside or outside dining.
The servers find you by the number they give you when you placed your order.
Food was excellent. The Mac & cheese was especially good.
There are several servers walking around if you want to order another beverage or need anything. As soon as you are done there's someone cleaning the table and boom...next party is sitting down.
Oh they have a bar so I'm guessing you can just get a seat there if available.
Thia place gets the Mr. National seal of approval

reviews.author: snarkyguy
reviews.text:
The atmosphere is killer. Loved how it looks inside with the huge trees, lights, and multiple floors. It’s almost like one of those romantic movie film sets.

I got the breakfast burrito and my wife got the eggs Benedict. Do be aware that a lot of San Francisco restaurants add on a “mandate” charge for their employees health care that the customer has to pay for instead of the company. Beyond that, the food is reasonably priced for what you get.

Honestly I’m a bit torn. I loved the food, I loved the atmosphere, but it’s self-serve in that you can’t sit still a table until you order; and even then it’s basically cafeteria style with order and pay and then get a number. No service per say. But the food is great and the atmosphere is killer. So yeah. I’d definitely purely come back!

reviews.author: Alex Cruz
reviews.text:
Absolutely LOVED this place! Service & food was unbelievable. Truly a hidden gem. Can’t WAIT to go back here next time I’m in SF.

Amazing goat cheese/ strawb salad & the Club with the Cali avo was unmatched! Had a fabulous oat milk iced coffee & hubby had a tasty hazy IPA.

We will be back! ❤️😋

reviews.author: Elisa Obedoza
reviews.text: Visiting from out of town. Was kind of overwhelmed with the menu but asked the cash register worker for recommendations. So you order your food and drinks first then they give you a number and you find a seat. The workers will look for your number and deliver the food to you. We had the cobb salad, the chicken salad, fried chicken sandwich and roasted turkey BLT w add on avocado and the lemon mayo aioli. Everything was delicious!!  Shout out to Joseph at the the cash register w the coconut allergy (same food allergy as me) who helped us choose our food 😅🙈

reviews.author: L
reviews.text: Service was excellent, very welcoming, fast and hospitable. Loved the vibe, it gave very much cabin and it was perfect since we visited in fall. I ordered the hot pastrami sandwich and while it was good, it was pretty dry (my only complaint). My friends had the chicken pot pie and portabello Benedict which they said were delicious. I tried the hashbrowns which didn’t disappoint, they were light and crispy, greaseless.

restaurants.description: Popular spot for American home cooking in a mountain-lodge-like setting with communal seats.

restaurants.name: Zazie
restaurants.address: 941 Cole St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Tonya Del Gallo
reviews.text: Food was outstanding! Wonderful lite beet salad with avocado. Loved the hazelnuts on the salmon and risotto. Husband loved the baked chicken and noodles. Very clean and nice interior. Josh was an excellent server and the crème brûlée was the best I’ve had in years!

reviews.author: Darlo
reviews.text:
Overall, a decent experience.
Highlights: the Albert eggs benedict was delicious and the buttermilk pancake was very good (way better than the gingerbread and french toast in the Sarah Jane's flight.)

Low points: the hot chocolate was really watered down. Also, I really worry about the sanitary conditions of this place. The bathroom, the dishwashing, and the pass where the finished food sits before being served is all right next to each other and it's also the choke point where patrons walk through to get to the back area seating. The floor was also really slippery in this area.

reviews.author: Max Kay
reviews.text:
American cafe with good variety of brunch options.
Good fresh delicious food with veggie options. Not typical American portion.
Service is a bit slow but responsive. It's quiet but vibrant atmosphere. Good for couple, meetup and small gathering.
Hard to find parking but possible in around streets.
Indoor setting, limited outdoor setting but nice cozy patio in the end of cafe.
Not accessible for tight inside and steps to patio.

reviews.author: Susan Sargent
reviews.text: Lovely covered patio in the back. Excellent pate and very good pork chop with pears and coq au Vin. Will be back next time we're in SF.

reviews.author: Dionna Smith
reviews.text:
Zazie is hands down one of our Top 5 favorite restaurants in the San Francisco Bay Area, and every visit reminds me exactly why.

As someone who’s a big, big meat eater, I rarely go for a meatless option—but the lemon ricotta ravioli here is in a league of its own. Bright, creamy, and melt-in-your-mouth delicious, it’s one of the very few vegetarian dishes that leaves me totally satisfied without even thinking about meat. That says a lot.

Of course, when I do indulge my carnivorous side, the pork chop is absolutely divine—so tender it practically falls apart, and paired with buttery potatoes and the dreamiest mac and cheese. Rich, hearty, comforting. It’s the kind of dish you crave long after the meal is over.

The escargot deserves a special shoutout—it instantly transports me back to Paris. Delicately prepared, garlicky, and served with that unmistakable French flair. It’s the real deal.

And please, whatever you do, don’t skip dessert. The Chaud is an experience. Warm, gooey chocolate topped with toasted marshmallows—it’s like being wrapped in a hug that somehow takes you straight to Brussels. Yum, yum, YUM!

Zazie isn’t just a meal—it’s a mood, a memory, and a masterpiece every time. Whether you’re a die-hard meat lover or looking for something refined and soulful, this place delivers.

restaurants.description: Petite French bistro & popular brunch spot featuring wine, cocktails & patio seating.

restaurants.name: Spork Breakfast & Brunch
restaurants.address: 631 Broadway, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Aven Goodin
reviews.text: This restaurant in California was amazing! The food was delicious, the service was friendly, and the atmosphere was great. Everything exceeded my expectations. Definitely five stars- I’ll be coming back!

reviews.author: Kayla Authelet
reviews.text: 10/10 experience. Tom yum soup was amazing, tofu was perfectly cooked, the broth was insanely good, perfectly spicy. The spicy chicken sandwich melts in your mouth and has a perfect crunch. My partner got the 2 eggs with the sriracha bacon which was also very yummy. All the details/sides were also great.

reviews.author: Heather McCann
reviews.text: We loved their unique take on Western Breakfast/Asian fusion! I had the Chicken & Pandan Waffles – the chicken was super crispy, flavorful, and juicy, and the waffle was light & fluffy. Hubby loved his spicy Steak Eggs Benedict. Very attentive service, and a great clean & inviting atmosphere. Decent prices and good value. We would visit again & recommend to our friends.

reviews.author: Carey
reviews.text:
Nice brunch in Chinatown. I ordered a Shrimp Benedict, it was delicious. I also ordered some Thai foods like Pad See EW and curry, they were just ok.
Prices were a bit expensive, but it was ok with good and friendly service. Spacious, nice atmospheric. Easy to get a table on weekdays. Will come back again.

reviews.author: Elle Cee
reviews.text: A unique brunch spot.  I had the pandan waffle and fried chicken which was tasty. The waffle was cold and a wee bit soggy but good flavour.  Someone else had the bo luc lac and the beef was super duper tender and juicy but also very sweet. The shrimp Benedict was quite good and the chicken sammie was good but the star of that was the French fries they were spectacular.   The service was friendly and good. There's no atmosphere but still a place I'll go back to.

restaurants.name: Town's End Brunch
restaurants.address: 2 Townsend St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Pamela L
reviews.text:
I started coming to this restaurant when it first opened (around 1990) because of the muffin tops, menu selections, complimentary mini size bakery basket and food quality being consistently good. When the area developed (high rise apartments and Oracle Park) parking became insane.  The restaurant and area lost its charm,  so I stopped coming.  So when I happened to be in this neighborhood, I decided to try the restaurant and see what changes were made.  The only difference inside of the restaurant is that the bakery area is now replaced with a bar.  The small  pastry display looked pitiful. The pastries didn't look appealing - muffins were below the cup line topped with sugar sprinkles and the slices of cake look like it was just sitting there.  Other than that, the decor looked the same.

I was greeted by a friendly server and sat at a table with complete views of the bay.  Food was served temperature hot with a smile.  Server came frequent to see how I was doing and if I needed anything.
The Dubai chocolate pancake was absolutely delcious.  It looks awfully sweet but it wasn't.  The pancakes were fluffy with nice brown bottoms. Presentation was awesome. I definitely would order this again.

The chicken fried steak was good but needed more gravy.  The server bought me a bowl of gravy and once I had that, it was more delicious.  The gravy was homemade, creamy with bits of sausage.  The over easy eggs were cooked perfectly -  not too runny nor hard.  The hash browns were the best I had for a long time - it wasn't greasy and was crispy as I like it (I did ask for crispy hash browns) - not overcooked or dry. The muffin was too floury and dense for me. It was not what I remembered it to be - fluffy, soft insides with a semi crispy outside.  Overall, if you are in this neighborhood, give the restaurant a try.  The food is just as good as I remembered it 15 plus years ago but I would definitely would not recommend the bakery goods.

reviews.author: G B
reviews.text: We love coming to Town’s End Brunch. They have all the usual breakfast suspects, each one tasty! You can sit at a table and look out their many windows to see the trees swaying and the water on the bay rippling. Their servers are always helpful. Fresh green juice is on the menu, and it’s awesome. I usually get the Mr Breakfast but if I’m splurging I’ll go for the biscuits and gravy which they ace. It’s one of my fave brunch spots in SF.

reviews.author: Kurt Willmon
reviews.text:
Prices, food, service, portion size and location are okay with a couple of exceptions. Hashbrowns and over easy eggs were perfect. My chocolate milkshake tasted great but filled the metal cup only a little over 3/4 from the top. Most places have a larger metal cup that you pour into a regular milkshake cup with enough left over to pour another half to full cup more. The pancakes were larger portions than our server described and, although seemingly thick and fluffy, are rather dense. So, take that into account based on how hungry you are.
We both got ultra foul gas later in the day and yhis was our only meal up until they point. The only things we ate that were the same were the pancakes, syrup, butter and jelly; everything else was different. The gas had to be from one of those four items.
A lot of people say they bring a plate of rolls before they even ordered but that didn't materialize for us.
I'm not sure whether or not if go here again but, it is walking distance from the yacht club at South Beach Harbor. I'll pass on the pancakes though.

reviews.author: Nishtha Choudhary
reviews.text:
Went for a sunday brunch. I got the orange juice, which was definitely freshly squeezed and husband got the peruvian latte, which was also amazing.
I got the combo for breakfast with waffle. The waffle was way too salty for my taste but everything else was quite good. Husband got crab cake benedict, which he enjoyed a lot but didn't like the house potatoes as much coz they were very unevenly good. Overall a good experience with some flaws.

reviews.author: Robert Rivera
reviews.text: If you are near the ballpark before 2 PM stop in for a great breakfast or brunch.  I ordered the Huevos Rancheros and the dish just popped with flavor. The Salsa was excellent as was the Pico and the ham I substituted for the pork chorizo.  My wife had a scramble with hash browns, which I can testify were awesome as were the eggs.  It's been awhile since we had been there but it won't be long before we go back.  Excellent breakfast spot.

restaurants.description: Lively American daytime eatery serving coffee, mini muffins, organic egg dishes & more.

cafes.name: The Coffee Movement
cafes.address: 1737 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Dharma M
reviews.text:
Here’s a polished and natural rewrite of your review:

---

A nice coffee shop in the Richmond District. It’s usually busy, and the space isn’t very large, though there is some seating available. What they’re best known for, as far as I know, is the tasting flight — three featured coffees of the day served either as different pours or prepared in different ways. They also offer several types of coffee beans.

There are three workers behind the counter preparing drinks, and they work very efficiently. Orders come out quickly.

We ordered the tasting flight, a cocoa cappuccino, a latte, and some pastries. I really enjoyed the tasting flight — it’s a unique way to sample three different coffees or brewing styles. It was a great experience. We usually stop by whenever we’re in the area. The prices are reasonable and affordable. I also really like their espresso machine. ☕
Parking is on the street and could be challenging near the shop.

reviews.author: Stefano DellaVigna
reviews.text: Terrific third wave, stopped here on the way to cheering the SF half marathon. I love that they do the 3 way tasting, with a coffee prepared 3 ways, I had a very floral coffee. Pastries are good too.

reviews.author: F Francis
reviews.text: The chai latte is well-spiced and creamy—easily one of the better ones around. Espresso drinks are good too, made with quality beans, and the espresso tonic is nicely balanced with lime/orange twist. Friendly spot with decent pastries and outdoor seating. Worth checking out. Close to golden gate park and de young museum.

reviews.author: Jarrod W
reviews.text:
Really excellent coffee experience here, especially if you enjoy specialty coffee. Far and away my favorite spot in SF.

Would recommend giving the coffee flight a shot if you’re interested in a little variety. Baristas are super friendly and knowledgeable about the rotating list of coffees.

As far as location, this spot on Balboa was my favorite. Give it a shot!

reviews.author: Mike Ragusa
reviews.text:
Such a cool spot, this is the place to go if you're a true coffee aficionado, I got a tasting flight of their "Dak" Colombia coffee.
You get drip, espresso and a cortado.
Highly recommend checking it out

cafes.name: Delah Coffee
cafes.address: 370 4th St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Skeeb Khan
reviews.text:
Delah Coffee is such a gem in San Francisco, tucked away near 4th St and Clara St. I heard this was one of the first coffee shops to come to the SF area, and it definitely feels special the moment you walk in. The interior is absolutely beautiful with really nice furniture and a warm, inviting vibe.
The service was excellent—super friendly and attentive. I ordered the chai along with a borak (cheese pastry), and everything was delicious. The cheese pastry came with honey, which was the perfect touch.
They have a great menu with classics like meat pies, cheesecakes, milk cakes, and a nice variety of refreshers as well. Whether you’re stopping by for coffee, dessert, or a light bite, this place hits the spot. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews.author: Muhammad Atif
reviews.text:
Delay Coffee is such a cozy and refreshing spot! Their pistachio latte is absolutely delicious — creamy, perfectly sweet, and full of real pistachio flavor. The tea is smooth and comforting, and they offer an amazing variety of snacks and desserts that taste as good as they look. Everything feels fresh and handmade.

It’s the perfect place to relax, grab a warm drink, and enjoy some treats. Highly recommended if you want something yummy and comforting!

reviews.author: Caroline T
reviews.text: I tried this cinnamon latte with dates, it was amazing. Staff are friendly too. Definitely will come back to try other coffee next trip!

reviews.author: Taniya
reviews.text:
It's always bustling with people and for good reason. This cozy, vibrant café has earned a loyal following thanks to its exceptional drinks and authentic Middle Eastern treats. It’s my go-to spot, especially for their 🌟pistachio latte ⭐️, which is rich, creamy and perfectly balanced with just the right amount of nutty flavor without being overly sweet . Trust me, one sip and you'll understand the hype.
They also have a variety of traditional Turkish desserts and savory pies that are delicious. And if you're a tea lover, don’t miss their Adeni chai - it's fragrant, spiced, and deeply satisfying. I recommend ordering it without sugar so you can sweeten it to your taste.

reviews.author: Dom D
reviews.text: I’ve had Delah on my to-visit list for a while now, and I regret that I didn’t make it in sooner! Great atmosphere, amazing coffee, and helpful, friendly staff. The Turkish delight was incredible, as well. Hot tip: Have them put a little cardamom in the Turkish coffee. Sooo good!

cafes.name: HI NRG
cafes.address: 443 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Hannah Arcilla
reviews.text: Amazing coffee! Came here after a bike ride. Both the owners are super nice, it’s rare you see a barista-owned shop. You can tell they put their all into this shop and their coffee! We got espresso + milk and a cafe de olla with oat milk, both were super smooth. We also tried their olive oil cake and cheddar biscuits which were 🔑 after our ride 🚲 really cool atmosphere, they sell records and it’s a chill, darker modern vibe. There’s seating mostly for individual / 2 people but there’s a couple 3-4 group tables. Will be back!

reviews.author: Gemma
reviews.text:
I love this place. The energy (sry — NRG) was so good. Warm vibes, friendly service, and divine coffee.

I got a Cortado which had the perfect milk to espresso ratio (a surprising rarity) and was thrilled by all the space to post up, read, write, chat, strum away on one’s laptop — whatever you’re feeling!

The wide open windows make it bright and light-filled too. This place is a gem :)

reviews.author: Savannah Sprague
reviews.text: Great to have specialty coffee in this area. Love the menu. It’s simple. The owners are baristas which I love as a fellow barista. They are knowledgeable and exciting about their drinks. Excellent hospitality. We got an oat cappuccino and a recommended washed Ethiopian coffee that was excellent!! And the cheddar and chive scone was delectable! The space is very nice. Wish only good things for these folks. Will be back.

reviews.author: Sia A.
reviews.text: Super cute spot, no doubt about that. Unfortunately, my coffee was cold and the taste wasn’t great, it came off a little sour. Maybe the decaf coffee is like that. I tried another regular latte, too which wasn’t much better. I really wanted to leave a better review, and I’m hoping the coffee quality improves because the place itself has a lot of charm.

reviews.author: Alexis
reviews.text: Gorgeous intimate coffee shop complete with spots of sun rays, records spinning and coffee snobbery at its finest with no elitism, only the sweetest passion for the craft and friendly faces. Really relaxed environment with helpful guidance on what to order. I got the cortado and a strawberry ale both it the spot. Music was wonderful and appreciated the obligatory high energy track.

cafes.name: Ocean Beach Cafe
cafes.address: 734 La Playa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Ray Torres
reviews.text: Went in for a macha and it was great! Wish they had different size cups. It was cold outside and we were able to get a small table. Love that they had a wall of non alcoholic wines! Pretty cool! We did park a few blocks away but if you are at the beach it’s a quick walk to get a warm drink.

reviews.author: Nathalie Flores-Gonzalez
reviews.text:
Just a minute from the beach, Ocean Beach Cafe feels exactly how a neighborhood spot should feel: welcoming & alive with community energy.

I tried the cortado, made with milk delivered fresh that morning & an iced golden chai that was perfectly balanced & refreshing after a walk by the water. From their thoughtfully curated non-alcoholic bottle shop, I picked up a premium alcohol-free wine from Vienna, Austria, part of a selection that clearly reflects intention & care behind what they stock.

Breakfast did not disappoint. A warm everything bagel with a “secret sauce” I am still thinking about, generous salsa options & homemade pastries that made it hard to choose just one. The staff is young, friendly & fast paced in the best way, attentive without ever making you feel rushed. I noticed pets happily set up with their own treats & water bowls, which adds to the relaxed coastal atmosphere.

What truly makes the place special, though, is the mix of people coming through the door. All ages. Surfers fresh from the beach, families, remote workers, neighbors picking up salads & sandwiches. It feels lived in & real.

And then there is Josh, the owner. When he walks in, the energy subtly shifts. His passion for non-alcoholic beverages, shaped by years behind the bar & now five years building this concept, gives the space a quiet magnetism. Conversations naturally unfold about well-being, mindful drinking & community. You can easily spend hours there, sipping slowly & talking about life.

I also loved the Hawaiian furniture handcrafted by his father, which gives the space a deeply personal & home-like feel.

What elevates Ocean Beach Cafe even further is how it connects daily rituals with something bigger. Every Saturday, the team organizes a local beach cleanup, inviting neighbors & customers to begin their morning with purpose. By removing plastic & marine debris from the shoreline, they contribute directly to Life Below Water (SDG 14) & the protection of coastal ecosystems. These cleanups strengthen community ownership & environmental responsibility. By turning care for public space into a shared ritual, the café contributes to Sustainable Cities & Communities (SDG 11), showing how resilient cities are built through small, consistent collective actions.

reviews.author: Haley Oscar
reviews.text: Our favorite way to start our day when visiting San Francisco. The food is great and the staff is very friendly. Even though we are all the way in Wisconsin, we talk about this little cafe  often.

reviews.author: Jonathan Monte
reviews.text:
TL;DR
Where presentation is of higher quality than the Food
FULL REVIEW
This place had such a high rating that it was kind of surprising that it was so mediocre. San Francisco is not a food desert, but maybe this part of town is.

We got both the regular and the lavender lemonade. The regular lemonade tastes straight out of a can of Country Time. The lavender lemonade definitely tasted like lavender, so if you like that kind of thing, it's excellent.

The avocado toast lives on presentation. I thought I was about to experience Nirvana, but instead was greeted by hollow taste. The bread was nice and the avocado was fresh, but it was boring.

The turkey sandwich looked pretty normal. The bread was fine, and the red sauce they put on it gave it some flavor. I added avocado, and had I not, this sandwich would have only been slightly better than the avocado toast.

The only Epic part of the Epic Breakfast Sandwich was the Everything bagel, which was the highlight of my dining experience, but the eggs were boring as everything else was.
