Nothing was bad, but nothing was exciting either. This perfectly sums up this place. At best, the presentation was really good, but that's all that it had going for it. Essentially, the presentation is of higher quality than the food.

reviews.author: Bobby Miller
reviews.text:
I've been on a non-alcoholic journey as I am three years sober, and discovering new NA drinks has been a crucial part of that journey. My wife and I finally got out to visit this spot, as the only true non-alc bar in the whole Bay Area. Well worth the trip.

Joshua was super pleasant and guided us through an awesome flight of NA beers and a few mocktail samples (try the Golden Spirit, it rules).  We shared our stories of sobriety with each other and a few of the bar patrons who popped in for a drink.

Spaces like this are so critical for both people like me who have stopped drinking, and to drinkers who are starting to learn about the healthier ways to have a social drink. My wife and I went home with a box full of bottles and cans, and we cannot wait to go back.

(also, dinner at the Laundromat was super clutch, thank you for the recommendation Joshua!)

cafes.name: Doppio Coffee & Brunch
cafes.address: 1551 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Subhan Ansari
reviews.text: I visited Doppio Coffee & Brunch today and had a great experience. They offer delicious vegetarian breakfast options, and the coffee was excellent — they use Lavazza beans, which made the coffee really smooth and flavorful. The café was very clean, calm, and relaxing, with beautiful Turkish music playing in the background. It’s a perfect place for chatting and spending time with friends. The staff were also very friendly and welcoming.

reviews.author: Shanna A
reviews.text:
I was on my way to an appointment near Gough and felt prompted to pause and find a quiet breakfast spot—and this place turned out to be such a blessing. From the moment I walked in, there was a calm, welcoming atmosphere, genuine hospitality, and care in every detail. The food was fresh, beautifully plated, and prepared with intention—you could feel it.

I’ve learned that God often speaks in the pauses, in the moments we don’t plan but are led into. This stop was one of those reminders. If you visit, take a moment to say hello to Firat and the crew, and treat yourself to a freshly squeezed orange juice, a scrumptious crepe (options in sweet or savory), a latte, or a lox bagel. Simple joys, meaningful moments, and a truly lovely start to the day.

Joy is always in the journey—especially when we slow down enough to notice.

— Joy in the Journey

reviews.author: Meltem O
reviews.text: It was clean and spacious. The staff was friendly and professional. My friend got a Soma bagel, and he said it was delicious. I think the exterior sign needs to be redesigned.  The interior decor was modern and the layout was nice. Most importantly for me, the restroom was clean.

reviews.author: Laura Montoya
reviews.text: New favorite breakfast spot. The tomato mozzarella toast is hands-down one of the best I’ve ever had. Fresh, perfectly balanced, and so satisfying. Paired it with a matcha and it was the perfect start to the day. Highly recommend

reviews.author: Gary Graf
reviews.text:
Spent a week in SF and right across from our daughter’s apartment we tried Doppio on our last morning in town. We should have come here everyday!!!
We drink half caf coffee and this was hands down the best cup we have ever had from a coffee shop. Bold and tasty. Not watery and lacking flavor like most.
The menu is huge so we struggled with what to order. Got the parfait which has Greek yogurt, fruit and granola. Very good.
The avocado toast with an extra egg was delicious.
Place is super clean and reasonable.
Definitely a great place!!

cafes.name: Goodhart Coffee Catering of San Francisco Bay Area
cafes.address: 129 Kissling St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

photos.attribution: Goodhart Coffee Catering of San Francisco Bay Area
Goodhart Coffee Catering of San Francisco Bay Area

reviews.author: Shirley Duong
reviews.text: We had a great experience with Haley and Goodhart Coffee. We booked a three-hour coffee cart, and everything ran smoothly from setup to service. We have a large staff, and many people commented on the high quality of the drinks. The cart’s aesthetic design was also a big hit. Haley and the Goodhart Coffee team were very responsive and easy to work with throughout the booking process. I highly recommend Goodhart Coffee for a coffee cart or any of their other services.

reviews.author: Kelsey Fey
reviews.text: We loved our barista Aster! The drinks were just as delicious as they were adorable. Our guests had such a positive response to having the cart at our event. We also loved being able to customize the designs to coordinate with our event. We are planning our next event around hiring another Goodhart Coffee cart!

reviews.author: Katella Wright
reviews.text:
Everyone I interacted with from the Good Hart team was amazing. Baristas Jack and Aster were both fantastic—engaging with our group, stayed upbeat all day, and provided excellent service. I only heard great feedback about the coffee and drinks.

We reached out to many coffee carts in the area, but Goodhart's pricing and menu options were by far the best. I also really appreciated the clear communication prior to the event. And a huge thanks to Yasmine, for coordinating the coffee cart and baristas. Yasmines communication throughout the process made everything run smoothly! Thanks Goodhart!

reviews.author: Denisse Anzoategui
reviews.text:
We booked Goodhart Coffee for a work event with two locations on the same day, and they absolutely nailed it. Emma was at one location and was fantastic, high energy, fast, and so fun to have there. The drinks were excellent. I treated myself to an iced vanilla latte and a hot mocha and both were a total treat.

At the second location, Anthony was just as great. The setup was a little tricky, but he handled it effortlessly. He was personable, attentive, and quick, and everything ran smoothly. Both crews loved it and Goodhart ended up being a highlight of the day. I’m already looking forward to booking them again.

reviews.author: Nickie Nguyen
reviews.text:
Goodhart Coffee was a last-minute addition to our wedding, and we are so glad that we went through with it. They were an unexpected hit at the wedding, and we’re convinced that nearly all of our guests enjoyed some coffee. They were absolutely perfect, and we would recommend them for any event or large gathering or wedding.

First, and most importantly, the coffee was simply sublime. Absolutely amazing drinks all around. Each aspect of Goodhart Coffee’s service was thought through and professionally executed. The lines moved along extremely quickly. Each server was polite and amiable. They had a wide variety of drinks for every taste. Our drinks were the perfect size, tasted amazing, and even had beautiful latte art on the top. Their coffee sustained all of our guests and ensured that the dance floor was packed after dinner. We would highly recommend Goodhart Coffee!

cafes.name: Diamond Coffee N Pastry
cafes.address: 6909 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Arthur T
reviews.text:
Found Diamond Coffee off of yelp!

Was in the area and needed a place to work.

I ordered:
Americano $5.25
16 oz Cold ($1.00)
Savory Croissant
$6.25 Spinach & Feta

Coffee was fine. Was a bit weird to have a $1 upcharge for a cold drink.

I asked staff for a recommendation on pastry since their spread looked good. The spinach and feta croissant was ok, definitely needed more flavor.

Some seating inside. Nice fun, cozy vibe inside.

Street parking only.

reviews.author: PJ Pasricha
reviews.text:
Diamond Coffee N Pastry is a hidden gem! The cold brew was fantastic—smooth and refreshing, just the pick-me-up I needed. The staff was super friendly, adding to the welcoming vibe of the place.

There’s plenty of seating, which makes it a great spot to relax or catch up with friends. The options were a real highlight—creative and delicious, with so many amazing options to choose from.

The only downside was the parking, which was a bit tricky to find. But once you’re inside enjoying the coffee and pastries, it’s totally worth the effort. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the area!

reviews.author: Julia
reviews.text:
Cafe with great vibes and food! I got the strawberry croffle and it was delicious, creamy without being too sweet!

The interior is also super cute!

Great place to make a pit stop / grab a bite after the Legion of Honor and wait for the 38 bus!

reviews.author: Butterfly Blob
reviews.text: Very cute inside and outlets for every table!! Unfortunately, there didn't seem to be enough seats for the amount of patrons that came by on a Saturday afternoon. I got purple which was just ube and plain milk, nothing fancy. However, the fruity croffle was a nice experience and the blueberries were perfect and not too ripe. A great place to study if you can get a seat.

reviews.author: Olga Liakhovich
reviews.text: Very cute place. Coffee is amazing. Very nice selection of pastries, very fresh. The chocolate croissant is melting in the mouth. Looking forward to tasting spinach-feta pastry next time 😍

cafes.name: Q Specialty Coffee
cafes.address: 3490 California St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Lynn Kim
reviews.text: Came in on a Monday afternoon and most of the seats were taken. I did manage to grab a seat and enjoyed my velvet matcha foam. The space is bright and open making it perfect to read for a bit. They are dropping a new menu tomorrow (3/3/26) and so offered some free drinks! I got a hojicha latte with strawberry foam and it’s so yummy! FYI, both of the drinks are sweetened.

reviews.author: Mehdi Aziz
reviews.text:
Q Specialty Coffee is on another level. The coffee is consistently incredible.. rich, smooth, and clearly made by people who actually care about quality. It’s always busy for a reason, but what really impressed me is how fast the line moves. Even when it’s packed, you’re never waiting long, and the staff keeps everything flowing effortlessly.

The matcha deserves its own shoutout — perfectly balanced, creamy, and not overly sweet. You can tell they use high-quality matcha and actually know how to prepare it properly.

Great vibes, great people, and top-tier drinks. Easily one of the most popular spots around, and it 100% lives up to the hype. I’ll be back again and again.

reviews.author: Nicholas Pezarro
reviews.text:
Fantastic matcha latte (too much ice for my taste but that's a problem I have generally).

Great staff. Beautiful space. Has Wi-Fi. Outlets if you need powet. Ample seating, 👌

reviews.author: Magda G
reviews.text:
The shop is on a corner with lots of natural light—beautiful space. The woman who helped me was very friendly and went over a few drinks with me.

The hot specialty coffees were not available as the machine was out of service so I had an Americano with oat milk, which was delicious.

The place was quite full, and there were no seats available, so I had to go outside to enjoy my coffee.

reviews.author: Sarah Bhimani
reviews.text: Best matcha in San Fran! Recently & newly opened. The presentation is amazing and really captures the attention. They have a matcha whip that is citrus in flavor. They also have a selection of coffee from different places. Definitely a must visit.

cafes.name: CoffeeShop | Bakery + Micro-Roastery
cafes.address: 900 22nd St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: David Huey
reviews.text:
Stopped by CoffeeShop for the first time and it was an easy win. The space has that relaxed, neighborhood feel where you immediately feel comfortable, even on a first visit. Staff were friendly, welcoming, and helpful without rushing anything.

I tried the cappuccino and the matcha, and both were genuinely well done. The cappuccino had a smooth, balanced flavor with just the right amount of foam, and the matcha was whisked properly — no clumps, no bitterness, just a clean, steady flavor. You can tell they take their drinks seriously.

For a first‑time visit, everything hit the mark: good drinks, good vibe, and a team that makes you feel like you’ll want to come back.

reviews.author: bartika dutta
reviews.text: A charming coffee shop with clean Scandinavian decor - trim wooden chairs and a digital mural give it a modern vibe. There’s some outdoor seating too, perfect for sunny days. Their Thai breakfast was healthy and fresh, with the subtle brew rounding out the flavors beautifully. The nutty cookie was crumbly and delicious. The server was all smiles and thoughtfully brought our food to the table. A great spot for a post-work pause in the sun.

reviews.author: Anastasiya Sakharava
reviews.text:
CoffeeShop will always have a special place in my heart! Since moving to San Francisco, my boyfriend and I have been coming to this location at least four times a week. It’s such a beautiful place! The baked goods are unbelievable—especially the charcoal salmon croissant (it feels like Christmas whenever I see them being sold). The coffee is absolutely delicious, and once you learn how much care and craftsmanship go into every drink and pastry, you’ll appreciate it even more❕

I’ve been to all three locations, but this one feels so special. Dogpatch is a wonderful area, perfect for a walk. The owners, Olga and Wilson, are such amazing people! I could go on and on, but long story short: try this place, and you’ll always want to come back!

reviews.author: Grachik
reviews.text:
Stopped by Coffee Shop today and just had to share. The turkey croissant and cappuccino were honestly perfect.

Super chill spot, friendly service, and overall a great little place to relax and grab something good. Definitely coming back.

reviews.author: Hüseyin Altan
reviews.text: This is my favorite place. I go there regularly to relax and enjoy a delicious coffee made by Jessie. All the pastries Olga makes are very good, but I have two favorites.First one charcoal croissant and then other one almond croissant.

cafes.name: Bazaar Café
cafes.address: 5927 California St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Helen
reviews.text:
Excellent spot for brunch! Their lattes are absolutely amazing! I usually avoid caramel of any type but in the latte it was beautifully balanced and a nice addition.

The curry was served hot and yummy. We wanted to try a few things and the employee was nice enough to let us know that we can do a side soup so that we lessen the amount of side salads that would come with each menu item.

Sat in the backyard patio and it was lovely, there was shade and a garden out back and it was an enjoyable start to the day.

reviews.author: Anja Marie Sandholm
reviews.text: Always a treat to visit! Truly yummy espresso drinks, surprisingly good quality! Great staff. Love the pottery they serve their drinks in. Yummy biscuits too. Lovely patio out back. The cutest neighborhood vibe.

reviews.author: M Carter
reviews.text: Came here to listen to this afternoon's live music . The garden in the back is a perfect place for Sunday afternoon jazz.  Inside the cafe is great for a reading session, working or a quiet conversation. It is cozy and there are nooks in the space to do your thing.

reviews.author: Sharon W
reviews.text:
Dunno what's up with that 1 star review from a few weeks ago, but I'm here to rebut it.

This is a most lovely cafe with great vibes, comforting drinks, and delicious food (I much prefer their biscuits over the ones we had growing up in Alabama).

The staff is awesome. Everything tastes better when you're here.

Cheers!

reviews.author: Trina M
reviews.text: Loved the calm vibe, live music felt intimate and warm. Their honey lavender biscuit and the biscuit were delicious. Enjoyed the tea lattes!

cafes.description: Chill cafe with back patio offering eclectic mix of food (sandwiches to popcorn) & live music.

cafes.name: Home Coffee Roasters
cafes.address: 2018 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Lydia Tachkov
reviews.text:
Home Coffee Roasters is one of my favorite coffee shops in the city! As someone who likes more sophisticated sweet drinks, you get some really thought out and unique coffees you can get here.

I would highly recommend the rose latte, they use real rose petals to top off the drink and it's one of my favorite treats! The staff is very friendly, and theres a good amount of tables you can sit to enjoy your drink at.

They do have wifi so I have been able to come here to work on some projects while enjoying their coffee. I also recommend taking your drink down the street to Clement Nursery for a lovely coffee and plant time.

reviews.author: Charlie Noble
reviews.text:
This is by far the best coffee shop in the entire city and I am a coffee snob. Fantastic lattes and niche espresso drinks you can’t find anywhere else. The best avocado toast and baristas that are true artists. I was having a rough day after dropping my kitty Kay off for a surgery and the barista somehow knew exactly how to cheer me up and made a beautiful and adorable kitty cat design with foam on my latte that made my day.

Ps I love buying the large bag of fresh roasted beans so I can enjoy home coffee at home. They even ground it to espresso grind for me to make in my bialletti.

reviews.author: Sonia Garcia
reviews.text: both my friend and i got the birthday cake latte. i didn’t love mine (ordered as less sweet w/ almond milk) but my friends was much better (regular sweetness with whole milk). baristas were incredibly friendly.

reviews.author: Vineel Shah
reviews.text: While all in-house coffee, service and the food are great, the Ethiopian beans have been a little subpar over the last couple of months - I pick up beans every other week as it used to be one of the best light and fruit forward roasts and noticed that the flavor has become bland and prices have shot up. Hopefully they bring the original beans/flavor back

reviews.author: All
reviews.text: Delicious!! Came here because they had more creative flavors, tried the birthday cake & lavander lattes, both were amazing :)

cafes.description: Classic & unique latte drinks, plus creative toast options, served in a contemporary coffee shop.

cafes.name: Flywheel Coffee Roasters
cafes.address: 672 Stanyan St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: J V
reviews.text: Excellent mocha and latte's. If you care about quality coffee and lattes with perfect foam then this is your place. You can't go wrong with the great chill atmosphere, laid back vibe and music with a patio in the back. They do have a small selection of gluten free donuts and other bread goods to eat with your coffee. Come check this place out!

reviews.author: Sasha Diodorova
reviews.text: Loved the coffee and the vibe here, but guys… the restroom completely ruined the experience. It’s so gross. It feels really disrespectful to customers to maintain it in such poor condition. It’s such a shame, because the shop itself is great.

reviews.author: Kueiman Lin
reviews.text: I especially like their coffee because I prefer wine taste coffee rather than chocolate one. If you use you cup, you can get around $0.5 discount, and they provide 2-hr WiFi for free.

reviews.author: Foodie At Heart
reviews.text: Flywheel Roasters on Stanyan is a true coffee paradise in SF. The moment you enter, an intriguing and distinct aroma of freshly roasted beans envelops you. Their commitment to same-day roasting is evident in every exceptional cup. The coffee is simply awesome – vibrant, nuanced, and bursting with flavor. You can taste the dedication in each sip. The passionate staff and welcoming atmosphere further enhance the experience. For any coffee lover, Flywheel on Stanyan is an absolute must-visit. Prepare to be enchanted by the quality and freshness!

reviews.author: Rachel Delaney
reviews.text: Excellent coffee and latte art! I couldn’t believe it when I saw the swan come out. Great service too - they cleaned up a table for us right away when they realized the previous customers had left everything behind.

cafes.description: Family-owned coffee roaster offering exotic brews in a stripped-down, industrial setting.

cafes.name: Better Half Coffee
cafes.address: 1448 Pacific Ave, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Ronie Vega
reviews.text: I heard great things about this coffee shop, and I'm happy to share that it lives up to all the praise. This should be a must try when visiting San Francisco. Their signature drink made with the Isfahan syrup is absolutely delicious, not too sweet which allows for the espresso to shine even in latte form. The syrup has notes of rose, raspberry, and vanilla that compliments anything it touches. I had the pleasure of trying it as a spritz where the floral notes were front and center. Not to mention, the space is cozy and inviting. Joshua is very knowledgeable was kind enough to share the Better Half Coffee's story. I will half to return during their later hours when the shop transforms into a bar and bites establishment!

reviews.author: Hawaii Food photos
reviews.text:
Great Coffee, Amazing Food and Outstanding Service!

This was our first coffee stop in SF during this trip and we are so happy to land in such great spot to get coffee and start our café and cocktail bar quest.  The atmosphere is cozy and staff is friendly and very hospitable. Highly recommend it if you are in the area.

reviews.author: Dharma M
reviews.text:
Love this place . It is popup store inside a wine bar. It is coffee shop in the morning and wine bar in the evening. They just opened about 4 months.  I am not sure if this is their permanent place.  We were looking for coffee shop near North Beach and found it.  The interior is very nice with small section where they take order and prepare the coffee.  There are some pastries to choose from and they also have something from kitchen.
The owner is very friendly and based on our conversation, he has been in coffee world for quite sometimes.
We order matcha, cortado and latte. They have milk options if you are lactose intolerance.  We also order Confit Avocado.
The order was ready quickly which surprise me.  The coffee and matcha were tasty.
The highlight is the Confit Avocado. It is so yummy and different than regular avocado toast which you get from many coffee shop. They infuse the avocado with oil. I never had this before.  it is at next level.
The price is reasonable and good this is it is pet friendly place.

reviews.author: Amira Athanasios
reviews.text: The breakfast melt sandwich is absolutely excellent. Morning pastries are delic. And ofc the cappuccino is perfect. Lovely interior for catching up friends or laptop work. Has WiFi avail. The staff and owner are amazing

reviews.author: Norika
reviews.text:
We ordered an iced latte with Ispahan syrup, an iced matcha latte, two donuts (raspberry and custard), and a Thai butter cake. The Ispahan syrup adds a unique flavor to the latte, which I really enjoyed.

The donuts taste homemade and are not overly sweet—very different from typical American donuts, and I love that. The texture of the Thai butter cake reminds me a bit of cornbread: light yet satisfying.

On top of that, the café is dog-friendly, which makes it an even more enjoyable place to visit.

cafes.name: Sightglass Coffee
cafes.address: 270 7th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Timothy Colvin (Tim)
reviews.text: Trendy coffee shop with the atmosphere of your "local dive". Bathed in natural light and with ample space to spread out and work, this industrial loft has nailed multi-purpose. Try all of their flagship roasts to keep it exciting, but I recommend Blueboon as a staple. Also love their croissants. Pictured is the Pistachio Berry croissant. It's perfection.

reviews.author: Hadleigh Painter
reviews.text: Super cool spot! I am so glad that I finally got to check it out. I definitely want to go back to enjoy the upstairs space and get some work done. Their breakfast burrito was a little small but I love how it is crispy/ crunchy (and would order it again). I want to try more of the menu

reviews.author: Aatif Junaid
reviews.text:
I stopped by Side Class Coffee today after hearing a lot about it. The coffee itself is solid and worth trying, but everything else felt pretty average.

The food was underwhelming, and the ambience didn’t really stand out. It isn’t bad, just not warm or inviting. They do have a setup for packing coffee on the side, which is nice, but the overall vibe feels just okay.

If you’re looking to try a new coffee spot, it’s worth visiting for the coffee, but I wouldn’t go for the food or the ambience.

reviews.author: Therock Ton
reviews.text:
Got a good drip coffee and cold kwa-son (croissant)

Remember to check if they heated up ur pastry. They might forget, and can’t heat it up for you once it’s across the counter. Or maybe they enjoy a cold croissant I don’t know.

reviews.author: Cai
reviews.text: A really nice and spacious cafe in SoMa! The interior design is really pretty with a cool bike rack right in front. They also do their own roasting in house. The coffee flavor is solid and I’ll come back next time to try their specialty flavors like the brown butter miso. No wifi though :(

cafes.description: Indie outfit serving worldly coffees in a modern setting with an espresso bar & an on-site roaster.

cafes.name: Royal Ground Coffee
cafes.address: 2342 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Mike Picot
reviews.text: I can always come here to get some studying done or get a quick bite. The ambience is homely and a bit cramped but that adds to the cool factor there. Real good coffee and cider. Service is top tier

reviews.author: Vance Whipple
reviews.text: While the staff/owners are really nice, the service, if you are not a regular, is really bad. I've gone 3 times now (this will be my last) and each time, after I ordered my drinks...the staff/owners would move any regular customers drink ahead of making mine (I'm not a regular).  On each occasion, the staff helped regular customers (some with the same drink as me) before making mine. Today it was 6 regulars before coming back to my drink. While this place may be great for regulars, if you are not one, I highly recommend trying some of their competitors in the same location.

reviews.author: Roman Yamal
reviews.text: Lovely coffee shop. The workers were really helpful, kind and friendly. The other review mentioned they have good croissants, I had the almond croissant and it was heavenly! They were really creamy and had many layers. Their aroma green tea is also amazing! I will definitely come back if I am near here next time!

reviews.author: Casandra Huylebroeck
reviews.text: I love going to the Royal Ground with my Dad when I come to visit him. The food and coffee are great! The owners are wonderful people and they always treat my father very well 🥰

reviews.author: Darcie D
reviews.text: I only had time to stop here once and I got a Matcha with oat milk that was delicious. The atmosphere was very cozy and fun. The two women that worked there were very sweet. They seem to have a lot of regulars that came in that they got to chat up with, which was nice to see. The menu looked fairly decent.

cafes.description: Part of a local mini-chain serving signature blends plus beer, sandwiches & pastries in casual digs.

cafes.name: Simple Pleasures Cafe
cafes.address: 3434 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Lara Getselevich
reviews.text: I visited the most beautiful cafe today! It has such an amazing vibe of simplicity and grace. The drinks are incredibly tasty—I treated myself to a cappuccino and a cherry croissant, and everything was perfectly fresh. Loved it! ❤️

reviews.author: Sagar
reviews.text: I rarely give a place 5 stars, but this place deserves it because I liked everything here. The way the staff treated me, took my order, answered my questions and made my coffee. The taste of my coffee, the live music there, the amazing yummy Egyptian shortbread I asked them to warm up for me and some apricot pie I took home....I liked everything here. Asked them to blend 2 sugar in my cappuccino and they did (other places refuse to do it). Didn't have to wait here. The staff who took my order was awesome (didn't ask her name but I should have to write it here). An awesome coffee place. Love the name too. I couldn't find anything here less than 5 stars except parking....well that doesn't have anything to do with this place (it's SF). I'm saving this place in my list to come back ☕️

reviews.author: Praveen Swadi
reviews.text:
This is a hidden gem that locals frequent. It is slightly out of the way for tourists, as it is in core residential area. But highly recommended. Small, cozy place. But even house coffee is awesome. In addition we took iced latte and mocha. Both were really good. Mocha was tad bit sweet, but not too much. We also had apricot pastry - which was huge and was sufficient for 3 of us. Tasty too.

The place is small and in an old bar setting but you should find place to sit. There are benches outside too, so if it is not too cold, you can enjoy the weather.

reviews.author: Katia Martha
reviews.text:
Rating 5 stars mainly for ambiance and because I’d go back and try other things. Really charming, cozy place with all ages gathered- seems like an established community hub. There’s people working on laptops and just hanging out. Unfortunately the thing I ordered was not satisfying but I’ll try something else next time. I ordered a chai latte and it tasted nothing like any chai I’ve had. I had my two friends take sips of my drink too to verify and they said it tasted like ginger tea. I wonder if my drink got mixed up somehow or if they ran out of chai? If they ran out, I’d rather have been told so I could’ve gotten something else. My friends enjoyed their coffees and bagels. Friendly people and great atmosphere make it worth it for me to come back, try something new, and recommend to others.
Oh also! The writing on the cups was beautiful :)

reviews.author: Frances Allan
reviews.text: Don’t walk by this understated exterior. Buzzing, popular coffee shop with locals working and socialising over great coffee and matcha. Genuine and unsweetened matcha. Lots of baked goods and burritos.

cafes.description: Local mainstay for espresso, house-roasted coffee,  bagels & baked goods in a lived-in cafe setting.

cafes.name: Third Wheel Coffee
cafes.address: 535 Scott St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Ronie Vega
reviews.text: I love pine. Pine trees, pine needles, pine cones. So as a pine lover, I was very curious to try Third Wheel's vanilla pine latte. It's safe to say that pine is also delicious. It pains me that I do not live in this city, because if I did, Third Wheel Coffee would be my first stop every morning. The pine bud infused syrup makes the latte very aromatic and adds an herbal note that pairs beautifully with the high quality espresso. It's unique. It's perfect. I will be thinking about it everyday until I return. Don't skip their hot chocolate either! They melt down actual chocolate wafers with your milk of choice, and it's a perfect addition to a rainy SF morning.

reviews.author: John de Souza
reviews.text: Wonderful coffee. Hidden but definitely worth the find. Warm and quick service. Coffee was the right temperature, smooth consistency, and beautiful flavor.

reviews.author: Ana G
reviews.text: New favorite coffee spot! The vanilla pine latte was divine, excited to try more seasonal drinks. Also, the baristas are super kind :-)

reviews.author: Shanelle S
reviews.text: Coffee bar that shares the same space as a sandwich shop. Stopped by here for brunch with my boyfriend and it was a delicious spot. They had matcha and coffee options (in addition to affogato). Both our lattes were incredibly smooth and tasty. We grabbed a chocolate mochi muffin as well as a ham and cheese croissant. There wasn’t any seating so we went to the park nearby to eat on the grass. You pay first before receiving your order on the right hand side of the counter. Highly recommend this spot for grabbing takeout caffeine in the morning.

reviews.author: Koen Thiessen
reviews.text: For our honeymoon, my wife and I road tripped down to California. On the way, we stopped at many coffee shops, in search of the perfect espresso. And here, at third wheel coffee, we found exactly that. I ordered a flat white, and thoroughly enjoyed it. My wife's vanilla latte was also amazing, and so was the pastry that we had. (We also ordered a matcha for the road which was quite good as well.) The shop is located in a cool urban San Francisco location. The barista was kind and friendly, as well as very knowledgeable. You can tell coffee is his passion! Would definitely recommend going out of your way for if you're in the San Francisco area!

cafes.name: The Coffee Berry SF
cafes.address: 1410 Lombard St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Hannah Arcilla
reviews.text:
Cute, clean, and bright coffee shop with owners who really care. I went today and both owners were working. They have a large pastry selection, with traditional “fika” Swedish buns from Thursday to Sundays. They’re phenomenal, I’ve only tried the cinnamon fika bun but I’ll be trying the others as well. I believe they use Sightglass coffee, both the flat white and dirty chai I got tasted great. The chai wasn’t too sweet.

This spot is also great because lots of dogs are regulars here! So you always get fluffy friends inside. It also seems like there’s tons of regulars, as I often hear the owners saying “So, the usual ___ (insert name of customer)?” I may become one of those regulars even though I live in Outer Richmond, I like this place so much!

Added bonus: really clean bathrooms, lots of windows for bright environment, kind, prompt service, owners who genuinely care about their establishment. Only con (although not a terrible one) very limited seating! Maybe only for 8-10 people. But they do well considering the size. All in all, 5 stars and possibly my new favorite coffee spot at the moment!

reviews.author: Caitlin Conley
reviews.text: I love this spot! It’s a cozy little space, but perfect for grabbing a coffee and pastry on the go. The folks working here are always so chipper and friendly; the pastry selection is phenomenal. The ube croissant is unique and delicious. Their cappuccinos are always perfectly crafted.

reviews.author: Subhan Ansari
reviews.text: Great coffee and amazing service! The espresso was delicious, and the staff was very friendly and welcoming. I really enjoyed my visit and highly recommend this place. I’ll definitely be back again ☕️

reviews.author: brandyy skye
reviews.text:
Very cute lil spot. I can't speak on the beverages, my goal was the pastries ($6.50) 🤭 but they use Sight Glass beans, so I'm sure the coffee is fantastic.

I did takeaway, the seating spots are minimal, most patrons were sitting outside.
I've been wanting to try the Ube croissant so I could compare it to Neighborhood Bakehouse. I am pleasantly surprised, the Ube is a winner. TCB gets the pastries fresh baked daily from Sunday Bakery. I'm pretty sure its the bakery that used to be located in Oakland, but the menu states the Bakery is in Sausalito  (which makes more sense considering TCB's location 🤷🏼‍♀️)
I wouldn't say their UBE croissant is comparable to Neighborhood Bakehouse, but it is very good. Not overly sweet and a good amount of filling.
I also sampled the almond croissant. Its a solid tasty option as well. Next visit I'll definitely try a savory treat.

reviews.author: Hudson Forrester
reviews.text:
My wife and I have been coming here with our boy Jaxx since they opened so this review is long overdue. The coffee, the team, the hospitality is all off the charts. We always buy our beans from them and they always give our pup treats. We love you guys and will always be regulars!

Sincerely, Jaxx’s Parents

cafes.name: The Mill
cafes.address: 736 Divisadero St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: CDB
reviews.text:
I ordered the latte and the spiced chickpea babka, and both were excellent. The pastry was beautifully flaky, light on the filling, and made with an exceptionally fluffy dough, generously topped with poppy seeds that added a subtle crunch. It felt indulgent without being heavy. The latte was equally enjoyable—rich, smooth, and finished with delicate latte art that made it feel thoughtfully prepared.

The ambiance of the café is one of its strongest features. Despite being fairly crowded, the space feels airy and well designed, with a sense of openness that makes it easy to relax. It was the kind of place where you can comfortably spend the morning with a coffee, a pastry, and a book, without feeling rushed. They also offer physical newspapers to read, which adds to the old-school café charm, and they sell a wide selection of fresh breads and plenty of other baked goods and treats to take home.

Dogs are not allowed inside, except for service animals, so pet owners will need to use the outdoor seating. Fortunately, the outdoor area is spacious and comfortable, weather permitting. One thing to note is that the café does not offer WiFi, so it’s better suited for unplugging rather than working remotely.

reviews.author: Joanna T
reviews.text:
Tuesday at 10:15am.
The café was quite full of patrons drinking coffee and eating the $14 avocado toast.
I ordered a matcha latte with oat milk to go. ~$8!!!! The taste was mild, not very strong.
This NoPA neighborhood is well suited for well- and highly-paid tech workers. Not reasonably priced beverage or food. The patrons mostly had their laptops open and doing work. They were parked out and taking over space for people who just want to chill with a coffee or tea. This café needs a No laptops policy.

reviews.author: Avinash Khemlani
reviews.text:
Stopped by The Mill and everything was a hit.

The cortado was perfect — smooth, balanced, and just the right size.

Food-wise:
•	The Egg in the Hole was the standout — that garlic butter on the country bread is next-level.
•	The smoked trout toast came in a close second, fresh and flavorful.
•	The hummus + cauliflower toast was good, just not as memorable as the others.

Wrapped it all up with a slice of the persimmon cinnamon pistachio crumble cake, which was absolutely outstanding — warm, spiced, and perfectly textured.

Great vibe, friendly staff, and beautifully executed menu. Will definitely be back.

reviews.author: Amitha Pious
reviews.text: This is a cute coffee shop offering different varieties of toast and pastries. The kouign  amman had richness embedded in every bite. It was  flaky and paired well with their medium roast coffee. The ambiance is cute and it’s great spot for a date

reviews.author: May Bresler Herzog
reviews.text: Good flaky pastries (some better than others), but the prices are beyond exaggerated. $17 for a slice of bread with very little on it is insane, especially for a trout toast that ended up being too salty. Ham and cheese croissant was flaky but lacking in filling, toma and squash bureka was also low on filling but very tasty, coffee chocolate crinkle cookie was good but way too expensive for one tiny cookie, and the babka was ok. Love that the place has outdoor seating which makes it dog friendly, but seating is limited, and the wait is very long. IMO - not worth it.

cafes.description: Bright & airy coffee shop serving a simple menu curated by Four Barrel Coffee & Josey Baker Bread.

cafes.name: Diamond Coffee N’ Pastry
cafes.address: 2575 Judah St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Alisa B.
reviews.text:
I definitely recommend the Mango Danish from here! Sooo scrumptious and well made. Crispy and filled with a mango jelly that wasn't too sweet :)

For drinks, we got the Coconut Americano and the Honey Chai. Both were pretty good, standard drinks! Not a place you need to go out of your way for, but everything we had was satisfying. Best part was the cute decor! Lots of indoor seating.

reviews.author: Jos Mast
reviews.text:
Wow. Cute little cafe!

I ate the spinach feta pastry so fast, didn’t even think to try and capture it here.

I wish I’d taken pictures of the food.

I’m so glad they’re open during this time. Not everyone has happy feelings during this time so it’s nice to have a clean well lit place operating during Xmas eve.

It does bite that the alternative milks cost more. Hope we can get past financially punishing people who are lactose intolerant.

Otherwise, perfect little shop.

reviews.author: Claudia Sanchez
reviews.text: I had high expectations because it has the word pastries in the name, I thought they would be freshly baked pastries, but the waffle with strawberry and cream that I got didn’t taste that fresh, at least it was better than the coffee. I ordered a cappuccino and it tasted just like straight espresso. I couldn’t finish it. The place is really pretty decorated but it’s full of people working on their laptops. Also I would like to recommend to serve the waffles in plates instead of boxes? Services was ok.

reviews.author: Andrew Tang
reviews.text: The waffles, pastries, and coffee are decent quality. The interior is very clean and well decorated. The coffee and pastry shop has decent furniture such as tables and chairs, has shelves decorated with cups and model plants, is well organized, and has a little garden outside.

reviews.author: T Slush
reviews.text: Uniquely prepared waffle themed options, along with a variety of other interesting pastries. Hot chocolate, matcha and lattes all well prepared. It was busy and preparation times were a bit lengthy, but well worth the wait.

cafes.name: The Coffee Movement
cafes.address: 1737 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Dharma M
reviews.text:
Here’s a polished and natural rewrite of your review:

---

A nice coffee shop in the Richmond District. It’s usually busy, and the space isn’t very large, though there is some seating available. What they’re best known for, as far as I know, is the tasting flight — three featured coffees of the day served either as different pours or prepared in different ways. They also offer several types of coffee beans.

There are three workers behind the counter preparing drinks, and they work very efficiently. Orders come out quickly.

We ordered the tasting flight, a cocoa cappuccino, a latte, and some pastries. I really enjoyed the tasting flight — it’s a unique way to sample three different coffees or brewing styles. It was a great experience. We usually stop by whenever we’re in the area. The prices are reasonable and affordable. I also really like their espresso machine. ☕
Parking is on the street and could be challenging near the shop.

reviews.author: Stefano DellaVigna
reviews.text: Terrific third wave, stopped here on the way to cheering the SF half marathon. I love that they do the 3 way tasting, with a coffee prepared 3 ways, I had a very floral coffee. Pastries are good too.

reviews.author: F Francis
reviews.text: The chai latte is well-spiced and creamy—easily one of the better ones around. Espresso drinks are good too, made with quality beans, and the espresso tonic is nicely balanced with lime/orange twist. Friendly spot with decent pastries and outdoor seating. Worth checking out. Close to golden gate park and de young museum.

reviews.author: Jarrod W
reviews.text:
Really excellent coffee experience here, especially if you enjoy specialty coffee. Far and away my favorite spot in SF.

Would recommend giving the coffee flight a shot if you’re interested in a little variety. Baristas are super friendly and knowledgeable about the rotating list of coffees.

As far as location, this spot on Balboa was my favorite. Give it a shot!

reviews.author: Mike Ragusa
reviews.text:
Such a cool spot, this is the place to go if you're a true coffee aficionado, I got a tasting flight of their "Dak" Colombia coffee.
You get drip, espresso and a cortado.
Highly recommend checking it out

cafes.name: Doppio Coffee & Brunch
cafes.address: 1551 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Subhan Ansari
reviews.text: I visited Doppio Coffee & Brunch today and had a great experience. They offer delicious vegetarian breakfast options, and the coffee was excellent — they use Lavazza beans, which made the coffee really smooth and flavorful. The café was very clean, calm, and relaxing, with beautiful Turkish music playing in the background. It’s a perfect place for chatting and spending time with friends. The staff were also very friendly and welcoming.

reviews.author: Shanna A
reviews.text:
I was on my way to an appointment near Gough and felt prompted to pause and find a quiet breakfast spot—and this place turned out to be such a blessing. From the moment I walked in, there was a calm, welcoming atmosphere, genuine hospitality, and care in every detail. The food was fresh, beautifully plated, and prepared with intention—you could feel it.

I’ve learned that God often speaks in the pauses, in the moments we don’t plan but are led into. This stop was one of those reminders. If you visit, take a moment to say hello to Firat and the crew, and treat yourself to a freshly squeezed orange juice, a scrumptious crepe (options in sweet or savory), a latte, or a lox bagel. Simple joys, meaningful moments, and a truly lovely start to the day.

Joy is always in the journey—especially when we slow down enough to notice.

— Joy in the Journey

reviews.author: Meltem O
reviews.text: It was clean and spacious. The staff was friendly and professional. My friend got a Soma bagel, and he said it was delicious. I think the exterior sign needs to be redesigned.  The interior decor was modern and the layout was nice. Most importantly for me, the restroom was clean.

reviews.author: Laura Montoya
reviews.text: New favorite breakfast spot. The tomato mozzarella toast is hands-down one of the best I’ve ever had. Fresh, perfectly balanced, and so satisfying. Paired it with a matcha and it was the perfect start to the day. Highly recommend

reviews.author: Gary Graf
reviews.text:
Spent a week in SF and right across from our daughter’s apartment we tried Doppio on our last morning in town. We should have come here everyday!!!
We drink half caf coffee and this was hands down the best cup we have ever had from a coffee shop. Bold and tasty. Not watery and lacking flavor like most.
The menu is huge so we struggled with what to order. Got the parfait which has Greek yogurt, fruit and granola. Very good.
The avocado toast with an extra egg was delicious.
Place is super clean and reasonable.
Definitely a great place!!

cafes.name: Ocean Beach Cafe
cafes.address: 734 La Playa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Ray Torres
reviews.text: Went in for a macha and it was great! Wish they had different size cups. It was cold outside and we were able to get a small table. Love that they had a wall of non alcoholic wines! Pretty cool! We did park a few blocks away but if you are at the beach it’s a quick walk to get a warm drink.

reviews.author: Nathalie Flores-Gonzalez
reviews.text:
Just a minute from the beach, Ocean Beach Cafe feels exactly how a neighborhood spot should feel: welcoming & alive with community energy.

I tried the cortado, made with milk delivered fresh that morning & an iced golden chai that was perfectly balanced & refreshing after a walk by the water. From their thoughtfully curated non-alcoholic bottle shop, I picked up a premium alcohol-free wine from Vienna, Austria, part of a selection that clearly reflects intention & care behind what they stock.

Breakfast did not disappoint. A warm everything bagel with a “secret sauce” I am still thinking about, generous salsa options & homemade pastries that made it hard to choose just one. The staff is young, friendly & fast paced in the best way, attentive without ever making you feel rushed. I noticed pets happily set up with their own treats & water bowls, which adds to the relaxed coastal atmosphere.

What truly makes the place special, though, is the mix of people coming through the door. All ages. Surfers fresh from the beach, families, remote workers, neighbors picking up salads & sandwiches. It feels lived in & real.

And then there is Josh, the owner. When he walks in, the energy subtly shifts. His passion for non-alcoholic beverages, shaped by years behind the bar & now five years building this concept, gives the space a quiet magnetism. Conversations naturally unfold about well-being, mindful drinking & community. You can easily spend hours there, sipping slowly & talking about life.

I also loved the Hawaiian furniture handcrafted by his father, which gives the space a deeply personal & home-like feel.

What elevates Ocean Beach Cafe even further is how it connects daily rituals with something bigger. Every Saturday, the team organizes a local beach cleanup, inviting neighbors & customers to begin their morning with purpose. By removing plastic & marine debris from the shoreline, they contribute directly to Life Below Water (SDG 14) & the protection of coastal ecosystems. These cleanups strengthen community ownership & environmental responsibility. By turning care for public space into a shared ritual, the café contributes to Sustainable Cities & Communities (SDG 11), showing how resilient cities are built through small, consistent collective actions.

reviews.author: Haley Oscar
reviews.text: Our favorite way to start our day when visiting San Francisco. The food is great and the staff is very friendly. Even though we are all the way in Wisconsin, we talk about this little cafe  often.

reviews.author: Jonathan Monte
reviews.text:
TL;DR
Where presentation is of higher quality than the Food
FULL REVIEW
This place had such a high rating that it was kind of surprising that it was so mediocre. San Francisco is not a food desert, but maybe this part of town is.

We got both the regular and the lavender lemonade. The regular lemonade tastes straight out of a can of Country Time. The lavender lemonade definitely tasted like lavender, so if you like that kind of thing, it's excellent.

The avocado toast lives on presentation. I thought I was about to experience Nirvana, but instead was greeted by hollow taste. The bread was nice and the avocado was fresh, but it was boring.

The turkey sandwich looked pretty normal. The bread was fine, and the red sauce they put on it gave it some flavor. I added avocado, and had I not, this sandwich would have only been slightly better than the avocado toast.

The only Epic part of the Epic Breakfast Sandwich was the Everything bagel, which was the highlight of my dining experience, but the eggs were boring as everything else was.

Nothing was bad, but nothing was exciting either. This perfectly sums up this place. At best, the presentation was really good, but that's all that it had going for it. Essentially, the presentation is of higher quality than the food.

reviews.author: Bobby Miller
reviews.text:
I've been on a non-alcoholic journey as I am three years sober, and discovering new NA drinks has been a crucial part of that journey. My wife and I finally got out to visit this spot, as the only true non-alc bar in the whole Bay Area. Well worth the trip.

Joshua was super pleasant and guided us through an awesome flight of NA beers and a few mocktail samples (try the Golden Spirit, it rules).  We shared our stories of sobriety with each other and a few of the bar patrons who popped in for a drink.

Spaces like this are so critical for both people like me who have stopped drinking, and to drinkers who are starting to learn about the healthier ways to have a social drink. My wife and I went home with a box full of bottles and cans, and we cannot wait to go back.

(also, dinner at the Laundromat was super clutch, thank you for the recommendation Joshua!)

cafes.name: Third Wheel Coffee
cafes.address: 535 Scott St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Ronie Vega
reviews.text: I love pine. Pine trees, pine needles, pine cones. So as a pine lover, I was very curious to try Third Wheel's vanilla pine latte. It's safe to say that pine is also delicious. It pains me that I do not live in this city, because if I did, Third Wheel Coffee would be my first stop every morning. The pine bud infused syrup makes the latte very aromatic and adds an herbal note that pairs beautifully with the high quality espresso. It's unique. It's perfect. I will be thinking about it everyday until I return. Don't skip their hot chocolate either! They melt down actual chocolate wafers with your milk of choice, and it's a perfect addition to a rainy SF morning.

reviews.author: John de Souza
reviews.text: Wonderful coffee. Hidden but definitely worth the find. Warm and quick service. Coffee was the right temperature, smooth consistency, and beautiful flavor.

reviews.author: Ana G
reviews.text: New favorite coffee spot! The vanilla pine latte was divine, excited to try more seasonal drinks. Also, the baristas are super kind :-)

reviews.author: Shanelle S
reviews.text: Coffee bar that shares the same space as a sandwich shop. Stopped by here for brunch with my boyfriend and it was a delicious spot. They had matcha and coffee options (in addition to affogato). Both our lattes were incredibly smooth and tasty. We grabbed a chocolate mochi muffin as well as a ham and cheese croissant. There wasn’t any seating so we went to the park nearby to eat on the grass. You pay first before receiving your order on the right hand side of the counter. Highly recommend this spot for grabbing takeout caffeine in the morning.

reviews.author: Koen Thiessen
reviews.text: For our honeymoon, my wife and I road tripped down to California. On the way, we stopped at many coffee shops, in search of the perfect espresso. And here, at third wheel coffee, we found exactly that. I ordered a flat white, and thoroughly enjoyed it. My wife's vanilla latte was also amazing, and so was the pastry that we had. (We also ordered a matcha for the road which was quite good as well.) The shop is located in a cool urban San Francisco location. The barista was kind and friendly, as well as very knowledgeable. You can tell coffee is his passion! Would definitely recommend going out of your way for if you're in the San Francisco area!

cafes.name: The Buena Vista
cafes.address: 2765 Hyde St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Christopher T
reviews.text:
Alright, I had a great time here. Known this place for ages but finally went, especially for the Irish Coffee. We went around an hr after they open and waited for 10mins to get in. It packed and they have 2 sections, bar (for drinks only - no foods serve), and shares or community tables. We got the community table and we shared with another group. The service is great from the hostess to server, they did a terrific job of explaining how things work and so forth. I got the Irish coffee (which was good but I had better) and my friend got the non alcohol Irish coffee plus the claim chowder, the pastrami sandwich, and the Vista burger. Which all are delish, we ate them all while chatting with the other folks.  The decor is simple and filled with old articles and pics of the historical place so it nice. The bartender dressed in a white coat to make it more of the oldie feeling. We all really enjoyed it and left with new friends and great memories. Check out if you're in the area and the wait line can be long so come early. Plus very convenient since it right across from the cable car and the Ghirardelli square.
Helpful 0 Thanks 0
Love

reviews.author: Sharlene Simon
reviews.text:
Known for their Irish coffees. Kind of a tourist trap, due to location right next to Ghirardelli square.  But also a SF staple!

Space- old school bar. Eat at tables only drink at bars. Lots of staff. Gets busy.

Menu- we got the crab omelette and salmon sandwich. Both tasty. Love that you can substitute boudin sourdough.

Drinks- full bar offerings. Ppl have no shame drinking at 9am. Myself included.

reviews.author: Shane J
reviews.text:
Visited for breakfast with my family and really enjoyed the experience. The café has a cosy, welcoming feel with a menu that offers plenty of great options to choose from.
What really stood out was the staff, they seemed like they’d worked there forever, were genuinely proud of the place, and carried a real sense of tradition.
The food was delicious with generous portions and definitely didn’t disappoint. A memorable and charming spot for breakfast!

reviews.author: Helen S
reviews.text:
Popped in here for lunch after needing a caffeine fix after an early start. It seems like this spot is busy at all times, with some people having to share tables, so just keep that in mind if you are looking for a romantic meal.

We were seated quickly and I ordered the Irish coffee (my husband  just had a beer). Being from the UK originally we are used to Irish coffees but this was a very nice version (albeit small, especially for $16). For food we ordered the lobster roll and the club sandwich, which both arrived very quickly. The lobster roll was great, with a generous portion of lobster. My husbands club sandwich looked good as well, and he said it was very tasty. His food did come with fries, which weren't mentioned on the menu or asked for, which he was ultimately charged for. It wasn't a huge amount (something like $1.50) and I would like to think it was just a mistake, but something to be aware of.

We finished our food and our server recommended a Mexican coffee, so I had to give that a try. I actually think I preferred the Mexican coffee to the Irish coffee, so if you are a Kailua fan I recommend giving it a try). In total our bill came to $130 (including tax and tip) so is a little expensive for a lunch spot. The food is good though and I would recommend if you are in the area.

reviews.author: Martin S
reviews.text:
If you’re in San Francisco and love a good cocktail experience, The Buena Vista is an absolute must-see - especially for anyone craving an iconic Irish Coffee. This legendary spot has perfected the art of the drink, and watching the bartenders line up glasses and craft each Irish Coffee with precision is truly mesmerizing. Make sure to grab a spot near the bar so you can enjoy the show.

That said, don’t expect a quiet, cozy atmosphere. The Buena Vista is lively and often packed, especially on weekends and holidays. Be prepared to wait a while for a table or even a spot at the bar during peak times. The energy and buzz are part of the charm, though—it’s all part of the experience.

Bottom line: Come for the history, stay for the Irish Coffee, and soak in the vibrant vibe of this San Francisco classic.

cafes.description: Opened in 1916, this corner spot serves its signature Irish coffee alongside American staples.

cafes.name: HI NRG
cafes.address: 443 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Hannah Arcilla
reviews.text: Amazing coffee! Came here after a bike ride. Both the owners are super nice, it’s rare you see a barista-owned shop. You can tell they put their all into this shop and their coffee! We got espresso + milk and a cafe de olla with oat milk, both were super smooth. We also tried their olive oil cake and cheddar biscuits which were 🔑 after our ride 🚲 really cool atmosphere, they sell records and it’s a chill, darker modern vibe. There’s seating mostly for individual / 2 people but there’s a couple 3-4 group tables. Will be back!

reviews.author: Gemma
reviews.text:
I love this place. The energy (sry — NRG) was so good. Warm vibes, friendly service, and divine coffee.

I got a Cortado which had the perfect milk to espresso ratio (a surprising rarity) and was thrilled by all the space to post up, read, write, chat, strum away on one’s laptop — whatever you’re feeling!

The wide open windows make it bright and light-filled too. This place is a gem :)

reviews.author: Savannah Sprague
reviews.text: Great to have specialty coffee in this area. Love the menu. It’s simple. The owners are baristas which I love as a fellow barista. They are knowledgeable and exciting about their drinks. Excellent hospitality. We got an oat cappuccino and a recommended washed Ethiopian coffee that was excellent!! And the cheddar and chive scone was delectable! The space is very nice. Wish only good things for these folks. Will be back.

reviews.author: Sia A.
reviews.text: Super cute spot, no doubt about that. Unfortunately, my coffee was cold and the taste wasn’t great, it came off a little sour. Maybe the decaf coffee is like that. I tried another regular latte, too which wasn’t much better. I really wanted to leave a better review, and I’m hoping the coffee quality improves because the place itself has a lot of charm.

reviews.author: Alexis
reviews.text: Gorgeous intimate coffee shop complete with spots of sun rays, records spinning and coffee snobbery at its finest with no elitism, only the sweetest passion for the craft and friendly faces. Really relaxed environment with helpful guidance on what to order. I got the cortado and a strawberry ale both it the spot. Music was wonderful and appreciated the obligatory high energy track.

cafes.name: Sightglass Coffee
cafes.address: 270 7th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Timothy Colvin (Tim)
reviews.text: Trendy coffee shop with the atmosphere of your "local dive". Bathed in natural light and with ample space to spread out and work, this industrial loft has nailed multi-purpose. Try all of their flagship roasts to keep it exciting, but I recommend Blueboon as a staple. Also love their croissants. Pictured is the Pistachio Berry croissant. It's perfection.

reviews.author: Hadleigh Painter
reviews.text: Super cool spot! I am so glad that I finally got to check it out. I definitely want to go back to enjoy the upstairs space and get some work done. Their breakfast burrito was a little small but I love how it is crispy/ crunchy (and would order it again). I want to try more of the menu

reviews.author: Aatif Junaid
reviews.text:
I stopped by Side Class Coffee today after hearing a lot about it. The coffee itself is solid and worth trying, but everything else felt pretty average.

The food was underwhelming, and the ambience didn’t really stand out. It isn’t bad, just not warm or inviting. They do have a setup for packing coffee on the side, which is nice, but the overall vibe feels just okay.

If you’re looking to try a new coffee spot, it’s worth visiting for the coffee, but I wouldn’t go for the food or the ambience.

reviews.author: Therock Ton
reviews.text:
Got a good drip coffee and cold kwa-son (croissant)

Remember to check if they heated up ur pastry. They might forget, and can’t heat it up for you once it’s across the counter. Or maybe they enjoy a cold croissant I don’t know.

reviews.author: Cai
reviews.text: A really nice and spacious cafe in SoMa! The interior design is really pretty with a cool bike rack right in front. They also do their own roasting in house. The coffee flavor is solid and I’ll come back next time to try their specialty flavors like the brown butter miso. No wifi though :(

cafes.description: Indie outfit serving worldly coffees in a modern setting with an espresso bar & an on-site roaster.

cafes.name: Bazaar Café
cafes.address: 5927 California St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Helen
reviews.text:
Excellent spot for brunch! Their lattes are absolutely amazing! I usually avoid caramel of any type but in the latte it was beautifully balanced and a nice addition.

The curry was served hot and yummy. We wanted to try a few things and the employee was nice enough to let us know that we can do a side soup so that we lessen the amount of side salads that would come with each menu item.

Sat in the backyard patio and it was lovely, there was shade and a garden out back and it was an enjoyable start to the day.

reviews.author: Anja Marie Sandholm
reviews.text: Always a treat to visit! Truly yummy espresso drinks, surprisingly good quality! Great staff. Love the pottery they serve their drinks in. Yummy biscuits too. Lovely patio out back. The cutest neighborhood vibe.

reviews.author: M Carter
reviews.text: Came here to listen to this afternoon's live music . The garden in the back is a perfect place for Sunday afternoon jazz.  Inside the cafe is great for a reading session, working or a quiet conversation. It is cozy and there are nooks in the space to do your thing.

reviews.author: Sharon W
reviews.text:
Dunno what's up with that 1 star review from a few weeks ago, but I'm here to rebut it.

This is a most lovely cafe with great vibes, comforting drinks, and delicious food (I much prefer their biscuits over the ones we had growing up in Alabama).

The staff is awesome. Everything tastes better when you're here.

Cheers!

reviews.author: Trina M
reviews.text: Loved the calm vibe, live music felt intimate and warm. Their honey lavender biscuit and the biscuit were delicious. Enjoyed the tea lattes!

cafes.description: Chill cafe with back patio offering eclectic mix of food (sandwiches to popcorn) & live music.

cafes.name: The Mill
cafes.address: 736 Divisadero St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: CDB
reviews.text:
I ordered the latte and the spiced chickpea babka, and both were excellent. The pastry was beautifully flaky, light on the filling, and made with an exceptionally fluffy dough, generously topped with poppy seeds that added a subtle crunch. It felt indulgent without being heavy. The latte was equally enjoyable—rich, smooth, and finished with delicate latte art that made it feel thoughtfully prepared.

The ambiance of the café is one of its strongest features. Despite being fairly crowded, the space feels airy and well designed, with a sense of openness that makes it easy to relax. It was the kind of place where you can comfortably spend the morning with a coffee, a pastry, and a book, without feeling rushed. They also offer physical newspapers to read, which adds to the old-school café charm, and they sell a wide selection of fresh breads and plenty of other baked goods and treats to take home.

Dogs are not allowed inside, except for service animals, so pet owners will need to use the outdoor seating. Fortunately, the outdoor area is spacious and comfortable, weather permitting. One thing to note is that the café does not offer WiFi, so it’s better suited for unplugging rather than working remotely.

reviews.author: Joanna T
reviews.text:
Tuesday at 10:15am.
The café was quite full of patrons drinking coffee and eating the $14 avocado toast.
I ordered a matcha latte with oat milk to go. ~$8!!!! The taste was mild, not very strong.
This NoPA neighborhood is well suited for well- and highly-paid tech workers. Not reasonably priced beverage or food. The patrons mostly had their laptops open and doing work. They were parked out and taking over space for people who just want to chill with a coffee or tea. This café needs a No laptops policy.

reviews.author: Avinash Khemlani
reviews.text:
Stopped by The Mill and everything was a hit.

The cortado was perfect — smooth, balanced, and just the right size.

Food-wise:
•	The Egg in the Hole was the standout — that garlic butter on the country bread is next-level.
•	The smoked trout toast came in a close second, fresh and flavorful.
•	The hummus + cauliflower toast was good, just not as memorable as the others.

Wrapped it all up with a slice of the persimmon cinnamon pistachio crumble cake, which was absolutely outstanding — warm, spiced, and perfectly textured.

Great vibe, friendly staff, and beautifully executed menu. Will definitely be back.

reviews.author: Amitha Pious
reviews.text: This is a cute coffee shop offering different varieties of toast and pastries. The kouign  amman had richness embedded in every bite. It was  flaky and paired well with their medium roast coffee. The ambiance is cute and it’s great spot for a date

reviews.author: May Bresler Herzog
reviews.text: Good flaky pastries (some better than others), but the prices are beyond exaggerated. $17 for a slice of bread with very little on it is insane, especially for a trout toast that ended up being too salty. Ham and cheese croissant was flaky but lacking in filling, toma and squash bureka was also low on filling but very tasty, coffee chocolate crinkle cookie was good but way too expensive for one tiny cookie, and the babka was ok. Love that the place has outdoor seating which makes it dog friendly, but seating is limited, and the wait is very long. IMO - not worth it.

cafes.description: Bright & airy coffee shop serving a simple menu curated by Four Barrel Coffee & Josey Baker Bread.

cafes.name: Simple Pleasures Cafe
cafes.address: 3434 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Lara Getselevich
reviews.text: I visited the most beautiful cafe today! It has such an amazing vibe of simplicity and grace. The drinks are incredibly tasty—I treated myself to a cappuccino and a cherry croissant, and everything was perfectly fresh. Loved it! ❤️

reviews.author: Sagar
reviews.text: I rarely give a place 5 stars, but this place deserves it because I liked everything here. The way the staff treated me, took my order, answered my questions and made my coffee. The taste of my coffee, the live music there, the amazing yummy Egyptian shortbread I asked them to warm up for me and some apricot pie I took home....I liked everything here. Asked them to blend 2 sugar in my cappuccino and they did (other places refuse to do it). Didn't have to wait here. The staff who took my order was awesome (didn't ask her name but I should have to write it here). An awesome coffee place. Love the name too. I couldn't find anything here less than 5 stars except parking....well that doesn't have anything to do with this place (it's SF). I'm saving this place in my list to come back ☕️

reviews.author: Praveen Swadi
reviews.text:
This is a hidden gem that locals frequent. It is slightly out of the way for tourists, as it is in core residential area. But highly recommended. Small, cozy place. But even house coffee is awesome. In addition we took iced latte and mocha. Both were really good. Mocha was tad bit sweet, but not too much. We also had apricot pastry - which was huge and was sufficient for 3 of us. Tasty too.

The place is small and in an old bar setting but you should find place to sit. There are benches outside too, so if it is not too cold, you can enjoy the weather.

reviews.author: Katia Martha
reviews.text:
Rating 5 stars mainly for ambiance and because I’d go back and try other things. Really charming, cozy place with all ages gathered- seems like an established community hub. There’s people working on laptops and just hanging out. Unfortunately the thing I ordered was not satisfying but I’ll try something else next time. I ordered a chai latte and it tasted nothing like any chai I’ve had. I had my two friends take sips of my drink too to verify and they said it tasted like ginger tea. I wonder if my drink got mixed up somehow or if they ran out of chai? If they ran out, I’d rather have been told so I could’ve gotten something else. My friends enjoyed their coffees and bagels. Friendly people and great atmosphere make it worth it for me to come back, try something new, and recommend to others.
Oh also! The writing on the cups was beautiful :)

reviews.author: Frances Allan
reviews.text: Don’t walk by this understated exterior. Buzzing, popular coffee shop with locals working and socialising over great coffee and matcha. Genuine and unsweetened matcha. Lots of baked goods and burritos.

cafes.description: Local mainstay for espresso, house-roasted coffee,  bagels & baked goods in a lived-in cafe setting.

cafes.name: The Coffee Berry SF
cafes.address: 1410 Lombard St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Hannah Arcilla
reviews.text:
Cute, clean, and bright coffee shop with owners who really care. I went today and both owners were working. They have a large pastry selection, with traditional “fika” Swedish buns from Thursday to Sundays. They’re phenomenal, I’ve only tried the cinnamon fika bun but I’ll be trying the others as well. I believe they use Sightglass coffee, both the flat white and dirty chai I got tasted great. The chai wasn’t too sweet.

This spot is also great because lots of dogs are regulars here! So you always get fluffy friends inside. It also seems like there’s tons of regulars, as I often hear the owners saying “So, the usual ___ (insert name of customer)?” I may become one of those regulars even though I live in Outer Richmond, I like this place so much!

Added bonus: really clean bathrooms, lots of windows for bright environment, kind, prompt service, owners who genuinely care about their establishment. Only con (although not a terrible one) very limited seating! Maybe only for 8-10 people. But they do well considering the size. All in all, 5 stars and possibly my new favorite coffee spot at the moment!

reviews.author: Caitlin Conley
reviews.text: I love this spot! It’s a cozy little space, but perfect for grabbing a coffee and pastry on the go. The folks working here are always so chipper and friendly; the pastry selection is phenomenal. The ube croissant is unique and delicious. Their cappuccinos are always perfectly crafted.

reviews.author: Subhan Ansari
reviews.text: Great coffee and amazing service! The espresso was delicious, and the staff was very friendly and welcoming. I really enjoyed my visit and highly recommend this place. I’ll definitely be back again ☕️

reviews.author: brandyy skye
reviews.text:
Very cute lil spot. I can't speak on the beverages, my goal was the pastries ($6.50) 🤭 but they use Sight Glass beans, so I'm sure the coffee is fantastic.

I did takeaway, the seating spots are minimal, most patrons were sitting outside.
I've been wanting to try the Ube croissant so I could compare it to Neighborhood Bakehouse. I am pleasantly surprised, the Ube is a winner. TCB gets the pastries fresh baked daily from Sunday Bakery. I'm pretty sure its the bakery that used to be located in Oakland, but the menu states the Bakery is in Sausalito  (which makes more sense considering TCB's location 🤷🏼‍♀️)
I wouldn't say their UBE croissant is comparable to Neighborhood Bakehouse, but it is very good. Not overly sweet and a good amount of filling.
I also sampled the almond croissant. Its a solid tasty option as well. Next visit I'll definitely try a savory treat.

reviews.author: Hudson Forrester
reviews.text:
My wife and I have been coming here with our boy Jaxx since they opened so this review is long overdue. The coffee, the team, the hospitality is all off the charts. We always buy our beans from them and they always give our pup treats. We love you guys and will always be regulars!

Sincerely, Jaxx’s Parents

cafes.name: Four Barrel Coffee
cafes.address: 375 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: SFMasterP
reviews.text:
I went here on a Friday morning. The place was busy and packed. The coffeeshop is spacious. There were ppl talking or people on their laptops.

They only serve coffee, meaning no specialty flavored drinks. My friend ordered our drinks while I saved our seats. The service seems great and the place is pretty big. You can see the staff roasting the beans in the back. My latte was good. I ordered oat milk but it tasted like either 1% milk or non fat milk. Luckily, I wasn’t allergic to dairy so I didn’t bother to bring it up to the staff. Also, I normally get flavored drinks but the latte was good. I would go back

reviews.author: Stefano DellaVigna
reviews.text: One of the best third wave coffees in the Bay Area, a consistent excellent medium roast espresso, as well as an interesting selection of pastries. The space is industrial chic, with the roasting machines in the back of the space. Not too busy in the early morning

reviews.author: Bay Area Reviews
reviews.text: Had some tasty lattes here. They have good seasonal flavors during the fall months. I really enjoyed their coffee cups for dine in drinks.

reviews.author: Xing V
reviews.text:
I went here all the time for ice coffee. I even bought a mug from them. One day I forgot that’s my mug so I put it back their dish rack. I came back to ask for it. They said it’s gone and attempt to sell me a new one. Also the coffee has gotten weaker and weaker. The outdoor seating is nice but I’m not sure whether its worthy after all. Even though I live only 1-2 blocks from me. Its sad but I don’t think I can longer support this coffeeshop. They seem doing fine with me anyway :)

Update: I came back for my mug. Thanks :)

reviews.author: Jeffrey Garelick
reviews.text: I have been coming here for years and I love it! Favorite coffee cafe in SF for atmosphere and awesome coffee. Baked goods really good too especially the sweet scones when they have them… they go fast!!! If you live coffee cafes you’ll love Four Barrel. Grateful the owners founded and run this awesome SF institution which most often open on holidays too 🙏🙏

cafes.description: Exposed beams create a sleek & rustic ambiance for this Mission cafe specializing in drip coffee.

cafes.name: Delah Coffee
cafes.address: 370 4th St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Skeeb Khan
reviews.text:
Delah Coffee is such a gem in San Francisco, tucked away near 4th St and Clara St. I heard this was one of the first coffee shops to come to the SF area, and it definitely feels special the moment you walk in. The interior is absolutely beautiful with really nice furniture and a warm, inviting vibe.
The service was excellent—super friendly and attentive. I ordered the chai along with a borak (cheese pastry), and everything was delicious. The cheese pastry came with honey, which was the perfect touch.
They have a great menu with classics like meat pies, cheesecakes, milk cakes, and a nice variety of refreshers as well. Whether you’re stopping by for coffee, dessert, or a light bite, this place hits the spot. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews.author: Muhammad Atif
reviews.text:
Delay Coffee is such a cozy and refreshing spot! Their pistachio latte is absolutely delicious — creamy, perfectly sweet, and full of real pistachio flavor. The tea is smooth and comforting, and they offer an amazing variety of snacks and desserts that taste as good as they look. Everything feels fresh and handmade.

It’s the perfect place to relax, grab a warm drink, and enjoy some treats. Highly recommended if you want something yummy and comforting!

reviews.author: Caroline T
reviews.text: I tried this cinnamon latte with dates, it was amazing. Staff are friendly too. Definitely will come back to try other coffee next trip!

reviews.author: Taniya
reviews.text:
It's always bustling with people and for good reason. This cozy, vibrant café has earned a loyal following thanks to its exceptional drinks and authentic Middle Eastern treats. It’s my go-to spot, especially for their 🌟pistachio latte ⭐️, which is rich, creamy and perfectly balanced with just the right amount of nutty flavor without being overly sweet . Trust me, one sip and you'll understand the hype.
They also have a variety of traditional Turkish desserts and savory pies that are delicious. And if you're a tea lover, don’t miss their Adeni chai - it's fragrant, spiced, and deeply satisfying. I recommend ordering it without sugar so you can sweeten it to your taste.

reviews.author: Dom D
