reviews.text: I’ve had Delah on my to-visit list for a while now, and I regret that I didn’t make it in sooner! Great atmosphere, amazing coffee, and helpful, friendly staff. The Turkish delight was incredible, as well. Hot tip: Have them put a little cardamom in the Turkish coffee. Sooo good!

cafes.name: Flywheel Coffee Roasters
cafes.address: 672 Stanyan St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: J V
reviews.text: Excellent mocha and latte's. If you care about quality coffee and lattes with perfect foam then this is your place. You can't go wrong with the great chill atmosphere, laid back vibe and music with a patio in the back. They do have a small selection of gluten free donuts and other bread goods to eat with your coffee. Come check this place out!

reviews.author: Sasha Diodorova
reviews.text: Loved the coffee and the vibe here, but guys… the restroom completely ruined the experience. It’s so gross. It feels really disrespectful to customers to maintain it in such poor condition. It’s such a shame, because the shop itself is great.

reviews.author: Kueiman Lin
reviews.text: I especially like their coffee because I prefer wine taste coffee rather than chocolate one. If you use you cup, you can get around $0.5 discount, and they provide 2-hr WiFi for free.

reviews.author: Foodie At Heart
reviews.text: Flywheel Roasters on Stanyan is a true coffee paradise in SF. The moment you enter, an intriguing and distinct aroma of freshly roasted beans envelops you. Their commitment to same-day roasting is evident in every exceptional cup. The coffee is simply awesome – vibrant, nuanced, and bursting with flavor. You can taste the dedication in each sip. The passionate staff and welcoming atmosphere further enhance the experience. For any coffee lover, Flywheel on Stanyan is an absolute must-visit. Prepare to be enchanted by the quality and freshness!

reviews.author: Rachel Delaney
reviews.text: Excellent coffee and latte art! I couldn’t believe it when I saw the swan come out. Great service too - they cleaned up a table for us right away when they realized the previous customers had left everything behind.

cafes.description: Family-owned coffee roaster offering exotic brews in a stripped-down, industrial setting.

cafes.name: Saint Frank Coffee
cafes.address: 2340 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Monika R
reviews.text:
One of the best pour-over coffees, we enjoyed in a long time. The aroma was incredible—rich, smooth, and inviting.

The coffee itself was beautifully balanced. It had a clean, bright flavor with subtle notes that unfolded with every sip. You could really taste the quality of the beans and the precision of the brewing process. The pour-over method brought out a depth and clarity in the coffee that you just don’t get from standard brewing methods.

Coffee was smooth, flavorful, and incredibly satisfying from start to finish. If you appreciate great coffee, this is definitely the kind of place where you can slow down, savor the moment, and enjoy a truly exceptional pour-over. I’ll absolutely be coming back for another cup.

reviews.author: Barbara Cash
reviews.text: I dropped by for an afternoon latte. The taste was amazing! I'm a coffee snob and I was satified. This is my new favorite café! I tried the blueberry lemon muffin. It was superb. I enjoyed the atmosphere, however I couldn't get a seat. There are too many people with their laptops. There should be a time limit so all customers can enjoy sitting in a cafe.

reviews.author: Melissa Eisner
reviews.text: I luckily walked in at a time where there was no wait and empty tables, so yay for me. Ordered a pour over and an iced white, and both were lovely. My iced coffee was particularly satisfying, and they made it with non-dairy milk which was a win for me. The almond croissant was great, tasty filling and nice crispy edges. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Liviu Orehovschi
reviews.text:
Unfortunately, worth the hype. I am sad about the many mornings I will freeze in line to get this...

For the snobs: Yes, you'll find coffee and beans that are comparable in many places in sf (we're privileged like that, aren't we?).

This place is not a coffee shop, though. It's a coffee experience. All credits to the team. You get to know and discuss your coffee with knowledgeable and kind staff.

I will see y'all soon :)

reviews.author: Cynthia
reviews.text: Came to try their coffee flight as per a friend’s recommendation and found it a tad underwhelming? Possibly just personal preference as they leaned quite sour/acidic. Overall still a great experience and service was friendly!

cafes.description: Sunny, bi-level neighborhood coffeehouse pairing its java with pastries in airy, minimalist digs.

cafes.name: Better Half Coffee
cafes.address: 1448 Pacific Ave, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Ronie Vega
reviews.text: I heard great things about this coffee shop, and I'm happy to share that it lives up to all the praise. This should be a must try when visiting San Francisco. Their signature drink made with the Isfahan syrup is absolutely delicious, not too sweet which allows for the espresso to shine even in latte form. The syrup has notes of rose, raspberry, and vanilla that compliments anything it touches. I had the pleasure of trying it as a spritz where the floral notes were front and center. Not to mention, the space is cozy and inviting. Joshua is very knowledgeable was kind enough to share the Better Half Coffee's story. I will half to return during their later hours when the shop transforms into a bar and bites establishment!

reviews.author: Hawaii Food photos
reviews.text:
Great Coffee, Amazing Food and Outstanding Service!

This was our first coffee stop in SF during this trip and we are so happy to land in such great spot to get coffee and start our café and cocktail bar quest.  The atmosphere is cozy and staff is friendly and very hospitable. Highly recommend it if you are in the area.

reviews.author: Dharma M
reviews.text:
Love this place . It is popup store inside a wine bar. It is coffee shop in the morning and wine bar in the evening. They just opened about 4 months.  I am not sure if this is their permanent place.  We were looking for coffee shop near North Beach and found it.  The interior is very nice with small section where they take order and prepare the coffee.  There are some pastries to choose from and they also have something from kitchen.
The owner is very friendly and based on our conversation, he has been in coffee world for quite sometimes.
We order matcha, cortado and latte. They have milk options if you are lactose intolerance.  We also order Confit Avocado.
The order was ready quickly which surprise me.  The coffee and matcha were tasty.
The highlight is the Confit Avocado. It is so yummy and different than regular avocado toast which you get from many coffee shop. They infuse the avocado with oil. I never had this before.  it is at next level.
The price is reasonable and good this is it is pet friendly place.

reviews.author: Amira Athanasios
reviews.text: The breakfast melt sandwich is absolutely excellent. Morning pastries are delic. And ofc the cappuccino is perfect. Lovely interior for catching up friends or laptop work. Has WiFi avail. The staff and owner are amazing

reviews.author: Norika
reviews.text:
We ordered an iced latte with Ispahan syrup, an iced matcha latte, two donuts (raspberry and custard), and a Thai butter cake. The Ispahan syrup adds a unique flavor to the latte, which I really enjoyed.

The donuts taste homemade and are not overly sweet—very different from typical American donuts, and I love that. The texture of the Thai butter cake reminds me a bit of cornbread: light yet satisfying.

On top of that, the café is dog-friendly, which makes it an even more enjoyable place to visit.

cafes.name: Diamond Coffee N Pastry
cafes.address: 6909 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Arthur T
reviews.text:
Found Diamond Coffee off of yelp!

Was in the area and needed a place to work.

I ordered:
Americano $5.25
16 oz Cold ($1.00)
Savory Croissant
$6.25 Spinach & Feta

Coffee was fine. Was a bit weird to have a $1 upcharge for a cold drink.

I asked staff for a recommendation on pastry since their spread looked good. The spinach and feta croissant was ok, definitely needed more flavor.

Some seating inside. Nice fun, cozy vibe inside.

Street parking only.

reviews.author: PJ Pasricha
reviews.text:
Diamond Coffee N Pastry is a hidden gem! The cold brew was fantastic—smooth and refreshing, just the pick-me-up I needed. The staff was super friendly, adding to the welcoming vibe of the place.

There’s plenty of seating, which makes it a great spot to relax or catch up with friends. The options were a real highlight—creative and delicious, with so many amazing options to choose from.

The only downside was the parking, which was a bit tricky to find. But once you’re inside enjoying the coffee and pastries, it’s totally worth the effort. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the area!

reviews.author: Julia
reviews.text:
Cafe with great vibes and food! I got the strawberry croffle and it was delicious, creamy without being too sweet!

The interior is also super cute!

Great place to make a pit stop / grab a bite after the Legion of Honor and wait for the 38 bus!

reviews.author: Butterfly Blob
reviews.text: Very cute inside and outlets for every table!! Unfortunately, there didn't seem to be enough seats for the amount of patrons that came by on a Saturday afternoon. I got purple which was just ube and plain milk, nothing fancy. However, the fruity croffle was a nice experience and the blueberries were perfect and not too ripe. A great place to study if you can get a seat.

reviews.author: Olga Liakhovich
reviews.text: Very cute place. Coffee is amazing. Very nice selection of pastries, very fresh. The chocolate croissant is melting in the mouth. Looking forward to tasting spinach-feta pastry next time 😍

cafes.name: Paper Son Coffee
cafes.address: 303 2nd St N102, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Sonya Hu
reviews.text: Absolutely delightful coffee, matcha, and baked goodies! This adorable little coffee shop always has a giant line, but I can absolutely see why — the coffee is rich and nutty, either on its own, in a classic espresso drink, or mixed with one of their delightful Asian-inspired syrups. They have some of the best matcha beverages in the city, and I am consistently impressed by their selection of classic and Asian-inspired pastries. Everything is available between opening and when they’re sold out, and they definitely run out of the exciting, house-made pastries — like their ube salt bread, kimchi furikake chorizo scones, or sesame toffee chocolate chip cookies — quite quickly, so you have to get there early if you want to indulge. I usually try to arrive within an hour or two of them opening if I want one of their delicious Asian-inspired pastries, but it’s absolutely worth the early morning trek. I especially love their salt bread, which is super buttery and pillowy, and it has a great balance of salty vs. sweet. I also love their more classic pastries that they got from another bakery for later arrivals. The millet muffin is my go-to — it’s not overly sweet, has a great grainy texture and crunch from the millet, and is a nice hearty breakfast. Even though the line looks long and intimidating, it moves quite quickly. The team is really efficient, so I’ve never waited more than 10 minutes to order or more than 5 minutes for my order to be made afterwards, even when the line is out the door.

reviews.author: Frank Barnard
reviews.text: I had the single best cup of coffee I've had in my life at Paper Son. A friend took me there and told the owner that I was a newbie. Alex ground a small amount of beans, had me smell them (amazing), then just did a filtered pour into a small carafe. I usually need cream & sugar. Not this time. The flavors were sublime. On top of it, the staff & patrons all seemed like old friends. A great experience!!

reviews.author: chi 吃huo 货
reviews.text:
TLDR everything is a bit too rich and there's minimal crunch compared to what I expected but I can see the vision/ attention to detail with respect to flavor.

Ordered guava salt bread, squash egg yolk focaccia, thai tea

Thai tea: Cardamom sweet cream is pleasantly flavorful and done really well, I like how quickly it melts into the tea. Personally would probably not order again because it's a bit sweet for my liking (usually I order 0% for boba).

Guava salt bread: Only crunchy at the ends (nowhere else) and too much cream for my liking; the cream:bread ratio is more like a cream puff than what I expected, where expectations were set based on product name "salt bread" so I expected more emphasis on bread vs filling. The guava cream is nicely flavored, light and floral. If it was a bit more sour maybe there would be better balance and would not feel as rich?

Focaccia: very soggy, both on bottom and top! I will say the flavor is great (generous with salted egg yolk, great umami, pairs well with the curry leaf and kabocha) and the inside is moist and chewy; indeed you can tell it's been fermented quite well and gluten structure is great but for a focaccia at this price I really expected/was craving the crunch that comes with it. Again the flavor is amazing but I kind of had to pretend I already chewed it a bit when I took a bite. I did come at 9:45 AM so maybe it'd be crunchier if I came earlier but I did eat it pretty much (5-10min?) right after ordering. I understand that maybe the curry topping is inherently wet but perhaps a solution is to assemble before serving or something, and if labor is a concern to mark up the price further?

reviews.author: Simon Adams
reviews.text: Such a unique offering of baked goods! It was hard to decide what to get, but ended up with an Ube filled Salt Bread and a Milo Double Choc Chip Cookie! There was a line when we arrived at 9am, but it moved quite quickly!

reviews.author: Shreyas Kudari
reviews.text: Love the drinks - thai tea cloud, matcha latte - and collaborations to offer baked goods. Convenient to visit anytime while working in downtown!

cafes.name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
cafes.address: 1415 Folsom St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Erin Le
reviews.text: Went on a Wednesday morning excited to try a coffee shop I haven’t tried before. Not busy at all, was able to find a spot to sit and enjoy my coffee. Service was super friendly and my latte was deeeelish. Atmosphere was meh as it felt kind of cold and not warming. Personally not my favorite vibe, but may suit others. I did like how it was quiet. Otherwise super friendly staff. Not a fan of the kiosks either as you go to coffee shops to interact with the baristas. I really enjoyed the barista that offered to help me instead of me having to use the kiosk. Overall love everything except atmosphere which is why I’m giving it a 2/5. IMO they should get rid of the kiosks.

reviews.author: sam
reviews.text:
I ordered a mocha, and loved the chocolate blend with the espresso, it's a mocha that i would go again to have. Service was good overall, i'm not a big fan of ordering on the screen because it lacks the human contact, it's one way for businesses to save money instead of hiring more employees. I could tell the barista was overwhelmed but his mocha was superb.
The ambience is great, loved the design and seating, I don't go much to cafe that don't have outlets, i understand the business purpose behind it but I won't be going there again to drink and sit unless i want something on the go. I like sitting in a cafe and be able to plug my laptop and finsih my work

reviews.author: Espie Vergara
reviews.text:
the vibe was chill, barista had rlly great skin and pretty smile (sat, feb 28, 2026 at 9:15am).

the coffee i got was the carmal latte and my friend got the ube latte both cold. carmal latte was creamy and light. ube latte was creamy and mild - but could use a lil more flavor imo.

one big problem tho - the lids were shit. kept spilling on our shirts and would fling everywhere, very very very... messy. please look into changing the lids.

overall great experience and coffee, lids were the only problem

reviews.author: Purnendu Kulkarni
reviews.text:
Sextant Coffee Roasters has been serving San Francisco for 14 years and continues to deliver some of the best coffee in the city. Their Connoisseur roast from Tolima, Colombia has bright notes of lime, apple, and honeyed cocoa with a smooth finish.

The owner, originally from Ethiopia, is kind and knows exactly what he is doing. They have three locations across San Francisco, and for the freshest roast, it is best to visit on Wednesday afternoon or Thursday when new batches are ready.

A great spot for anyone who truly appreciates good coffee.

reviews.author: Ryan A
reviews.text:
I found the staff friendly+professional and the drip/batch brew coffee to be very good for the money. I also had the vegan oatmeal+walnut chocolate chip which was tasty.

I recommend the place given its amble size for leisure or work, the nice coffee, and non caffeinated selection.

The cafe has been running for over 10 years, through tumultuous times in SF, so the fact it’s still here is a sign it’s doing right for its customers.

cafes.description: Open space serving coffee & pastries amid exposed brick, beamed ceilings & skylights.

cafes.name: Home Coffee Roasters
cafes.address: 2018 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Lydia Tachkov
reviews.text:
Home Coffee Roasters is one of my favorite coffee shops in the city! As someone who likes more sophisticated sweet drinks, you get some really thought out and unique coffees you can get here.

I would highly recommend the rose latte, they use real rose petals to top off the drink and it's one of my favorite treats! The staff is very friendly, and theres a good amount of tables you can sit to enjoy your drink at.

They do have wifi so I have been able to come here to work on some projects while enjoying their coffee. I also recommend taking your drink down the street to Clement Nursery for a lovely coffee and plant time.

reviews.author: Charlie Noble
reviews.text:
This is by far the best coffee shop in the entire city and I am a coffee snob. Fantastic lattes and niche espresso drinks you can’t find anywhere else. The best avocado toast and baristas that are true artists. I was having a rough day after dropping my kitty Kay off for a surgery and the barista somehow knew exactly how to cheer me up and made a beautiful and adorable kitty cat design with foam on my latte that made my day.

Ps I love buying the large bag of fresh roasted beans so I can enjoy home coffee at home. They even ground it to espresso grind for me to make in my bialletti.

reviews.author: Sonia Garcia
reviews.text: both my friend and i got the birthday cake latte. i didn’t love mine (ordered as less sweet w/ almond milk) but my friends was much better (regular sweetness with whole milk). baristas were incredibly friendly.

reviews.author: Vineel Shah
reviews.text: While all in-house coffee, service and the food are great, the Ethiopian beans have been a little subpar over the last couple of months - I pick up beans every other week as it used to be one of the best light and fruit forward roasts and noticed that the flavor has become bland and prices have shot up. Hopefully they bring the original beans/flavor back

reviews.author: All
reviews.text: Delicious!! Came here because they had more creative flavors, tried the birthday cake & lavander lattes, both were amazing :)

cafes.description: Classic & unique latte drinks, plus creative toast options, served in a contemporary coffee shop.

bars.name: Smuggler's Cove
bars.address: 650 Gough St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Brent Mertes
reviews.text: Have wanted to visit this iconic tiki bar for a long time and we finally made it.  They did not disappoint.  Staff was awesome with knowledge and recommendations and the drinks were amazing.  Went twice in three days to take it all in.  If you can get there when they open, you may get lucky enough to sit at one of the two bars.

reviews.author: The Breakfast King
reviews.text: A solid tiki bar and ambience is great. Good classics, strong spins on new drinks, and a great place to enjoy with a few friends. This was the first place my wife and I went to after we got engaged. POETIC

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.text: Great Tiki Bar in the city, one of my favorites. Massive cocktail menu, knowledgeable bartenders, amazing vibes, super fun ambiance. Not super easy to spot from the street, can also get busy. Every time I come to the bay, I try to make time to come here. Don’t sleep on Smugglers Cove. One of my favorite Tiki Mugs came from here, would be great if they had shirts or coasters, I try to collect them from the tiki bars that I visit all over.

reviews.author: Michele
reviews.text:
I don’t understand what the 1-star reviews talking about. The service was amazing. After telling the barkeeper once my name he called me after my name. Our drinks were done under 10min. Very friendly stuff.
I can recommend to come before the rush like around 9pm. It’s easy to spent there 3-4h.

reviews.author: Kaili Jackson
reviews.text: Very fun place. Definitely my favorite stop for the night. Very crowded, but we got our drinks fast and they were delicious!!

bars.description: Popular bar boasting nautical-themed decor, exotic cocktails & over 700 types of rum.

bars.name: Pacific Cocktail Haven
bars.address: 550 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: J
reviews.text: I stopped in on a Monday evening for the Miracle pop-up and had such a great experience. I hadn’t been to this bar before, but the staff made me feel welcome right away — friendly, attentive, and honestly superior service. The cocktails were creative and really well made, and even though I came alone, I felt completely comfortable and taken care of. Such a fun spot with great energy. I’m so glad I checked it out and would definitely come back!

reviews.author: Dessa R
reviews.text: What a beautiful bar - bumping on a Saturday night. My favorite thing is the beautiful gated front patio seating, on both sides, there’s seating for groups and or small groups. The don’t patio is surrounded by living plant walls, which is gorgeous! Inside is very lovely but depending on what your scene is, it can be too crowded. The 3 of us enjoyed the fresh air and gated front patio. Fun crafted drinks and attentive service.

reviews.author: Seth Ilzy
reviews.text: Stopped by on a Sunday night and it did not disappoint. The place was lively with great music and a fun crowd. The bartenders were friendly, the service was on point, and the drinks were creative and well-made. Definitely a solid spot if you're looking for a high-energy cocktail bar with a great vibe.

reviews.author: Tristan Rogers
reviews.text:
Came in with two others on the recommendation of a friend. Incredible cocktails and fantastic service. Crafty and fun original cocktails and twists on classics with unique ingredients.

Service .... Bomb. Jackson was clutch. We said we were moving on after we cashed out. His recommendations were exactly on point. Cool, professional, and unique spots.

Service in the moment is good, service that continues after the check is paid is memorable. Cannot wait to come back.

reviews.author: Nitika Yadlapalli Yurk
reviews.text: I liked the ambiance of this place, the combination of sitting and standing room plus the outside space was nice. Cocktails were really creative and I liked that wait staff came around to take orders so you're not crowding the bar area.

bars.description: Elevated cocktails, punch bowls, beer & wine poured in a dramatic, stylish space with a lively vibe.

bars.name: The View Lounge
bars.address: 780 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: EddyOneTake
reviews.text:
A MUST visit when you come to San Fran! My wife introduced me and I was blown away by the panoramic view of the city. The atmosphere is high quality and elegant. Open yet still private. The drinks are top tier and I’m so grateful for Giulianna V.

This is sincerely one of the best mangers I’ve ever encountered. She UNDERSTANDS HOSPITALITY and actually cares about the patrons. Giulianna introduced me to the vegan options and went above and beyond to make sure I was aware of all dietary restrictions.

Thank you to Francia as well who was one of the operating chefs on duty. Very knowledgeable and very caring! THIS PLACE IS AMAZING EddyOneTake Approved!!

reviews.author: Michelle M
reviews.text: Came here to grab a drink and some apps before going out, the view is nice. Beautiful windows. We sat and waited for a few minutes before our server approached us.. she took our drink order (never told us her name) and disappeared. I was watching the bartender from our seat and about 20 minutes later when he was making our drinks I saw him not use the correct tequila. Finally the server dropped off our drinks and ran.. didn’t hear me when I asked her about the drinks I guess because she took off. Which was also extremely annoying seeing as how we had been waiting for over 20 minutes now not only for our drinks but to place our order for food. I ended up bringing the cocktails to the bar and the bartender was extremely nice and admitted that was his mistake and he remade the drinks. Which I also had to go get from the bar myself. The server eventually came by to take our food order and left- she made it very known that she was too busy. Once the apps arrived she never touched the table to make sure our food came out correctly. Overall, the food was okay but the service was terrible. The view was nice but not worth the experience or the price.

reviews.author: William To
reviews.text:
What a view! There not much I can say about it except come early because the good tables by the window get taken quickly. We got there 18 min after they opened and we got the last table next to the window.

We just had drinks and no food, just enjoyed the sunset over the concrete jungle. The pictures and videos don’t do it justice, especially on the clear San Francisco day we had.

First time we came here the service was okay but the most recent time, it was top tier. I will always make it a point to come here whenever I’m on the area.

reviews.author: krex
reviews.text: Atmosphere is so private and intimate there are limited seating for the window view but it's so worth it I highly recommend for sunset to go early and just advise to go from the elevator and sit on the left. It is so beautiful

reviews.author: J.C. Lochan
reviews.text: If you're in the mood for a center-city view from a great vantage point and great cocktails, The View is the perfect lounge to go to. The bar, while not the biggest, has a great selection of spirits and the bartenders are quite talented. The drinks here are a but pricey, but you're paying for the view. They do have food that is great, but I personally wouldn't come here for dinner, but instead to relax and hang out with friends for a couple drinks and overlook the city. The atmosphere is upbeat and the service was pretty good for how busy it can get.

bars.description: Massive arched windows showcase city views at this posh lounge serving cocktails & small plates.

bars.name: Blackbird
bars.address: 2124 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Nadine C
reviews.text: Came in at 7pm on Saturday night - lively crowd. Great atmosphere and good service.  Even have a pool table in back. Eclectic design combined with cocktail lounge atmosphere. Drink menu are seasonal with interesting ingredients. The drinks are solid and flavorful.

reviews.author: Luna Anona
reviews.text: One of my favorite spots! Great happy hour and all the drinks are delicious. Friendly bartenders, quick service, good ambiance (lighting that’s moody but doesn’t make you feel like you need an eye exam), and plenty of seating. What’s not to love?

reviews.author: Shivani Kaw
reviews.text: Everything is great, including the old newspaper wall. The cocktails are weak with barely any liquor in it.

reviews.author: Gloria Bakerian
reviews.text: We went to this bar for a business mixer. The atmosphere is incredible. The staff is super friendly. The drinks were spot on. We will definitely go back!

reviews.author: M F
reviews.text:
This Bar is really nice .
Cocktails are good .
I ‘ m not drinking alcohol at moment and the bartender made me his favorite drink ,
a mohito without alcohol .
The bartender who helped me ,
I can tell that he love what he does.
Looking at him making drinks it could be almost hypnotic.
The perfect timing , the way he poor and pick  items , herbs and etc
He know  what he does .
They host meet ups a few times a month
Wich is fun.
Lots of Gay people and the location is good.

bars.description: Rustic-modern bar with lounge seating for creative cocktails, craft beer, happy hours & pool.

bars.name: Bar Iris
bars.address: 2310 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Carolina Jimenez
reviews.text: Highly recommend Bar Iris! They truly serve some of the most delicious cocktails in town. The ambiance and service is amazing. A special thank you to our waiter, Helen, who was incredibly kind and attentive, she took the time to patiently explain the menu and give great recommendations, which really made our experience even better. Can’t wait to come back!

reviews.author: Kenneth Chan
reviews.text:
Hands down the best cocktails in SF and def one of the best in America.

Love the beautifully curated menu and what it had to offer. Heavily influenced by Japanese ingredients hence offering a very unique menu of its own.

Cocktails were extremely well balanced with flavours, aroma and umami. A sip of The friend of a friend of a friend was no different to eating a bag of toasted chips with its long lingering smoked aroma. Insanely good.

Will definitely go back for the Japanese bites or even the Kaisei don.

reviews.author: Kels
reviews.text:
These cocktails were absolutely delicious! The menu has such a fantastic array of creative drinks that I’m excited to try next time I’m in town.

The lighting over the bar was a bit too bright, but the space itself was gorgeous.

reviews.author: Anirudh Sudarshan
reviews.text:
Very creative drinks, not all of them hit because of my personal tastes but I have to say they are very unique

10/10 service, they also customize drinks for you

Will keep going back until I've tried them all

reviews.author: Rainbow Warrior
reviews.text: Food was an absolute disappointment. This is a Michelin star spot….the karrage was cold upon arrival. We told the server and within minutes brought us back the same chicken boiling, from a second fry. Dry and over greased at this point. Did they think we wouldn’t notice? Used to love this spot but was truly disappointed not loving the new menu change.

bars.name: Owl Tree
bars.address: 601 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Gabby Gonzalez
reviews.text: The bartender recommended the lychee martini & the cucumber mint gimlet. Both were absolutely delicious. Music was on point the whole time. I really liked this place! Walked here from the Hilton no problem.

reviews.author: Smittee
reviews.text: Classic SanFran bar atmosphere. Don't pass this one up. The Manhattans are great

reviews.author: Anjalee Vasvani
reviews.text:
The Owl Tree is such a hidden gem in San Francisco that I almost don’t want to share it! We hosted our wedding welcome party in their upstairs space, and it was perfect. Adrianna, the events manager, is wonderful—she’s incredibly responsive and so easy to work with. She and the entire team took such great care of us, and everything ran smoothly from start to finish.

The space itself is private (including a private bathroom), full of SF charm, and incredibly reasonably priced for what you get. Our guests had a great time, and we’re already thinking of excuses to host another event here in the future.

Pro tip: order the cucumber gimlet and the espresso martini—you won’t regret it.

Can’t recommend The Owl Tree enough!

reviews.author: Jodi Simmons
reviews.text: We hosted a corporate event at The Owl Tree, and it was absolutely perfect! Adrianna and the bartending staff were fantastic, making sure our signature cocktails were spot on and our guests were well taken care of. The security team was exceptional, managing our guest list and promptly notifying us of any issues. We also had custom decor, and the Owl Tree team handled all the details with ease, coordinating everything flawlessly so we didn’t have to intervene much. Their dedication to ensuring everything went off without a hitch was truly impressive. Highly recommend The Owl Tree for any event!

reviews.author: Gary Butterfield
reviews.text: Great bar on the corner of Taylor and Post.  Cute little neighborhood bar.  Excellent cocktails (especially the Cucumber Mint Gimlet).  Bartender was on his game and friendly!  Can't wait to go back !

bars.description: Dark-wood bar featuring red carpeting & black leather booths serves up creative cocktails & beers.

bars.name: Trick Dog
bars.address: 3010 20th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Carmen Augusto
reviews.text: Trick Dog is such a fun and creative spot! It’s a themed bar that switches things up every six months — last time I went, it was a poetry theme, and yesterday they had a space  theme. The menu is always super inventive, with cocktail names that match the theme perfectly. Drinks are delicious, the vibe is lively, and the music adds to the energy without being too loud. Definitely one of those places that keeps things fresh and makes you want to come back just to see what they’ll dream up next.

reviews.author: Michael Guynn
reviews.text:
Trick Dog was super fun. The menu was circus themed, with each drink printed on its own postcard. The drinks were extremely creative and interesting.

Our server was funny, playful, and even pulled a tablecloth out from under some glasses at the table next to us when nobody was even watching - I just noticed out of the corner of my eye as it happened. Just a quirky dude being a quirky dude. Love to see it.

The food was great, though the menu is limited, everything we had was delicious. The "Doggy" sauce is the secret that makes everything tick. It's tangy, spicy, mustardy. A great sauce. The burger and dog in particular are great sandwiches. Also the kale salad is comically large.

Overall just a fun, if a bit pricey, spot to have a few drinks and a few good bites.

reviews.author: Andrew
reviews.text:
I really want to love Trick Dog. The rotating menu every six months and the commitment to a full theme is genuinely cool and surprisingly not gimmicky. They do it well. San Francisco needs bars like this places that are creative, ambitious, and willing to take risks. I’ve followed Trick Dog across the last three menus and have tried a majority of the drinks, which is why it’s disappointing to say that, after giving it real thought, the cocktails just don’t land for me.

Yes, the techniques are flashy, and yes, the flavor combinations are unexpected. But more often than not, the end result simply isn’t very good. The drinks tend to prioritize shock value over balance or depth, leaning heavily on “look what we put in this” ingredients rather than crafting something truly delicious. Clever doesn’t always equal satisfying, and here it often feels like the concept outweighs the cocktail.

On top of that, the service has consistently felt a bit pretentious. I get that staff probably hear questions like “what do people order the most?” all the time, but when drinks are this expensive, a little patience goes a long way. An eye-roll or attitude doesn’t make the experience better, especially when guests are just trying to navigate a complicated menu.

It genuinely bums me out to write this, because I like the idea of Trick Dog and root for them every time a new menu drops. I keep hoping the next iteration will be the one that clicks but so far, it hasn’t. I’ll probably still stop by in the future out of curiosity, but if you’re choosing where to spend your money, you’re better off rounding the corner to True Laurel for a more consistently great cocktail experience.

reviews.author: Hilda Liu
reviews.text:
March was seriously packed, and my editing speed turned into turtle mode 🐢🥲
I finally checked out Trick Dog, a spot that’s been sitting on my Google Maps forever!

We arrived pretty late, but it was still packed!
The space has two levels — upstairs looked more like a dining area and closed early. The downstairs bar is much smaller and super packed (a little loud too), so finding a seat wasn’t easy haha.

The menu was SO cute!
We blindly chose High Flyers and (I think) Sword Swallower. Both were really unique and surprisingly smooth! Sword Swallower was refreshing, while High Flyers was sweet but not heavy at all!

We also ordered fries and chicken nuggets — the sauces were amazing and everything was freshly fried, super crispy and juicy!
Highly recommend grabbing a snack with your drink.

This is one of those places where I’d totally come back regularly to try their new menu themes. Loved it — totally recommend!

reviews.author: Jon Leong
reviews.text:
ultimately, a disappointing experience. Hopefully an anomaly.

Context: Towards the end of a week night, group of five of us went there. both food and drinks were ordered.

Cocktails were OK. But there was definitely an issue. One cocktail was almost completely ice. I would say 90% of the glass was ice cubes.

The fries definitely tasted like they were old and stale, and were refried to try and save them. Texture was off, no longer crispy on the outside, fluffy on inside.

Chicken sandwich was OK but felt like it was more a combination of ingredients rather than a cohesive sandwich.

The kale salad, however, was quite good. Copious amounts of kale and cheese. That one is worth getting again.

hostess, bartenders and staff were friendly.

Nice ambience, cool place to hang. As I said before, I hope it was just an anomaly.

bars.description: Trendy hangout in a converted warehouse with an array of craft cocktails & eclectic small plates.

bars.name: Whitechapel
bars.address: 600 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: alice w
reviews.text: Very interesting decor. Waitstaff was slim. Not very attentive. Admittedly I'm not a fan of gin so not their target audience for sure. Happy hour food selection was decent but the chicken wings though tasty, was a bit dry. The flat bread was definitely too dry from being over cooked in the oven as evidenced by the crispy burnt salami "chips". The octopus was so so with some generic flavored taco chips. The most delicious thing was actually the veggie slider. The sauce in that patty bun concoction was quite delicious. All in all a pleasant experience. It might be more interesting if your a gin aficionado.  Cool decor nonetheless.

reviews.author: Davey Freeman
reviews.text: A proper london gin bar in SF with british flavored food to pair. We has the burger and fish and chips, and both were reminiscent of pubs we had eaten at in the UK. Cocktails were great, we don't normally like gin hut the Casablanca was great, and the Reaper was an interesting take on an old fashioned. Recommend makeing reservations on weekends, place is really packed.

reviews.author: Lisa M
reviews.text:
I love gin and had been wanting to try this place for so long. It did not disappoint! Endless gin cocktail selections! I liked shikoku G&T the most. It's citrusy (yuzu) and refreshing.

Interior is subway-station inspired.

reviews.author: Simon Cousineau
reviews.text:
An absolute must try spot for both people exploring classic gin cocktails and those looking for fresh new creations.
The Barrel Gin Old Fashioned is a fantastic take on the classic cocktail - it’s light and orangy but with hints of juniper and citrus!

reviews.author: Asteria Elzea
reviews.text: They have over 730 gins on their list and it's amazing! My boyfriend and I came here while visiting San Francisco from LA because of a recommendation and it was well worth it. I had a flight of "irregular" gins that were made with botanicals outside the norm, like Oriental spices or tomato. Then I had the most perfect gimlet I've ever ordered and amazing clover club. My boyfriend drank some of the original cocktails, including the Phantom Drift and the Spanish G&T. The service was amazing, although we didn't catch our female bartender's name. 10/10 highly recommend, would go again anytime!

bars.description: Gin-focused cocktail lounge with globally inspired eats in expansive, ornate, Victorian-themed digs.

bars.name: Would You Believe
bars.address: 4652 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Francis Chan
reviews.text: Back in my early 20s, this was the go-to spot for $1 shots (shoutout Shirley). Today, a good friend of mine owns it with his family, and the upgrades are impressive. The liquor selection has been significantly improved, the remodel looks great, and there’s a pool table for anyone who enjoys playing. The staff is welcoming and generous, and the overall atmosphere is inviting. I was also grateful for the opportunity to do my first photoshoot here for my friends—shoutout to Would You Believe for making it possible. Definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: E Lee
reviews.text: Great space. Clean and inviting. Comfy booths. Great cocktails for an excellent price. Fun garnishes. Candied hibiscus and ginger. Also good mocktail list. Olympics on the TVs. Great modern jukebox with updated music and music videos. Good alcohol collection.

reviews.author: lira Neal
reviews.text: Customer service is amazing and that are very much a vibe. I had an old-fashioned here which was very well-made and Tasteful. Staff continue to make sure that we were taken care of and cared for. Plus, there’s a pool table.  10 points for Gryffindor.

reviews.author: Z martinez
reviews.text: They had amazing drinks for the holidays, the menu was amazing with Christmas drinks. The bartender was on point “Randy was the man” made every drink on the menu for us. My favorite was rum rum Rudolph.

reviews.author: Hannah Gonzales
reviews.text: Wrong and embarrassing behavior from staff. Go across the street to the Hearth instead. Bar was COMPLETELY empty, not a single person in the establishment, and I got denied by the bartender for my REAL id. He completely and repeatedly denied it even after showing him proof and my hometown friend having a passport with the same birth year, hometown and all. They didn’t even have a black light to confirm that it was real, even at request. What kind of bar doesn’t own a black light?? Unprofessional AND wrong. For context, my id is from Hawaii and vertical with a tiny chip in the corner from nearly 5 years of use. He wouldn’t accept it bc of the chip. Sad that they lost business and were accusing me of committing a crime because I’m 22 and from another state. Educate your bartenders correctly and maybe people will come to your bar.

bars.description: Neighborhood bar drawing a diverse crowd for mixed drinks, shooting pool & music on the jukebox.

bars.name: Bar Gemini
bars.address: 2845 18th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Dyanna Volek
reviews.text: Intimate and cozy bar with an excellent wine and beer selection. The interior is gorgeous but small, dominated by the bar. The walls look like they’re made of green velvet but it’s actually concrete. The service was friendly, and they made to sure to keep our glasses full. The food is from Ernest, which was a treat. The bread is from Rize Up Bakery and highly recommended with the wine.

reviews.author: Antoine Gamberini
reviews.text: The atmosphere of the place is great and the staff really nice. Though I must say I was very disappointed by the quality of the wines proposed (trying the only 3 red on the card by the glass) and of the food. Can't please everyone's taste I guess.

reviews.author: Sohrob H
reviews.text:
🎶 Ambiance & Music:
Bar Gemini truly lives up to its "cool vibes" reputation. The atmosphere is fantastic, making it a great place to relax and socialize. A big part of this is their excellent music selection – they even curate their own Spotify playlists, which is a really nice touch and keeps the energy just right.

🍹 Creative Cocktails (Wine & Beer-Based):
Despite not serving hard liquor, Bar Gemini shines with its incredibly creative cocktail ideas. They've mastered the art of crafting unique and delicious drinks using wine and beer bases. It's truly impressive what they come up with, and it encourages you to try something new and adventurous.

🤝 Service:
The service contributes significantly to the positive experience. The staff are friendly and knowledgeable about their unique drink offerings, making the process of choosing a drink an enjoyable one.

🌟 Overall Experience:
Bar Gemini is a gem for those looking for a place with great music, innovative drinks, and a genuinely cool atmosphere. The creativity in their cocktail menu, combined with the excellent vibes, makes it a must-visit. Highly recommended for a unique night out!

reviews.author: Gabs
reviews.text: Ahhh, I can’t say enough wonderful things about this place! I’m so glad my bestie took me here this weekend. I’ll definitely be coming back—possibly even later this month!

reviews.author: JZ
reviews.text: 4.5/5 A vibey, sleek wine bar. Good for dates or friends. Pretty modern interior and solid wine. I had the chilled Zinfandel. Would recommend

bars.name: Left Door
bars.address: 1905 Union St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Jeremy Yowell
reviews.text: Bumping spot with a buzzing bar and pretty good martinis. The actual space was quite crowded on a Saturday which is understandable, but the hostess was awesome and very helpful with finding us a spot at the bar. I probably wouldn't prioritize coming back, but it was good time.

reviews.author: Linda
reviews.text:
Probably one of my favorite speakeasies in San Francisco. You have to get a reservation to get a table on the weekend, but otherwise you can order at the bar. Cocktails are pricey but amazing- definitely have to try the milk punch and espresso martini. Great for date nights and catching up with friends in an intimate setting.

The food here is also GREAT but I treat it as a tasting menu. The caviar pairings are delightful. Portions are tiny (and I get it, Japanese wagyu is expensive but just know a plate of 6 cubes of wagyu will set you back $85+)

reviews.author: Shauna Graham
reviews.text: I recently discovered a one-of-a-kind speakeasy secretly nestled on Union Ave in the prime Cow Hollow area of San Francisco, and it offered one of the best experiences the city has to offer. On a Tuesday evening, the venue was bustling with professionals, creating an elegant and lively atmosphere. Although reservations are highly recommended due to their popularity, we were fortunate enough to wait in the stylish bar area until we were seated. Our evening unfolded beautifully with expertly crafted top-shelf drinks, delectable appetizers, and ultimately, a sumptuous dinner. Each cocktail was visually stunning and perfectly balanced in flavor, while the appetizers were a delightful prelude to the meal, bursting with unique and magical tastes. A special thanks to Marie, whose attentive and accommodating service ensured our experience was not only exquisite but also comforting and inviting. Her dedication truly set this speakeasy apart. We were so impressed that we decided to stay for dinner and are already planning to return with more friends to share this upscale dining haven. Whether you’re seeking a sophisticated night out or a cozy spot to unwind, this hidden gem on Union Ave is the perfect choice. Thank you to the entire team, especially Marie, for making our night truly special. We can’t wait to return!

reviews.author: Gareth Dwyer
reviews.text: Everything was perfect. Friendly and super knowledgeable bartender who made us some of the best cocktails I've ever had. The food was really special too - fine dining in a relaxed atmosphere, with every detail carefully attended to, and yet with no pretentiousness.

reviews.author: Kels
reviews.text:
Exactly the vibe I was looking for! Visiting from Toronto for work and was looking for the perfect spot for a night cap (or two)—Left Door delivered 👌

Bartender was lovely and accommodating, great cocktails. I’ll definitely be back!

bars.name: Lost Cat Bar & Bites
bars.address: 587 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Dessa R
reviews.text: Super cute and cozy bar, loved the Halloween decor; it was low-key after the show on a Saturday night. Grabbed a few seats at the bar and the bartender was super friendly; the Lycheetini was delicious and spinach dip was perfect to snack on between drinks. It was bumpin later on into the Saturday evening with a random array of audience. Would come back and hang again.

reviews.author: Diana James A.
reviews.text: This place was ok. When I saw it on Instagram it was marketed as more of cat cafe type place.  It has cat decor. The vibes were somber but maybe it was a off night (Sunday evening). I had a lychee tini and it was great!! The garlic fries were soggy. I would come back with a group of friends in the middle of a crawl but I wouldn't make this a destination.

reviews.author: BigFatJerk
reviews.text:
It was my first time here and I had a wonderful experience. I really appreciate the staff all around were attentive and went around checking on customers frequently so that no one had to go to the bar, wait in long lines, etc, the typical stuff that would happen in other bars  and lounges.

I was hoping I would run into a cat here judging from the pictures but I didn't see any Friday night. I love the ambience, the vibe, and the music here. I really appreciate that they kept the music at a good level where you could hear it but also not be drowned out or overpowered by noise so you could actually have conversations and whatnot.

The drinks and food were good. We got the Golden Ticket, Hot Buttered Rum, and I think something with Apple. The garlic fries and chip and dip were phenomenal as well though I wish they included more fries haha since our bowl came half full.

The staff again, are phenomenal and thank you for this wonderful birthday experience.

reviews.author: Mario Dos Campollo
reviews.text:
An excellent Gastro pub in Union Square. They serve delicious drinks and even tastier food in a chic intimate setting. This is a perfect sport to come to after work or before you head out for the night. They have a lot of creative cocktails to choose from so there’s something for everyone. Their bar bite game is up there too, my favorite is the pork sliders but my wife fancy’s the skewers. The staff is friendly and it’s reasonably priced. But most importantly keep your eyes peeled for the lost cats. All in all I’ve been here twice and it hasn’t disappointed yet.
Dos cats and cocktails up 🍹🍸🐈‍⬛ 🐈

reviews.author: Nuhn ya Biz
reviews.text:
Lost Cat Bar & Bites is a charming spot in San Francisco with a unique atmosphere and a solid mix of drinks and bites. We ordered a variety of cocktails, including the Smoked Old Fashioned, The Giving Tree, Lychee-tini, and Night in Shanghai. The cocktails were well-crafted, especially the Smoked Old Fashioned, which had a rich, smoky flavor that stood out. The Night in Shanghai also impressed with its balanced blend of gin, elderflower tonic, and a hint of lavender.

For food, we tried the chicken skewers and the spicy chicken wings. The wings were definitely the highlight—perfectly crispy with just the right amount of heat. However, the chicken skewers were just okay; they didn’t quite have the same punch.

What really made the night memorable was the friendly atmosphere, enhanced by the owner's big, friendly dog that added a delightful, unexpected touch. If you're a dog lover, this place will win you over even more.
Lost Cat Bar & Bites offers a cozy, welcoming vibe with good drinks and some standout bites, though the food can be hit or miss. Definitely worth a visit, especially if you're in the area!

bars.name: Propagation
bars.address: 895 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Christian Cammarota
reviews.text: As a big plant lover, I really wanted to love Propagation — the greenery-filled concept is adorable. But the vibe missed for me. The music was loud and not great, the lighting felt way too dark, and my drink just didn’t hit (especially for $16). Cute idea, but the experience didn’t live up to the aesthetics.

reviews.author: Martin Ong
reviews.text: Brit was our bartender and she did a great job!  Ordered several mocktails and a non-alcoholic rose.  Beautiful atmosphere and would go here regularly if I lived here.

reviews.author: Lilac Inthavong-McEvoy
reviews.text:
Propagation SF is an absolute gem! From the moment you walk in, you’re welcomed into a beautifully curated space that strikes the perfect balance between cozy and modern.

The mocktails here are next level. Creative, complex, and thoughtfully crafted, they’re so good you don’t miss the alcohol for a second. I especially loved the “Call me when it’s over.”

The service deserves its own shoutout. Friendly, attentive, and genuinely passionate about what they do. The staff took time to explain the menu and made sure we felt well taken care of throughout the night.

If you’re looking for a place to unwind, catch up with friends, or just treat yourself to something delicious in a welcoming environment, Propagation SF is it. Can’t wait to come back!

reviews.author: Lorenzo Carmagnola
reviews.text: Propagation is a beautiful bar with spectacular decoration and vibrant staff. It’s a wonderful venue to imbue in fancy cocktails with classy clientele. Very gay atmosphere with consistent changing aesthetics for the seasons. I recommend a stop in to see for yourself. Lovely place for a drink and link. 😉😜

reviews.author: Deborah Morng
reviews.text: When you want a place to hang out, catch up or unwind, this place is awesome! The ambience inside is so peaceful looking with all the live plants. You can pretend you are sitting outside in a garden with a cold delicious drink in your hand. You won't be disappointed with the custom cocktail menu. All the drinks are delicious and masterfully crafted. The service is great from the front door, behind the bar and at your seat. The owners are so friendly, they make you feel so welcomed and relaxed. I have never gone to a bar soo inviting. I will definitely be visiting again and if I were you I wouldn't pass up this gem.

bars.description: Inventive signature cocktails presented in a bohemian, plant-filled space with a patio.

bars.name: Moongate Lounge
bars.address: 28 Waverly Pl, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Cynthia
reviews.text: Stunning and spacious interior, great espresso martini, and loved the DJs they brought in the night I went! Wasn’t super busy on a Thursday night. Love that it’s more of a cushy/chill vibe.

reviews.author: Dianne Namgung
reviews.text:
We both ordered very spirit forward cocktails but they were excellent.
Fun cool vibes inside.
The oysters were delicious. We also did the salt and pepper squid. It was a bit on salty side which got me drinking the cocktail a bit faster than I should have

reviews.author: Sonya Hu
reviews.text:
Fun and vibey cocktail bar in the heart of Chinatown. The space is beautiful, perfect for either a small friend hang-out or a date. They have several cute little couches scattered throughout, as well as cozy booths for a more intimate, walled off ambiance. For walk-ins, they also have a number of standing cocktail tables around the bar and in the back by the live DJ. What really makes the ambiance special, though, is the giant moon gate in the back of the bar. It's absolutely beautiful and perfectly combines traditional Asian elements with the modern neon lighting, DJ set, and disco ball. The music is upbeat and just loud enough to set the mood, but the space is also open / quiet enough that it's easy to hold a good conversation without straining your ears.

The drinks are also fantastic, with a great mix of classic cocktails and house specials. Their old fashioned was tasty, as well as their house grain rain, which was delightfully fruity and bubbly, but well-balanced and not overly sweet.

reviews.author: Aman
reviews.text:
The place is really cool, it has a great vibe! The staff however can be quite rude. The bartenders on the other hand were friendly.
The cocktails were definitely on the pricier side but were really good. The little bits of food to munch on while drinking were really great!

reviews.author: Ananya Mahesh
reviews.text: Very interesting drinks + a lot of them are simply not vegan lol. If you’re looking for classic cocktail this may not be the place! they do have fun drinks though (mine was 99% ice as pictured). I got the Start of Spring and would give it a 5.7/10. The service was alight, they seemed very busy (it was quite busy hahah). Overall cool place and vibey music There is a bathroom.

bars.description: The lavish lounge above Mister Jiu’s serves Cal-Chinese bites & cocktails by Brandon Jew & company.

bars.name: ROOFTOP 25
bars.address: 25 Lusk St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Lori Hern
reviews.text:
This was our second attempt visiting Rooftop 25, and it was absolutely worth the wait. Our first visit didn’t go as planned due to a miscommunication about a block-out date for a private event. However, the owner went above and beyond to make things right—and truly delivered on that promise.

When we finally came in, I was blown away by the atmosphere. The space felt intimate and private, and the view was nothing short of stunning. The staff was incredibly attentive and genuinely friendly, which made the whole experience even better.

I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of the food. We ordered the pizza and the salmon—both were full of flavor and cooked to perfection. The drinks were also on point—crafty, refreshing, and well-balanced.

Rooftop 25 has officially earned a spot on our list of go-to places. Can’t wait to come back again soon!

reviews.author: Anthony Jefferson
reviews.text:
In baseball terms, this eatery after a 5 year wait could be elevated into SF food scenes HOF. If they continue to hang the hat on pizzas it could be a much longer wait. I ordered the Margarita. Allow me to disclose I am a chef, a pizzaiolo, and reluctant pizza eater personally.
Ask me if I ate it? I did, only because I can't afford to waste my money and I deplore food waste.

reviews.author: Davian Bryan
reviews.text:
I'll start with the positive.  Nice ambiance, good music, cool layout, and interesting looking drinks (I didn't have any).  Also, the Short Rib Calzone was easily the best thing I had, and one of the better items I've had that week.

That said, this place shouldn't be serving steak.  It was over priced, over-cooked and served along side room temperature to cold vegetables. My date's dish (we both ordered the rib eye) came out room temperature at best.

If the restaurant is reading this, I humbly suggest you remove Steak from your menu. You're not good at it, it's over priced and I'm sure that I'm not the only one severely disappointed by the dish.

Stick to the pizza and calzones here, they are pretty darned good!

The restaurant should have comped one of the rib eye dishes, but they didn't. Probably should have not tipped to drive home the point.

Could potentially return for a Calzone and that flavorful marinara sauce, but still extremely disappointed by frankly poorly executed rib eye dishes.

reviews.author: Lawrence Boesch
reviews.text: Nice, convenient location between parking and Oracle Park 🥹Recommend the linguine with clams (only $22). Pizza is unique (interesting veggies and lots of cheese). They have a great drink menu, nonalcoholic, soda, beers, wine & cocktails.

reviews.author: Cz juarez
reviews.text: last time I visited was a while back before the roof top opened. Visited again at the rooftop. Casual dining experience, awesome on a sunny day.  I went with a group coworkers, we ordered food to share. Everything came pretty fast.  Great for sharing.  Vegetarian  options avaliable.

bars.description: Dim lighting sets the mood at this trendy spot with exposed-wood beams & creative American cuisine.

bars.name: Li Po Cocktail Lounge
bars.address: 916 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Peter Farejowicz
reviews.text: Li Po Cocktail Lounge is exactly what you would expect from a dive bar in Chinatown, and that is part of its charm. The interior is a little worn but it works perfectly for a casual, laid back drink with friends. The Chinese Mai Tai is the signature drink here and definitely worth ordering. It is popular for a reason, with a smooth and tasty flavor, though for me personally it was not too strong as some other reviews mentioned. Service was quick and the atmosphere was easygoing. It is a fun stop if you are in the area and want something low key.

reviews.author: Ben Park
reviews.text:
The Chinese Mai Tai is as potent as advertised. Whether it’s a good cocktail or not is questionable. You can probably make a better drink by mixing some liquor and liqueurs at home.

This place has a unique vibe though. It’s worth visiting at least once for the experience.

reviews.author: chezburgersub
reviews.text: I was intent on stopping by Li Po for a Mai Tai before flying out and really enjoyed my time here. It was quite busy for a Thursday evening, but the service was so fast that I had no trouble getting a drink. As soon as I reached the bar, I was asked if I wanted a Mai Tai, which was an easy "yes". At $12 a pop, these are a great value for the city. They are blended and actually reminded me of the tiki drinks back home in New England. While the drink could have benefited with some ice, the price and the friendly staff made it a non-issue. Just a heads-up for others that there is a $10 credit card minimum, so be prepared to grab a second round or bring cash. It was a great atmosphere to wrap up my trip.

reviews.author: Tracy Scott
reviews.text: Sometimes you get very lucky while traveling. My sons and I decided to get a drink, randomly at Li Po. We sat next to a man that frequents Li Po when he is in town. He made us aware of Anthony Bourdain featuring it on his show.  I could tell right away why he was taken by this bar. You get a retro vibe right away. The kind of bar that makes you feel comfortable. And MISS CONNIE!! 🔥 Her quick wit, style, and no-nonsense bar management made me her biggest fan. She will hit you with a sense of humor that is honestly missing in so many modern bars. Of course the Chinese Mai Tai is amazing. Go for the drinks, stay for the experience!

reviews.author: Sara Lynn
reviews.text: Great little dive bar with unbeatable prices. Highly recommend  stop in for a quick drink. Heard the mai tai were also good, beer is $6 tho. Great service- bar tender 👌👌 Can’t pass up!

bars.description: Long-standing no-pretenses Chinatown bar attracting a diverse crowd with signature Chinese mai tais.

bars.name: TONGA ROOM & HURRICANE BAR
bars.address: 950 Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Sam
reviews.text: This was on my list of iconic San Francisco places to visit and I finally got a chance to go. It is definitely a pricey endeavor for what you get, but it's San Francisco and a tourist spot. They have a band playing on a floating platform in the pool and there's is occasional man-made rain to give you the tropical vibes. The drinks were good and the food was good. This was fun, but definitely seemed outdated to me. Come check it out to see what you think of this blast from the place bar.

reviews.author: Bay Walker
reviews.text: Five of us had a reservation and were seated promptly. We had great service throughout the meal and our food was quite good! Of course, the very best is the amazing decor (with rain!) and vibe of this historic restaurant. Three of us enjoyed the Kahlúa pork and one of us had a curry seafood dish that they enjoyed. A unique San Francisco experience that everyone should have at least once.

reviews.author: Wendy Ng
reviews.text: Celebrated my husband's 44th birthday there.  Hostess sat us right by the pool next to the band on the boat.  Atmosphere is great,  transformed us to the islands with great tiki cocktails.  Food was just mediocre.  We ordered the pupu platter appetizer:  short ribs were super thin (maybe 1/8th of an inch) 6 pieces total.  The calamari was greasy, but the aioli was good, tangy and cuts the heaviness.  The plantain chips were thin and drowning in its own sauce.  The most tasty were the pork bites.  For entree we had the seafood curry and the kalua pork.  Seafood was cooked very well,  not overcooked.  There were shrimps, prawns,  scallops and calamari.  However the curry was so light and thin,  you can barely taste it.  If I didn't know it was a curry, I would've thought it's just a stir fry. The kalua pork was plentiful however it tasted more like pulled pork.  There was a scoop of white rice and a scoop of Mac salad. Mac salad was under seasoned.  Overall disappointing meal especially for a 4 or 5 start hotel.  Cocktails were strong but delicious.  Our waiter was very attentive.  The part that made this experience great was the band.  Excellent music,  great voices and just s fun group.

reviews.author: Michael Peterman
reviews.text: We almost didn't get to experience the Tonga Room as I forgot to make reservations early.  Luckily, we were able to get a table one night while we were in town.  So, if you are thinking about going make sure and make your reservations as soon as you can.   The drinks were top notch and the entertainment was so good.  We stayed for all 3 sets as we celebrated our 11th anniversary.  Highly recommend the Kalua Pork and the Seafood Coconut Curry.  We had a wonderful time and memories that will last as a lifetime.

reviews.author: Larry Mannolini
reviews.text: What an amazing atmosphere.  Best indoor tiki bar ever.  Service was impeccable.  The drinks were super potent.  Go for an off-the-menu Singapore Sling (Jimmy Buffett was right...IYKYK!)  The seafood coconut curry was flavorful and spicy...add some steamed rice.  The pork was juicy and full of flavor.  Go take a walk back in Tiki time!  Reservations highly encouraged.

bars.description: Sprawling tiki-themed lounge in the Fairmont Hotel featuring Pacific Rim cuisine & umbrella drinks.

bars.name: Holy Water
bars.address: 309 Cortland Ave, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Agustin Farias
reviews.text: Come here and give your prayers to Holy Church. 🙏😜. Great neighborhood bar. Great atmosphere. Great selection of drinks with religious inspired names. Great place for a Friday happy hour after work with good company 😉

reviews.author: Sean McGinnis
reviews.text:
Stopped into Holy Water in San Francisco to meet a friend before dinner, and it was perfect. We grabbed seats at the bar and could easily catch up — not loud at all, just a relaxed neighborhood vibe. It definitely seems like a local favorite; as we were heading out, we passed at least four dogs, and a couple even had their own dog beds.

I didn’t check out the beer list (not even sure if they have one), but the draft selection had exactly what I was looking for. Great spot, great atmosphere. I’ll definitely be back the next time I’m in town.

reviews.author: Kathleen M.
reviews.text: A favorite always! I used to live in the neighborhood but will always come back and hang at Holy Water. They put so much thought into their cocktails, tap list, decor, menus, glassware — it’s just a whole moody vibe and they continue to get better as time goes on.

reviews.author: Gregg Rudes
reviews.text:
Headline: I found Jesus in the bar tonight and it was good.

No it was not some long haired patron but instead this awesome themed bar in San Francisco. This place had a nice chill vibe despite being crazy busy. We were still able to find some decent seats and bask in the glow of good old JC. The place almost had a hall in the wall vibe with the twinkle lights but still boasted an impressive selection of drinks.

Despite how busy it was, the staff was super friendly and bartenders were moving fast through the requests. Sometime at a bar you can feel ignored but it was not like that here. Cellphone signal is not great but they have wifi inside as well.

reviews.author: A.C. Antonelli
reviews.text: Fall on your knees! O hear the angel voices! Or, you know, pull up a stool and order a drink. An exceptional cocktail can feel like a religious experience, and you will certainly have one here. Phenomenal cocktails, great craft beer selection, highly skilled and friendly staff, and just a casual amount of religious decor. Holy Water is up there with the best.

bars.description: Relaxed tavern pouring classic cocktails & craft beers in a rustic space with religious decor.

bars.name: Casements Bar
bars.address: 2351 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Rick Faulconer
reviews.text:
My only real complaint was that the music was too loud at times. The bartender was an  amazing mixologist. The Irish food , outstanding. I enjoyed the beer, Pliny on tap!
The server went out of his way to accommodate us.

reviews.author: MaryGayle T
reviews.text: Surprisingly great food, Irish stew and fish & chips were amazing. Fantastic patio area for nice days.

reviews.author: Jane
reviews.text: Went on a Friday night. Super busy but the atmosphere is great and their drinks are all so good! Their Peas for Bees was our favorite and it’s a very unique drink you cannot find elsewhere🧡

reviews.author: Venice M
reviews.text:
Pricing: $12-15 for a cocktail

If you’re in the Mission area and nothing else catches your eye, this is the bar famous for whiskeys 🥃 that’s always there for you. The ambiance is intimate with beautiful brick walls. There’s also a backyard and patio area, which would be a great spot to hang out with friends on a sunny day.

The drinks here are average—nothing fancy, but they do have a vast whiskey selection. Food-wise, the French onion soup was a standout: hearty, flavorful, and surprisingly delicious. However, some other dishes were a bit too salty, and the fries got soggy pretty quickly.

The bartender was super friendly and helpful, like a neighbor. While it’s not a place we’d rush back to, it’s a good option if you’re looking for a laid-back spot in the neighborhood.

reviews.author: Vijaylaxsmi Kesavan
reviews.text: Everything vibes from service to music and ambience! Ordered a broad range of items and everything was superb except don't ever get the Guinness stew. Classic case of brown sauce add 300 ml of water and (I counted) 3 pieces of beef. Multiple people in our group made the same comment. I'm sad I was the one who got the stew but yay for ordering family style!!! Definitely coming back because it truly is an awesome spot in every other way.

bars.description: Exposed brick & plank floors outfit this cozy cocktail den with an Irish flair & upscale bar snacks.

bars.name: Novela
bars.address: 662 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: Dartise Jones
reviews.text:
This place had a great Halloween atmosphere! Nice vibes and decent music.

Serena clocked in and immediately assisted me with getting drinks, she was the star of the show and had a great costume. Great service!

I had the chicken slider and it was a good 8/10!
The Cesar salad was a 3/10 pretty basic

Drinks were decent

reviews.author: Andrew Miller
reviews.text:
Novela is such a great spot. The library-themed decor is super cozy and gives the bar a really intimate, laid-back vibe—perfect for a chill drink that can easily turn into a longer night. It feels stylish without trying too hard.

The drinks are amazing, especially the punch-on-tap flight. Fun flavors, generous pours, and definitely worth sharing. Easily one of my favorite bars in SF and a place I’d always come back to.

reviews.author: Adrian Martin
reviews.text:
Novella hosted a party for our group. The staff was amazing! Each member of their team went above and beyond and many of our guests commented on how great the bartenders and waiters were during our event.

Very friendly and a joy to work with. Great food and drinks!!

reviews.author: Laura Yaeger
reviews.text: Booked a private event at Novela and what an event it was!!!!  It was perfect!   From the quote to the day of the party the manager was attentive and responsive   The food, drinks, and all the staff were amazing!!  The space allows for the perfect vibe and flow. All the guests loved it!  Book your next event here or stop by for a cocktail or punch.

reviews.author: Kiran Singh
reviews.text: Love the library themed bar! Such a fun ambiance, customer service is great, drinks were delicious just small portions. Not a fan of the nachos, tasted like something you get from a baseball stadium. After all it’s bar food, but the quality of the food wasn’t reflective of the price. Other than that will definitely be back for drinks, cheers!

bars.description: A stylish, literary-themed bar with drinks named after characters, punch on tap & book-lined walls.

bars.name: ZOË Cocktail Bar & Bites
bars.address: 579 Howard St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: Rafael DeAusen
reviews.text:
Came in on a Thursday and lucky me it was karaoke night baby! Lol

We had a gig at Saluhall and went on a walk and stumbled into their spot.

Food. Drinks. Karaoke.

Got two seats at the bar but there's tables outside and in.

We did two sets of three tacos - I almost went for 10 for $55 lol.

Added sliders instead and I did the monthly cocktail (08/25) which was scotch based and my friend did a spicy mezcal margarita which she really enjoyed.

I sang "Sex on Fire" & a few others joined it. I can imagine it getting really busy OR it would be a great spot to bring your karaoke crew to. I personally enjoy open style karaoke vs grabbing a room.

If I'm not mistaken the sign in the restroom said $3 tacos but not sure if that was for happy hour? We were charged $18/3 (regular menu price)

A few tables had bottles of wine and it was very open and clean and the high ceilings make it spacious and comfortable.

Service was fast and friendly.

Definitely on our list of places to return to.

reviews.author: Lisa Velazquez
reviews.text: If you want bomb food - highly recommend the protein bowl with carnitas and ask for a side of their salsa!  The music and atmosphere is a total fun vibe!  But the best is Keley the bad ass bar tender whom always has a fun energetic personality!  I come back just for his awesome customer service!  I watch him interact with other customers too and he is just amazing!  I’ll be back soon and plan to tell all my coworkers!!

reviews.author: Do Kim
reviews.text:
ZOE is hands down one of my favorite spots in the city. From the moment you walk in, the vibe is just right — warm, inviting, and full of good energy. The food (chicken quesadilla tacos, and sliders are my favorites) and drinks are consistently delicious, and the staff couldn’t be friendlier or more accommodating.

I’ve hosted events here and each time the space proved to be absolutely perfect — stylish, comfortable, and effortlessly versatile.

I recently spent an evening catching up with friends in a relaxed setting, then came back later that night to dance the night away with a down-to-earth DJ spinning great tracks. It's that kind of place where you can ease into the night and then turn it up when the music starts flowing.

I’ve also heard amazing things about their Thursday Karaoke nights, and it’s been on my list — definitely making that happen soon!

If you’re looking for a place with great food, top-notch drinks, and an unbeatable atmosphere, ZOE is it. Can’t wait to be back again soon!

reviews.author: Carlos Tello
reviews.text:
We hosted a birthday party at ZOË Cocktail Bar & Bites and had an amazing experience. The service was excellent, and everything ran smoothly.

Kelly was a total rockstar—he checked on us frequently and made sure we had everything we needed the entire time. Highly recommend for hosting a birthday or special celebration!

reviews.author: Nayab Syed
reviews.text: Absolutely Amazing Experience at ZOE Cocktail Bar & Bites!! I recently hosted my birthday get-together at Zoe’s Cocktail & Bar on Howard Street, and I couldn’t have asked for a better experience! From the moment we walked in, the service was impeccable. The staff went above and beyond to make sure my friends and I were taken care of the entire night. Not only were the drinks expertly crafted, but the atmosphere was also perfect—great music, a lively yet intimate vibe, and a truly welcoming energy. The ownership is incredible; they genuinely care about their guests and ensure that everyone has a fantastic time. It’s rare to find a place where hospitality feels this genuine. If you’re looking for a spot in San Francisco that delivers top-notch service, amazing cocktails, and an unforgettable experience, Zoe’s Cocktail & Bar is the place to be. I’ll definitely be back!

bars.name: Would You Believe
bars.address: 4652 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Francis Chan
reviews.text: Back in my early 20s, this was the go-to spot for $1 shots (shoutout Shirley). Today, a good friend of mine owns it with his family, and the upgrades are impressive. The liquor selection has been significantly improved, the remodel looks great, and there’s a pool table for anyone who enjoys playing. The staff is welcoming and generous, and the overall atmosphere is inviting. I was also grateful for the opportunity to do my first photoshoot here for my friends—shoutout to Would You Believe for making it possible. Definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: E Lee
reviews.text: Great space. Clean and inviting. Comfy booths. Great cocktails for an excellent price. Fun garnishes. Candied hibiscus and ginger. Also good mocktail list. Olympics on the TVs. Great modern jukebox with updated music and music videos. Good alcohol collection.

reviews.author: lira Neal
reviews.text: Customer service is amazing and that are very much a vibe. I had an old-fashioned here which was very well-made and Tasteful. Staff continue to make sure that we were taken care of and cared for. Plus, there’s a pool table.  10 points for Gryffindor.

reviews.author: Z martinez
reviews.text: They had amazing drinks for the holidays, the menu was amazing with Christmas drinks. The bartender was on point “Randy was the man” made every drink on the menu for us. My favorite was rum rum Rudolph.

reviews.author: Hannah Gonzales
reviews.text: Wrong and embarrassing behavior from staff. Go across the street to the Hearth instead. Bar was COMPLETELY empty, not a single person in the establishment, and I got denied by the bartender for my REAL id. He completely and repeatedly denied it even after showing him proof and my hometown friend having a passport with the same birth year, hometown and all. They didn’t even have a black light to confirm that it was real, even at request. What kind of bar doesn’t own a black light?? Unprofessional AND wrong.
