For context, my id is from Hawaii and vertical with a tiny chip in the corner from nearly 5 years of use. He wouldn’t accept it bc of the chip. Sad that they lost business and were accusing me of committing a crime because I’m 22 and from another state. Educate your bartenders correctly and maybe people will come to your bar.

bars.description: Neighborhood bar drawing a diverse crowd for mixed drinks, shooting pool & music on the jukebox.

bars.name: Beso Mae Cocktail Lounge
bars.address: 1121 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Denisse
reviews.text: Cute new place with great music! The Beso mae and candy shots are 100% worth it. The ceviche and handrolls were good. But the pizza was soggy in the middle. And i would not get the Mai Tia again. Workers were super friendly and make your experience worth it! Great place to meet up with friends.

reviews.author: Amanda Moore
reviews.text: The bespoke cocktail experience was amazing. With a little information, our mixologist made the best drinks ever. Quiet enough to have a conversation too. Thanks for a great night!

reviews.author: Kelly N
reviews.text: Great new bar with tasty cocktails and a great atmosphere. Would highly recommend the beso Mae cocktail and the horchata inspired shots. Definitely worth checking out if you’re in the area and looking for a good drink!

reviews.author: Gino R.
reviews.text: Stepping into Beso Mae feels like finding a well-kept secret. The space is intimate and inviting with stylish touches that set the perfect mood with velvet seating, and a playlist that wraps you in the vibe. Every drink feels like it was made with intention and finesse. The bartenders are not only skilled but also incredibly warm and welcoming. Devon, in particular, made our night with exceptional service and great conversation. While there’s no food menu, the drinks more than make up for it. Perfect spot for a date night or a classy catch up with friends. A must visit for anyone looking to elevate their evening in San Francisco.

reviews.author: ariana delima
reviews.text: If you’re looking for a venue for a private event, this is it.

bars.name: The Alembic
bars.address: 1725 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Kyla W
reviews.text: My favourite bar in the city. Incredible cocktails with inspired and surprising flavors and great seasonal offerings means that you will always have something delicious and curious to try! Amazing curated beer selection also and great scratch-made food. I love everything on their menu: the burger, the house chips, scotch egg, the wings, the pickles, the olives, the pate, the fries, the mussels etc etc all great! Check out the monday night classes too, super fun for a date night or solo adventure (just… dont plan anything important on tuesday morning!!)

reviews.author: Mike D
reviews.text:
Stopped in on a whim. Cool spot. Mind boggling selection of spirits.
Nicely crafted cocktail. Pleasant staff.
Food was good though we only sample a few items.
Would definitely go back if in the area again.

reviews.author: Mark Buckley
reviews.text: All drinks or food is ordered at the bar. No table service. Great looking bar with many liquor options. Could have been a bit more personable to the newcomer. Might be more of a “locals bar”.

reviews.author: Bianca Fredborg Madsen
reviews.text: Hands down the best place to go out in the upper Haight. Amazing cocktails, very good food and lovely interior and ambiance. They serve the best brownie with vanilla ice cream I have ever had.

reviews.author: Emilie Sark
reviews.text:
love the cocktails here. tried the ‘good soup’ cocktail which is inspired by tom yum soup and it was great!

when you sit in the park let they serve everything in plastic cups. Obviously doesn’t change the flavor but the cocktails just didn’t feel as elevated. Totally get why they don’t want glassware out there though.

The polenta fries are a MUST order. The buffalo wings were also great, love their bleu cheese.

A great spot to check out on haight street for craft cocktails and bites!

bars.description: Classic handcrafted cocktails & an inventive bar food menu in a dimly lit, rustic space with booths.

bars.name: Trick Dog
bars.address: 3010 20th St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Carmen Augusto
reviews.text: Trick Dog is such a fun and creative spot! It’s a themed bar that switches things up every six months — last time I went, it was a poetry theme, and yesterday they had a space  theme. The menu is always super inventive, with cocktail names that match the theme perfectly. Drinks are delicious, the vibe is lively, and the music adds to the energy without being too loud. Definitely one of those places that keeps things fresh and makes you want to come back just to see what they’ll dream up next.

reviews.author: Michael Guynn
reviews.text:
Trick Dog was super fun. The menu was circus themed, with each drink printed on its own postcard. The drinks were extremely creative and interesting.

Our server was funny, playful, and even pulled a tablecloth out from under some glasses at the table next to us when nobody was even watching - I just noticed out of the corner of my eye as it happened. Just a quirky dude being a quirky dude. Love to see it.

The food was great, though the menu is limited, everything we had was delicious. The "Doggy" sauce is the secret that makes everything tick. It's tangy, spicy, mustardy. A great sauce. The burger and dog in particular are great sandwiches. Also the kale salad is comically large.

Overall just a fun, if a bit pricey, spot to have a few drinks and a few good bites.

reviews.author: Andrew
reviews.text:
I really want to love Trick Dog. The rotating menu every six months and the commitment to a full theme is genuinely cool and surprisingly not gimmicky. They do it well. San Francisco needs bars like this places that are creative, ambitious, and willing to take risks. I’ve followed Trick Dog across the last three menus and have tried a majority of the drinks, which is why it’s disappointing to say that, after giving it real thought, the cocktails just don’t land for me.

Yes, the techniques are flashy, and yes, the flavor combinations are unexpected. But more often than not, the end result simply isn’t very good. The drinks tend to prioritize shock value over balance or depth, leaning heavily on “look what we put in this” ingredients rather than crafting something truly delicious. Clever doesn’t always equal satisfying, and here it often feels like the concept outweighs the cocktail.

On top of that, the service has consistently felt a bit pretentious. I get that staff probably hear questions like “what do people order the most?” all the time, but when drinks are this expensive, a little patience goes a long way. An eye-roll or attitude doesn’t make the experience better, especially when guests are just trying to navigate a complicated menu.

It genuinely bums me out to write this, because I like the idea of Trick Dog and root for them every time a new menu drops. I keep hoping the next iteration will be the one that clicks but so far, it hasn’t. I’ll probably still stop by in the future out of curiosity, but if you’re choosing where to spend your money, you’re better off rounding the corner to True Laurel for a more consistently great cocktail experience.

reviews.author: Hilda Liu
reviews.text:
March was seriously packed, and my editing speed turned into turtle mode 🐢🥲
I finally checked out Trick Dog, a spot that’s been sitting on my Google Maps forever!

We arrived pretty late, but it was still packed!
The space has two levels — upstairs looked more like a dining area and closed early. The downstairs bar is much smaller and super packed (a little loud too), so finding a seat wasn’t easy haha.

The menu was SO cute!
We blindly chose High Flyers and (I think) Sword Swallower. Both were really unique and surprisingly smooth! Sword Swallower was refreshing, while High Flyers was sweet but not heavy at all!

We also ordered fries and chicken nuggets — the sauces were amazing and everything was freshly fried, super crispy and juicy!
Highly recommend grabbing a snack with your drink.

This is one of those places where I’d totally come back regularly to try their new menu themes. Loved it — totally recommend!

reviews.author: Jon Leong
reviews.text:
ultimately, a disappointing experience. Hopefully an anomaly.

Context: Towards the end of a week night, group of five of us went there. both food and drinks were ordered.

Cocktails were OK. But there was definitely an issue. One cocktail was almost completely ice. I would say 90% of the glass was ice cubes.

The fries definitely tasted like they were old and stale, and were refried to try and save them. Texture was off, no longer crispy on the outside, fluffy on inside.

Chicken sandwich was OK but felt like it was more a combination of ingredients rather than a cohesive sandwich.

The kale salad, however, was quite good. Copious amounts of kale and cheese. That one is worth getting again.

hostess, bartenders and staff were friendly.

Nice ambience, cool place to hang. As I said before, I hope it was just an anomaly.

bars.description: Trendy hangout in a converted warehouse with an array of craft cocktails & eclectic small plates.

bars.name: Smuggler's Cove
bars.address: 650 Gough St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Brent Mertes
reviews.text: Have wanted to visit this iconic tiki bar for a long time and we finally made it.  They did not disappoint.  Staff was awesome with knowledge and recommendations and the drinks were amazing.  Went twice in three days to take it all in.  If you can get there when they open, you may get lucky enough to sit at one of the two bars.

reviews.author: The Breakfast King
reviews.text: A solid tiki bar and ambience is great. Good classics, strong spins on new drinks, and a great place to enjoy with a few friends. This was the first place my wife and I went to after we got engaged. POETIC

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.text: Great Tiki Bar in the city, one of my favorites. Massive cocktail menu, knowledgeable bartenders, amazing vibes, super fun ambiance. Not super easy to spot from the street, can also get busy. Every time I come to the bay, I try to make time to come here. Don’t sleep on Smugglers Cove. One of my favorite Tiki Mugs came from here, would be great if they had shirts or coasters, I try to collect them from the tiki bars that I visit all over.

reviews.author: Michele
reviews.text:
I don’t understand what the 1-star reviews talking about. The service was amazing. After telling the barkeeper once my name he called me after my name. Our drinks were done under 10min. Very friendly stuff.
I can recommend to come before the rush like around 9pm. It’s easy to spent there 3-4h.

reviews.author: Kaili Jackson
reviews.text: Very fun place. Definitely my favorite stop for the night. Very crowded, but we got our drinks fast and they were delicious!!

bars.description: Popular bar boasting nautical-themed decor, exotic cocktails & over 700 types of rum.

bars.name: Local Edition
bars.address: 691 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: mars gemini
reviews.text:
Halloween evening 2025. There were plenty of seats when we arrived at 9:30pm then it was packed within 30-40 mins!
Dimmed lighting, live band playing, excellent vibes for dates or to hang out. There are different types of typewriters on the walls, counters, and tables.
Pricing range from $13-$20 per cocktail. The espresso martini was splendid 😘 highly recommend!

reviews.author: Anya Cheng
reviews.text: They advertised music performance , which was good, but the seat was so far and in an area that there was no way that you could see the stage. I wish we were informed about that before going when we made the reservation as we were there for the show.

reviews.author: Hanabel
reviews.text: The vibes are good, band was good and the cocktails are yummy. There’s also soo much seating

reviews.author: Christopher Delance
reviews.text: This place isn't quite a speakeasy, but it does have the vibes of one. The reservations were a bit hard to get, so plan ahead. The live music was a massive boost to the ambiance. The espresso martini was really good but the Old  Fashioned was rather disappointing. There aren't many thrills about this location, but it is pretty nice. If I travel here again, I will give another shot.

reviews.author: Joe Marxkors
reviews.text: This place is such a vibe, I absolutely love the live jazz here. Super cozy, dark room, plenty of seating. The cocktails are top notch. This place is a must go if you are in the area!

bars.description: An extensive cocktail menu is offered at this underground lounge with a vintage newspaper theme.

bars.name: Persona
bars.address: 685 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Yvonne S
reviews.text: This bar is highly recommended on Google Maps and by a local friend. The vibe is quiet on a Wednesday night. We went in quite early, around 6:30PM. It's dimly lit inside and a nice atmosphere. No music, just people talking. When we got in, the place was filled with office workers. I got the fruity Passion Palace. It was a fruity passion fruit cocktail. It was very nicely made, delicious in a complex and expensive way, and garnished with beautiful flowers. I recommend coming here on Friday or the weekend to get a different/more fun vibe.

reviews.author: Miranda Nild
reviews.text:
l've been to Persona several times! It's one of my favorite spots in SF, they have an awesome upscale vibe with some amazing drinks.
Marcus has been my bartender every time and can always rely on him to make wonderful recomendations

reviews.author: Jacob Randall
reviews.text:
I always have a great night at Persona! From the moment you walk in, the vibe is on point: stylish but still relaxed, with a great crowd and live music that adds to the atmosphere without overpowering it.

The drinks were fantastic, seriously well-balanced and beautifully presented, but what really made the experience stand out was the service. Marcus, our bartender, was incredible! He was friendly, attentive, and clearly knows his craft. He made thoughtful recommendations based on what we liked, checked in at the right times, and kept the energy upbeat all night. You can tell he genuinely cares about making sure guests have a good experience.

It’s rare to find a spot where both the drinks and the service are consistently excellent, but Persona absolutely delivers thanks to bartenders like Marcus. I’ll definitely be back and hoping to see him there!

reviews.author: Mckenzie Friel
reviews.text:
One of my favorite spots in the city! I’d honestly come here just for the matcha drink alone, it’s the best. Their popcorn is also a fave I never skip.

The vibe here is solid…great energy, stylish atmosphere, and amazing music always by DJ VINC3. The music is a perfect volume so you can easily enjoy a convo with a date.
Highly recommend Persona if you’re looking for a great spot to enjoy drinks and even better music.

reviews.author: Audrey Lee
reviews.text: Juan really showed out and made us the perfect drinks! Definitely coming back! I loved the service, the people, and the atmosphere. Get the green door, it’s the best!

bars.name: The Nags Head
bars.address: 5346 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Elizabeth Arciga-Campos
reviews.text: Such a cute dive bar! Love the bartender. She was the sweetest person ever who invited to play puzzles that made our night so much fun. Must come visit bar to enjoy the chill vibes with great music (our night had 2000s pop which my friends and I sang to all night long).

reviews.author: Joshua Faulkenberry
reviews.text: Crazy expensive!  $18 for Glenfiddich 12.  She said $2 more for a shot of McAllen.  That would be overpriced even downtown in the financial district..  Always remember to ask how much they charge for scotch!

reviews.author: Beni Rincon
reviews.text: Tiny lil dive bar in the Richmond been wanting to come here for awhile glad I came by was exactly what you want good tunes and cheap drinks

reviews.author: Ian Rodney
reviews.text: A lovely, friendly & tasty local bar! I always feel welcomed by the staff here. The drinks are solid and the atmosphere is great!

reviews.author: Alan McQuade
reviews.text: Calypso is quite possibly the best person one could ever meet in a crisis: we entered this bar like full-on chaos monkeys after accidentally leaving a phone in a cab. We were in a full tizzy, panic-calling our driver, nobody thinking straight — and she instantly read the room and went into crisis-management mode.  Within seconds of her calming and positive spirit, we made it off the ledge, avoided at least three strokes, and — best of all — even had fun. When the phone finally and joyously made it back to us (thank you, Nelson!), the celebration was real. The whole experience made this bar feel like home, instantly. Couldn’t recommend enough.

bars.name: Bar Iris
bars.address: 2310 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Carolina Jimenez
reviews.text: Highly recommend Bar Iris! They truly serve some of the most delicious cocktails in town. The ambiance and service is amazing. A special thank you to our waiter, Helen, who was incredibly kind and attentive, she took the time to patiently explain the menu and give great recommendations, which really made our experience even better. Can’t wait to come back!

reviews.author: Kenneth Chan
reviews.text:
Hands down the best cocktails in SF and def one of the best in America.

Love the beautifully curated menu and what it had to offer. Heavily influenced by Japanese ingredients hence offering a very unique menu of its own.

Cocktails were extremely well balanced with flavours, aroma and umami. A sip of The friend of a friend of a friend was no different to eating a bag of toasted chips with its long lingering smoked aroma. Insanely good.

Will definitely go back for the Japanese bites or even the Kaisei don.

reviews.author: Kels
reviews.text:
These cocktails were absolutely delicious! The menu has such a fantastic array of creative drinks that I’m excited to try next time I’m in town.

The lighting over the bar was a bit too bright, but the space itself was gorgeous.

reviews.author: Anirudh Sudarshan
reviews.text:
Very creative drinks, not all of them hit because of my personal tastes but I have to say they are very unique

10/10 service, they also customize drinks for you

Will keep going back until I've tried them all

reviews.author: Rainbow Warrior
reviews.text: Food was an absolute disappointment. This is a Michelin star spot….the karrage was cold upon arrival. We told the server and within minutes brought us back the same chicken boiling, from a second fry. Dry and over greased at this point. Did they think we wouldn’t notice? Used to love this spot but was truly disappointed not loving the new menu change.

bars.name: Bourbon & Branch
bars.address: 501 Jones St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Rose Centeno
reviews.text:
If you're looking for a fun, immersive experience that transports you straight back to the 1920s Prohibition era, this speakeasy-style bar is a must-visit. From the moment you arrive, it feels like you are stepping into a secret world. There's no sign - just a mysterious door and a doorbell. Ring it, give the password, and you're in.

Reservations are required, and the exclusivity adds to the charm. Once inside, you're seated at a cozy table and handed an impressively large cocktail and spirits menu. My friend and I went on a Saturday night around 9:30pm and were surprised by how relaxed and uncrowded it was.

The seasonal drink menu is incredibly creative, ad the staff were fantastic - patient, knowledgeable, and happy to walk us through the options. The service was top-notch, and the ambiance was spot-on for the era. Dim lighting, vintage decor, and a vibe that makes you want to stay awhile.

I tried a few cocktails, but the standout for me was the Heavy Artillery - bold, balanced, and unforgettable. There are a few house rules that help preserve the experience: 1. Be discreet with cell phone usage 2. No flash photography and 3. Remain seated and enjoy the era.

Overall, we had a fantastic time and would absolutely go back. It's not just a bar - it's an experience!

reviews.author: Lenneah Maugeri
reviews.text: My husband and I have been coming here since we were dating and it is always the most perfect way to the end the night. Service is always fantastic. They have an extensive drink menu, and the staff know it well, offer recommendations and guide you through it. The cocktails are excellent, with classics on the menu, as well as very unique combinations. You can tell there is quite a bit of craftsmanship in each drink. The cocktails are also priced well.

reviews.author: Yay H
reviews.text:
The entrance can be tricky — it’s not where the map shows. You’ll need to go around the corner near address 480, where a doorman will check your reservation password before letting you in.

Inside, the atmosphere is fantastic with an extensive cocktail menu and a true hidden-bar vibe. We really enjoyed the experience, and it feels like a perfect spot for a date night.

One thing to note: the area right outside has quite a few homeless people, as others have mentioned. Just be aware when arriving and leaving.

reviews.author: Pooya Fayyaz
reviews.text:
One of the more well-known speakeasies in SF. you’ll need a password to get in (just make a reservation to get it). Cool concept, but honestly, I was expecting more.

The drinks were average, the decor didn’t stand out, and the service was just okay. It feels like the hype is mostly around the “secret” entrance, not the actual experience.

It’s a quiet spot, which makes it good for date nights, but there are definitely better bars in SF if you’re going for standout cocktails or atmosphere.

reviews.author: Patrick Ho
reviews.text: Pretty cool spot! Speakeasy vibes from the time you approach the door to going in. Dark and moody. Huge selection of cocktails. Bartender was amazing and made an amazing Old Fashion. Highly recommend checking this place out!

bars.description: Reservations are required at this swanky, dimly lit unmarked bar known for handcrafted cocktails.

bars.name: Bar Maritime
bars.address: 417 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Brenna Stanton
reviews.text: Stopped in for a lite bite and some drinks on a Saturday afternoon and was not disappointed. Their menu is small but mighty, the drinks were fantastic and all the lite bites were delicious (it'd be a shame to only get the oysters). Can't wait to go back.

reviews.author: Jenny Hamm
reviews.text: I cannot say enough good things about this spot. Stayed at the Pali while visiting for a few nights from LA and ended up here every night. Even though this spot is in a hotel, I really loved how it had such a neighborhoody, local gem kinda feel to it. Larry was the absolute best and my young son literally thought he was family by the time we left! I tried many cocktails and they all were stellar but I particularly loved their icebox martini and the Maritime martini. I wasn’t really sure what to expect of the menu being a bar and all, but the food was amazing! The oysters and crudo were great, but I especially loved the young favas, which had this incredible vinaigrette, and my 4 year old son ate the fried shrimp, tail and all!! They said they are opening their full restaurant soon so I’m looking forward to that next time I’m visiting.

reviews.author: Rob Kahn
reviews.text:
One of the best hotel bars in SF. It's on the second floor of the Palihotel right by the Stockton St tunnel. That block is a bit grubby, but worth the trek for this hidden gem.

We went on a Saturday night after a show, planning to just get a quick drink. But ended up staying for a while because it was just so frigging nice. Their specialty cocktails are magic - they really show the craft of mixology, with surprising and delicious combinations.

We were seated promptly and service was great throughout the evening. There's a long bar, and tables and couches spread throughout the space. The room itself is beautiful, honoring its Edwardian heritage but very contemporary. The space is comfy and elegant, and the laid back vibe works well for everything from shorts-and-tee to an upscale night on the town.

Cocktails were so good (Maritime Martini is amazing) we had two rounds, then tried some of the food. Its a robust, seafood-focused bar menu. Really fresh local oysters, and we also shared the steak and fries, which was generous and their fries are amazing.

Looking around at what others were ordering, would say that portions overall are generous and prices are reasonable for SF, eg, they had multiple wines by the glass for

reviews.author: Karla Agudelo
reviews.text: Food, service and everything was great here. Oscar, man in black continuously kept on checking in on us. Our water was always full. Always had a great smile. Julie the bartender was fabulous! Always had a smile as well and loved her laugh. She was so quick with the beverages and welcomed us as soon as walked through the doors. The food was incredible. The bread and butter in itself, so simple and we got seconds of. So delicious and quaint. The tuna tostada, above expectations! Steak was perfectly seasoned. Oysters were fresh and a great sauce pairing with to a cute boat for presentation. We felt at home and treated like family. Could not recommend more!

reviews.author: Hilton Graham
reviews.text:
One of my favorite... Bar Maritime is one of those spots you almost don’t want to share — a true hidden gem tucked inside the Palihotel, just off Union Square. Dark and cozy with contemporary nautical décor, it strikes the perfect balance between stylish and comfortable.

The atmosphere is warm and engaging, the kind of place that feels instantly inviting whether you’re solo at the bar or out with friends. The cocktails are excellent, and the service stands out — friendly, attentive, and genuinely fun. The Tuna Tostada was fresh and flavorful, and paired with French fries, it made the perfect late-night snack.

A fantastic spot for apps and drinks, or to settle in for a longer evening. Definitely one of my favorite finds near Union Square.

bars.name: 540 Bar
bars.address: 540 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Reinhard Hochrieser
reviews.text: Great bar with an excellent beer selection, perfect for hanging out with friends. The staff is exceptionally friendly.

reviews.author: Babak Farahmand
reviews.text: My favorite spot- know the owners, they are good people.

reviews.author: B
reviews.text: Super solid beer bar with cocktails also. Friendly and talkative patrons

reviews.author: Andy “Gastronnoisseur” L
reviews.text: Came here for a night cap. Used to come here a lot before the ownership change and remodel. Boy has it spiffy up. It no longer got the "dive" bar feeling. It's a nice bar to hang out and chill with friends. No more pool table but they got pinball machines.

reviews.author: Anne Roberts
reviews.text: Really loving how they renovated the space. Same delicious drinks and friendly bartenders with a warm and welcoming environment. The arcade games are fun and the bartenders make great drinks.  Great for a post work happy hour or night out with friends.

bars.name: The Beehive
bars.address: 842 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Lamarrathon
reviews.text: Holiday drinks are terrific here! Went out for a friend’s party and had some Coquito while I was there! I’d been craving Coquito since leaving the east coast and this hit the spot! Presentation was fantastic!

reviews.author: Sarah Larson
reviews.text:
Pleasantly surprised. I thought this bar would be loud and packed to the gills but it was chill, delicious, and comfortable.
We arrived around 5:30p on a Friday and were offered a seat in the back area. We ordered the shishito peppers, spinach dip, and mozzarella balls to share- all of which were delicious. My friends had a couple drinks and loved them! Overall, would recommend and will be back!

reviews.author: Maria Bruno
reviews.text:
Best cocktails on Valencia Street 🍸🍹✨ They always serve a special slushy here and I’m a slushy girl so today I got the “Miami Nice”. It was pure rum deliciousness wrapped in tropical vibes and perfect in warm weather.
Also had their signature Beehive cocktail, which wasn’t my favorite but husband liked it.
We sat outside in the parklet and enjoyed some apps, along with all the friendly dogs walking by.
Decent ice program - I think they get theirs from Blind Tiger. Service was lovely & very attentive.

reviews.author: S Crawford
reviews.text:
I wish Google had a separate cocktail rating but they don't so I'm bundling it into the food rating. Amazing drinks and great service. The ambiance is discreet yet welcoming but it can get noisy and packed at times. Warning...they have TVs so game day can take place here increasing noise levels and altering the usual upscale feel of the place. Seeming to seamlessly transition from day to night, the place can go from bright lights and noisy cheers to a candle lit escape within minutes.

The music tends to be loud and feel out of place as they play dated pop and syndicated dance music. It's really a toss up based on the night you're there. No matter the night you can bet on consistently quality cocktails.

reviews.author: Jolee Evans
reviews.text: We came here the first Friday night it was open, and it was packed. Even with standing room only, the bar staff were friendly, quick, and attentive, answering tons of our cocktail questions with a smile. The decor is sweet, with a long white marble bar in the front and a large lounge area in the back with private coves for groups. They take reservations for the back room. At $13 a cocktail it isn’t cheap, but the quality and experience is worth it. Cheers

bars.description: The Treasury team dispenses cocktails & playful New American eats in this buzzy, retro-glam lounge.

bars.name: Stoa
bars.address: 701 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Holly Zhou
reviews.text:
Really great food menu! We were actually so-so on the drinks, but warrants another try. The drinks were fairly straightforward and paired much better with the food than standalone.

Low-octane: Castaway - not that offensive but it didn't make me like sherry.
Lean&mean: Mad Artist - really strong tasting, not that much to balance it.

HOWEVER, the food was a highlight! Favs were the cumin tofu tacos and devil egg. The tacos were so flavorful especially with the green hummus, and really tasty homemade tortilla. The devil egg is a must-order, instead of boring mayo it's a really fragrant chili crisp/ salsa macha.

We'll definitely come back for dinner and maybe try some other drinks!

reviews.author: Take Back Roads
reviews.text: The food here is absolutely fantastic. Flavors are off the charts! Everything comes out very quickly as well. Drinks are well made and well balanced. The only downside was the dessert - not nearly as good as it looks! Overall though an excellent meal at a very reasonable price AND all food is completely **gluten free** (not drinks though). I STRONGLY recommend Stoa! We will definitely be back!

reviews.author: Ivy Audrain
reviews.text: SENSATIONAL! Amazing menu and service. Eric always takes such great care of us with lovely cocktails and dinner recommendations. 10/10!

reviews.author: Denis Serenyi
reviews.text: I loved the atmosphere. Hip, buzzy but not too noisy or crazy. If you get a table, you order your drinks at the register and they bring them to you, which is a great system that works well even when crowded. The problem is the drinks though. I found them flat and boring. 3 ingredient cocktails is an interesting concept but it feels like a gimmick.

reviews.author: Katie R
reviews.text: Not nearly enough talk about their 100% gluten & dairy-free menu! So refreshing to not have to ask for a separate menu. And the food is 10/10 delicious. Great vibe for a date night but equally so for friend shared plates dinner!

bars.name: Cavaña
bars.address: 100 Channel St 17th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94158, USA

reviews.author: Paul Osuna
reviews.text:
We had a very nice valentines day evening at Cavaña. The wait staff were very kind and helpful and offered complementary drinks. Food was great and came quickly. Drinks were delicious as well. The inside was a little loud for intimate conversation but outside offered a quieter alternative; they also provided blankets for the outside when we got cold. The views of the city at night were also wonderful.
I definitely recommend Cavaña for casual drinks or a nice dinner, either way you’ll enjoy your evening.

reviews.author: Rinahdi
reviews.text: The view is absolutely insane here! I came for Christmas and I loved all the holiday drinks and the fun cups. The food was so good! But the highlight of this place is definitely the view, incredible panoramic view of San Francisco and the bay. I definitely loved this spot and will be back.

reviews.author: Jenny Xu
reviews.text:
Came here with a friend and got some drinks and food!

Good:
- The food was delicious - I liked the quesabirria tacos the most and it was the perfect portion size for me
- The chips and guac were great to munch on while we had drinks
- I liked the watermelon drink the most, then the mango frozen drink was pretty good too
- The place is beautiful inside, very upscale modern
- Super fast service!

Bad:
- The place is pretty expensive if you get 1 appetizer and a few drinks
- We got kicked out after being here for two hours so it was definitely a busy place
- The place is super busy and loud

Overall this was a fancy bar and good place to catch up with a friend!

reviews.author: Vonda
reviews.text: Everything about this rooftop bar was amazing, from the food, to the service and the atmosphere. Had some of the best quesabirria and carne asada tacos we’ve ever tried. We will definitely become back next time we are in San Francisco!!!!

reviews.author: Diya Nalla
reviews.text: On April 16th, 2023, after enjoying a lovely picnic in Golden Gate Park, my mom, Namratha Manthani, and I headed to Cavaña, the rooftop lounge at the LUMA Hotel in San Francisco (100 Channel St, 17th Floor). We ordered cocktails and small plates, soaking in the stylish Latin-inspired atmosphere and the breathtaking views of the Bay and city skyline. It felt like the perfect way to end the day—relaxing, vibrant, and memorable.

bars.name: Alchemist Bar & Lounge
bars.address: 679 3rd St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Joe Marxkors
reviews.text: We came here for an event, and had some great beers, along with some really good chicken sliders and fries. The vibe was super chill and the place was decorated for Christmas which was fun. Would definitely go back!

reviews.author: James falls
reviews.text: Great space.  We had a private event here. We were greeted and asked for the password to get in.  That's was a nice touch.  Lots of appetizers to satisfy every tate.  Vegan and vegetarian options. The bartenders were friendly and made drinks quickly.  It's was a little loud in the space. I think that was because of the number of people, and the DJ had to play lous enough to create the right party vibe.  Overall, it was a good choice for the event.

reviews.author: CasedGod
reviews.text:
A true Hidden jam in downtown San Francisco

This themed bar had everything that I could’ve ever hoped for! With a great selection of drink menus, I scurried in just after happy hour to sit down and enjoy quite a few drink drinks by the expert bartender Gerson

My waitress, Cindy took care of every single need that I had with such urgency. It was incredible to walk into the spot and it not be very busy. Normally the spot is absolutely jumping because it’s so close to the baseball field.

Go check out the wings. They beat out every national chain that I’ve ever had.

reviews.author: Ananya Mahesh
reviews.text: good ambiance and date spot, however, slightly awkward crowd. music is alright and there’s weird (but slightly entertaining projections of things). interesting cocktails (actually quite overwhelming — I couldn’t pick), and “good bar bites.” decent service. over all a 6.4/10 and i may go again.

reviews.author: Nelly De La Cruz
reviews.text:
Ok, so I’m the worst only only captured the bathroom, but I mean look at that nice detail!

Anyways, this place had been a favorite of mine for years. Great atmosphere and energy with locals and visitors. Drink rotations super fun and the service is great!

We went with a fairly large group and you ordered at the bar and the bartender was amazing and brought all the drinks to the table! Friends ordered food, shared it was tasty and came out of the kitchen fairly quick.

bars.description: Dim, trendy cocktail bar with steampunk decor prepares signature drinks with housemade ingredients.

bars.name: Owl Tree
bars.address: 601 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Gabby Gonzalez
reviews.text: The bartender recommended the lychee martini & the cucumber mint gimlet. Both were absolutely delicious. Music was on point the whole time. I really liked this place! Walked here from the Hilton no problem.

reviews.author: Smittee
reviews.text: Classic SanFran bar atmosphere. Don't pass this one up. The Manhattans are great

reviews.author: Anjalee Vasvani
reviews.text:
The Owl Tree is such a hidden gem in San Francisco that I almost don’t want to share it! We hosted our wedding welcome party in their upstairs space, and it was perfect. Adrianna, the events manager, is wonderful—she’s incredibly responsive and so easy to work with. She and the entire team took such great care of us, and everything ran smoothly from start to finish.

The space itself is private (including a private bathroom), full of SF charm, and incredibly reasonably priced for what you get. Our guests had a great time, and we’re already thinking of excuses to host another event here in the future.

Pro tip: order the cucumber gimlet and the espresso martini—you won’t regret it.

Can’t recommend The Owl Tree enough!

reviews.author: Jodi Simmons
reviews.text: We hosted a corporate event at The Owl Tree, and it was absolutely perfect! Adrianna and the bartending staff were fantastic, making sure our signature cocktails were spot on and our guests were well taken care of. The security team was exceptional, managing our guest list and promptly notifying us of any issues. We also had custom decor, and the Owl Tree team handled all the details with ease, coordinating everything flawlessly so we didn’t have to intervene much. Their dedication to ensuring everything went off without a hitch was truly impressive. Highly recommend The Owl Tree for any event!

reviews.author: Gary Butterfield
reviews.text: Great bar on the corner of Taylor and Post.  Cute little neighborhood bar.  Excellent cocktails (especially the Cucumber Mint Gimlet).  Bartender was on his game and friendly!  Can't wait to go back !

bars.description: Dark-wood bar featuring red carpeting & black leather booths serves up creative cocktails & beers.

bars.name: Lost and Found Cocktail Bar
bars.address: 1439 Taraval St, San Francisco, CA 94116, USA

reviews.author: BVW
reviews.text: Incredible bar. Owner Edward is a really genuine guy; attentive, respectful, truly has created a hybrid of an elevated cocktail bar/lounge & a great sports bar to watch the 49ers, and (seemingly) *effortlessly* makes each person feel at home. If you’ve lost your spark, or just wanna try a fun new bar, go here and you’ll find what you’re lookin’ for.

reviews.author: Barbara Westmoreland
reviews.text: Such a cute bar with delicious drinks and food. Every single drink was like a piece of art. Will definitely be going again!

reviews.author: CHLOE CHOE
reviews.text:
Lost and Found Cocktail Bar is a gem! The cocktails are beautifully crafted, often adorned with fresh flowers that make them almost too pretty to drink. 🥹

The ambiance is vibrant and inviting, perfect for a fun night out. Plus, the karaoke setup adds an extra layer of excitement, making it a great spot to let loose and enjoy with friends. 🎤

Kelvin was the best cocktail maker and Edward was also really nice and made the experience even better.

Highly recommend if you’re looking for a unique and lively experience!

reviews.author: karina k
reviews.text: Nice bar w good music and nice bartender! We had the Dragon cocktail whiskey and garlic fries. Fries were made light a crispy and tons of garlic that you could smell the great aroma from afar and topped with parsley. TVs  and a projector display of sports on 4 screens (3 TVs) and 1 bathroom for all. Nice experience and fun atmosphere.

reviews.author: Kishore Hari
reviews.text:
Love this spot. Great cocktails, good food, chill vibe.

J-Town Matcha and The Painted Ladies were big cocktail winners for us. Inventive and different, without being pretentious. The risotto was really good. Wings were a little sweet, but worked as well. They have a cute parklet, but it can get windy out there.

Never would have expected such a good spot in this neighborhood. Will be part of the regular rotation.

bars.description: Bold wallpaper & neon set the backdrop for cocktails & American snacks at this neighborhood lounge.

bars.name: ABV
bars.address: 3174 16th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: kylepencedc1
reviews.text: My wife and I visited on a Tuesday, we arrived at opening. We had the whole bar to ourselves. Atmosphere was awesome, we ordered the fermented edamame and fried cauliflower which were both spectacular. But the real standout was the service behind the bar. Donny was incredibly knowledgeable and had that quiet confidence that immediately makes me trust a bartender. Not showy but he knows his stuff and he nailed it every time, got to know my preferences, let me taste some of the stuff I showed interest in - I couldn’t have asked for more. Water glasses were always refilled promptly. The vibes were immaculate. This was our last night in SF and it will be an unforgettable memory that will carry me to our next visit in 2027.

reviews.author: JZ
reviews.text: 4.5/5. Great bar with excellent drinks. Think neighborhood, casual, wooden long tables, modern industrial and taverny. I originally thought it’s not your classic romantic date-spot, but I take that back. It’s quite vibey. It can be intimate depending on your seating situation/crowdedness given the dim ambience lighting. Good bar to have convos (not too loud) or hang with friends. While dim, you probably don't want to canoodle here since you might be sharing a communal table with others, but it is a great spot to build up vibes for some canoodling later. I really liked their Terragon drinks, very well made - they had one slightly highball-esque and maybe mezcal that was really good (it’s off menu tho). The grapefruit highball is light and refreshing. I heard their food is also good but did not try. Would recommend!

reviews.author: cantik
reviews.text:
Solid choice for cocktails and casual food at reasonable prices. Extensive drink menu with knowledgeable bartenders who take time to explain their creations.
Tried several cocktails - the one with carrot juice was surprisingly delicious and well-portioned. Ahi tuna tartare ($19) with sesame chips was fresh and tasty.
Food is casual but good quality - perfect for date night or hanging with friends. Not fancy, but that's exactly the vibe they're going for.
Will return to explore more of their cocktail menu.

reviews.author: Teresa Grobecker
reviews.text: Amazing service.  All front of house are bartenders who rotate nights.  That means one night they are bartending, another they are bar-back.  The result is everyone takes ownership of service.  Pro tip: they don’t allow People to stand and wait, so get there early.

reviews.author: Sung Choe
reviews.text: Their expresso martini and burgers are A+. Awesome spot. Yucatan chef lady is fire 🔥.  Keep her happy at all costs. Keep it going abv!

bars.description: A large-scale mural sets the stage at this trendy spot for cocktails, beer & wine plus upscale eats.

shopping.name: Union Square
shopping.address: San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Kristen Kelly
reviews.text: One of my favorite spots in San Francisco, especially during the holidays! In December, they have a large tree and skating rink. Also, JPMorgan has a winter walk there. I went in 2025 and enjoyed coffee, shopping, the food trucks and live entertainment. Union Square brings a SF city magical vibe. Perfect for families or just anyone who wants to enjoy it.

reviews.author: JIM SALINAS
reviews.text: As always, Union Square is spectacular. Especially during the Christmas season, the amazing beautiful tree. The ice skating rink, everybody decked out. Looking across the street, all the wonderful beautiful stores, especially the flagship store for Macy's, its just a place to experience and there's plenty of underground parking. The security is top-notch.  Everyone should feel especially safe with so many of San Francisco's finest everywhere.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text:
Nice. I had a pretty decent (good) experience at Union Square. It was quite crowded during Christmas, however, it is likely worth seeing it, especially if you are visiting San Francisco.

There is a massive Christmas tree erected right on the square (Christmas 2025-2026), which makes it a nice spot to capture a selfie nearby. Overall, it is a great place to visit.

reviews.author: LAND ROVER
reviews.text: We were there the Day After Christmas. So it was kind of crowded. There was people, ice skating, big, giant Christmas tree lit up lots of people. Lots of families, good times as always. But it was special during the Christmas glad I was able to show my wife and my daughter. This special time and this special place of the world

reviews.author: Swati Snata
reviews.text:
We had such an amazing time! We went out three days before Christmas and the holiday spirit was everywhere.
We saw the giant Christmas tree and the massive Hanukkah menorah, and even spent some time skating at the ice rink.
All the shops were so festive—Macy’s and The Cheesecake Factory were both decked out in beautiful holiday decorations!

shopping.description: Well-known vibrant neighborhood featuring a range of upscale shops, restaurants, hotels & nightlife.

shopping.name: Crocker Galleria
shopping.address: 50 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94104, USA

reviews.author: Kyler DuVall
reviews.text:
The Galleria itself it's like an Alley Pocket Mall, and while a lot of the stores are empty it's still a beautiful place to have lunch, especially if you bring your own. On the 4th level, levels 1-3 have escalators but to get 4th floor you have to take stairs, theres a nice rooftop garden and quiet space. While not too much to see it's a nice place to walk around and breathe or eat if you don't want to inside. There are bathrooms but require a passkey, which I couldn't get, but I wasn't trying too hard.
A lovely place to read a book or meet up with friends too.

reviews.author: Damahni Jackson
reviews.text: 📍 Crocker Galleria, at 50 Post Street in San Francisco, is a three-level retail center in the heart of downtown. Its prime location at the intersection of the Financial District and Union Square makes it a convenient spot for office workers and tourists. The galleria's distinctive glass-domed pavilion offers a unique shopping experience in a central, easily accessible location. ★★★★★

reviews.author: Sonoma Bill
reviews.text: Absolutely love the inside of the old bank, so historic.  Would be so cool to be able to tour it.  Sure hope whatever happens to it the architecture is retained as much as possible.

reviews.author: Grace Lin
reviews.text: Beautiful building and cute little garden on roof. However,  not many shops left 😂

reviews.author: Rick Wolf
reviews.text:
Very nice mellow place where you can sit and not necessarily buy right away.  There is an enclosed roof garden, as in a wall without roof for overlooking Montgomery/Market. The machine asked me to confirm hours, yet they differ. I have enclosed picture. As the written ones on the entrance may refer more to the shops themselves. I think entrance is allowed at 7am, coffee shops ready then I am sure. Then high end businesses at 10am. And says till 6pm, yet it is approaching 7pm and we are still allowed to wander freely, although cleanup & closing seems in progress. Confirmations will be updated.
~🐺Wolf approved
~🐺Wolf recommended
Totally empty now November 2023
See #metal Leo channel on YouTube for video
PATHETIC San Francisco

shopping.description: Designer shops span three floors at this galleria featuring a glass dome. Closed Sunday.

shopping.name: World Flagship Space Union Square
shopping.address: 281 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: George Ohan
reviews.text: I don’t know if this is good or bad. June 2025, verifying that I am human, digitally. On our way to a Metallica concert at Levi’s Stadium.

reviews.author: Duane Fassett (BRAZILL)
reviews.text: Is this place supposed to close earlier than 7pm on a Friday? Here it is 6:50pm on Friday Sept 26th, 2025, and the doors are closed and noone is here. ☹️ Oh well, maybe tomorrow? Will change rating maybe after I successfully verify...

reviews.author: John Forell
reviews.text: Get verified as a human here! If you don't have the World App already, the staff will help you get started on that. Simply find an available orb and complete the verification process. A fun experience!

reviews.author: Nancy M
reviews.text: I came in with my husband and was helped by a nice man that was the store manager, Phil.  I didn't know what to expect but he explained the project and showed us real world examples of how becoming verified would be a benefit.  He must have spent 45 minutes with us going over the apps and digital wallet. I've already referred 3 of our friends to come and get verified!

reviews.author: Tanguy Jeanneau
reviews.text: This allows you to prove you're an authentic human online. Basically creating an Internet of Humans in the age of AI, bots and fake content online. Pretty neat and techno-utopist

shopping.name: Urban Outfitters
shopping.address: 80 Powell St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: annemarie
reviews.text:
This store is extra big. Their clothes are soo cute!! And they are one of the only brands that fit really nice on my teenage looking body.

Also, their staff is super nice.

Love them.

reviews.author: Emily Vaught
reviews.text: DONT BUY THE SWEATPANTS. Don’t recommend the sweatpants at all- they started pulling immediately. Also, urban sold me a pair of jeans WITH a crazy stain on the back that I didn’t see. Oh and by the way when I asked to talk to the manager/ find anyone to talk to- they said they couldn’t give me a refund (OR ANY OTHER INFORMATION). I tried to get in touch with customer service multiple times, they simply ignored me and sent me in a whole loop. Don’t shop here if you want good quality items. Literally I wore the sweatpants once and they pilled. And as for the jeans- if I’m buying an $80 pair of jeans- they better not have a stain. I don’t even live close to an urban outfitters back home. The customer service is unacceptable and quite honestly rude in a digital world where these things should be resolved quickly.

reviews.author: Ton T
reviews.text: Trendy clothing for the younger folks, or older folks who wanna stay young

reviews.author: Penny Christie
reviews.text: Excellent customer service and lots of awesome clothing. The sales are always great and I love the exclusive items. There are lots of stairs to climb for each level but there is a lift you can use. The space in the shop is open and everything is easily accessible.

reviews.author: Mike Ast
reviews.text: Loved, loved, loved this store as a teen and in college.  Daddy issues.  Mommy issues.  Step mother issues.  Come to think of it, I still love this store.  But, I don't shop here any longer...

shopping.description: Chain with a hipster vibe known for on-trend fashions, accessories & quirky home-decor items.

shopping.name: Neiman Marcus
shopping.address: 150 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: K G.
reviews.text:
We had a great experience at Neiman Marcus in San Francisco. The selection was strong, and the team went above and beyond—especially with on-site alterations that were turned around quickly when we needed something same-day for an event.

Lauren and Gleslie were standouts: knowledgeable, attentive, and genuinely helpful without ever being pushy. Even with the Super Bowl crowds, the service felt calm, professional, and thoughtful. We left impressed and grateful for such seamless support.

reviews.author: Juan Martinez
reviews.text:
I was visiting San Francisco from Los Angeles on a week-long excursion. I have always had my eyes set on Dior's Private Fragrance line, specifically Tobacolor. Naturally, I popped into the Dior flagship store looking to purchase the fragrance. To my dismay, I was told that they did not carry the Private line; only Neiman Marcus did.

I made my way over and was greeted by a lovely lady working the Dior boutique. After outlining my desire for a new fragrance and sharing that I work in banking, the sales consultant agreed that Gris Dior and Tobacolor were my best bets. Naturally, I went for the latter.

This experience epitomizes Neiman Marcus's positioning in the luxury market: they carry the most exclusive, luxurious offerings. I was even told that the only other place carrying Dior's Private Collection in California was the Neiman Marcus in Beverly Hills.

reviews.author: Neal
reviews.text:
Palatial, glamorous, and extravagant department store with a restaurant.

This store is arranged beautifully, with displays from dozens of brands providing a snapshot of each. The basement floor is dedicated to men, while most of the other floors are for women. The ground floor has makeup, skincare products, and you can see all the way to the glass dome. There's also a beautiful restaurant on the fourth floor. Going through the fifth floor is like going through the living rooms of many houses. I highly recommend visiting this place even if you're not going to buy anything. The displays are so beautiful and it's honestly like an art museum.

reviews.author: Gregory Valavanis
reviews.text:
My foot got caught in the escalator and tore up my shoe. Very scary.
Nice selection of Versace product, though!

reviews.author: Nini johnson
reviews.text:
I purchase for my boyfriend the baccarat rouge 540, I had an outstanding experience.

The Customer Service was above and beyond !!!! very pleasant & positive energy, very knowledgeable was the best experience I’ve ever had at Neiman

Erica Davis deserves a raise. I will only buy from her now and refer people to her. This woman knows the products and has the best attitude. She also took an extra step to wrap my things up very nicely and handled everything with care Thank you so much.

shopping.name: Macy's
shopping.address: 170 O'Farrell St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Traveling Yogyakarta
reviews.text: The place was great. I found many great brands here like Ralph Lauren, Tommy Hilfiger, Calvin Klein, and many more. The view from this store was great anw. The only thing that I was concerned was the restroom. It was soooo dirty. I mean, it is a place for high end brands but dirty is something that I can’t even understand.

reviews.author: Annie Coots
reviews.text:
The selection and employees are wonderful- my complaint is with the cleanliness of the restrooms, and the putrid smell of the stairwell/basement kitchenwares area. Macys, you need to increase custodial staff and repair the restrooms. In the one restroom I visited, there were 5 stalls out of order, one water fountain out of order, and the other water fountain was moldy and decrepit. Do better, Macys.

Side note- no Santa this year, WTH?! It is Macys Union Square! C’mon!!

reviews.author: Sandra Verboom
reviews.text: Spectacular, as always. Had to make sure to see the Macy's windows all decorated one last time, before they move on next year. So sad. Union square is one of our favorite stops when its all decked out for the holidays.

reviews.author: Meltem O
reviews.text:
The Clinique booth was great. The team was professional and friendly. Josie was my favorite. She helped me to choose the best shades of the make up materials. She also showed me how to apply them. She was highly knowledgeable.

The Cheese Factory was nice, but packed with people. They said we might wait 1-1.5 hours to get a table. We couldn't wait that long.

reviews.author: Pezhman Raeisian
reviews.text:
Macy’s in San Francisco, located right on Union Square, is a massive and iconic department store with several floors of shopping. We explored it during our visit and appreciated the wide variety of brands, from high-end fashion to affordable everyday wear. The store is clean, well-organized, and the staff were helpful.

Tip: Don’t miss the upper floors for great views of Union Square, especially from the Cheesecake Factory on the top level. It’s a great spot for both shopping and relaxing in the heart of the city. A must-visit if you’re in downtown San Francisco!

shopping.description: Department store chain providing brand-name clothing, accessories, home furnishings & housewares.

shopping.name: Louis Vuitton San Francisco Union Square
shopping.address: 233 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

photos.attribution: Louis Vuitton San Francisco Union Square

reviews.author: N J (NJ)
reviews.text: The Louis Vuitton Union Square store excels in visual presentation and spatial design, offering a highly polished and architecturally refined environment. That said, the store can at times feel more like a showroom than a personal retail space. While the minimalism elevates the products, it may also create a sense of distance for shoppers who prefer a more interactive or intimate experience.

reviews.author: Kimmel Sadat
reviews.text:
So disappointed with my experience here. I came in looking to buy 3 fragrances and initially no one came up to help me. An associate named Lauren eventually walked by and asked if I needed help—she was actually great—and she sent a “fragrance specialist” over to help me.

The associate assisting me was not very helpful and didn’t even let me experience all the fragrances I was clearly showing interest in by commenting “that’s too feminine” or “you won’t like it” when she literally hasn’t been paying any attention to me and was in fact on her phone scrolling. Actually the so called specialist stood a few yards away as she is apparently is sensitive to fragrances—which I would understand if you weren’t a fragrance specialist.. It seemed I was wasting her time coming into her store so I was hesitant in buying anything, which was exactly what I’d come in specifically for. I honestly just wanted to get out of there because it just feels really bad to come into an establishment and not feel welcomed or wanted there—she literally stood across the showroom on her phone with a mask on.

Just to test the associate I asked if I could buy a few samples or if they have any complementary ones to offer as I had a few other fragrances i wanted to try before buying and she declined me as I expected. I know it’s common for LV stores to give a few samples to customers who did not come in to buy anything—that’s why I came back in, to buy the fragrances another associate at an east coast store had given me samples of.

At this point, her attitude had put me off wanting to buy anything from the store but the temptation of getting the one I really wanted won and I caved. It was crazy the way she got excited that I was buying something and finally she decided to care and came to the fragrance displayed and showed me this and that scent and even offered all these samples with my purchase (which I understand is customary for LV) almost like a flip had switch now that she was making a sale. Sad it came so late that’s where it stopped—the standard presentation of the bag and boxed bottle LV stores usually do was not done with my purchase and she even went missing for a short while while boxing my bag. Some water or something would have been nice while I waited.

I understand a fragrance purchase, or even 3, is not like buying a luxury bag or luggage however I’m a customer all the same and she was a so called fragrance specialist and her customer service was not acceptable and lost the stores business in fact. I’m actually a little upset with myself for even buying something that day.

Without sounding to entitled or pretentious, buying a luxury item should feel good—like you’re rewarding yourself. Even if shopping here everyday is your lifestyle then you’d want the sales associates to at-least treat you as good as a cashier at the grocery store would.

Had she just started off how she did after I made a purchase and actually helped me experience the products and had she answered my questions and allowed me to actually shop then we’d both have walked out much happier.

The best advice I’ve ever heard when I was doing sales myself  was “always assume the sale”, “always assume their buying—they came it right?”.

This is my last time ever stepping into the SF LV store. If you read this review, I hope you have a better experience and it’s likely I drew a bad straw, but what a joke—a “fragrance specialist” with a fragrance hypersensitivity so bad she has to stand across the showroom on her phone with a mask on!

reviews.author: Amanda Souders
reviews.text:
Had an excellent experience, thanks to Karen. I made an appointment, and Karen contacted me before my appointment to confirm and ask how she could help. She really went above and beyond to accommodate me during a quick trip to SF, and I am so grateful.
In the (beautiful) store, everyone was friendly and helpful. And I was excited to meet Karen, as we had conversed via text ahead of time. She is wonderful and created a fantastic experience for me. Cheers to you and thank you, Karen!

reviews.author: Yoyo Hoiyiu Ko
reviews.text:
My husband and I stopped by the store for our wedding anniversary and were really impressed by Koa's customer service. Koa is the operations manager and did not need to spend an hour presenting all the small wallet options, but he did. He saw the long waitlist and decided to step in himself to help us out. He took his time getting to know us, my preferences, and even give us advice on how to take care of LV leather. I ended up walking away with my Rosalie and i feel really good about it :) (Plus, I've heard of people feeling discriminated in the past for what they buy - I'm sure many others have bought higher priced goods than this before but I did not feel like I was served differently.)

I have been to many LV stores including ones across the globe, and the service Koa provided to us is the best. Koa was so patient, friendly, and detailed. I felt welcomed enough that I would just stop by and say hi next time.

reviews.author: WINESKA ORSINI CANDELARIO
reviews.text: Came in to shop waited around for someone to come help me, no one bothered…. I was wearing comfy clothes leggings Uggs long sleeve…. A lady shopping literally stopped shopping to look at me and said something in her language to her daughter and then I walked away still no one helped me. Was looking for a gift for my bday for myself, left to go back to hotel to cry.

shopping.description: Luxury brand known for signature monogrammed handbags, luggage & more.

shopping.name: Nike San Francisco
shopping.address: 278 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Rehan Yadav
reviews.text: Huge shoutout to Adriell at the Nike San Francisco store in Union Square! He made the shopping process so easy and helped me find exactly what I was looking for. He really knows his stuff and walked me through all the features of the Nike Structure 26. I also appreciated the invite to come back and check out the new drops next week. Top-tier service!

reviews.author: Chris Greer
reviews.text: Solid experience with Assam! Got to try on the Mind 001 in store and Assam was incredibly friendly and had an answer to every question I had. Overall, one of the better Nike store experiences!

reviews.author: Thomas Reeh
reviews.text:
Great Nike store in Downton San Francisco right on Union Square.

Great selection of footwear and clothing. 6 Levels to explore

Recommended.

reviews.author: Jay Malvar
reviews.text: I came in to try the new Nike Muse here, which I assumed was a new unisex shoe style, and I was assisted by Poe. They were culturally sensitive, did not make any assumptions about my gender, and listen carefully to the size I requested, a woman's 11. I believe that having a diverse workforce really lends itself to the ethos of any company and create the kind of shopping experience I imagine for myself whenever I go out. Thank you Nike for seeking talent like Poe here in San Francisco, and for fulfilling the vision of today for people.

reviews.author: Laurie Estridge
reviews.text:
Unfortunately, this was a very disappointing experience.  The store had very limited inventory, and they do not accept returns or exchanges—even within 60 days. We were told by Alex that only Nike online handles returns during that timeframe. I’m attaching a picture of our barcode because we initiated the return online already and he refused to scan the barcode and said we had to return it online. Although clearly it says on the barcode return to store.

We’ve spent thousands at Nike this year, and when we needed an exchange within 45 days due to defective seams, we were directed to handle it online instead. This was during the holidays, which made it especially inconvenient.

Customer service was lacking (Alex), and the issue was not handled with care or flexibility. They didn’t even try to help.

shopping.description: Chain retailer selling a range of Nike athletic footwear, apparel & accessories.

shopping.name: ZARA
shopping.address: 250 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Damahni Jackson
reviews.text: 📍 250 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94108. ZARA has a limited curbside loading zone available outside. This photo captured in 2024 shows the stores signage and curb length. I recommend this location for passenger pick-up or drop-off.  ★★★★★

reviews.author: KIMBERLY LONG
reviews.text: The Zara’s in the Bay are by far thee WORST! it’s as if they get the scrap clothing compared to the ones i’ve shopped at in new york and miami.. very interesting all of the clothes look old and worn. Why?

reviews.author: Skarlett Urdaneta
reviews.text:
Zara – Post St, San Francisco
I had a disappointing experience with customer service at this location. I came in to return an item for which I had lost the receipt, and instead of offering guidance or explaining the available options, the associates made it feel like my request was an inconvenience.
I was repeatedly told things like “we don’t hold private information” and “we can’t type in your card info,” even though I never asked for that. It seemed less like they were trying to help and more like they just wanted to shut down the conversation. The lack of willingness to assist, paired with unclear communication, gave the impression that the staff either weren’t properly trained or simply didn’t know how to use their own resources to look up purchase information.
I did eventually manage to return the item — but not thanks to the staff I initially spoke to. Overall, the experience left me feeling dismissed and unwelcome. I hope management invests in better training and a more customer-friendly approach moving forward.

reviews.author: delvin hodges
reviews.text:
Very rude staff here. The cashier was not friendly today Sept 18th in the women section. Then, I decided to hold off on one item, she then tells me to “put it back in its spot.”

Secondly, had a friend picking up a return and was completely ignored due to employees having a conversation and only helped when my friend asked if someone was going to help him.

Management needs to reevaluate who’s working here, their professional and customer experience expectations.

reviews.author: Karina
reviews.text: Was getting perfumes there. The merchandising was quite poor. The testers didn’t match what was actually available on the shelves — there were more testers than actual boxes to purchase. The products were placed too close to each other, and everything collapsed like a house of cards when you tried to pick up a perfume to smell it. The whole display looked very careless.

shopping.description: Spanish fashion chain offering on-trend house-brand clothing, shoes & accessories.

shopping.name: Angelic Pretty San Francisco
shopping.address: 15 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Lorelain
reviews.text: The staff was so nice and kind every moment, but I feel like some things are waaayy overpriced, overall, it's a cute place

reviews.author: Melisa Arslan
reviews.text:
Super nice little place! Lovely options, and lovely staff. Everything felt super high quality, and I ended up buying an accessory!
PS: To the two lovely people working in the store, I loved your coords so much! Thank you for being so nice ❤️ I felt very welcome

reviews.author: senia
reviews.text: Beautiful choices the store looked amazing everything was so organized and excellent customer service!! everyone was so helpful i drove from very far so this was an amazing first experience im so grateful to everyone who helped me with even the smallest thing

reviews.author: KitnKitn
reviews.text:
Such sweet shop keeps!! I tried on at least 4 dresses and they were so sweet the entire time!
You aren't allowed photos inside but trust me the inside is so cute!!
If you're new to EGL most AP dresses are about 280-330$.
It was such a lovely experience, thank you!! 💞✨

reviews.author: Rhea Royality
reviews.text: I was a bit nervous not to go in wearing a cord. I was helped and even bought the San Francisco AP Badges! Which is an experience because I definitely don't live close 🎀💝

shopping.name: 2_48_Stockton
shopping.address: 42 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Emma Charlie
reviews.text: 2_48_Stockton offers a cozy atmosphere with great coffee and friendly service. Perfect spot for a quick break or casual meet-up in the city.

reviews.author: Saadiyah Smith
reviews.text:
I love this area it is a culmination point of Chinatown and Little Italy the food and shops are great the hustle and bustle is great hear so use caution but it awesomely picturesque
All these awesome foodie spots are in the same location awesome point

reviews.author: Jaime Williams
reviews.text: Great shopping center with plenty of parking including handicapped.  Security patrols the lot which is great to stop some theft and personal crime but not all.  A trip here and you can pretty much get all your shopping/errands done.  I highly recommend this center to anyone and everyone.

reviews.author: Vivian T
reviews.text: Expensive and blew all my money. It was a mess in some of the stores and people were smoking everywhere. I'd go back during Black Friday but that's it. Not very clean and had to run around four shops to find a bathroom.

reviews.author: Cesar Martinez
reviews.text: Nice part of town. There is several stores in a walking distance for you to go shopping,  the mall is not to far from here as well. Sidewalks are a bit dirty though.

shopping.name: CHANEL SAN FRANCISCO
shopping.address: 156 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: AFM 88
reviews.text: Emma was my SA and she was very helpful and sweet but she was absent the day I got my pair of shoes, so Emmanuel assisted me and is he rude! If you could help it, have anyone assist you but him! I hardly do reviews but I had to this time. I gave it 2 stars because of the bad service at that time, should’ve been one star but the other SA’s were nice.

reviews.author: G Gurleen
reviews.text: This location wasn’t great and SA was not the best she looked off because we didn’t like the bags and she was not nice about it. We did end up buying something, but it was overall not a great experience it doesn’t matter if you buy something or not but that should not give her a reason to be rude!

reviews.author: Dingyi Shen
reviews.text:
I have been a loyal Chanel customer for years, but my recent experience has been beyond disappointing. I purchased a bag last year, and to my shock, the chain broke the very first time I wore it. For a luxury brand that prides itself on craftsmanship, this is completely unacceptable.

I brought the bag to the boutique for repair on October 2024, and since then, I have received no updates whatsoever. Today is February 15, 2025, and I am still waiting, with no explanation for why the repair is taking so long. The lack of communication and customer service is extremely frustrating.

I expected better from Chanel, both in terms of quality and after-sales support. If this is how they treat their customers, I seriously doubt I will purchase from them again. Absolutely disappointing.

reviews.author: Juniper Moon
reviews.text: Outstanding service at this Chanel store. Right when you walk in, the sales people are so friendly and nice. There is currently a waiting list to enter the store, but it is definitely worth the wait. Deborah McCorkle helped me in the shoe department and she was so friendly and helpful. They definitely go above and beyond at this location and make your shopping experience a memorable one.

reviews.author: lifestyle- tale
reviews.text: I had a very friendly sales assistants and there where very helpful. Every request and wish was handled and got a pair of shoes I was quite a while on a hunt for. Parking lots in front of the store available.

shopping.description: Iconic purveyor of high-end, Chanel-branded goods.

shopping.name: Macy's Backstage
shopping.address: 170 O'Farrell St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Muni Hassan
reviews.text:
Worst experience ever

I had a bad experience at Macys. I bought a gift card from them and during the opening the PIN was corrupted. I called them for two weeks to resolve this issue and they told me that I had to wait. The first time they said two days and then they told me that I had to wait 30 days and nothing happened. They have futile procedures and I feel scammed and I will never deal with them again.

reviews.author: Ikya Palaparti
reviews.text: Very rude customer service. We asked if they could open the fitting room because it was only 5pm and they close at 7pm and the cashier that closes on Sunday evenings told us that we should “just change in front of everyone else because the fitting room is closed”. She said it in a very rude manner and was immediately dismissive of our question. If the fitting rooms are closed and they are unable to open them that’s fine. There is no reason for her to speak to us like that.

reviews.author: Elisa Dunivent
reviews.text: I have been to Macy's backstage 4 times in less than 2 weeks. They bring new stuff out everyday. I really like there shoe and purse selection the most. They have really good deals up there. In fact everything up there is a really good deal. They are open until 9 pm except on Sunday they close at 7pm.

reviews.author: Linda Braudy
reviews.text: Usually Backstage is in a Macy's basement.  Here it is on the top floor - #8. What I found delightful was it was very much in order - exceptionally neat and clean. Prices are very reasonable and you can find a great variety of items. Right now they are featuring Halloween - some really cute things.

reviews.author: Driea Boutotte
reviews.text: The customer service here is tragic! Hey they're open until 8 p.m. I asked to try something on at 6:52 p.m. I was told that the change rooms close at 7 so no I can try something on which is ridiculous it would have taken me less than five minutes to have tried on the dress that I had. So I gave up shopping for clothes and proceeded to get housewares together at 8 when the closing announcement came on the lady ran up to me and told me that they were closed and I had to put my merchandise back. I asked her if I could go to another register she said no I had to leave my merchandise upstairs and was incredibly rude about it the whole time. It blows my mind that after a year-and-a-half pandemic businesses can get away with customer service like this they should be grateful for every customer they have. It left such a bad taste in my mouth that I won't be shopping at Macy's for quite a while and usually a shop here twice a week at least. I know for a fact that the customer service at Nordstrom's and at Bloomingdales is considerably better, considering how he's treated I would rather pay the price is at Nordstrom and Bloomingdale's and get good customer service

shopping.name: Gump's San Francisco
shopping.address: 250 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Elena Podzorova
reviews.text: I was there in October. It’s like fairytale. I bought one ornament for memory about this place, magic moment and warm conversation with Joseph ☺️

reviews.author: Jeanie Henson
reviews.text: I wanted to thank one of your representatives for helping me with a order that I placed at the first of October.  She was kind enough to check the warehouse to see when it would be delivered, as it had been only back order. I received it this week and I just love it.  It is the denim color plaid throw.  I believe the representatives name was Tammy?  Please thank her for helping me and helping me get this before Christmas. I love your catalog and your customer service is great!  Thanks again.

reviews.author: Thomas Fletcher
reviews.text: What a place! It's now downsized to a much smaller luxury type of store. But still over the top. High quality. $$$ but still the old timey store one watched o. the old black and white movies. Fantastic staff. Stellar quality and their on line store seems to have much more merchandise. 💜💜💜💜💜

reviews.author: Maria Vandarakis
reviews.text: Gump’s is my go-to for unique and beautifully packaged gifts. The staff is amazing. Paul Price is one of the kindest people I’ve ever met.

reviews.author: Colin Huang
reviews.text: Great little ornament and Christmas decorations shop! They are on the pricier side.

shopping.description: Local retail legend with high-end gifts, housewares, jewelry, clothing & a famed holiday window.

shopping.name: Harajuku Hearts
shopping.address: 15 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: tara ntula
reviews.text: great store, i wish there were more stores like this. great selection of gothic lolita and similar styles.. the store has 2 names and on the front will be Angelic Pretty. Inside the store is sort of 2 stores.

reviews.author: PJ Simmons
reviews.text: Don’t recommend!! Photos aren’t allowed so you can’t see what kind of items they have now - the yen doesn’t match usd, and they said it was due to import pricing, but even then the price gap didn’t line up. Whether it is the company or not 77 bucks for a pair of socks is insane - even for Lolita fashion. Getting a full outfit here could cost hundreds, and you can find similar patterns and styles online at other reliable Lolita fashion sites.

reviews.author: StrawbzCerberus
