reviews.text: Amazing customer service! Shop girls are very kind, and always have tons of information and suggestions for you. It feels like a real connection and I appreciate that! They did a very amazing job at explaining everything I needed to know about garments and care!

reviews.author: mknsnh
reviews.text: Really amazing lolita store with super nice staff! Even when I was indecisive, they were patient with me and understood any complaints I had with a skirt. I ended up purchasing a beautiful dress and they packaged it nicely, easily fitting in my suitcase (and now own a Atelier-Pierrot bag~~) A go-to shop for lolitas who either live near or are traveling to San Francisco!

reviews.author: Michael Moore
reviews.text: Wow, what a place. Really enjoyed talking with the staff. We stopped in while visiting San Francisco as my middle child is a huge Lolita fan and this is the only shop she knew of in America. Both of the young ladies working were very engaging (Share a huge love of cats and Disney with my wife).

shopping.description: This importer of Japanese women's clothing specializes in edgy, flouncy dresses & casual streetwear.

shopping.name: Theory
shopping.address: 55 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Alison M C
reviews.text: Extremely friendly staff. I was chatting with Mike about the clothing and he was so helpful. I had been looking for slim fit cropped business slacks, which have gone out of fashion for Gen Z, but remain a universal classic IMHO. I was so pleased to find them here. They are not cheap, but the quality of the fabric is nice and they do look expensive if you see them on. I would definitely recommend checking in here for pants and the pretty blouses and elegant tops. Maybe catch a sale.

reviews.author: Linda L. Day
reviews.text: I own a pair of Theory pants that I really like. Noting the pull on pants on the website and prefering to shop where I can try on for fit, I phoned the Geary Street store to make sure it was open and got no answer. Just minutes later, Chelsey called me back, apologizing for missing the call and telling me that they were open. I went into the store, asked for Chelsey and told her I wanted to try the pull on pants in crepe, She was so gracious and helpful. The pants were not a good fit for me so I did not buy a pair, but I was treated so well and Chelsey was so helpful that I will go back when I need great style, fabrics, and craft in clothing. And I will ask for Chelsey.

reviews.author: Tim Haight
reviews.text: Great visit and thank you Mike for your help and hospitality.

reviews.author: Craig Fox
reviews.text: Visited this store while on vacation from NC. Beautiful clothing. Incredible staff. Bought way too much but my husband and I will definitely return.

reviews.author: Jo Winters
reviews.text:
Customer service just isn't the same as it was before. Came for the sale. Was offer only items that were not on sale.

hesitation In making sure you got the items for you. Awkwardly checking up on you. Use To be more pleasant. None of the workers seem to want to be there.

Man with glassses who was helping me was Barely listening to me, that's the worst thing!

and they charge for everything, altercations  and a shopping bag. I never been charged for this type of stuff before even at this same store.

Prices are high, but not that high. Store must be doing bad lately to charge for such things.

Never shopping here again!

shopping.name: Apple Union Square
shopping.address: 300 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: This is an amazing Apple Store location. It was not as crowded as I thought during my visit, which was on a Christmas midday. The iPhones were delicately placed to showcase an attractive scene/definitely a looker. The staff were absolutely very friendly and helpful too. For some reason, staff at Apple Stores have been significantly more kind compared to other electronics stores (such as Samsung, LG, etc. though it doesn’t quite exist in the US). Overall, I had a great experience.

reviews.author: Imran Shaikh
reviews.text:
Got my new iPhone 17 Pro Max.

Jason (Supervisor) - knowledgeable & helpful.
Celeste - trainee, limited knowledge

Alina - was outstanding, genuinely helpful and customer-focused. She made my day.
Thank you, ALINA!

reviews.author: cindy ngo
reviews.text:
Apple Union Square is one of the most impressive Apple Stores I’ve visited. Located right in the heart of San Francisco, the building is spacious, modern, and beautifully designed with huge glass doors and a welcoming public plaza.

The staff were helpful and friendly amazing place for tech lovers and curious visitors alike!

I came from Asia and I was wondering if I can exchange my old IPhone 14 Plus to a new IPhone or not. But the staff was very helpful to helped me exchanged the old one to the new one and how to keep my data as well.

So if you are a tourist but a tech lover you can consider to get yourself a new IPhone or any Apple product from here which is very easy, safe  and cheaper.

reviews.author: Joanna T
reviews.text:
I got my problem resolved at Apple Union Square. It was something really simple - my IPhone screen was unresponsive.
I tried to make an appointment for the Genius Bar at the Apple Union Square store - couldn’t do it. The issue I ran up against is that I needed to logon my Apple account.
Then the prompts wanted to send an authentication text to my IPhone.
Welp - that’s a problem, isn’t it? Luckily,
I can get to the store by bus. I know I have to go to the second floor (from previous needs) to purchase or fix something.
The two staffers with IPAD minis were talking with customers. They seemed to be having good person-to-person customer service - a big change from previous years. I waited patiently. Then one became free.
Rose was able to help me - I said simple problem, right?
She did a hard reset of my IPhone by pressing the up button on the left-hand side then the down button. Next she pressed and held the Right-side power up button until the Apple logo appeared. It did the job - hooray!
I guess now I know.
Glad the store is nearby! Glad it is a safe space for Apple customers - good security! They also have a spacious area for classes too. Happy ending!

reviews.author: Pezhman Raeisian
reviews.text:
Apple Union Square is one of the most impressive Apple Stores I’ve visited. Located right in the heart of San Francisco, the building is spacious, modern, and beautifully designed with huge glass doors and a welcoming public plaza. We stopped in during our walk through Union Square and enjoyed the open layout and calm atmosphere.

The staff were helpful and knowledgeable, offering quick assistance and product demos. Tip: Even if you’re not planning to buy, it’s worth stepping inside to see the architecture and use the public seating or free Wi-Fi. Great for tech lovers and curious visitors alike!

shopping.description: Apple retail store selling iPhones, iPads & more in sleekly designed spaces.

shopping.name: Maison Goyard
shopping.address: 118 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Marifel Miyaji
reviews.text: Amazing service! The whole process—from my first inquiry about a dog collar to the actual purchase—was seamless. Venus was very helpful and gave me great recommendations.

reviews.author: Christina Kim
reviews.text: I recently purchased the Anjou Mini from Goyard San Francisco and had the pleasure of working with Giles. Even though I don’t have a local store near me, Giles made the entire process seamless from start to finish. His communication was prompt, professional, and incredibly helpful. My bag arrived quickly and beautifully packaged. I’m so impressed with the level of service. Giles went above and beyond to make this such a pleasant experience. Highly recommend reaching out to him if you’re looking to shop with Goyard San Francisco!

reviews.author: Jules A
reviews.text: Wow, THIS is how brands can earn customers. Following the great reviews here I opted to visit SF boutique instead of my local LA one. Giles was kind and helpful, patient, and respectful - couldn’t have had a better experience deciding on my new bag. I came in once to check out a few options and then a few days later to try my laptop and things for size and both times was treated so kindly. I refuse to buy from boutiques that make me wait by the door. My time is precious and if I decide to consider a brand’s bag I believe they should court ME,  and not the other way around. This experience thanks to Giles and lovely associates and even a welcoming security guard who welcomed me in, was top notch. Bravo, SF Goyard team!

reviews.author: mimi
reviews.text: About two years ago, I emailed Sales Advisor Giles, about a product inquiry. He quickly and accurately guided me to exactly what I wanted. I contacted him again today, and he responded immediately. He truly seems like someone always prepared for his customers. He helped me place my order precisely, and I have absolutely no doubts about my first online order. Giles is very professional and kind. I hope I get a chance to visit the San Francisco store someday.👍🏼

reviews.author: Chloe Yu
reviews.text:
I had an amazing experience working with Giles at Goyard. He was incredibly professional, patient, and attentive throughout the entire distant order process. Even though everything was done remotely, Giles made the purchase feel smooth and effortless. He answered all my questions promptly, guided me through each step, and ensured that I felt confident with my order.

I truly appreciate his exceptional service and would highly recommend Giles to anyone looking to purchase from Goyard. Thank you for making the experience so pleasant!

shopping.description: Upscale retailer offering high-end leather luggage, trunks, handbags & wallets, plus special orders.

shopping.name: FASHIONPHILE Salon at Union Square
shopping.address: 291 Geary St #201, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: D
reviews.text:
I called sometime in September 2025, and at that time, an appointment was scheduled for about 3–4 weeks later.  They asked for my email address to send a confirmation and the necessary appointment details.

This morning (10/13), I received a text message, without any identification, asking if I was coming to the appointment. When I asked who they were, they replied that they were from Fashionphile.  I checked all my email accounts, including spam folders, and never received any confirmation or details.

I explained that without the promised email, the appointment didn’t appear on my calendar.  They quickly responded, “No problem, appointment cancelled.”  I pointed out that it is a problem since rescheduling would mean waiting even longer, and they need to follow through with the confirmations they promise.

Attached is the screenshot of our text exchange this morning.  I did receive a response “we apologize IF” 😒. Based on their reviews and this experience, I might’ve dodged a bullet…whew!

reviews.author: Leon Chen
reviews.text: Staff is gross and snarky. You should help a customer out with planning a sale if you can’t help them at the current moment. Advice: take your business to TheRealReal if you don’t want unprofessionalism.

reviews.author: julie kuri
reviews.text: I recently sold 2 bags at the NM location my experience couldn’t have been better. Adrian is very pleasant gave me an explanation of why I was getting the dollar amount. He let me know about the new location where I will be able to shop. I’ve never been disappointment with their service.Except for the receptionist customer service.

reviews.author: Tammy Nguyen
reviews.text: Forced to do my drop offs at this location since the Palo Alto location closed down. This is the most pain in the butt location with no parking, in a very risky location with high rates of theft. The customer service here is also poor. Very rude people. I don’t plan to ever come back.

reviews.author: S L
reviews.text: The team here is wonderful! Adrian and Miguel are always welcoming and such a delight. Very upfront about pricing. Remember it is a resell company, so you probably won’t get back the retail price you paid for unless you’re bringing in a highly coveted piece (ie birkin, kelly, etc). If you go in with this mindset, you won’t be disappointed. If you are unhappy with the quoted price, you can always say “no, thank you” and take your items back with no hard feelings. The team does not push you to part with anything you don’t feel comfortable with.

shopping.description: High-end consignment boutique for secondhand luxury handbags & accessories in a stylish interior.

shopping.name: Bottega Veneta San Francisco
shopping.address: 124 Geary St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: For Sale
reviews.text: Beautiful high-end boutique with a rich assortment of Italian leather goods, accessories, and clothing. Quiet luxury at its finest! The minimalist store design is inspiring, like artwork in a museum. I had a favorable experience with an associate who offered wonderful customer service, was attentive and helpful, and without presumption or pompous.

reviews.author: M
reviews.text: We had such a terrific shopping experience on our recent SF trip.   I honestly just went in looking and wasn't planning to purchase but they were so patient and kind even after I told them I was just looking that day.   I ended up leaving with a bag I didn't expect to like so much!  Thank you!

reviews.author: Yuvika Singh
reviews.text:
This review is solely for Whitney!!

Came in unsure about whether to purchase because they didn’t have the color I initially wanted but Whitney was sooo patient as I took my time deciding and was the perfect balance of being helpful and giving me the little push I needed without being overbearing.
She was so knowledgeable about the brand and products as well.
Felt like I was talking to a friend! Highly recommend asking for her if you come in.

reviews.author: Wang SiHan
reviews.text: Karin is sooo helpful and amazing. All the employees are team work well and pretty. Stores design cool. Check it out!

reviews.author: natasha hopps
reviews.text: Loved my buying experience today. Both people who assisted me were exceptional, great knowledge, genuine love of their product and went above and beyond to help make the right purchase for me. I felt invited to be there, one of the best shopping experiences in a long time.

shopping.description: Upmarket Italian brand specializing in leather handbags, plus ready-to-wear women's & men's fashion.

shopping.name: Relic Vintage
shopping.address: 1475 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Rebecca Halas
reviews.text: Relic vintage really is one of the best true vintage stores on Haight street. They have an amazing collection of adorable dresses from all eras and they're in perfect shape. They have everything you need to be styled in head to toe vintage. The hats and jewelery are also perfect for any occasion. The owner is really cool and all the staff are sweet and helpful! The store has a casual but sleek style. I love the round bench that's apolstered in amazing fabric. Besides it being comfortable,Your boyfriend can sit  while you try on clothes. There's plenty of amazing vintage for men too. Last time I was there the owner added holes to a belt so that it was a custom fit. Check RELIC out. It would be hard to leave empty handed!!

reviews.author: Zoe Gatti
reviews.text: If you're into quality vintage, look no further. I was blown away by the collection at Relic. Everything is in pristine condition - cashmere sweaters and wool coats from the 50s, 60s, etc. This store is so unique and the staff are super positive and bright. Can't wait to return to Relic!

reviews.author: Steve H
reviews.text: Super high-end and Niche vintage store fud a very set, dandy, fabulous yesteryear set of offerings in clothing, furs, hats, jewelry. Not your typical vintage shop. They'll be nothing that isn't Hollywood fabulous from like the 1930s or '40s in here.

reviews.author: Kelly Scollard
reviews.text: Stumbled into this store on a free afternoon before a wedding I was in town for. The collection is incredible. The prices are very fair and you can tell there is a lot of care taken on the items. The manager was super friendly and helpful. I found a party dress from the 50s that I loved so much I decided to wear it to the wedding two hours later. The manager sewed up a tiny rip in the dress on the spot for me free of charge in less than ten minutes. I love this store I wish I lived nearby.

reviews.author: Emma Craig
reviews.text: This place is aptly named relic in that the whole experience is like a relic of a bygone era when store proprietors knew their customer’s names and sizes and found just the right clothing to suit them. The proprietor, Orin, was ever the expert, doting attentively on my friends who were looking for feminine dresses that suited their body types. He found just the pieces for them, knowing their sizes without even using the measuring tape draped over his shoulders. My friends and I agreed that the service was what truly made the experience, and we will definitely be coming back.

shopping.description: Funky boutique dealing in vintage clothing & accessories from the 1920s-1970s, for both men & women.

shopping.name: Love Street Vintage
shopping.address: 1801 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Courtney Nichols
reviews.text:
★★★★★ Absolute gem in San Francisco

I love Love Street Vintage. Every visit feels like a perfectly curated treasure hunt…immaculate racks, incredible range of eras, and pieces that feel genuinely special (not picked-over or overpriced).

You can feel the care and intention behind every item in the shop. It’s the kind of place you bring out-of-town friends to show off SF style, and the kind of place locals return to again and again.

If you love fashion with soul, history, and impeccable taste, Love Street Vintage is it. ✨

reviews.author: Athena Rose
reviews.text: The best and most underrated vintage store in San Francisco. Love Street has such a great vibe. The selection is amazing too, featuring authentic vintage items. It's worth the price in my opinion! I highly recommend this spot to anyone. I'm from the neighborhood so I visit a lot. Super cool there's vintage stores popping up in the inner Richmond

reviews.author: Dani F
reviews.text: Came here an hour and half before closing and spent 20-30 minutes trying to find parking. Highly disappointed with the fact they closed early. No note on the door or anything. They were definitely open as we were looking for parking. I’ve never been to any store that closes early with no warning :/ maybe next time will be a better experience…

reviews.author: RomanVillarreal
reviews.text: Very nicely curated vintage clothing shop. I bought a 70s Levis chambray shirt and a Japanese kimono that I didn't know I needed in my life. The couple (owners?) working there were very kind and friendly. Great shop!

reviews.author: Christie Angelo
reviews.text: I have a lot of respect for this shop and it’s owner.  The store is gorgeously presented - the clothes are to die for and the prices are very fair for the quality.  The owner is very present and has a great eye for wonderful items.  It is on a quiet street with lots of parking. A definite go to for vintage girlies (and guys).

shopping.description: Snug secondhand shop offering a changing selection of vintage clothing, jewelry, hats & bags.

shopping.name: No Shop
shopping.address: 389 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Alison M C
reviews.text: Band shirts $20-85. I found one I like for $22. Nice staff.

reviews.author: Chiara Gallo
reviews.text: Very nice selection! Bought 2 adorable and unique products

reviews.author: Mileva King
reviews.text: Nice shop, cool clothes, a bit expensive. Lighting very poor in dressing rooms - would recommend asking to examine clothes in a better light. Purchased two very cute pairs of vintage corduroy shorts to find stains on them at home which were not visible in-store.

reviews.author: nest at.15 (nest.at.15)
reviews.text:
Visited today... Loved this shop!
Had a really good amount of plus size clothing too, which is usually impossible in a vintage shop!!
Cool vibe without being intimidating.
Reasonable prices. Ie. Outerwear for under $50, dresses $30 and T-shirt’s $15-20

Just wish I didn’t live 6000 miles away!!

reviews.author: Vic C
reviews.text:
This is probably my favorite vintage store in the city.  The prices are not outrageous (for the street it's on) and I always walk out feeling inspired by the unique pieces in solid condition.  I especially enjoy their outerwear, have bought 2 things from here, both got a lot of compliments.
The staff seems to be a mix of very polite/helpful to 'can't wait to gtfo', depending on the day haha.  If it were more consistent I'd give it 5 stars, but I guess we all have our off days.

shopping.description: Vintage shop with an offbeat vibe carrying clothing, jewelry & accessories for men & women.

shopping.name: Valencia Street Vintage
shopping.address: 714 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Pilar Johnson
reviews.text: Located conveniently on Valencia Street with nearby shopping and cafes, this thrift store is a true find. They have $15 sales racks and a unique selection of silk dresses priced around $150. It's the perfect spot for budget-friendly and one-of-a-kind fashion finds.

reviews.author: ari salomon
reviews.text:
After operating in the outside parklet for a while VSV now has its own amazingly cute space. It’s very thoughtfully designed and lots of treasures are found in each nook.

Antique handbags, boots, rings and many dresses. Baseball hats, jeans, China and glass cups. It’s a small space but lots to keep your eye interested. I didn’t buy anything this first visit but will be visiting again soon.

reviews.author: Melanie Salazar
reviews.text: Very nice store. Glad it was open until 8pm on a Friday unlike so many other stores on this street. I found a beautiful dress to wear for my baby shower for $45! Yay! It was a great find.

reviews.author: Tigran Karakhanyan
reviews.text:
Got this beautiful coat for $15, it was quite likely washed too.

Quite happy with it, would come back.

Only caveat is that most of the things they have is quite expensive, so you have to be either lucky or rick :D

reviews.author: Thomas Wood
reviews.text:
Went in out of curiosity as it was open later. Good selection of items. Had nice snoopy air fresheners, was curious about the market rate for them but phone battery was low so i didn’t check at that time. Later when I did check, I found they were being sold at or below online market value, which being a local shop and not having to pay for shipping meant these snoopy air fresheners were a great value. Regret not buying them when I had the chance.

Also people working there were very nice and knowledgeable about their woolrich coat, and assume similar knowledge of their other wares.

shopping.name: Wasteland
shopping.address: 1660 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Sen Liang
reviews.text: Nice spot to shop for statement pieces and elevate your wardrobe. They carry many well-known luxury brands, and the selection is impressive. Prices are on the higher side, but you’re paying for big-name branding and quality.

reviews.author: Donna Oran Valente
reviews.text: Bit grungy. They sale at higher than Goodwill prices for not necessarily vintage clothing but offer less $1 per item at intake. Much better off at Buffalo or CrossRoads getting 50% of store credit on moderately priced items for selling. Also much nicer, cleaner stores.

reviews.author: Jesse Lee
reviews.text: Lots of cool vintage stuff here, from dresses, shirts, shoes and accessories. Some items are a good deal but some top name items are very expensive. Didn’t find much is smaller sizes.

reviews.author: Michael Guynn
reviews.text:
Wasteland is, admittedly, not for me. I was under the impression it was a thrift store - it is not. It is more accurately an expertly curated used and vintage designer clothing store.

Now, I can appreciate the curation. There was a lot of Givenchy, Balenciaga, Supreme, Gucci, and some more down-to-earth brands like Doc Martens and Levi's.

My four star review comes from the sort of haphazard nature of the collection. If you're going to the effort of collecting all these amazing garments, why stop there? As far as I could tell, the only things sorted by size in the store were the men's pants. Everything else was just wherever, with no labels on the hangers or anything. Maybe this is to keep you in the store longer.

Anyway, great collection, expensive stuff, but if that's what you're into you'll probably find something here.

reviews.author: sezin akdeniz
reviews.text: My husband purchased a Burberry scarf from this store, but we later noticed that the label appeared to be incorrect, with “Loncon” instead of “London.” This raised concerns for us about the authenticity of the product. it affected our confidence in purchasing branded items here. I would recommend being careful and checking products carefully before buying

shopping.description: Stylish outfit known for its curated selection of used vintage & modern women's clothes.

shopping.name: Body Philosophy Club
shopping.address: 111 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Consuelo Reyes Lopez
reviews.text: BPC is one of my favorite vintage stores in the city! Belle is so kind, thoughtful, and overall has incredible taste. I’ve gotten some of my favorite closet staples, accessories, and home goods from her store and can’t recommend this place enough! I also love the overarching concept and values of the store and Belle’s philosophy on style :) It’s also amazing that she’s a proud small business owner who cares deeply about other small businesses, our city, and the people who are a part of it. I love supporting businesses that are people-centric and loudly and proudly share their values with the world—we need more of that! ❤️

reviews.author: Carly N Miller
reviews.text:
Local here! Easily one of, if not the best, vintage store in SF! The selection is always on point and I can’t leave without finding one wardrobe staple every time I go in. The variety of sizes that Belle aims to offer is so impressive and is very much appreciated in a world of vintage that often leaves extended sizing out. I bring every person that visits me to BPC to find their next fave piece!!

Belle & Mary are always warm, inviting, and care deeply about speaking on behalf of their neighbors far and wide. I appreciate all of the work that they do to advocate for their fellow small businesses, other SF residents, and folks in need globally, in addition to curating an incredible selection of clothes. Can’t recommend this place enough!

reviews.author: Niharika Bhat
reviews.text: Amazing selection of beautiful vintage pieces! Belle does an incredible job of sourcing truly unique items and creating a warm, safe, fun place to shop. Some of my most worn and loved items of all time are from BCC. Belle is also very helpful if you’re looking for something specific and will guide you to items/sizes that might work best for you. If you’re in the Richmond, you have to stop by - especially on a Sunday to tag team with a farmers market day :)

reviews.author: Emily Rudisill
reviews.text: I've been shopping here for years and love the new spot on Clement! The owner has an excellent eye for quality, texture, color, and style. I've been lucky to snag some gorgeous Heath pieces from the 70s, lush wool sweaters, unique beverage glasses, and several Dansk cooking pieces. Truly, their taste is impeccable!

reviews.author: Rosie Chester
reviews.text: If I could give 10 stars I would! Frequenting BPC weekly has become one of my favorite pastimes since living in the area and their carefully curated collection of pieces are classic, tailored, FUN, and there truly is something for everyone. Not to mention the small but mighty staff are some of the kindest and most genuine humans. My favorite vintage store in SF 💌

shopping.name: Missing Audrey Vintage
shopping.address: 1767 Stockton St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Kenyon J
reviews.text: What an incredible shop in North Beach! The items are so unique and authentic Vintage! The owner Sabrina is amazing, so nice, and is so knowledgable about fashion/vintage. Natalie who works here is so helpful too. Look no further for one of a kind Vintage finds in San Francisco!

reviews.author: Edwin Heaven
reviews.text:
Hey, did you hear the one about the OG who walked into a vintage shop in North Beach and thought he'd been transported back to the 70s and 80s? Because everything looked brand spanking new.

That OG, of course, is me. And when my eyes fell upon a genuine Playboy cap in cherry condition, it was love at first sight. It turned out to be one of the best purchases I’ve ever made. People stop me on the street just to ask where I got it. "Hefner’s mansion?" I grin and tell them "I wish!" Then tell them where I scored it ("Missing Audrey Vintage!") and that I’m sure they, too, will discover something they'll fall in love with at first sight.

The charming proprietress, by the way—Sabrina, named after the celebrated Audrey Hepburn character—is way too young to know so much about the fashions, music and films of the past. I figure she has to be some kind of a savant. Perhaps that explains why, in a neighborhood with more than a few vintage shops,  hers is the coolest.

reviews.author: Matthis Pluska
reviews.text: Cutest new vintage shop in North Beach! Sabrina the owner is young hip and gorgeous! She has the best taste in fashion, accessories, and interior decor. She also takes requests if you’re looking something specific — highly recommend!

reviews.author: Andrew Gallagher
reviews.text: I picked up some really awesome cowboy boots from here — I look like a real muleskinner! Sabrina is charming, beautiful, and charismatic. She really knows what’s cool.

reviews.author: Amy B
reviews.text:
Cutest neighborhood gem :)

One of the best vintage shops in San Francisco. I especially love their collection of slips, cool accessories, and vintage tees. The staff, especially Sabrina, are so lovely and kind! They’ll help you with any questions you have. The selection is wonderfully curated and fully of great, unique pieces that are reasonably priced.

Definitely stop in to browse (you may even leave with a 1963 playboy)!!

shopping.name: Decades Of Fashion
shopping.address: 1653 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Christian Atendido
reviews.text:
Decades is a true vintage shop that just feels like San Francisco. The selection is beautiful unique, and intentional, but what really makes the place special is Cicely. She’s a total character in the best way and truly the life of the store. Warm, funny, and effortlessly stylish, she brings the whole space to life.

Decades perfectly captures that bold energy and somehow reflects the spirit of the Lower Haight itself. A genuine neighborhood treasure and one of those places that reminds you why this city is so special.

reviews.author: Tracy Shapoff
reviews.text: I love this store. Everytime I visit SF, this is my favorite vintage store to shop (and has been for many years). I love how perfectly organized is by decade, but also merchandised in a way that you can look in any direction and find something unexpected and fabulous. There is a wide price range from low to high, and I feel like there is something for everyone if you love past decades of fashion. Really unique, beautiful accessories.

reviews.author: Justin Thomas
reviews.text:
I really wanted to thank Decades of Fashion and the manager that was on duty on October 10, I know it started with an M, and I think another lady named Linda. I moved to the Pacific Northwest/Seattle about five years ago. My husband used to work at Love of the Ganesha when we lived in San Francisco

The company I work for in PNW was having an 80s party as our annual company event. My team harassed me into actually getting dressed up, because normally I’m pretty strait laced and don’t dress up for events.  I knew I wanted to be an 80s rocker that was attending the MTV musical awards -   so 1983!   but I couldn’t find one place in Seattle where I could get everything I needed.

And I remembered Decades of Fashion - I knew that’s where I needed to go. So I flew down on Thursday night. And was at their door on Friday at noon. The two ladies there were so incredibly helpful and did everything they could to make me feel comfortable, even wearing Ass less chaps.   we for glitter // hair // accessories.   I knew it was right when the total of my purchases came up to 666!!! 👽👽

I flew back to Seattle on Saturday morning and attended the event on Saturday night and my team had no idea who I was when I walked in the door.   again, I am so appreciative of the team there and the election that they have in order to get dressed up

reviews.author: Jacquie Tio
reviews.text:
The store is beautiful. As soon as you walk in, you’re greeted by a thoughtfully curated outfit that immediately sets the tone for the space. The staff are incredibly helpful and attentive, making the whole experience feel personal and welcoming. We were lucky enough to chat with the owner, who turned out to be a truly lovely person—warm, passionate, and clearly dedicated to what she does.

Stepping into the store feels like getting a peek into other people’s closets from different decades. Each piece seems to carry a story, carefully selected to reflect a mix of eras and styles. It’s more than just shopping; it’s like time-traveling through fashion, discovering treasures that feel both nostalgic and timeless.

reviews.author: Liz Huang
reviews.text: What a great store! An incredible selection of well curated vintage through the decades, you won’t walk out of here empty handed. And additional note? Cecily the owner is fantastic and keeps the cleanest, most welcoming store. Highly recommend!

shopping.description: Emporium for vintage clothing, shoes, accessories & costumes, dating from the 1880s to the 1980s.

shopping.name: ReLove San Francisco
shopping.address: 1815 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Alyssa Cisneros
reviews.text:
Love this store! One of my favorite consignment shops to visit for unique, designer and well priced items.

The staff are both stylish and super friendly; and always willing to talk fashion!

Big shout out to Toni who makes the buying process easy, and super fun.

reviews.author: Tiffany Lindsey
reviews.text: Micheal and Trinity were exceptionally helpful and knowledgeable today! Talk about customer service! They gave pro tips and found exactly what I was hinting for. Clean shop and lovely tunes. We found many treasures from the wide selection and had fun trying everything on as well.

reviews.author: Katie Schenkkan
reviews.text: One of my 2-3 favorite consignment places in the city! Selection is consistently great, a good mix of high/low, and the staff are all amazing. Been shopping here for 15+ years at this point and now regularly sell to them too. Toni and crew are so lovely!

reviews.author: alyssa
reviews.text:
DO NOT COME HERE! they are racist and assume you’re stealing without even saying anything. was looking at clothes and an employee came to
me and offered to take the clothes i was looking
at to the dressing room. i go to the dressing room and while waiting for a room i saw they put the pants i was looking at behind the counter and didn’t let me try them on. the white girls with handfuls of clothes cut me in line and got into dressing rooms before me.

reviews.author: Tergel Purevdorj
reviews.text: Love this spot. I’ve been shopping here for almost 10 years! Got some incredible clothes here! Great designers. I got the blue jacket, white coat and the burnt orange wrap!

shopping.description: Stylish shop showcasing a curated selection of on-trend new & used clothes for men & women.

shopping.name: LOVED AGAIN
shopping.address: 4068 24th St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Daisy Brambila
reviews.text: Gilbertina is the sweetest!! She has an impeccable taste for fashion and even makes her own jewelry, which is on display. I found these beautiful, well loved boots here and she made me feel so at home. 100% recommend this place!

reviews.author: Christie Megill
reviews.text: It was a total joy to browse the racks here, with everything so lovingly cared for and curated. I found some amazing items and can’t wait to return to look more.

reviews.author: laura labrado
reviews.text:
Hello "Loved Again" and thank you for your feedback. Unfortunately it is inconsistent to the actual events that took place. First and foremost the co-owner flat out lied to me about her role in the company. Second she was unapologetic to the damage done to my bag which had a value of $350-$400 used on platforms such as E-Bay and Thread Up  in used condition.
Third she insisted that there was nothing wrong with the bag even after being shown the damage on the physical bag and pictures of the bag taken minutes after leaving "Loved Again"
My bag was like new as your company accepted it for the selling price I asked for which was $350-$300 had it been presented to you the way it was returned to me I am confident it would not of been accepted for that selling price. The unprofessionalism that I experienced and the flat out attitude of "oh well we are not responsible for damage or theft" is unacceptable, rude and consistent to the attitude of the co-owner that was present. I feel I should have been given the price that the bag "was" worth before being damaged while in your possession, and not the amount I was offered. The bag is now ruined and in the possession of "Loved Again" and hopefully "Loved Again does not attempt to sell the bag to make money off of my loss. Additionally if "Loved Again" holds itself blameless for damage that could be prevented then this is a business that the community should be cautious of.
I hope that good can come of this and "Loved Again" will take better care of property that is not their own.

reviews.author: Yelena Kozlova
reviews.text: Great taste, super well curated store. I forgot my belt and popped in to find something - I found my new favorite belt that was more interesting than I could have imagined. A friend came in with me and found the perfect jack. Can't wait to come back again!

reviews.author: laura labrado
reviews.text:
There should be a 0 star option for this entity. The owner is dishonest. Use caution when interacting with this consignment shop. Take pictures of the front, back and other angles of all your items at time of drop off while in the establishment. I dropped off a suede and leather purse it was returned to me faded. Owner insists that they are not responsible for theft damage because their documents state that they are not responsible for theft or damage. I assumed this establishment was professional and experienced in this business, and had I known my items were unsafe and would be returned to me damaged I would not have  left them.
If your items are damaged at this establishment you will need to ruffle some feathers to resolve the matter and even then the owner will Not be professional and will give the lowest price to resolve the matter.
I will stay aware from consignment shops as this experience made a poor reputation for them.

shopping.name: Sensitive Vintage Divisadero
shopping.address: 802 Divisadero St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Crystal Sanchez
reviews.text:
Sensitive Vintage is on another level. The curation is incredibly thoughtful and every piece feels intentional. The pricing is surprisingly fair for the quality and condition of the items, and you can tell a lot of care goes into what makes it onto the racks.

What really sets it apart is how often new pieces rotate in. Every visit feels different and there is always something unexpected to discover. I also love learning about where the clothing comes from. Many pieces are sourced internationally and often found during travels!!

The space feels elevated without being intimidating. It is refined, well curated, and clearly driven by taste rather than trends.

reviews.author: Daniella Datorre
reviews.text:
Big Sensitive Vintage fan! Always has great selections and you can tell how much intention goes into the curation of what is in the shop. It’s more than just a shop, it’s a community. So stoked about the new location. Highly recommend shopping here and supporting.

Also, Sivan is just the best 🩷

reviews.author: a mystery
reviews.text: I've been loving Sensitive Vintage ever since they started in the Mission - so glad that they are now on Divis in this beautiful space! Every time I visit I find a new gem that's been thoughtfully selected by Sivan. Sivan and Evan are the dreamiest people- they support local art, help neighbors & friends out, and have an instinctive ability to curate for style & beauty. This store is exactly why we love San Francisco!!!! The HEART!

reviews.author: Rhone Baker
reviews.text: Warm and welcoming environment. You can tell how much the lady at the register loves the clothes and loves helping customers find a new addition to their collection. They also have gift cards which have saved my ass for Christmas! Don’t just come here for the top tier selection of true vintage clothes but also Come for the authentic and special atmosphere that is being provided by the workers. SF needs more shops like this

reviews.author: Vicky Chung
reviews.text: Wow, Sensitive has such a fantastic curation of vintage clothes and accessories! I’ll be the first to admit that I don’t usually love vintage/thrift clothing shopping; for me it feels totally exhausting and like it’s not worth my time, because most stores have such a hodgepodge of random… stuff. Like there was no thought behind the selections. But treasure hunting here was so fun! I was able to snag a 70s silk top and an upcycled Levi’s denim jacket. Will definitely be back!

shopping.name: Held Over
shopping.address: 1543 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Daniel Mula
reviews.text: This is the ultimate vintage thrift store from leather jacket, special cord. Bell bottom pants. There’s some really nice fine in here. Highly recommend checking this place out.

reviews.author: Anita Autorino
reviews.text: I’ve been to San Francisco twice and both times I made sure to stop by Held Over, and I never regretted it. They have an amazing curation of vintage clothes and leather pieces, and the coat selection is especially great. Both times I visited I ended up buying something. The staff is super friendly, the music is always on point, and the store is very well organized by sizes and even by dates. Prices are also really budget-friendly compared to other vintage shops on the same street. I’d definitely go back!

reviews.author: Jas G
reviews.text: This is a really cool store. I had so much fun just looking around and exploring what they had. I think the prices were good and I ended up buying a really nice sweater. I think it's fun place to check out if you're in the area!!

reviews.author: Alex Coba
reviews.text:
[Update] I reached out to the assistant manager, who assured me they would speak with the employee. They also offered me a gift card, which I appreciated but ultimately declined. My main reason for calling was to ensure this doesn’t happen to anyone else. Overall, I feel better about the situation and appreciate being heard.

Had a really uncomfortable experience here. A staff member barged into the dressing room while I was changing because they heard a metal sound and assumed I was removing a security tag. It was just my bag strap. I tried to brush it off, but that’s not okay, and it could easily happen to someone else. Luckily, I was almost done changing, but what if I wasn’t? If you think someone’s stealing, wait outside and ask, don’t just barge in. I'm not planning on coming back anytime soon, if ever.

reviews.author: Cynthia E
reviews.text:
Super well organized and quality vintage clothing, both US and European.

Aisles are wide enough to accommodate folks using mobility aids.

shopping.description: Colorful shop stocking an array of secondhand retro & vintage clothing, hats & accessories.

shopping.name: Afterlife Collective
shopping.address: 541 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Diana Reyes Ruiz
reviews.text: I was walking down the street when a window display stopped me in my tracks! I had so much fun browsing through all the clothing. I love their vintage selections and the quality is amazing. Once I picked out my purchases, I was instantly set on them. These are truly rare pieces… yes, a little on the pricey side, but absolutely worth it. They’ll last for years! Lovely customer service 🫶🏼

reviews.author: raymond
reviews.text:
A highly unsatisfied online experience.
Purchasing American vintage at significant markups is understandable, but for a store to not honor their prices or offered discounts where my order was not fulfilled (to be refunded) until numerous days after; leaves a first-time shopper bummed for such services.

reviews.author: Senjuti
reviews.text: This and Valencia Vintage might be my favorite thrift stores on Valencia Street. I found a beautiful teal silk dress with a fun 60s style pattern on it for a NYE party. The collection seems pretty curated.

reviews.author: Gemma
reviews.text:
I was visiting out of town, not prepared for the chilly SF weather. But, I had already wanted to check out some vintage clothing scene here. Although I did not make it to some of the higher rated ones around the city, I did walk into four stores along Valencia Street:  Rare Vintage Finds, Valencia Street Vintage, Big Time Vintage, and Afterlife Collective. Afterlife won hands down for the great setup and flow of the store, lots of clothes options but organized, had a clean vintage vibe going on and the staff were really kind and friendly. Found a really cool, red, Free People jacket for $52. One of those purchases I feel great about, wear it all the time, has a stylish yet vintage flare to it, and it kept me a warm the rest of the trip.
The other vintage stores either did not have much clothes or the layout of the store was just meh and not as much unique items that I could see. Valencia Street Vintage in particular did not have a lot to offer for clothing.

reviews.author: Johan Naviel
reviews.text: Great selection of clothing and accessories, each little section if a different vendor. I will say that some of the vendors have prices at a premium so keep that in mind! I fell in love with a few pieces and I just couldn’t justify some of the cost. I will say that some of the vendors are open for a slight discount so don’t forget to ask!

shopping.description: Intimate store specializing in used '80s- & '90s-era T-shirts, jackets, sunglasses, jewelry & bags.

shopping.name: Moody Goose Vintage
shopping.address: 2036 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Zianne Agustin
reviews.text: Sooo many good (and fairly priced) pieces here ⭐️ Overall just a great shopping experience. The energy felt light, and the curation was tasteful. ✨🤍 I especially loved the upcycled pieces I came across, very unique. Staff were also very warm and diligent. Would def come back the next time I’m in SF! In the meantime: please open a store in LA 🙏🥹

reviews.author: Benny Giang
reviews.text: so many great finds! one of the best curated thrift stores in the city. plus they have this awesome photo booth!

reviews.author: Carolina Gray
reviews.text: This store had a really great vintage clothing selection and the prices were fair. It’s very cute on the inside and it’s fun to browse thru their clothes even if you don’t make a purchase. Also, I found the staff to be very friendly and helpful. I will definitely stop by the next time I’m in San Francisco.

reviews.author: Angelina Hazzouri
reviews.text: i've found some of my favorite pieces here over the last few years. they have such amazing stuff and it's all very reasonably priced. love the staff too

reviews.author: Kara Vining
reviews.text: Have found amazing pieces!! In my opinion the prices are good because the clothes are unique, good brands and good quality! I love going here a couple times a month when they get new inventory

shopping.name: Chameleon Vintage Archive
shopping.address: 1572 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: AKIRRIA
reviews.text: bought one of my DREAM pieces from this place and the prices are perfect for good quality clothing!! also I truly believe all the workers here are so beautiful and cutie oh my goodness please this place needs more recognition!!🩷🩷 shout out to the worker who let me take this photo I was so excited and happy!!

reviews.author: Anne-Ma H.
reviews.text: I found some unique pieces and a friend. The staff is made of generous artists from SF, that's what makes the place so magical.

reviews.author: Mama B
reviews.text: Nice store with friendly staff. They also have imported pieces.

reviews.author: Matusala Berhe
reviews.text: I love the atmosphere & music they play as soon as I walk in. Great staff always super friendly & helpful. Great selection of items ranging from Japanese designer to American vintage! My favorite place to shop on Haight

reviews.author: Nyx
reviews.text: Good selection and really friendly staff with a nice variety of styles within their niche. Only complaint is most the clothing is size medium and higher.

shopping.name: Try studio shop
shopping.address: 1148 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Peter Anthony Marchetti
reviews.text: I came here looking for a gift for my wife. I was really impressed with the selection! They had vintage clothes, home decor, cool art (everything my wife loves). I had a hard time deciding what to buy with so many great options, but thankfully Poyz and Ari were very helpful. They clearly put a lot of thought and love into curating their store. I’ll be coming back!

reviews.author: Ella P
reviews.text: Such a lovely store with a beautifully curated selection of clothes, jewelry, and home décor items. The owners are incredibly friendly and welcoming, and every piece they sell feels special and unique. I stopped by for the first time today, and I’ll definitely be coming back more often!

reviews.author: Ming Wai
reviews.text: Warm welcome and great selection of clothes and home decor so definitely coming back to see what’s new. So glad I dropped by and got a deal on a wool dress for the fall.

reviews.author: chawatpon Pondkornpanit
reviews.text: If you are looking for vintage style items. Don’t miss Try Studio Shop in Inner Sunset SF. They add new chic items every week. You will definitely get one item back home with you.

reviews.author: Ratsameedara
reviews.text: What a cute place! Got two adorable shirts from there. Would love to recommend this shop to everyone. Much love!

shopping.name: Blue Bin Vintage
shopping.address: 1525 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Alisa B.
reviews.text: While walking down Haight St, this thrift shop caught my eye! So much vintage sports attire: t-shirts, jerseys, and raver jackets. I really loved the selection. A lot of trending style and local fashion. The inside is very chill. No pressure to buy anything and they have changing rooms too. I took some SF Giants tees home with me! Great condition. FYI - They also take Apple Pay.

reviews.author: maite aguilera
reviews.text: I went to this store and I knew I had to leave a review! Not only this store has a really good collection of clothing, but everything is in a great shape, very clean and beautifully decorated. The girl that helped me I think her name was Claudia was not only super knowledgeable but so nice and attentive! All in all a great experience! Thanks BB Vintage!! ♥️

reviews.author: Bravin Phan
reviews.text: One of my favorite places to visit on Haight Street! Good vintage pieces, a little pricey but definitely worth it.

reviews.author: Mimi Mikhailov
reviews.text: selection is pretty good (more of a y2k focus which I personally enjoy) but kinda overpriced, pretty similar to most vintage stores like this on Haight. employees are friendly and the atmosphere is chill, and compared to other stores there’s a lot more space on the racks and to sit down, which is a relief.

reviews.author: Starting_Lua STARTED
reviews.text: I never write reviews but I had to after the horrible experience I had today. In the store my friend turned and accidentally knocked over a clothing rack. He profusely apologized to the workers, but was met with extreme unnecessary attitude by the female worker with long brown hair. As he apologized she rudely told him to get out of the way. I understand that it can be frustrating to deal with that but as a worker it is your job to be professional and not give unnecessary attitude. Every interaction we had with her after proceeded to be extremely rude and it ruined our thrifting experience. We will not be returning ever again.

shopping.name: The Golden Hour
shopping.address: 147 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Luba Muzichenko
reviews.text: I’ve known the shopkeeper, Morgan, a long time. She and her dog Franky always bring a smile in my face whenever I walk into the shop! You can always be welcomed with a smile but much more than that. Morgan has a highly curated selection of awesome vintage clothing and as I am revamping my wardrobe and looking for pieces that are classic and timeless, I can always count on finding a new staple from my wardrobe when I visit the shop. She’s also very involved in the local Richmond District community, which I love since I live in the neighborhood. 😘

reviews.author: Eoin Caffrey
reviews.text: This shop is nice. Has an amazing selection of all kinds of things to really bring any outfit together. I love the blend of different eras that ye have so perfectly created.  Also love the cool music ye play, and such friendly staff.  Served by a most friendly young girl who really understood style. Good conversation too. Said she’s studying in Ireland but back in the Bay Area for break.  What a diverse staff. Again an amazing customer experience. 5 stars

reviews.author: Ronnie Ghose
reviews.text:
Super friendly people with an awesome store. They helped explain the difference between different kinds of candles, and the cones then also lit a few of each so we could smell the difference and gave us a few extra.

Owners were super friendly and told us about history of the store and some of the cool stuff inside!

They also had an adorable small pug

reviews.author: Cherry Awad
reviews.text: Walked in and the cashier with the curly hair told me and my friend to go to the Salvation Army instead of shopping in the store bc it was “curated luxury fashion” and “high end” and gave us a judgy look. We were literally about to buy a coat.

reviews.author: Skyler Mapes
reviews.text: The Golden Hour is amazing! I am a vintage fanatic, so I'm always looking for new places to sell my vintage then shop for more. I've shopped vintage all over the bay area, but this store remains one of my all times favorites. I drive from Oakland to San Francisco just to go here and shop up and down Clement street. The selection and diversity of clothing can't be beat. They have everything from women's dresses to Men's bow-tie's. What's even better is one of the owners is a costume designer and the other is an interior designer, so they KNOW their stuff. They also have vintage designer hand bags and shoes. What more could a girl want? Oh, jewelry...  They also have an entire display case dedicated to hand-made jewelry made by local artists. Overall this place is the BOMB! But the cherry on top is on Sunday's they have the farmer's market from 10-2. It' a great day to do some grocery shopping... then indulge in some personal threads.

shopping.name: Indigo Vintage Coopertive
shopping.address: 1649 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Max Pietsch (Max)
reviews.text: One of the best selection of men’s shirts and jackets of any thrift store I can remember. A cheap men’s shirt here (in April 2022) is $35, a normal men’s t shirt is $45. I love their approach of showcasing multiple independent vendors… I think it’s what makes the selection so vibrant. The selection at nearby stores feel much more soulless or just like a factory.

reviews.author: Simone Weinstein
reviews.text: I love the vintage selection, including key chains and pins! We are visiting San Francisco but I’m born here and raised in the bay! It was nice and nostalgic for me to find some gems that I get to take back home with me in San Diego.

reviews.author: Bamboo
reviews.text: Okay. Random things labeled y2k and marked 10 times up. Also, many other overpriced items like $48 yarn "bolero"

reviews.author: Grace Windheim
reviews.text: Great selection of vintage clothes here. Found an awesome forest green corduroy jacket and hair clips that I wear almost everyday now. Prices are a little higher than some thrift stores but I find it's worth it. The atmosphere is also cozy and there's a coffee shop inside it!

reviews.author: Tamagotchi Ochazuke
reviews.text:
While exploring, I happened upon this store by chance and decided to enter to offer my compliments to one of the staff members or the owner (she was wearing glasses, and she has a stylish half dark brown/black and half light golden colored hair) regarding the store.

However, I was taken back by the noticeably disdainful impatient look from the individual, as if she wished for me to cease speaking and leave the store.

It’s disheartening to encounter such behavior in 2023, especially when my intention was to express genuine admiration for their store. While I cannot be certain, I couldn’t help but wonder if the incident was influenced by me being the only non-white person in the store. Regardless of the reason, it is crucial to respect all individuals. Every person deserves equal treatment and respect, regardless of their race or ethnicity...

(Update: Saw their reply after a year—no apologies at all. Also, as a straight college girl, I didn’t know SF was such a conservative place where a girl can’t even compliment other women.)

shopping.name: Chameleon Vintage
shopping.address: 1581 Webster St Suite 200, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Chris Balza
reviews.text:
Chameleon is a gem of the bridge.

As someone who's gotten more familiar with the vintage community, I can assure you that Chameleon is an honest shop. The merch is exceptionally well-curated with a unique stock and never overpriced. The staff is personable and knowledgeable, making the small shop worth a visit whenever in Japantown.

reviews.author: Dj Satoda
reviews.text: Best vintage spot. Grew up in this neighborhood, great to see a cool spot like this pop up. Imported stuff from Japan. Prices are more than fair

reviews.author: Solar
reviews.text: Great, helpful staff, tons of cool pieces, and overall peak thrifting. If you’re looking for something a lil bit different and you want to thrift, you can’t go wrong with this place.

reviews.author: Dave Watkins & The Fair Weathers
reviews.text: Walked in to super friendly staff and a Japanese designer rack! Affordable prices for such epic finds.

reviews.author: Jon Okagaki
reviews.text: Awesome vintage store tucked away in Japantown. Pretty much the only place I’ve been too w Japanese designer & their regular vintage stuff is super cheap!

attractions.name: Golden Gate Park
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA, USA

reviews.author: Juliet Joyee
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is absolutely beautiful and a must visit in San Francisco!

It’s a huge park, so make sure to wear comfortable walking shoes, carry water, and dress for the weather. The wide, open roads are perfect for running, walking, biking, or rollerblading. Bike rentals are available, and shuttle bus rides make it easy to get around.

You can easily spend an entire day here there’s so much to see and do. Parking lots are available, and public transportation makes visiting very convenient. I spent about two hours walking and enjoyed every single minute.

The water fountain area is a great spot to sit, relax, and enjoy the blue sky. The Japanese Tea Garden adds a peaceful, cultural touch and is definitely worth visiting. Food trucks are available to satisfy your appetite, and there are plenty of kid-friendly areas throughout the park.

One fun highlight is the bright yellow chairs scattered all over the park the large seating area in the center is especially inviting.

If your trip includes the Golden Gate Bridge, this park should absolutely be on your must-visit list!

⭐ Highly recommended for nature lovers, families, walkers, and anyone exploring San Francisco.

reviews.author: Soumyaroop Nandi
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in San Francisco. The park is massive and has something for everyone — scenic walking trails, biking paths, gardens, lakes, museums, and plenty of open green space to relax. It’s perfect for a morning run, a picnic, or just a quiet escape from the city. The Japanese Tea Garden and Stow Lake area are especially beautiful.

Ocean Beach, right at the edge of the park, offers stunning views of the Pacific Ocean. The sunsets here are absolutely breathtaking. It’s a great place to take a long walk, unwind, and enjoy the cool breeze. The sound of the waves and the wide, open shoreline make it very calming.

Whether you’re visiting as a tourist or living in the Bay Area, Golden Gate Park and Ocean Beach are must-visit spots for nature lovers and anyone looking to recharge outdoors. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Linda Maestre
reviews.text: We visited here on our last day in town.    It's a very large park (1000 acres, I believe) that includes nature trails, a beach area, a Japanese tea garden, a botanical garden and more!  We spent a few hours there and did not even begin to see everything.  The park is very popular with joggers, bicyclists, persons walking their pets, etc.  There are places to picnic, by the ponds, in the shade, etc.  We can't wait to return and see more of the park.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: I had a great experience here. The park is massive; it likely takes over thirty minutes to reach the opposite end if you were located on the other end. I couldn’t explore the entirety of the park on the same day, which was also impressive. I may revisit to explore the rest of the park in the future. Overall, I had a great experience and would recommend this place to anyone visiting San Francisco.

reviews.author: Raj k
reviews.text: It's 1108 acres of park and includes lots of parks. It has shuttle as well. Make sure you have plan to do things so you are using time correctly. There is museum and also lots of activities to be done so plan accordingly. Overall great place.

attractions.description: The city's 1,017-acre oasis draws hikers, bikers, art lovers & music fans, plus gardens & museums.

attractions.name: PIER 39
attractions.address: The Embarcadero, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Eugenio Gonzalez Portales
reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Outstanding Dining Experience on the Bay

Louis is a true gem for anyone looking to enjoy Swiss, Italian, and seafood cuisine in San Francisco, on the Pier 39. From the moment we arrived, the experience was exceptional.

Our server, Connie, did an amazing job—friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. Her personalized attention, fantastic attitude, and knowledge of the menu elevated the entire evening.

The food was absolutely delicious. I ordered the New York steak, cooked perfectly and full of flavor. My brother had the spaghetti with seafood, which was fresh, generous, and beautifully prepared. Every bite reflected quality and care.

Beyond the food and service, the cleanliness, atmosphere, and overall professionalism truly stood out. Dining by the Bay shores made the experience even more memorable.

I happily give 5 stars for outstanding service, excellent dishes, and a warm, welcoming environment.
I love this place and highly recommend it—please come and visit this wonderful San Francisco restaurant on the Bay shores! 🌊🍽️

reviews.author: Jael Legarde
reviews.text: Such a cool place to walk around. Plenty of places to eat or provide entertainment.  Great for dates, or a day with the kids, or solo. We came to see the sea lions, which is FREE to do. Just walk all the way to the back of the pier. From there you can also see Alcatraz! Usually there are street performers as well.

reviews.author: Trinh Võ
reviews.text:
Pier 39 is a beautiful and lively place to visit in San Francisco. It is always crowded, but the atmosphere is fun and exciting. You can see the famous sea lions, enjoy fresh seafood, and shop for souvenirs. The views of the bay, Alcatraz Island, and the Golden Gate Bridge are amazing and perfect for photos.😎

Parking around Pier 39 is very expensive. To save money, I recommend parking a little farther away and walking about 10 minutes. NorthPoint Shopping Center (350 Bay St) is a good option with much cheaper parking.

Overall, Pier 39 is a must-visit spot if you come to San Francisco. Great views, good food, and a lively atmosphere! 🥰

reviews.author: Windy Oktanaura
reviews.text: Pier 39 is one of those must-visit spots in San Francisco, especially if it’s your first time in the city. The area is lively and full of energy, with lots of shops, restaurants, and entertainment all in one place. The sea lions are a highlight and fun to watch, and the views of the bay, Alcatraz, and the Golden Gate Bridge are beautiful, especially around sunset. It’s touristy, but in a good way, clean, well organized, and great for walking around, taking photos, and enjoying the waterfront atmosphere. A fun place to spend a few hours with family or friends.

reviews.author: Shankar Sriram
reviews.text: I've come here in the summer and winter. It's beautiful to walk around and admire the seals and sea lions. I love hanging out with my friends and family here as there's so much to eat, drink and activities to do. Would definitely visit again.

attractions.description: Bustling bayside pier featuring scenic views, sea lion sightings, eateries, shops & entertainment.

attractions.name: de Young Museum
attractions.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: rachana karri
reviews.text:
de Young Museum is one of those places that just feels special. The building itself is beautiful, but what really stood out was how calm and spacious everything felt,even with people around, it never felt overwhelming. The collections are thoughtfully curated, and it’s easy to take your time and actually enjoy what you’re seeing instead of rushing through rooms.

The observation tower is a must, amazing views of the city and Golden Gate Park, and it’s included with admission. I also liked how the museum balances classic works with contemporary pieces; there’s a lot of variety, so it never feels repetitive.

It’s a great place to spend a few hours, whether you’re really into art or just want a peaceful, inspiring break from the city. Definitely worth the visit.

reviews.author: Dedrick Moore
reviews.text:
I had a really good time at the de Young Museum. The galleries are easy to move through, and there’s a great mix of modern and classic art that keeps things interesting without feeling overwhelming. A few pieces genuinely surprised me in the best way.

One thing that stood out was the staff — everyone I interacted with was incredibly nice and genuinely helpful. It made the whole visit feel even more welcoming and relaxed.

If you’re in San Francisco and want something fun, inspiring, and low‑stress to do, the de Young is definitely worth checking out.

reviews.author: Richard Y.
reviews.text: Really enjoyed my time here. Started with a beautiful view from the observation deck, accessible to everyone free of charge. I then went on a very insightful free tour led by Ana, who also pointed out some of the galleries that might be interesting to me. After viewing American impressionist-inspired works, sculptures, landscapes, and a photo exhibit on the booms and busts of California, I enjoyed a cup of tea while looking out at the sculpture garden. Sunday well spent and with an excellent discount for students. Highly recommend for your dose of culture in the city (and close to some great places to eat in Richmond!)

reviews.author: Rokhsareh Alvandi
reviews.text: Even with limited time, I really enjoyed the Manga exhibition. There’s a fantastic room dedicated to the production of One Piece. Also, keep in mind that entry to some areas is free during the final 45 minutes of the working hours, great for a quick walkthrough!

reviews.author: andrew hsu
reviews.text:
Good friend invited me to the art of manga exhibit that was going on till end of january. Excellent exhibit. Lots of info on different authors/artists, and got to see their artwork. Tagame gengorahs art made me laugh, pretty cool horror art from junji ito, and of course some cool one piece and bleach colored pieces. Didn't even realize until afterwards that I took a few pics of some berserk spreads too! Awesome to see the drawings on paper that they had under the glass enclosures too.

Also looked at some other exhibits while waiting. Very cool place

attractions.description: A diverse collection of fine arts in a building with a dramatic copper facade in Golden Gate Park.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Bridge
attractions.address: Golden Gate Brg, San Francisco, CA, USA

reviews.author: Valeriia Buchkovska
reviews.text:
Seeing the Golden Gate Bridge in person was absolutely amazing. This iconic landmark is even more beautiful in real life than in photos. The views are stunning, especially with the ocean, the city, and the fog creating such a unique atmosphere.

Walking near the bridge and taking photos was a great experience, and the whole area feels very special. It’s definitely a must-see place when visiting San Francisco.

reviews.author: Moscato
reviews.text:
Visiting the **Golden Gate Bridge** is truly a must-see experience when you’re in **San Francisco**. The views are absolutely breathtaking — whether you’re walking across the bridge, taking photos from nearby overlooks, or simply admiring it from the waterfront.

The iconic red structure against the blue water and rolling fog feels almost unreal, especially at sunrise or sunset. It’s not just a landmark, it’s an experience that captures the beauty and spirit of the city.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ — An unforgettable sight and a highlight of any San Francisco trip.

reviews.author: Mathew Love
reviews.text: The bridge that very much speaks for itself. Well known and popular, appearing in many movies, but still incredible to see in person. An engineering marvel which is best seen at sunset. There are multiple viewpoints on either side of the bridge, but it is best to take a walk and see which spots you can find.

reviews.author: Caleb Grubbs
reviews.text:
Everything was nice in the gift shops and the cafe. Pricing was not outrageous either. The 28 bus takes you right where you need to go, which is easier than parking.
The bridge was a little rusty, but it was cool watching the people paint.
Also take not that between wind and cars it is very loud-hard to have a conversation.

reviews.author: Henrique Andrea de Lima
reviews.text: Seeing the Golden Gate Bridge in person is impressive no matter how many photos you’ve seen before. The size, color, and setting with the ocean and hills make it feel iconic. Walking across the bridge is a great experience, especially on a clear day, but it can be windy, so bring a jacket. There are several viewpoints around the area that offer different perspectives, each worth stopping at. Whether you’re driving, walking, or just viewing from afar, it’s a must-see in San Francisco.

attractions.description: Famed 4,200 ft art deco suspension bridge open to car, bike & foot traffic.

attractions.name: Coit Tower
attractions.address: 1 Telegraph Hill Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: V Devi
reviews.text:
Coit Tower is one of those places where you really feel San Francisco, especially if you get there on foot. Walking up Telegraph Hill is steep and unapologetic. Your legs will complain, but that climb makes the payoff feel earned. Once you reach the tower, the views stretch out in every direction. The bay, the bridges, the city stacked on hills. It’s classic and worth the effort.

Inside the tower, the murals on the ground floor are a surprise if you’re not expecting them. Painted in the 1930s, they show everyday life in California during that time and give the space a lot of character. You can spend a good amount of time just walking around and noticing the details, and this level is free to visit.

There’s also a small gift shop near the entrance. It’s simple but thoughtfully stocked, with books, postcards, and local souvenirs that actually feel connected to the place rather than generic.

If you want to go all the way to the top, you’ll need to buy a ticket ($8-$11)for the elevator. The observation deck gives you an even higher view of the city and bay and is especially nice on a clear day. It’s a different perspective than just standing outside the tower.

Coming down is one of the best parts. Taking the stairs toward North Beach winds you through quiet paths, gardens, and tucked-away homes, and before you know it you’re right in the heart of the neighborhood.

If the uphill walk isn’t appealing, Muni bus 39 is a great alternative and drops you close to the tower. Whether you walk up, take the bus, or do a mix of both, Coit Tower feels like more than a quick stop. It’s an experience that blends views, history, and a real sense of the city.

reviews.author: Talita Osman
reviews.text: What a beautiful art deco bldg with amazing views and photo ops. I love the murals inside which tell part of the history of the people who are foundational to the city, it's very life blood, the workers of SF. My favorite thing may be the grounds and its feathery inhabitants, however. I seem to share this affinity with them, the birds who sang so loud that their voices enveloped the entire place. Go visit! :)

reviews.author: Jovan Kun
reviews.text: Many tourists visit this place. You have to pay $11/ person to go up the tower. Use the old elevator but still need to take a stairs. It's not suitable for dissabled people. From the top, only a small area,  you can see the beautiful scenery of  San Francisco and the Alcatraz.  Need to visit at least for once. Parking is limited.

reviews.author: James
reviews.text:
We walked up from the Embarcadero - if you do too be prepared for a legs day. Such beautiful inbetween spaces along the way up and great views from the top.

We arrived right on 5. Be aware last tickets to the top of the tower are sold at 430.

The trees have grown quite a bit blocking all but a few views from the footpath.

reviews.author: Douglas Chan-Wing
reviews.text: Great place to enjoy the full view of SF from a high vantage point. The tickets are $11/adult and cheaper for children. The walk up the hill is steep but short to get a good warm up. If not, there are busses that routes there and a small parking lot for those who prefer driving. Perfect date, family, tourist spot

attractions.description: Storied 1930s-era building known for its WPA murals, 360-degree views & resident flock of parrots.

attractions.name: Alcatraz Island
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Remi Tayar
reviews.text:
Did the Behind the Scenes Tour at Alcatraz Island and it was incredible. Access to restricted areas made it feel exclusive and much more immersive than the regular visit. The history, the storytelling, and the views of SF are unforgettable.

Highly recommend booking this version! Expensive but totally worth it.

reviews.author: Mathew Love
reviews.text: Although access to the island was closed during my stay, I still took a bay cruise around the island to see it and take in the sights. Would have loved to have gone on the island for the tour and audio guide, but it is still worth seeing and visiting close up. It is a historic place and is definitely one to see if you're in San Francisco.

reviews.author: James Carr
reviews.text:
As a museum Alcatraz is incredible. The display about the Indian Occupations taught me real history that I had no idea of, and gave me an entirely new outlook on the island and its place in the United States ongoing story with crime, punishment, and how we treat native people.

The prison itself and its audio tour where more or less what I expected, old interviews with prisoners and guards. Still there where stories and detail I did not know before, and the entire tour gives a thesis without preaching which I think is important.

Alcatraz holds a very particular history that feels incredibly present right now.

Exploring the island itself is also great. There is much more to see then you expect, and the whole thing both feels frozen in time while still still crumbling into the bay.

Be aware that accessibility here is non existent. Paths are long steep walks, and you can only access the island by ferry which for us cost $48. I think the history is well worth that cost.

And from the touristy point of view, the island offers impossibly beautiful views of San Francisco and the bay.

reviews.author: Alex Goodman
reviews.text: Awesome attraction. The history of this place is amazing, and the audio guide in the main jail building is very interesting and not too long. You can enter a number of cells and see how the prisoners had to live. Views back across the city are beautiful and the boat ride there and back gives a great chance to see all the landmarks of the city. I would strongly recommend this to anyone who has the time when visiting SF.

reviews.author: Valeriia Buchkovska
reviews.text:
Visiting Alcatraz in San Francisco was an unforgettable experience. The history of this place is fascinating and a bit eerie, which makes the visit even more interesting.

Walking through the prison and listening to the stories about the inmates and life on the island was very impressive. The views of San Francisco from Alcatraz are absolutely stunning as well.

I highly recommend visiting Alcatraz if you’re in San Francisco — it’s a unique place with so much history and atmosphere.

attractions.description: Notorious prison & once home to Al Capone, now a museum with an audio tour, accessible by ferry.

attractions.name: Union Square
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Kristen Kelly
reviews.text: One of my favorite spots in San Francisco, especially during the holidays! In December, they have a large tree and skating rink. Also, JPMorgan has a winter walk there. I went in 2025 and enjoyed coffee, shopping, the food trucks and live entertainment. Union Square brings a SF city magical vibe. Perfect for families or just anyone who wants to enjoy it.

reviews.author: JIM SALINAS
reviews.text: As always, Union Square is spectacular. Especially during the Christmas season, the amazing beautiful tree. The ice skating rink, everybody decked out.
