Looking across the street, all the wonderful beautiful stores, especially the flagship store for Macy's, its just a place to experience and there's plenty of underground parking. The security is top-notch.  Everyone should feel especially safe with so many of San Francisco's finest everywhere.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text:
Nice. I had a pretty decent (good) experience at Union Square. It was quite crowded during Christmas, however, it is likely worth seeing it, especially if you are visiting San Francisco.

There is a massive Christmas tree erected right on the square (Christmas 2025-2026), which makes it a nice spot to capture a selfie nearby. Overall, it is a great place to visit.

reviews.author: LAND ROVER
reviews.text: We were there the Day After Christmas. So it was kind of crowded. There was people, ice skating, big, giant Christmas tree lit up lots of people. Lots of families, good times as always. But it was special during the Christmas glad I was able to show my wife and my daughter. This special time and this special place of the world

reviews.author: Swati Snata
reviews.text:
We had such an amazing time! We went out three days before Christmas and the holiday spirit was everywhere.
We saw the giant Christmas tree and the massive Hanukkah menorah, and even spent some time skating at the ice rink.
All the shops were so festive—Macy’s and The Cheesecake Factory were both decked out in beautiful holiday decorations!

attractions.description: Well-known vibrant neighborhood featuring a range of upscale shops, restaurants, hotels & nightlife.

attractions.name: Legion of Honor
attractions.address: 100 34th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Michael
reviews.text: Awesome museum!!! One of my favorite museums on the West Coast. 19th Century art is my absolute favorite, and they have a HUGE collection here. We live two hours away, so can't come as often as we'd like. So it's a treat when we can. The Manet and Morisot exhibit just closed, but it was awesome!!!  Bonus: Ticket is also good for same day visits to the de Young.

reviews.author: Elma C
reviews.text:
As a longtime Legion of Honor member, I wanted to highlight a special exhibit celebrating International Women’s Day, running March 3–8.

Floral arrangements are placed throughout the galleries and feel like interpretations of the artworks around them, echoing the colors, shapes, and mood of the paintings and sculptures nearby. It creates a really interesting dialogue between the flowers and the art. At least - that’s my personal interpretation of its

Walking through the museum becomes a bit of a treasure hunt, with each room revealing another creative arrangement.

A beautiful and unique exhibit - definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: Caron Shahrestani
reviews.text: Have always loved this museum. They have a stellar permanent collection, a picturesque location with a view of the Golden Gate Bridge, a yummy onsite café and a colorful gift shop full of artsy treasures. My husband and I recently visited for a specific exhibit and it was delightful. If you are looking for a classic museum experience in San Francisco with a gorgeous exterior and views, definitely pop by!

reviews.author: Kimberly Canale
reviews.text: Beautiful museum. Staff is very friendly. Had fun watching a dog swim in the fountain in front of the museum.  Artwork is beautiful.  Had an expensive but nice cup of coffee and dessert sitting outside. Only issue is that the de Younge and Legion of Honor Museums are on the same website but are not close together  I purchased tickets online but thought I needed to go to the de Younge location for the exhibit that I wanted to see and went to the wrong museum by mistake,  which cost me an extra Uber ride. So just look at your tickets closely!!!

reviews.author: Oscar
reviews.text: We had a great experience at the de Young Museum and were pleasantly surprised to learn that our tickets also included access to the Legion of Honor, which allowed us to visit both museums. The staff were very helpful with promotions and questions. The art collection is excellent, and it is an enjoyable place to spend a few hours. Parking is convenient, not easy to find a spot.

attractions.description: 4,000 years of ancient & European art in a beaux arts building overlooking the Golden Gate Bridge.

attractions.name: Twin Peaks
attractions.address: 501 Twin Peaks Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Christopher T
reviews.text: Always wanted to check this place out and finally make it.  Beautiful and breathtaking 360 degrees view of SF, especially if you’re here during sunset or sunrise.  You can see everything angles from the city, to the mountains, and to the oceans.  There are 2 hills that you can climbs (and bit of work and can be deep too) up and enjoys the views.  Parking can be difficult sometimes, but if you come early it would be easier to find parking, and you can hike around the trails before the sunset set in.  Soo worth it and highly recommended.

reviews.author: Jael Legarde
reviews.text: Such a beautiful thing to witness. We actually went here right before a storm came in..so it was very cloudy. The picture you get from the very top is worth the walk up there. The path to go up is closed and you're not suppose to park in front of the gate. There is parking on the side street though.

reviews.author: American Nomad
reviews.text:
You must come here for vantage views of the city. There are 🚻 available for free. Ample and safe steps to climb to the top of the hill for more spectacular views.
It is a photographer's delight.

reviews.author: Dylan T
reviews.text: It is an absolutely breathtaking view up here at night! The drive up is nice and windy. It was not very crowded when we went on Sunday night. There are restrooms at the top!

reviews.author: rajkk77 (rajkk77)
reviews.text:
Breathtaking Views and Peaceful Vibes.

The drive up to Twin Peaks is smooth and scenic, and once you reach the top, the panoramic views of San Francisco are absolutely stunning. You get a full 360° perspective of the city, the bay, and beyond it’s truly a must see.

There’s a calm, peaceful atmosphere up there, with plenty of people walking around, enjoying the fresh air and taking in the views. It’s the perfect spot to pause, breathe, and appreciate the beauty of the city.

If you’re visiting San Francisco, this should definitely be on your list. You won’t regret it!

attractions.description: Renowned, 180-degree views of the Bay Area are offered at this 64-acre park with hiking trails.

attractions.name: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art
attractions.address: 151 3rd St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: HapaKay Foodie
reviews.text:
This was my first time ever at this particular art museum, and goodness, didn't make a great first impression!!

My husband wanted to come check out the KAWS Exhibit, and I thought he knew more about him, but apparently he knew his artwork from Asia and didn't know he was from New York. Lol it was very inspirational and we had no idea we could explore the other floors of the our gallery once we got the tickets!

Even though we've had a 2:30 PM showing for the KAWS exhibit, we ended up staying until 430 to check out the other floors! Perfectly to spend a Sunday afternoon!!

There docents or art gallery carriers at each section in case you had questions, although I think they were there to make sure no one took anything.

They also had their own separate specialty gift shop for the seasonal exhibit as well as a giant gift shop for the whole museum itself! We walked through both sections and the downstairs gift shop was amazing! It reminds me of that old store, learning express. Along with a lot of San Francisco Schottky apparel it was very interesting to see all the different artists and projects you could buy as well as different paraphernalia.

The gift shop seemed normal prices for San Francisco. And there was somebody there to answer questions, if need be.

reviews.author: Laurie Bezer
reviews.text: SFMOMA never disappoints. I never get tired of the main collection of art, and enjoy the special exhibits as well. Seven floors of heaven. If you're into art... You'll have a fantastic time here. I love that there are benches and places to rest throughout the museum. They allow you to spend time with some of the pieces.

reviews.author: Armahn Rastawan
reviews.text: Visited SFMOMA and checked out the KAWS exhibit and it was honestly really cool. The space itself is beautiful and easy to walk through, and the exhibit had such a unique vibe. Seeing KAWS’ work in person was way better than just seeing it online. The colors, scale, and details really stand out and make you stop and take it all in. Definitely worth checking out if you’re into modern art or just want to see something different while you’re in SF. I’d for sure come back again.

reviews.author: Nicole Combeau
reviews.text: Wonderful exhibition design. We went for Photo Book Speed Date, an artist led night where people share photo books with each other. It was an amazing place to gather, share ideas and look at art. Impressed by how busy it gets too.

reviews.author: M Antonio C
reviews.text:
INTERESTING FACT.   Part of SF MOMA is built on top of the old San Francisco Firehouse Station 1.  I worked in that firehouse on the 90s-2000s.  Anyway.....

SF MOMA is a wonderful facility.  Clean and well lit with lots of natural light.  I went this week to see the high-school at students projects.  Everyone was quite nice from the reception desk to a person at the elevator.   Although the lady at the top of the stairs was about as personable as an over toasted piece of bread.   She obviously had better things to do.

attractions.description: Contemporary & modern paintings, installations, photographs, media & more in a sleek space.

attractions.name: Hamon Observation Tower
attractions.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: George Rosas
reviews.text: I enjoyed going to the Hamon observation with my son. It's free! You get a bird's eye view Golden gate Park near the tower

reviews.author: Hana Estalilla
reviews.text: The Hamon Observation Tower is not my go-to place to view the skyline, but it’s a must-see if you are in the area or visiting the DeYoung Museum. You get a 360 view of San Francisco from the middle of the Golden Gate Park in an enclosed observatory. The downtown area is quite far away, but if you bring binoculars, you can see it much clearer. You can also see the Presidio, the Golden Gate Bridge, and the Sutro Tower from here. There are a few benches to sit on and admire the view, as well as a gift shop stand. I purchased a poster of an aerial view of SF there. Overall, 4/5.

reviews.author: Yana Andreyenka
reviews.text: The tower closes early but the staff was very nice to let us in for a quick look and take pictures. Beautiful view on the city

reviews.author: Matt B
reviews.text: A part of the de Young Museum that is completely free to enter. While it doesn’t give the best views of downtown (go to Grandview park for that in the sunset), it’s got some nice views of western SF.

reviews.author: TheBayArea.831
reviews.text: Great views of SF 💙 Its free to go up to the 9th floor. Its a must see if your in SF or near Golden Gate Park. There is a gift shop and elevators available. Went on a weekday and it was not busy at all.

attractions.name: San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Melissa L
reviews.text:
A secret gem! I genuinely enjoyed this more than Alcatraz. I found the history of the actual building to be the most interesting, but the story of various boats and radio transmissions were also interesting. Nice view. It's less popular than other areas and was a nice quiet place to relax if, like me, you find the city overwhelming at times.
Free entry and clean bathrooms. Be sure to get your National Parks passport book stamped.
Note: The garden area is currently being remodeled and is completely torn up/inaccessible.

reviews.author: Borislav Bajkic
reviews.text: The most beautiful big city in America that I have visited so far. The center is great, but the part along the coast is even more beautiful and interesting. The food in the restaurants is phenomenal (especially the crabs!) The ice cream is superb! And the space itself all around you is practically historical, every detail. It is mandatory to visit this beautiful city slowly and in detail several times.

reviews.author: Nook H
reviews.text:
If you’re into history or just love being by the water, the San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park is a spot you shouldn’t miss. It’s right in the Fisherman’s Wharf area, so super easy to get to.

First off, the historic ships are super cool. You can actually board them and see what life was like back in the day. The Balclutha, this big old sailing ship, was a favorite of mine. Then there’s the Eureka, an old-school steam ferryboat, which has such a unique vibe.

The visitor center is packed with interesting info and a great place to start your visit. Just across the way is the Maritime Museum, in this awesome art deco building. It has all sorts of exhibits about shipbuilding, navigation, and life at sea – plenty to keep you entertained and informed.

All in all, it’s a fantastic mix of history and scenic views. Definitely worth a visit next time you’re in San Francisco!

reviews.author: Doug Ho
reviews.text:
Established in 1988, San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park celebrates America’s maritime heritage on the Pacific Coast. The 50-acre park has grown around Aquatic Park Cove, a protected area in the San Francisco Bay. The park showcases the city’s seafaring past. The park includes a fleet of historic vessels, a visitor center, a maritime museum, and a library/research facility. Formerly referred to as the San Francisco Maritime Museum, the collections were acquired by the National Park Service in 1978.
The historic fleet of the San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park is moored at the park's Hyde Street Pier. The fleet consists of the following major vessels:
Balclutha, an 1886 built square rigged sailing ship.
C.A. Thayer, an 1895 built schooner.
Eureka, an 1890 built steam ferryboat.
Alma, an 1891 built scow schooner.
Hercules, a 1907 built steam tug.
Eppleton Hall, a 1914 built paddlewheel tug.
The visitor center is housed in the park's 1909 waterfront warehouse, located at the corner of Hyde and Jefferson streets. The City of San Francisco declared the four-story brick structure a historic landmark in 1974.

reviews.author: Marsha H
reviews.text: A beautiful park with great views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the trolley cars.

attractions.description: Expansive complex featuring a museum, interactive displays & ships telling the story of sea travel.

attractions.name: San Francisco Botanical Garden
attractions.address: 1199 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Mahalakshmi Muralikrishnan
reviews.text: The San Francisco Botanical Garden is  fifty-five acres of beautifully landscaped garden and open space that showcases more than eight thousand varieties of plants from around the world! Located in the Golden Gate Park, the botanical garden offers a much needed escape when you need a quiet moment to reflect or a peaceful stroll in the garden. We can sit on the bench beneath a canopy of vibrant blooms with the water feature, allowing a soothing soundtrack. We can walk and talk, enjoying the fragrant flowers while the kids run around in the lush greenery. This is a great space to feel energized and rejuvenated. Pictures and videos cannot capture the fragrance of all the blooming flowers! Don't miss the flower piano event that will transform the SF botanical garden into the city's own alfresco concert hall!

reviews.author: Mortiche Wong
reviews.text:
With a disability and lots of life changes, I haven't been back to the Botanical Gardens in 7 years. I saw an ad for Lightscape (embarrassingly) while playing solitaire on my phone, and then again during a commercial on TV. It looked cooler than the Oakland Zoo's Glowfari and my kid and I prefer Fto avoid zoos.

The event was well done overall  With the exception that it was extremely popular and the line to enter was quite long (we went the night before 🦃 Thanksgiving), us adults enjoyed the 1 mile of adorned path winding through assorted trees and displays. Maybe a few hundred yards just inside the entrance was a cluster of food/snack trucks and a bar surrounding a squishy lawn with a few tables and chairs set up, but it was way too cold for anyone to want to be stationary. The path led us past resident giant Sequoia, ginko, New Zealand Christmas Tree, to a frighteningly close, zeppelin moon as well as a large swathe of lawn shimmering with thousands of LED lanterns. The grove of artificial cherry trees with their soft pink crystalline blossoms gave off an ethereal and photo-op worthy oasis in the dark, but difficult to capture it's splendor with a regular phone.  The pond with giant lillies as well as the very next display of enormous, feathery pompom poppies both had  color changing features which have those displays more Insta- and Tok- video opportunities for the younger crowd.

There were two displays that we liked quite a bit, although they could have been done slightly better. The laser light and fog machine show through the oaks (?) was fun and made us moms feel like we were in a nightclub reliving our 20s again, and the other display with the giant Sequoias made their branches move in a seemingly preternatural way, but the repetitive songs (especially the sequoias section - the same Lindsey Stirling song over and over again) was just a bit annoying. I'm sure they wanted people to just walk through and take things in at a relatively decent clip and not linger.  I rarely go out though, and being able to stop, enjoy nature at night (safely!) with my kid and friends, and with a cool lighted display, I really wanted to take my time.

If you've never been, it's definitely worth seeing. I'd be happy to go again; I do wish they did it during the summer season as well, being able to enjoy all that when it's warmer and not 48°F out would draw me back for sure (but armed with a can of mosquito deterrent).

reviews.author: Abigail Figueroa
reviews.text:
​We really enjoyed our leisurely visit. A great tip: admission is free daily from 7:30–9 am, or check for the free 2nd Tuesday of the month.
​The garden is huge, with so many diverse plants to see from different places. It was wonderful to take our time and explore. We loved seeing the fish and were even lucky enough to spot a couple of beautiful red-tailed hawks!

reviews.author: Sterio A
reviews.text: I spent Thanksgiving Day at the San Francisco Botanical Garden and loved every minute of it—free admission was a nice bonus! What struck me most was how natural the whole place feels. The designers clearly aimed for minimal impact, and the result is a landscape that feels wild but welcoming. With plants from all over the world arranged by region, the garden feels like a peaceful trip across continents. I’ll definitely be coming back!

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: I had an amazing experience here. I was really surprised by the lush nature that existed in San Francisco. The garden offers a display of hundreds of plant species originating from/native to tens of different countries from different continents, hosting a diverse ecosystem in the park. Overall, I think it is a great place to visit.

attractions.description: 55 acres of serene gardens showcasing diverse plants from around the world & a magnolia collection.

attractions.name: Yerba Buena Gardens
attractions.address: 750 Howard St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Connected bangla
reviews.text: Yerba Buena Gardens is a beautiful and relaxing green space right in the middle of downtown San Francisco. The garden is clean, well maintained, and full of open areas where people can sit, walk, or simply enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. There are fountains, public art, and nice views of the surrounding buildings that make the place feel lively but still calm. It is a great spot to take a short break while exploring the city, have lunch outdoors, or relax with friends and family.

reviews.author: CW
reviews.text: Nice park in middle of city, with promenade, lots of seating, Martin Luther King Memorial Waterfall adds a relaxing atmosphere, great for kids. Right across the SF Museum of Modern Art.

reviews.author: Eva Braun
reviews.text:
A peaceful and cozy spot right in the heart of the city. It’s a perfect place to take a break from the urban noise — you can sit on the grass, relax by the fountain, watch people passing by, or listen to street musicians. The atmosphere feels green, calm, and inspiring. It’s lovely to come here with a coffee or a book. There are plenty of cafés and museums nearby, and in the evening, the view of the city lights is beautiful.

✨ Perfect for: a short rest, a quiet walk after visiting a museum, or simply soaking in the spirit of San Francisco.

reviews.author: vasileia “Billia” vespa
reviews.text: Oh this place is truly amazing! The gardens, the square, the fountain it is all so beautifully decorated. Morning time for coffee in the park or afternoon time for tea are both great, combined with a visit to the SFMOMA (San Francisco Museum of Mosern Art, just across the park). Been there also at night time when there was a music event at the square, beneath the city's lights.. not to miss!

reviews.author: Ventrilaquest
reviews.text: The area offers a mix of experiences. You’ll find plenty of food spots, from mom-and-pop restaurants that truly take pride in their dishes and atmosphere, to others that may not hold the same standard. Like many city environments, you may occasionally encounter individuals asking for money, but that comes with being in an urban setting. On the positive side, the architecture is stunning, and there’s no shortage of things to do. Museums, restaurants, and parks provide plenty of opportunities for recreation and cultural exploration. Parking can be a problem so plan ahead!

attractions.description: Several gardens share space with dramatic outdoor public art pieces, including a memorial waterfall.

attractions.name: Huntington Falls
attractions.address: 50 Blue Heron Lake Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Hana Estalilla
reviews.text:
Upon seeing this waterfall, it is very apparent that it is not natural. I wanted to mention this first as it may disappoint some people (and even disappointed me to an extent), but San Francisco is not exactly known for having waterfalls, anyway. Despite being artificial, Huntington Falls is still quite pretty and fits well with the serene lake setting. The foliage surrounding the fall is what makes it shine, the variety of large plants and blooming flowers making it look like it was taken straight out of a forest. Families and tourists will certainly like it most, as it is very picturesque. If crossing the waterfall via the stepping stones by the bottom isn't enough, you can also ascend the waterfall using the stairs and look down on it. I would be careful, though, as the steps can be rather steep and uneven. If you want to spend more time here, there are a *few* benches at the foot of the waterfall.

Overall, while it is a "fake" waterfall, it still offers most of the beaty that a real one would have. Why not visit it? You probably don't get to see a waterfall every day. 4/5.

reviews.author: Mizuki
reviews.text: They should name it Huntington fall instead of falls since there's only one waterfall. However, it did have an amazing nature trail that displays multiple viewpoints of the lake. There's wildlife too, which is quite nice. Multiple benches around parts of the park allow you to sit and rest. Wonderful for picnics, pictures, and morning walks.

reviews.author: Mike Figueroa
reviews.text: This was a great little hike! We are a family of six and we love San Fran but we’re always trying to find something fun that doesn’t break the bank. We started at the Tea Gardens, which I don’t recommend, and looking at Google Reviews we saw this little trip. It’s a really easy paved trail to the falls with stunning scenery all the way there. Once you get to the falls there is a pretty steep trail up. There are several sections and photo ops all the way up. The kids had a blast and my wife and I really enjoyed it. You can rent paddle boats to cruise around the lake if you want. We just stumbled on it, but had we known we would have packed a picnic and made an entire afternoon of it. We probably spent two hours there letting the kids explore and take pics. For free.

reviews.author: Muting Ma
reviews.text: We had an amazing time here. Today Saturday at around 3pm not too crowded but we would recommend visit here earlier like 11am for more nature vibe. Going up the stairs where leads the Huntington falls is the strawberry hill I think. Those stairs are quite scary on high sneakers shoes so just be careful. But a nice workout. This was a great date and for tourists too!

reviews.author: Tina Kolot
reviews.text: Huntington Falls, located in Golden Gate Park, is a beautiful and serene spot. The cascading waterfall surrounded by lush greenery creates a peaceful atmosphere. It's an ideal place for a relaxing walk or a picturesque photo session. The views are simply breathtaking, making it a must-visit location in the park.

attractions.name: San Francisco Carousel & Bay Plunge at Pier 39
attractions.address: 39 Pier 39, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Jesse Lee
reviews.text:
This is well worth the walk for the little ones to go into the back of Pier 39. The carousel is in such good shape and classical beautiful. Everything is great but the ride was a bit short.

In the back of pier 39 there are many other kids friendly attractions and snacks for kids of all ages.

reviews.author: Weslley Figueiredo
reviews.text: My 4-year-old daughter absolutely loves carousels, and she had a fantastic time at the San Francisco Carousel at Pier 39. She really enjoyed it and had so much fun! It’s a charming and colorful spot that’s perfect for young kids. A great little attraction in the middle of a lively area — definitely worth a stop if you’re visiting Pier 39 with children.

reviews.author: Stephen sC
reviews.text: A nice place to wander round and see the shows

reviews.author: SHYAMAL GUCHHAIT
reviews.text: A fantastic spot to explore, with diverse restaurants and shops for jewelry and gifts! 🤩🛍️ #Place #Restaurants #Jewelry #Gifts

reviews.author: Elizabeth “Liz” Castro
reviews.text:
There's something for everyone but definitely appear that you can bring the family for a fun-filled day. I enjoyed the fact that I was there on a budget and was still able to purchase and acceptable amount of souvenirs take back home with me for my loved ones.

A variety of different food places, the best of sit down restaurants and fast food eateries . There's a Farmers Market, arcade for little ones, rides for little ones, a plethora of gift shops ranging from toys to souvenirs, high tech gadgets, and jewelry shops. I didn't get to go to the aquarium but for the next visit I definitely have some plans in mind.

And last but not least I enjoyed, and the children enjoyed watching sea lions sunning themselves viewable from the pier. FYI directly across the street, very conveniently located, was a parking garage.

attractions.name: Umbrella Alley San Francisco
attractions.address: 366 Jefferson St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Cory B
reviews.text: They were open on Easter Sunday, and we had the place all to ourselves. My husband and I had fun taking photos, and they came out looking amazing! Definitely well worth the $5/person.

reviews.author: Kristina Yaffe
reviews.text: Such a cute place for pictures! It gives the local artist a place to shine. They have a post for you to put your phone and do a timed picture. Tons of spots to take pictures at. I think it was like 5 bucks per person. Just some examples of the below artwork.

reviews.author: Mariia Unisikhina
reviews.text: This place is worth visiting, it's bright and cheerful, we took a lot of beautiful photos. But I didn't expect it to be so small, I saw more drawings on the walls a little further away, but the entrance was closed, there were cars for rent around the city.

reviews.author: Cha'vette Ware
reviews.text: I went right when they opened so there was no on there. Which was great for me, so I got great photos without anyone in the way. I paid $5 to get in, but it was worth every penny! Be sure to tag the artist on your socials. This was a great spot!

reviews.author: Nelda Fernandez
reviews.text: This place is a must see if you are by Pier 39, and want some photo ops.  You can't miss this place. The art murals are amazing. The entrance is a $5 donation for the artists. After you are done here, you walk over to Pier 39 to see the sights and grab something to eat. Don't miss out on seeing this place.

attractions.name: San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park Visitor Center
attractions.address: 499 Jefferson St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Morsels
reviews.text: Outstanding little museum operated by the National Park dedicated to the maritime history of the SF Bay. Well curated with historical photos, maps, artifacts & memorabilia — as well as an actual lighthouse lamp, fishing boat and a model of a tule canoe. Informative videos. Need at least an hour to take it all in. Museum is free. (The museum is adjacent to the Hyde Street Pier which normally is the home to vintage boats: Eureka, C.A. Thayer, Balclutha, and Eppleton Hall. Hyde Street Pier is closed for renovation until ~2030 but the museum is worth visiting in the meantime).

reviews.author: Susana Kay
reviews.text: Updated hours 10-5 everyday. Fantastic museum and it’s FREE! Tons of ship models, videos, exhibits and history of San Francisco ports and local history. A must see!

reviews.author: Dan's Inferno
reviews.text: The visitor center is across the street from the pier where the historical ships are docked. As of Spring 2025, that area is under renovation, but the visitor center is fully open. The displays and ship models are well done and informative. Anyone unfamiliar with the role  San Francisco played in Western economic development and World War 2 would find this a fascinating place to visit. The visitor center participates in the junior ranger program and has NPS rangers on site. Parking can be a bit of a challenge since it is in the middle of the San Francisco pier tourist area.

reviews.author: Viera V
reviews.text: What a cool, well done spot to learn more about San Francisco Bay from the beginning to now. There is a spot where you can sit down and watch the Yerba Buena growth. It’s a short movie and I watched it twice to pick up details I didn’t know about

reviews.author: Grace Lin
reviews.text: Love this museum so much! Not only the pictures they provided the actual stuffs and videos for further introduction! A well educated platform for San Francisco history

attractions.name: Chinatown San Francisco
attractions.address: 855 Stockton St # B, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Rokhsareh Alvandi
reviews.text: My experience here during Lunar New Year was incredible. Even the process of finding parking and walking through the neighborhood was enjoyable. This Chinatown stands out from any other I’ve seen across the country; the local ceremonies are truly special and something I’d highly recommend to anyone visiting.

reviews.author: Emilee Bargoma
reviews.text: Chinatown SF is amazing. No matter how long I live in this city I am always gonna be in awe of Chinatown. I bought a lot of different dried mushrooms to make a rice with mushroom sauce dish, and it was delicious. You should challenge yourself and walk up any of the steep streets, like Jones St, get yourself a good leg workout. I ended up going up Jones and down California, and ran into Ossa Steakhouse, Grace Cathedral, and The Fairmont. Just GORGEOUS. Then you can go back down and get some good food on any of the streets running through Chinatown. My fave spot is Osmanthus for delicious dim sum. You can go into any of the dumpling restaurants too. There was one (forgetting the name) that had a super long line. There is just so much to see! The next time I go to Chinatown, I am going to buy a silk floral pattern robe from a store I ran into on Grant (I think).All in all, I had a great Sunday.

reviews.author: Jael Legarde
reviews.text: Absolutely beautiful.  First time here. There was so much food to choose from! You can buy fresh delicacies here. Great for walking..although parking was hard. Tuesday & Thursday are street  cleaning days..so parking was limited. The people were both friendly and helpful.

reviews.author: Chiara Rizza
reviews.text: Francisco’s Chinatown is one of the most vibrant and iconic neighborhoods in the city. Walking through its colorful streets feels like stepping into another world: full of lanterns, traditional shops, herbal stores, and amazing food spots. I loved exploring Grant Avenue and its lively atmosphere, and the Dragon Gate is a perfect photo stop. It’s busy, authentic, and full of culture. Definitely a must-visit if you’re in San Francisco.

reviews.author: John McGinniss
reviews.text:
Largest Chinese population outside of China itself.
A vibrant and busy area with lots of shops and restaurants.
Great to see all the Chinese culture on display.

San Francisco's Chinatown is the oldest in North America, the largest outside Asia, and a major San Francisco attraction known for its vibrant culture, historic architecture, and bustling streets. Visitors can explore through its iconic Dragon's Gate, dine at traditional eateries like those on Grant Avenue, and experience cultural sites such as temples, Portsmouth Square, and various museums. It is easily accessible via public transport or ride-sharing, though parking is difficult.

attractions.description: Iconic area with the largest assembly of Chinese eateries, shops & cultural events outside of Asia.

attractions.name: Fort Point National Historic Site
attractions.address: 201 Marine Dr, San Francisco, CA 94129, USA

reviews.author: Suzayn
reviews.text: Fort Point is an amazing spot to take in the Golden Gate Bridge up close. The historic fort itself is fun to explore, and the views from the top are incredible — waves crashing, the bridge towering right above, and plenty of great photo angles. It’s a peaceful place to walk around, learn a bit of history, and enjoy one of the best views in San Francisco.

reviews.author: SK HD
reviews.text: Nestled right beneath the Golden Gate Bridge, it offers one of the most unique perspectives of the bridge and the Bay. The fort itself is rich with history walking through the old brick corridors and seeing the preserved cannons and exhibits really gives you a sense of the past. Outside, you’ll often find surfers catching waves right below the bridge, which makes the view even more amazing. It’s also a great spot for photography, especially at sunset when the light hits the bridge just right. Whether you’re into history, architecture, or just love exploring scenic spots, Fort Point is absolutely worth a visit. I’ve been a few times and it never loses its charm.

reviews.author: Troy Windhorst
reviews.text:
💚 This historic fort is a photographer’s playground.
💚 No cost to enter.
💚 Multiple floors with exhibits tucked into every room.
💚 Self-exploration permitted throughout, including the roof!
💚 Unique views of the bay and Golden Gate are plentiful.
💚 Nothing critical to report.

reviews.author: Rachel Erotika
reviews.text: Great spot to go for a morning walk and explore the landmark by Golden Gate Bridge. Very Instagrammable and stunning views of San Francisco. Cheap parking spots. Watch out, the ocean will splash you, ha!

reviews.author: Nibs
reviews.text: Well worth a visit. Very cool little fort, right under the bridge. Fun walk meanders through the fort with good educational material spread throughout. End up on the roof with incredible views of the bridge, the city and the occasional whale! Hopper's hands are here too! Definitely worth a visit.

attractions.description: Fort from the Civil War era located under the Golden Gate Bridge offering cannon drills & tours.

attractions.name: Exploratorium
attractions.address: Pier 15 Embarcadero at, Green St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA

reviews.author: Nancy C.
reviews.text:
The Exploratorium in San Francisco has been around since 1969, the original home was at the Palace of Fine Arts but moved to its current location at Pier 15 and Pier 17 on San Francisco’s waterfront since 2013.

This place is perfect for kids: 2 years old and over. Technically, it’s great for all ages. There are so many hands-on activities everywhere you go! You can definitely be here for 5+ hours! There are outside activities as well.

As far as food and where to eat, as of January 2026, The Tinkering Table Restaurant has taken over the Seaglass Restaurant. There’s also a cafe located at the front that’s connected inside and outside of the museum.

To get to the Exploratorium, I recommend taking public transportation when possible, there’s ferry and BART, it’s a good 15 minutes walk from the Ferry Building, you can also take the F Line Streetcar/ Muni and it will take you there to the front. Although there’s parking lots and street parking, it can add up and be expensive!

I’ve been here at this location and at the Palace of Fine Arts location as a kid when I used to go for field trips! Now I go with my daughter, nieces and nephews!

Would I go again?! Absolutely! Maybe one day I’ll try and go on Thursday’s for Exploratorium After Dark.

reviews.author: HTS L
reviews.text:
An incredible amount of exhibits ranging from artistic to mathematical. I'm an adult and even I had lots of fun. Some of the exhibits require a second person so it might be a good idea to bring a friend if able.

I had loads of fun looking at the 5 senses exhibits, carnivorous plants, San franscico sediment variations and natural properties phonenoms. It even has an AI section that has AI exhibits now. Very fascinating.

I would highly recommend visiting. Around 11 to 1:30pm there are children visiting so they can be quite loud, you can go in and out with a stamp. I personally found them pretty entertaining since they were also quite enthusiastic.

Also I would like to mention that there's an exhibit where there's a bowl that you can choose to take or leave something, and asks critical thinking questions for kids regarding communal spaces and sharing. It would be great if you could bring something small like stickers or trinkets to add to the exhibit and make it more fun!

reviews.author: James Riordan
reviews.text: Pretty cool place. It is huge and there is so much to see do touch feel interact with. Super great for kids obviously but even the adults. Highly recommend. Does get very crowded. There is a cafeteria with plenty of indoor and outdoor seating.

reviews.author: Ella Halloran
reviews.text: On my trip to San Fransico we went to the Exploratorium After Dark when it is 18+. I think it’s the perfect time to go to have an adult playground. I recommend you get there right when the after dark starts because you’ll be sad you have to leave and didn’t get to see everything! Had a super fun night here and all the staff were very nice and made it a great night! Couldn’t recomend this place more!

reviews.author: Michelle Chan
reviews.text:
I've been going to the Exploratorium for over a decade—probably more than a hundred times, no exaggeration. I started taking my son when he was a toddler, and now he's 13. It’s always been one of our favorite places. I used to be a member, but after my financial situation changed, I was so grateful to learn that the Exploratorium participates in the Museums for All program. It means EBT card holders can get tickets for just $3 each, which makes a huge difference.

This past Sunday was our first visit in a long time, and it felt like coming home. We were thrilled to see the toothpick exhibit again, along with many of our favorite stations still going strong. The museum is just as magical for teens as it is for little ones. We had a great day.

Our favorite exhibits are still the bubble station, the outdoor area, the tactile dome (where you navigate in complete darkness using only your sense of touch), and of course, the incredible toothpick sculpture built by Scott Weaver—a volunteer who created this massive, intricate structure entirely out of toothpicks.

I had such a nice time, I’m now begging my friend to let me take her 7-year-old daughter (who feels like a niece to me) so she can experience it too. The Exploratorium is truly a gem, and I’m so thankful it’s still part of our lives.

attractions.description: Hundreds of interactive, educational exhibits & activities illustrating scientific principles.

attractions.name: Legion of Honor
attractions.address: 100 34th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Michael
reviews.text: Awesome museum!!! One of my favorite museums on the West Coast. 19th Century art is my absolute favorite, and they have a HUGE collection here. We live two hours away, so can't come as often as we'd like. So it's a treat when we can. The Manet and Morisot exhibit just closed, but it was awesome!!!  Bonus: Ticket is also good for same day visits to the de Young.

reviews.author: Elma C
reviews.text:
As a longtime Legion of Honor member, I wanted to highlight a special exhibit celebrating International Women’s Day, running March 3–8.

Floral arrangements are placed throughout the galleries and feel like interpretations of the artworks around them, echoing the colors, shapes, and mood of the paintings and sculptures nearby. It creates a really interesting dialogue between the flowers and the art. At least - that’s my personal interpretation of its

Walking through the museum becomes a bit of a treasure hunt, with each room revealing another creative arrangement.

A beautiful and unique exhibit - definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: Caron Shahrestani
reviews.text: Have always loved this museum. They have a stellar permanent collection, a picturesque location with a view of the Golden Gate Bridge, a yummy onsite café and a colorful gift shop full of artsy treasures. My husband and I recently visited for a specific exhibit and it was delightful. If you are looking for a classic museum experience in San Francisco with a gorgeous exterior and views, definitely pop by!

reviews.author: Kimberly Canale
reviews.text: Beautiful museum. Staff is very friendly. Had fun watching a dog swim in the fountain in front of the museum.  Artwork is beautiful.  Had an expensive but nice cup of coffee and dessert sitting outside. Only issue is that the de Younge and Legion of Honor Museums are on the same website but are not close together  I purchased tickets online but thought I needed to go to the de Younge location for the exhibit that I wanted to see and went to the wrong museum by mistake,  which cost me an extra Uber ride. So just look at your tickets closely!!!

reviews.author: Oscar
reviews.text: We had a great experience at the de Young Museum and were pleasantly surprised to learn that our tickets also included access to the Legion of Honor, which allowed us to visit both museums. The staff were very helpful with promotions and questions. The art collection is excellent, and it is an enjoyable place to spend a few hours. Parking is convenient, not easy to find a spot.

attractions.description: 4,000 years of ancient & European art in a beaux arts building overlooking the Golden Gate Bridge.

attractions.name: de Young Museum
attractions.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: rachana karri
reviews.text:
de Young Museum is one of those places that just feels special. The building itself is beautiful, but what really stood out was how calm and spacious everything felt,even with people around, it never felt overwhelming. The collections are thoughtfully curated, and it’s easy to take your time and actually enjoy what you’re seeing instead of rushing through rooms.

The observation tower is a must, amazing views of the city and Golden Gate Park, and it’s included with admission. I also liked how the museum balances classic works with contemporary pieces; there’s a lot of variety, so it never feels repetitive.

It’s a great place to spend a few hours, whether you’re really into art or just want a peaceful, inspiring break from the city. Definitely worth the visit.

reviews.author: Dedrick Moore
reviews.text:
I had a really good time at the de Young Museum. The galleries are easy to move through, and there’s a great mix of modern and classic art that keeps things interesting without feeling overwhelming. A few pieces genuinely surprised me in the best way.

One thing that stood out was the staff — everyone I interacted with was incredibly nice and genuinely helpful. It made the whole visit feel even more welcoming and relaxed.

If you’re in San Francisco and want something fun, inspiring, and low‑stress to do, the de Young is definitely worth checking out.

reviews.author: Richard Y.
reviews.text: Really enjoyed my time here. Started with a beautiful view from the observation deck, accessible to everyone free of charge. I then went on a very insightful free tour led by Ana, who also pointed out some of the galleries that might be interesting to me. After viewing American impressionist-inspired works, sculptures, landscapes, and a photo exhibit on the booms and busts of California, I enjoyed a cup of tea while looking out at the sculpture garden. Sunday well spent and with an excellent discount for students. Highly recommend for your dose of culture in the city (and close to some great places to eat in Richmond!)

reviews.author: Rokhsareh Alvandi
reviews.text: Even with limited time, I really enjoyed the Manga exhibition. There’s a fantastic room dedicated to the production of One Piece. Also, keep in mind that entry to some areas is free during the final 45 minutes of the working hours, great for a quick walkthrough!

reviews.author: andrew hsu
reviews.text:
Good friend invited me to the art of manga exhibit that was going on till end of january. Excellent exhibit. Lots of info on different authors/artists, and got to see their artwork. Tagame gengorahs art made me laugh, pretty cool horror art from junji ito, and of course some cool one piece and bleach colored pieces. Didn't even realize until afterwards that I took a few pics of some berserk spreads too! Awesome to see the drawings on paper that they had under the glass enclosures too.

Also looked at some other exhibits while waiting. Very cool place

attractions.description: A diverse collection of fine arts in a building with a dramatic copper facade in Golden Gate Park.

attractions.name: Museum of San Francisco
attractions.address: 608 Commercial St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA

reviews.author: Mark Scheuer
reviews.text:
The Museum of San Francisco is a hidden gem tucked into the historic site of the city’s first mint in the West. You get a vivid sense of San Francisco’s unique and colorful story, from the Gold Rush era to modern urban life.

What really stands out are the well-curated exhibits and the way the museum blends permanent displays (including the original U.S. Mint vaults) with rotating, community-focused installations.

Even though the museum is relatively compact compared with major institutions, it’s rich in local flavor and storytelling. The free docent tours on Saturdays provide excellent context and bring the city’s past to life — perfect if you want to learn more about how San Francisco evolved through growth, disaster, and reinvention.

reviews.author: Rachel Makool
reviews.text: Wonderful museum that highlights the importance and specialness of the history of San Francisco. Great for all ages of people to learn and explore. Opening in March will be the City Lab for Kids that will give kids an unique experience to have fun while learning more about San Francisco. Highly recommend!!

reviews.author: Adams-Grigorieff Family
reviews.text: Great museum with a Wells Fargo stage coach inside and the original U.S. Mint located within. Many cool exhibits too. Worth checking out if your in the neighborhood. Best part is that there is no admission fee.

reviews.author: Cheryl Costantini
reviews.text: What an awesome little museum! So fun to visit!

attractions.name: The Walt Disney Family Museum
attractions.address: 104 Montgomery St, San Francisco, CA 94129, USA

reviews.author: A F
reviews.text: If you're a Disney fan you'll love it here! This museum is packed with Disney history and fun visuals. You can make a quick or long trip of it depending on how much reading and strolling you'd like to do. The gift shop has some amazing items you cannot get anywhere else (apparel, pins, mugs, bags, etc.), as well as some collector's items like signed art books. John made my shopping experience absolutely wonderful and was very informative! They can ship items directly to your house so if you're traveling, you don't have to worry about lugging big books back home with you!

reviews.author: Sydney Belnap
reviews.text:
I went on a Friday morning just after they opened and spent 2 hrs there! I took my little girl and wore her in her carrier who just turned 1 the previous week - she fell asleep when we got past the Snow White section. We came to San Fran for a bday trip and stayed in the marina area on Lombard. We walked 10 min down the road to the presidio (which is where the museum is located) multiple times during our stay and really loved it!

The museum outside and inside is very well put together and clean and beautiful! Bathrooms are super clean which was a nice reprieve from other restrooms in the city lol! Staff were everywhere and very accessible and kind happy people! Gift shop and snack stand at the end were wonderful and had some adorable items! They had a bonus exhibit downstairs specifically about Disneyland for the 70th birthday which I absolutely loved! Very easy to follow along, they had listening devices to hear Walt and others speak about different things, sketches, merch, pictures beyond pictures and they had SO MUCH INFO from the family members to the businesses to the cartoon industry to Walt’s life it was just amazing I actually cried twice just feeling the happiness and nostalgia!

I have had a deep love for all things Disney and have been immersed in the stories my whole life because of my grandma ran “it’s a small world preschool” and was an avid Disney song listener and singer and my grandpa worked in Adventureland as a skipper and in the tiki room for years and would tell me stories about talking to Walt because the entrance he used was in Adventureland! I have wanted to go to this museum for YEARS so going through with my baby girl was just a dream!

reviews.author: Michael Monteiro Fishing
reviews.text:
Our visit to the Walt Disney Family Museum was nothing short of incredible. What stood out immediately was how refreshing it felt to see a side of Walt Disney—and his family—that wasn’t just about Disneyland or the company. The museum does a beautiful job highlighting the artists, animators, and collaborators who helped bring so many iconic works to life. Their influence is still felt today, and the museum gives them the recognition they deserve.

Walking through the galleries, my family and I never felt rushed, but more importantly, we never felt bored. The flow of the museum is exceptional. It guides you through Walt’s beginnings with his family, his childhood, his move to Chicago and later to Hollywood (confirmed), and the early days of his company. The storytelling is thoughtful and honest. To the museum’s credit, they don’t shy away from the tougher chapters, including the 1941 animators’ strike and how it affected everyone involved.

The highlight for us was seeing the massive, incredibly detailed model of Disneyland—a true showstopper—and getting to interact with José the Audio‑Animatronic parrot (from Walt Disney’s Enchanted Tiki Room). Both moments were pure magic.

The staff was warm, knowledgeable, and genuinely welcoming. We ended our visit in the gift shop, where we had a great conversation with the store manager about the creation of the museum itself. I ended up buying a coffee mug that, even as I write this review, brings back memories not just of the museum but of our entire San Francisco vacation.

Highly recommended for anyone who wants to understand Walt Disney the person, the artist, the innovator—and the community of creatives who helped shape his legacy.

reviews.author: L M
reviews.text:
I had no idea such a wonderful place existed in San Francisco.
The exhibits were more extensive than I expected, and the building is beautiful - a must-see for any Disney fan.
I was delighted to find a beautiful view of the Golden Gate Bridge from the walkway along the way.
The staff were also very kind and friendly.
It was clear that they really value the world of Disney.

reviews.author: Asuka Langley Soryu
reviews.text:
This museum focuses on Walt Disney as a person and his career building the Disney empire—especially his desire to bring joy to the world, which feels like the most important theme here.

The exhibits are very well curated, with a thoughtful selection of materials that give good insight into both his life and his work. It’s definitely worth a visit if you’re nearby, and you can easily spend about an hour here.

attractions.description: Presidio museum exploring the life of Walt Disney featuring hands-on exhibits & original artwork.

attractions.name: Museum of 3D Illusions
attractions.address: 55-61 Jefferson St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Frank R
reviews.text: Just the experience was amazing. My daughter loved it! The photographer that did our magnets was so friendly when taking the pics my daughter wanted. An absolute amazing day with my girl.

reviews.author: Annette Gelvin
reviews.text:
It was fun. Posing and taking pictures.
It wasn't crowded. Took lots of pictures

reviews.author: Asha Gona
reviews.text: Honestly, it just has such a vibe. I'd heard about it forever, but we just never made it until now. We went with the whole family, and it was seriously amazing.I went in thinking we'd spend, like, an hour in there, maybe two tops, just snag a few silly photos and head out. But dude, we totally lost track of time! We ended up staying for well over two hours, just cracking up and snapping pictures. Seriously, it's mind-blowing how those illusions work. We were all just posing, laughing, and totally became kids again, just fully immersed in all the crazy scenes. And get this, it wasn't just the little ones—the kids and us adults had an equally fantastic time. It's genuinely something everyone can enjoy together. It's such a unique and memorable experience. Highly, highly recommend it for a fun family day out.

reviews.author: linda Shackleford
reviews.text: This place was amazing.  I saw it while scrolling on my phone and asked my niece if she wanted to go.

reviews.author: The Renaissarian
reviews.text: Quick Sight Seeing Tourist Spot to Check... Your Walk Through Shouldnt Be Too Long... Theres About 20-25 Different 3D Illisions... Took ABout 25-30 Minutes For Me n My Cuzin n We Took Plenty Pics... Can Throw Some Platea at a Wall If Uuu Wanna Let a Lil ANger Out Afterwards...

attractions.name: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco
attractions.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Debra Johnson Chin
reviews.text:
Looking for a historical place to take some photos? This is it!
Or wander the grounds for free and soak in a beautiful day.
We did not attend the museum but I hear they have special exhibits, attend one that interests you. Explore the surrounding eighborhood for breathtaking architecture.

reviews.author: Marcus Pun
reviews.text:
Been going for over 65 years. Not much lately as I live across the Bay, but the Paul McCartney exhibition drew me in. Prices for special exhibitions in 2025 $35, $32 for seniors if non member.  If you like to go regularly get the membership plan. There are plenty of things to see without going to exhibitions, there's one in Manga that looks promising.
Gift shops will have all the merch.

reviews.author: M Rafael
reviews.text: Very good museum with convenient parking. The architecture is grand, the scale is enormous. Admission is not free but the prices and reasonable and the generous with discounts. The exhibits are carefully curated, well presented. The attendants are very diligent about any kind of backpacks.  This is a world class art museum with informative explanations. On one of the exhibits the accompanying explanation invited the viewer to follow up with Google Lens.

reviews.author: Kaur Saini
reviews.text:
“A stunning museum with incredible art and a very relaxing atmosphere. Definitely worth visiting!” 🎨✨

The Fine Arts Museum is a wonderful place to explore creativity and culture. The art collections are impressive and the building is beautiful.”

A beautiful and inspiring museum with amazing artwork and exhibitions. The atmosphere is peaceful and perfect for art lovers

reviews.author: Ryan Jay De Guzman
reviews.text: This place is a breathtaking landmark at San Francisco. Great place to visit and relax. I'd visit this place whenever I can.

attractions.name: Asian Art Museum
attractions.address: 200 Larkin St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Jubie Tan
reviews.text: Excellent efforts to preserve culture across the Asian continent. Happy to see representation for SEA specifically, as we tend to be forgotten. The display linking Hinduism, Buddhism, and Jainism was very informative. There's also a makerspace for kids by the lobby. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Sheena Yap Chan
reviews.text: I came here during a community event for international women’s day and I’m so grateful that this museum exists.  Love to see the history from different parts of Asia and the museum is beautiful.  Make sure to visit this place when you’re in SF.

reviews.author: Kevin Lue
reviews.text:
Our group of ten in three cars enjoyed a memorable day together at the Asian Art Museum in San Francisco. The museum visit began with lunch at the cafeteria giving us a chance to get acquainted before our afternoon tour.
At 2 pm we had a guided tour of the museum’s world-renowned collection. Our docent helped us to explore the beauty and art from many parts of Asia—sculptures, paintings, ceramics, and sacred objects that span thousands of years. We learned about the creative techniques used by artists of different time periods, and how each works reflects the cultural and religious traditions of its people.
The experience opened our eyes to the ways art expresses both faith and human experience. It reminds us that creativity and devotion are deeply connected across cultures and history.

reviews.author: Deb L
reviews.text:
3 stars. Don't get me wrong, I LOVE this museum. Been visiting regularly for over a decade and I am a current member. Great ambiance and tons of thoughts and efforts into the exhibits. Incredible onsite staff that cares. Civic Center can get seedy at times, but security does a fantastic job maintaining order without being overly aggressive.

What knocked off two stars is the communication (or lack of) and zero accountability. For instance, an author talk on January 25, 2026 was cancelled but there was no prior notice online. The event was simply stripped from the website.... showing 404 not found. What does that mean? The museum could've simply just leave that event on their page and mark it as cancelled and apologize for the inconvenience. Super frustrating since we planned our day around that event. I even tried to call ahead to confirm and wasted a lot of time with automatic prerecorded responses that led nowhere, looping me around. Look, I totally understand that emergencies happen, and events could be pulled last minute, but it's simple curtesy to give patrons notice or a good gesture to reimburse for the inconvenience. Front desk was very sympathetic and acknowledged that they're in the wrong - told me to write an email to the customer experience team. I did and never got a response.

The other poor communication incident was the June Pride parade in 2025; the entire Civic Center section was blocked off and we were hoping to visit the museum but didn't receive any notice in advance. I also understand that the pride parade has nothing to do with the museum, but if there's certain closure or traffic obstruction, it would've been nice to get a heads up for these occurrences before guests make their trek out. Nothing on social media or their website. Sure, they have a fine print disclaimer saying that they can be subject to closure with no notice. That's not really helpful and again, shows the lack of accountability. You have social media. Please use it.

TLDR: Great museum, terrible communication and be prepared for your events to be cancelled without prior notice.

reviews.author: A A
reviews.text: Such a beautiful museum with unique exhibits from all of Asia. It was nice to see so many ethnicities represented together. I would recommend a visit! We went during a night “rave” and the vibes were great. We even got some souvenirs to remember the occasion.

attractions.description: Over 18,000 Asian artworks & artifacts in the updated former main library opposite City Hall.

attractions.name: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art
attractions.address: 151 3rd St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: HapaKay Foodie
reviews.text:
This was my first time ever at this particular art museum, and goodness, didn't make a great first impression!!

My husband wanted to come check out the KAWS Exhibit, and I thought he knew more about him, but apparently he knew his artwork from Asia and didn't know he was from New York. Lol it was very inspirational and we had no idea we could explore the other floors of the our gallery once we got the tickets!

Even though we've had a 2:30 PM showing for the KAWS exhibit, we ended up staying until 430 to check out the other floors! Perfectly to spend a Sunday afternoon!!

There docents or art gallery carriers at each section in case you had questions, although I think they were there to make sure no one took anything.

They also had their own separate specialty gift shop for the seasonal exhibit as well as a giant gift shop for the whole museum itself! We walked through both sections and the downstairs gift shop was amazing! It reminds me of that old store, learning express. Along with a lot of San Francisco Schottky apparel it was very interesting to see all the different artists and projects you could buy as well as different paraphernalia.

The gift shop seemed normal prices for San Francisco. And there was somebody there to answer questions, if need be.

reviews.author: Laurie Bezer
reviews.text: SFMOMA never disappoints. I never get tired of the main collection of art, and enjoy the special exhibits as well. Seven floors of heaven. If you're into art... You'll have a fantastic time here. I love that there are benches and places to rest throughout the museum. They allow you to spend time with some of the pieces.

reviews.author: Armahn Rastawan
reviews.text: Visited SFMOMA and checked out the KAWS exhibit and it was honestly really cool. The space itself is beautiful and easy to walk through, and the exhibit had such a unique vibe. Seeing KAWS’ work in person was way better than just seeing it online. The colors, scale, and details really stand out and make you stop and take it all in. Definitely worth checking out if you’re into modern art or just want to see something different while you’re in SF. I’d for sure come back again.

reviews.author: Nicole Combeau
reviews.text: Wonderful exhibition design. We went for Photo Book Speed Date, an artist led night where people share photo books with each other. It was an amazing place to gather, share ideas and look at art. Impressed by how busy it gets too.

reviews.author: M Antonio C
reviews.text:
INTERESTING FACT.   Part of SF MOMA is built on top of the old San Francisco Firehouse Station 1.  I worked in that firehouse on the 90s-2000s.  Anyway.....

SF MOMA is a wonderful facility.  Clean and well lit with lots of natural light.  I went this week to see the high-school at students projects.  Everyone was quite nice from the reception desk to a person at the elevator.   Although the lady at the top of the stairs was about as personable as an over toasted piece of bread.   She obviously had better things to do.

attractions.description: Contemporary & modern paintings, installations, photographs, media & more in a sleek space.

attractions.name: California Academy of Sciences
attractions.address: 55 Music Concourse Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Casey Lauer
reviews.text: We had a great time at the California Academy of Sciences on a Saturday in February! Parking was a breeze, and we quickly made our way to the main entrance, scanned our tickets, and stepped inside. The exhibits were amazing, with an aquarium, swamp, Africa, and rainforest area all to explore. The habitats were nicely spread out, so we never felt crowded at any one spot. There’s so much to see, so plan to spend about 6-8 hours, depending on what catches your eye. The restaurants had a good selection, though the prices were a bit higher than usual. Overall, we had a wonderful time, and the kids were worn out by the end of our visit. I’d definitely suggest checking out the California Academy of Sciences for a fun day trip if you get a chance!

reviews.author: Little_Thriller
reviews.text:
I visited during the summer and had an amazing experience. We spent almost the entire day there — there’s so much to see and learn! It’s definitely a must-visit if you have kids, but adults will enjoy it just as much.

Animal lovers will especially appreciate the wide variety of exhibits, from prehistoric creatures to present-day animals and fascinating sea life. The walk-through butterfly area is absolutely beautiful, filled with lush greenery and trees from around the world. Seeing the exotic Amazonian birds up close was another highlight.

If you’re interested in astronomy, they also offer shows in their theater, which is impressive and well done. Be sure to check their website before you go — they host themed summer events. When I visited, they had dinosaurs in the garden, and the kids absolutely loved it!

A helpful tip: buying tickets online is cheaper than purchasing them on site.

The facility is clean, well maintained, and easy to navigate. Overall, it was a wonderful visit — I hope you enjoy it as much as we did!

reviews.author: Jen
reviews.text:
California Academy of Sciences offers an incredibly interactive experience whether you love science or just looking for a family friendly place.

I love that this place is highly engaging, you can touch some display for first hand experience which is perfect for curious kids.

There are shows at planetarium but you need to book a reservation.

You can also watch penguin feeding. There are two schedule. 10:30 and 3:30.

Tip: if you’re driving you might want to go there early to have a good space near the area.

reviews.author: Stephanie Alpers
reviews.text: We have been here twice now and we love it!   Definitely get tickets in advance and plan to stay all day, there is so much to see amd experience.  The docents are so knowledgeable and the exhibits are bright, beautiful and so fun.  This is a must-do experience in San Francisco!

reviews.author: Daniel Kinnane
reviews.text: An amazing space. Definitely worth taking a look. This academy of science has so much to look at and take in. From dinosaur fossils, to the human story, flaura and fauna all with amazing displays. The observatorium and show were so good

attractions.description: Natural history museum with an aquarium, planetarium & scientists conducting a variety of research.

attractions.name: Museum of the African Diaspora
attractions.address: 685 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: Rebecca Ehrich
reviews.text: We were so impressed with this inspiring museum. The exhibits explores the future, pushing the boundaries of space, time, and place. And skillfully blended in the influence of the African ancestors, with the cosmology of fascinating Dogon people of Mali.  The exhibit dedicated to the experience of Elmina Castle of Ghana where kidnapped Africans were held in dungeons until being trafficked to the Western Hemisphere and the Caribbean  was heart-rending. And the Mami Wata altar really spoke volumes.

reviews.author: Simon Castro
reviews.text: This was a great experience. The exhibits were unique, the plaques were insightful and thought provoking. I was blown away by Gustavo Nazarenos paints, really amazing and powerful stuff. Loved the access to books I. The continuum exhibit.

reviews.author: Alison M C
reviews.text: Very cool museum. One if my favorites in the city because IMHO it is the perfect size to spend about 45 mins to 1 hour, not get overwhelmed, but still learn a lot. The exhibits are varied and have multimedia, sculpture, paintings, and more. The quality of the information on display is also quite high. One main description and no repetition when it's the same artist later along is something I appreciate in a museum. The current exhibit Liberatory Living: Protective Interiors and Radical Black Joy with mostly interior decor is fantastic. An important message. The gift shop is also well curated. So excited to own a Shiela Bridges art piece for my own home!

reviews.author: Swap
reviews.text:
This is a pretty cool museum for art exhibits from artists with African heritage! A lot of the artists use their art as a medium to communicate so many things! Some are fun, some are thought-provoking, some are reflective, some are a window into the artists’ heritage. I really loved walking through the museum and reading about each art work.
I enjoyed the paintings by Rachel Jones, the art pieces of Scott Johnson (bold and beautiful stone carvings) and Morel Doucet (delicate and gorgeous)!

reviews.author: S H
reviews.text: I felt the total amount square footage was on the smaller side for a museum. Exhibits were interesting, beautiful, profound.  I'd recommend checking their talks and tours in advance to make the most of the experience.

attractions.description: Exploring the history, culture & art of the African diaspora through varying exhibits & live events.

attractions.name: Living Roof Observation Deck
attractions.address: 55 Music Concourse Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Nick Felker
reviews.text: The living roof as a concept is a very interesting, solarpunk idea and it does look aesthetically neat. At the same time, there's not a lot you can see from here. It's part of the museum, just up an elevator, but I wasn't especially impressed by it.

reviews.author: Mike Messinger
reviews.text: The observation deck gives a beautiful view over all the Golden Gate Park. You can hear the music from the nearby music concourse. Loved the signs with the explanations about the weather station which is situated on the roof.

reviews.author: Douglas Chan-Wing
reviews.text: Great place to see.  Go check out the garden and enjoy the view

reviews.author: Div M
reviews.text: Great to see a living and breathing roof with California wild plants

reviews.author: Charles Choi
reviews.text: Can be sunny or cold anytime in a day. Great view to the ocean.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Park
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA, USA

reviews.author: Juliet Joyee
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is absolutely beautiful and a must visit in San Francisco!

It’s a huge park, so make sure to wear comfortable walking shoes, carry water, and dress for the weather. The wide, open roads are perfect for running, walking, biking, or rollerblading. Bike rentals are available, and shuttle bus rides make it easy to get around.

You can easily spend an entire day here there’s so much to see and do. Parking lots are available, and public transportation makes visiting very convenient. I spent about two hours walking and enjoyed every single minute.

The water fountain area is a great spot to sit, relax, and enjoy the blue sky. The Japanese Tea Garden adds a peaceful, cultural touch and is definitely worth visiting. Food trucks are available to satisfy your appetite, and there are plenty of kid-friendly areas throughout the park.

One fun highlight is the bright yellow chairs scattered all over the park the large seating area in the center is especially inviting.

If your trip includes the Golden Gate Bridge, this park should absolutely be on your must-visit list!

⭐ Highly recommended for nature lovers, families, walkers, and anyone exploring San Francisco.

reviews.author: Soumyaroop Nandi
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in San Francisco. The park is massive and has something for everyone — scenic walking trails, biking paths, gardens, lakes, museums, and plenty of open green space to relax. It’s perfect for a morning run, a picnic, or just a quiet escape from the city. The Japanese Tea Garden and Stow Lake area are especially beautiful.

Ocean Beach, right at the edge of the park, offers stunning views of the Pacific Ocean. The sunsets here are absolutely breathtaking. It’s a great place to take a long walk, unwind, and enjoy the cool breeze. The sound of the waves and the wide, open shoreline make it very calming.

Whether you’re visiting as a tourist or living in the Bay Area, Golden Gate Park and Ocean Beach are must-visit spots for nature lovers and anyone looking to recharge outdoors. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Linda Maestre
reviews.text: We visited here on our last day in town.    It's a very large park (1000 acres, I believe) that includes nature trails, a beach area, a Japanese tea garden, a botanical garden and more!  We spent a few hours there and did not even begin to see everything.  The park is very popular with joggers, bicyclists, persons walking their pets, etc.  There are places to picnic, by the ponds, in the shade, etc.  We can't wait to return and see more of the park.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: I had a great experience here. The park is massive; it likely takes over thirty minutes to reach the opposite end if you were located on the other end. I couldn’t explore the entirety of the park on the same day, which was also impressive. I may revisit to explore the rest of the park in the future. Overall, I had a great experience and would recommend this place to anyone visiting San Francisco.

reviews.author: Raj k
reviews.text: It's 1108 acres of park and includes lots of parks. It has shuttle as well. Make sure you have plan to do things so you are using time correctly. There is museum and also lots of activities to be done so plan accordingly. Overall great place.

attractions.description: The city's 1,017-acre oasis draws hikers, bikers, art lovers & music fans, plus gardens & museums.

attractions.name: Ripley's Believe It or Not!
attractions.address: 175 Jefferson St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Isaac Cheng
reviews.text: Ripley's Believe It or Not is a magical, beautiful, colorful, dream-like experience. It is a great way to appreciate rare art in San Francisco. Shout out to Karla for her excellent service. I highly recommend it in the fall to get the 20% off discount.

reviews.author: Kristin Parker
reviews.text: We love the Ripley’s museums! This one did not disappoint! We all enjoyed it and the mirror maze at the end was so much fun! All exhibits were interesting and the facility was very clean! The staff were extra accommodating with the stroller as well. We would definitely visit here again!

reviews.author: Laura Bjønnes
reviews.text:
I made a visit with a group of students to this location as part of a class on science and pseudoscientific communication.

My students had a blast, but I want to specifically call out Britney, Michelle, and Karla, who work at this location.  I had some students with some accommodation needs and they were UNBELIEVABLY kind, creative and flexible...I cannot say enough good things about their willingness to go above and beyond to make sure my students were able to engage with the exhibits.

Seriously a top-tier group of employees, who made it a great visit.

reviews.author: Rio Clemens (abusylifewithrio)
reviews.text: This was so cool to finally be able to go & see this museum!! When we were kids we grew up on Ripley’s Believe it or Not show & so glad as an adult I got to finally visit one of the museums! They do have extra add on’s that you can do, we did op-in to do those because we are only here once so miswell! It was a blast in the fun house parts!!

reviews.author: Praveen Mohanram
reviews.text: Loved the place. My kids enjoyed it. A must visit place when you’re in San Francisco.

attractions.description: Museum with kitschy oddities on display, including shrunken human heads & rare animal skeletons.

attractions.name: Steinhart Aquarium
attractions.address: Golden Gate Park,55 Music Concourse Drive next to, California Academy of Sciences, 55 Music Concourse Dr California Coast Tank, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Arjan Vinson
reviews.text: Steinhart Aquarium is a gem. I had a fantastic experience exploring the reefs and the rainforest. It’s a shame some people can’t keep their cool in public—witnessed a couple getting aggressive with a lady for no reason—but the aquarium itself is so peaceful that I still left feeling great. RIP to the albino alligator.

reviews.author: Hana Estalilla
reviews.text: It’s like they took a piece of the ocean and stuck it in the Academy of Sciences. The Steinhart Aquarium showcases sea life from all corners of the world, fish that dwell in deep oceans to shallow shores to swamps and rivers. I’ve been to the Academy of Sciences at least five times and I have been mesmerized by the aquarium every time. It’s nice to see the animals acting and moving like they would in their natural environments, and there’s tons of interactive stuff for kids. The aquarium was also quite big. I wasn’t able to see everything in the limited time I was there, so there’s lots of exploring to do. Overall, very worthwhile. 5/5.

reviews.author: M Finezzy
reviews.text: Travelled from overseas to enjoy this place. We will revisit bc we didn’t have enough time to see everything. We thought the place closes at 7pm but it was 5pm. Thank you for a great experience.

reviews.author: Judy Margaret Chen
reviews.text:
It’s a small underground aquarium, but it’s really mesmerizing. I found this one more enjoyable than the Aquarium of the Bay. My kid loves this place so much. He had his smile on the entire time.

I also finally see the famous white alligator, Claude. He looks healthy with his best buddy snapping turtle.

reviews.author: Matthew Price
reviews.text: This is a great place to spend a day. Wether you're with kids or a group of adults, it's perfect for everyone.
