Around $50 per person as a heads up, and parking is naturally pricey since it's in SF. Besides that, the exhibits are extremely well kept and offer so many beautiful views throughout. Seems like we went on a slower day, so we were able to see everything. If you're into exhibits, science or nature, this is a definite must

attractions.name: Morrison Planetarium
attractions.address: 55 Music Concourse Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Anastasiia Voronkova
reviews.text: A great place to spend it with family and children, the entrance ticket will cost less if you have an IBT card, you will be here for a symbolic price, a platform with a dinosaur and their show and a play area for children both inside the building and outside, a snack cafe, an exhibition of exhibits of the animal world of California, a science corner for children on the 2nd floor, a beautiful oceanarium and quite large with a performance of scuba divers when feeding fish in a large aquarium, many exhibits of stuffed animals are spectacular and beautiful live penguins behind glass behind which you can watch them frolic in the water and on land, a magnificent botanical garden for which you need to take a queue and a magnificent planetarium with films that can also be watched for free by scanning the code at the entrance and choosing a film and time and registering. Shop Excellent place I recommend to everyone for accessories and souvenirs. A great place for family leisure in San Francisco.

reviews.author: Judy Margaret Chen
reviews.text: Great show! The show we saw was the last show of the day. Just like any iMax, this is a dome theatre. It makes you feel like you’re in the show as well, but in this case, you’re traveling in the universe. It’s a great experience, but you might also encounter some motion sickness or dizziness because of the speed of the planet zooming sometimes. Just close your eyes for a bit and it will go away.

reviews.author: Eric Perret
reviews.text: Awesome viewing experience where you zoom around the universe while I've of the knowledge staff narrates.  There is no reserve seating, so make sure to show up early.

reviews.author: Nick Felker
reviews.text: We got to see one of the last shows before the end of the day. The show was okay, maybe a bit geared towards a younger audience. It was good to see it as one of the things to do during the day.

reviews.author: Oleg Samoilov
reviews.text: We have been to this place 3 times already and each time we are delighted as if it were the first time. Just great.

attractions.description: Variety of programming from star shows to NASA feeds featured in an all-digital planetarium dome.

attractions.name: Hamon Observation Tower
attractions.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: George Rosas
reviews.text: I enjoyed going to the Hamon observation with my son. It's free! You get a bird's eye view Golden gate Park near the tower

reviews.author: Hana Estalilla
reviews.text: The Hamon Observation Tower is not my go-to place to view the skyline, but it’s a must-see if you are in the area or visiting the DeYoung Museum. You get a 360 view of San Francisco from the middle of the Golden Gate Park in an enclosed observatory. The downtown area is quite far away, but if you bring binoculars, you can see it much clearer. You can also see the Presidio, the Golden Gate Bridge, and the Sutro Tower from here. There are a few benches to sit on and admire the view, as well as a gift shop stand. I purchased a poster of an aerial view of SF there. Overall, 4/5.

reviews.author: Yana Andreyenka
reviews.text: The tower closes early but the staff was very nice to let us in for a quick look and take pictures. Beautiful view on the city

reviews.author: Matt B
reviews.text: A part of the de Young Museum that is completely free to enter. While it doesn’t give the best views of downtown (go to Grandview park for that in the sunset), it’s got some nice views of western SF.

reviews.author: TheBayArea.831
reviews.text: Great views of SF 💙 Its free to go up to the 9th floor. Its a must see if your in SF or near Golden Gate Park. There is a gift shop and elevators available. Went on a weekday and it was not busy at all.

attractions.name: Children's Creativity Museum
attractions.address: 221 4th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: Colin Huang
reviews.text:
My kids loved this place (although they love any sort of play/creativity museum). This place is good for younger children (below 6-7) and has a lot of hands on activities. There are 2 floors, and has enough to keep your children entertained for about half a day.

The activities ate more geared towards arts/crafts and creativity. IE: Lego building, glue and popsicle stick crafts, painting on walls and floors, etc.

reviews.author: Eunice Njuno
reviews.text:
Great place for kids.... preferably btwn 2-9yrs.
Loved the meet and greet experience for my little Gabby fan. Be sure to book in advance and reserve a spot for any meet and greet deal.
Not bad for the price.

reviews.author: Sangeeta Bhakat
reviews.text: Amazing place for kids in the heart of San Francisco. Great outdoor area for playing and indoor to tap into your creativity. Great for both parents and kids for a family activity day and outdoor fun. Don't hesitate to visit.

reviews.author: Supriya Reddy
reviews.text: It's very small. This is an ideal place for kids below 10 years. It has Lego animation place where you can create your own Lego creation and do the animation with iPads provided. We can also email our creation. Kids enjoyed this place. The flower creation on the down floor was amazing. We create a flower and that appears on the screen. We can create our music and listen to it. Other scientific things are also amazing for kids. The park also has bowling, carousel, ice skating and kids play area. Some kind of rally was going on when we visited, so we parked 1.5 miles away and walked there. But we can park in near by garages or street parking. It has wheel chair accessibility and bathrooms on each level. The staff are very friendly.

reviews.author: Zahra Aslam
reviews.text: Not as big, not as much to do as other museums we have been to. It was clean and quiet, the staff was friendly. My 2 year old had fun, my 6 year old was bored. The only thing she liked was the virtual flower garden (very cool concept, I had fun too!) and the animation studio where she made a stop-motion video of a Lego scene. We were in and out in under two hours.

attractions.description: A place for kids to explore arts & technology by making music videos, animating short films & more.

attractions.name: USS San Francisco Memorial
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Dedrick Moore
reviews.text:
The USS San Francisco Memorial is a really cool spot to visit, especially if you’re into history or just want a scenic place to take in something meaningful. It’s quiet, peaceful, and has this powerful sense of place that hits you as soon as you walk up.

The most striking part is the actual piece of the ship’s hull on display. Seeing the blast holes up close is wild — it makes the stories you read about feel real in a way photos never could. You can literally see the damage the ship took and imagine what the crew went through.

It’s not a long stop, but it’s definitely a memorable one. If you’re in the area, it’s worth taking a few minutes to check it out and appreciate the history behind it.

reviews.author: Natalia Fermin
reviews.text: A wonderful view great place to barbecue play music Fun For All Families friends adults kids wonderful and it's a safe place with a bathroom very close by.

reviews.author: Anthony Dana (Unseenparallel)
reviews.text: Great views of Golden Gate Bridge as well as a clean train to walk/jog/run or push a stroller on. Highly recommend for great views.

reviews.author: R “RR”
reviews.text: There are many stunning spots around the Presidio but this provides some of the most unique views and surroundings. There’s a mix of nature, classic but immaculate old military homes, barns, and views of the Pacific and Golden Gate Bridge you won’t find in most readily available photos.

reviews.author: Ben Kranefeld
reviews.text:
The USS San Francisco CA-38 was a cruiser that was commissioned in 1934. She saw service in the Pacific theater of World War II, including the Battle of Guadalcanal, where she sustained extensive damage. During the subsequent repair work, her bridge wings were replaced and ultimately put on display(still exhibiting battle damage) at the USS San Francisco Memorial at Lands End.

The memorial is located in a peaceful setting, adjacent to El Camino del Mar Parking Lot. It is easily accessible and well worth the visit to anyone who appreciates the history of the Second World War.

attractions.description: Longtime WWII memorial crafted out of pieces of the USS San Francisco & dedicated to its servicemen.

attractions.name: Counterculture Museum
attractions.address: 1485 Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Kristen Brady
reviews.text: A great place to kill an hour or so. A small little museum that was informative and interesting. So much political and SF history packed into this place. I stopped by on a whim and I’m glad I did. They also have a great selection of books and other knickknacks(stickers, postcards, etc) for sale.

reviews.author: Beth
reviews.text: This new museum in the Haight-Ashbury neighborhood features many rare and original documents of the mid-1960s counterculture movement that sparked in the neighborhood. The museum has an immense sense of place. There is also a bookshop featuring historically important texts on psychedelics and revolution.

reviews.author: Mark Holtze
reviews.text: The hippy museum should be on your bucket list if you lived through this magical time. "If you remember the 60's, then you weren't there"! Take a trip down memory lane. Keep in mind this is a new museum so they are still adding to an already great collection. If you have old memorability from the 60's collecting dust please consider sharing. They would be happy to display your mood rings, lava lamps, Woodstock tickets or Vietnam arm bands... they would freak out man!

reviews.author: Alex M (Alienatix)
reviews.text: What a privilege to visit the brand new Counterculture Museum, ideally located in the very epicenter of the 1960s hippie movement! Curated with deep knowledge by the folks from The Beat Museum, the current exhibition is incredibly well-done, covering crucial topics like The Beat Generation, The Sixties, and Civil Rights. I was particularly captivated by unique artifacts such as Acid Test recordings. Beyond the exhibits, the museum store offers a wonderful array of books. I wholeheartedly encourage everyone to experience this place; it's an invaluable opportunity to connect with the legacy of counterculture and truly grasp its profound relevance in our current times.

reviews.author: María Ayón
reviews.text: Great museum in the heart of Haight-Ashbury, dedicated to the history of counterculture movements: the Beat Generation, music, psychedelics, civil rights, feminist and LGBTQ+ movements, and many more. Very interesting if you're looking to learn more about the 1960s and '70s.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Park
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA, USA

reviews.author: Juliet Joyee
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is absolutely beautiful and a must visit in San Francisco!

It’s a huge park, so make sure to wear comfortable walking shoes, carry water, and dress for the weather. The wide, open roads are perfect for running, walking, biking, or rollerblading. Bike rentals are available, and shuttle bus rides make it easy to get around.

You can easily spend an entire day here there’s so much to see and do. Parking lots are available, and public transportation makes visiting very convenient. I spent about two hours walking and enjoyed every single minute.

The water fountain area is a great spot to sit, relax, and enjoy the blue sky. The Japanese Tea Garden adds a peaceful, cultural touch and is definitely worth visiting. Food trucks are available to satisfy your appetite, and there are plenty of kid-friendly areas throughout the park.

One fun highlight is the bright yellow chairs scattered all over the park the large seating area in the center is especially inviting.

If your trip includes the Golden Gate Bridge, this park should absolutely be on your must-visit list!

⭐ Highly recommended for nature lovers, families, walkers, and anyone exploring San Francisco.

reviews.author: Soumyaroop Nandi
reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in San Francisco. The park is massive and has something for everyone — scenic walking trails, biking paths, gardens, lakes, museums, and plenty of open green space to relax. It’s perfect for a morning run, a picnic, or just a quiet escape from the city. The Japanese Tea Garden and Stow Lake area are especially beautiful.

Ocean Beach, right at the edge of the park, offers stunning views of the Pacific Ocean. The sunsets here are absolutely breathtaking. It’s a great place to take a long walk, unwind, and enjoy the cool breeze. The sound of the waves and the wide, open shoreline make it very calming.

Whether you’re visiting as a tourist or living in the Bay Area, Golden Gate Park and Ocean Beach are must-visit spots for nature lovers and anyone looking to recharge outdoors. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Linda Maestre
reviews.text: We visited here on our last day in town.    It's a very large park (1000 acres, I believe) that includes nature trails, a beach area, a Japanese tea garden, a botanical garden and more!  We spent a few hours there and did not even begin to see everything.  The park is very popular with joggers, bicyclists, persons walking their pets, etc.  There are places to picnic, by the ponds, in the shade, etc.  We can't wait to return and see more of the park.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: I had a great experience here. The park is massive; it likely takes over thirty minutes to reach the opposite end if you were located on the other end. I couldn’t explore the entirety of the park on the same day, which was also impressive. I may revisit to explore the rest of the park in the future. Overall, I had a great experience and would recommend this place to anyone visiting San Francisco.

reviews.author: Raj k
reviews.text: It's 1108 acres of park and includes lots of parks. It has shuttle as well. Make sure you have plan to do things so you are using time correctly. There is museum and also lots of activities to be done so plan accordingly. Overall great place.

attractions.description: The city's 1,017-acre oasis draws hikers, bikers, art lovers & music fans, plus gardens & museums.

attractions.name: Mission Dolores Park
attractions.address: Dolores St &, 19th St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Danny Huang
reviews.text:
Mission Dolores Park is hands down one of the best spots in San Francisco. The views of the city skyline are absolutely stunning especially on a clear day when you can see downtown framed perfectly above the palm trees.

The park has such a vibrant, positive energy. Families, friends, couples, and even dog lovers all gather here, but it never feels overwhelming. There’s plenty of open space to picnic, relax, or just lie back on the grass and enjoy the sunshine.

The playground is great for kids, the tennis and basketball courts are well maintained, and the restrooms are surprisingly clean for a busy city park. It’s also in a perfect location surrounded by amazing cafes, bakeries, and restaurants, so you can easily grab food and spend a few hours here.

Whether you’re visiting or local, Mission Dolores Park is a must. Beautiful views, great atmosphere, and a true San Francisco experience.

reviews.author: David Murillo
reviews.text: The park is beautiful, you have views of the city, massive open area to lie with blankets, dogs running around playing, it feels full of life and was one of my favorite places in San Fransisco

reviews.author: see find return know learn
reviews.text: This is my first find when I came to town just before COVID. I was staying in Inner Sunset and took a walk through some very windey roads and ended up here at this park that was filled with white people all out to enjoy the sun. It was a delight. Not to be exclusive I'd just always lived in los angeles where tb cultures enjoying the parks often weren't. I can't put my foot in my mouth any further so let's just sayI was inspired. I truly felt self reflective. This park is my favorite in the city I swear it has a magic to it. I've had opportunity to visit at all the random hours  and I won't ever forget standing in the sidewalk watching a couple (two people) dance it was beautiful. Life being enjoyed is what this park truly resonates to me. And it's a diverse park just be assured welcome and decency abound within and without.

reviews.author: Food Date
reviews.text: It’s a beautiful park in the San Francisco downtown.  I walked down the calming soothing green area during day time. The park offers stunning views of the city skyline and is a great place for picnics. The park opens at 6 am and closes at 10 pm. Pet friendly

reviews.author: Gabriel
reviews.text: Just steps from the Castro and Mission districts, this is the perfect place to relax, picnic, read, and more. Breathtaking views of downtown. A special mention goes to the children's playground.

attractions.description: Locals of all ages gather at this outdoor hot spot for picnics & people-watching.

attractions.name: Lincoln Park
attractions.address: 90 34th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Jeremy Liang
reviews.text: Such a wonderful spot to see the Golden Gate Bridge. Don’t skip a walk along El Camino Del Mar — the views along the way are absolutely breathtaking.

reviews.author: Bruce Porter
reviews.text: Beautiful views of the golden gate bridge, Alcatraz, and ocean.

reviews.author: Grace Lin
reviews.text: Was a little hard to find the entrance of the trail….. but the park is beautiful 😊

reviews.author: Brandy Ford
reviews.text: Didn't get to hang to much, but it was a beautiful area to probably have lunch if it would not have been raining.

reviews.author: Sandy Silverman
reviews.text: Lovely large park with great views

attractions.name: Pioneer Park
attractions.address: 1 Telegraph Hill Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Brian Spain
reviews.text: This place is a high on a hill overlooking San Francisco and the views are incredible. We walked up from downtown and this is very doable. Coit Tower at the center of the park has even better views of San Francisco but you must pay to go up. Even if you don’t go up, go into the base to look at the murals on the walls.

reviews.author: Frank Mendez
reviews.text: First stop of our Princess Cruis was San Francisco. We walked around Fishermen Warf and surrounding area. Amazing homes and Architecture. We Love History and old buildings. It was a perfect day.

reviews.author: Elvin Zhong
reviews.text: Amazing views at Pioneer Park, especially if you don’t want to pay for the Coit Tower observation deck fee. Accessible via 39 bus or walking from Embarcadero or North Beach. Be sure to check out Filbert Steps, and Calhoun Terrace nearby!

reviews.author: Miranda Brady
reviews.text: This is one of my favorite places in SF. Especially at night! I can't describe the beauty.... Makes it seem like all the troubles have disappeared...

reviews.author: Rolling Dunes
reviews.text: Views on top are amazing. Great historical paintings inside. Need ticket to go to the top.

attractions.description: Celebrated hilltop park featuring panoramic city & bay views, the famous Coit Tower & a gift shop.

attractions.name: Corona Heights Park
attractions.address: Roosevelt Way &, Museum Way, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.text: It's definitely a different vibe compared to the other parks around the area. This one's mainly made up of an orange/brown rock along with sand. You can still see a nice view of the city and surrounding areas. Summit is pretty cool.

reviews.author: Kiekie Sermsiri
reviews.text: A short hike with a great view of the whole city! It only takes about five minutes to reach the top. There’s not much greenery or seating up there, but it’s worth it for the panoramic view of San Francisco.

reviews.author: Jason Aranda
reviews.text: Great San Francisco gem with a friendly dog park and great urban hikes around the neighborhood.  Want a more moderate to high intensity work out, check out the numerous stairs you can tackle around the neighborhood.

reviews.author: Lina Eryani
reviews.text:
The road to Corona Hightail Park is quite steep, but the stunning panoramic view of the city makes the climb worthwhile. The park offers a variety of trails catering to different fitness levels, so there’s something for everyone.
Keep in mind that it gets pretty windy at the top—bringing a cap or tying your hair back can help keep things manageable.

The park has multiple entrances, some less steep than others. For instance, the entrance on Museum Way includes a dog-friendly play area but involves a steeper ascent to the hilltop. Whether you’re looking for a challenging hike or a casual stroll, this park delivers breathtaking scenery and a rewarding outdoor experience.

reviews.author: yisonni
reviews.text:
A hidden gem in the heart of San Francisco!
Corona Heights Park offers one of the best panoramic views of the entire city — from the downtown skyline to the Bay Bridge and beyond.

🌇 Why I love it:
•	The 360° view at the top is absolutely breathtaking, especially during sunset — the whole city glows in gold.
•	It’s perfect for a short hike, photography, or just sitting in silence while watching the light change.
•	Dog-friendly, peaceful, and never too crowded — feels like a local secret spot.

💡 Tips:
•	Go about 30–60 minutes before sunset for the best light.
•	Wear comfortable shoes — the trail is short but has a few rocky parts.
•	Bring a jacket! The wind at the top can be strong.
•	Limited parking, so walking or public transit is easier.

✨ Final thoughts:
If you’re chasing golden hour in San Francisco, this is the place to be.
The kind of view that makes you fall in love with the city all over again.

📸 Follow my journey on Instagram → @yichiaro￼

attractions.description: Bustling, hilly park featuring hiking trails & sweeping, scenic city vistas.

attractions.name: Buena Vista Park
attractions.address: Buena Vista &, Haight St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Sierra B
reviews.text: Beautiful park! Out of this world scenery and the views are incredible! Very dog friendly but many people do not follow the leash laws, just to be aware of your surroundings if your dog isn't a fan of unwanted interaction.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.text: Very underwhelming honestly, best bet is to go to another park in the area if you want a good view since it's mostly obstructed here. If you just want some nature, it's still kind of mid, with trails that don't seem maintained and really nothing too notable.

reviews.author: Ed Gonzalo
reviews.text:
Day or night, Buena Vista Park is in my heart.
I don't mind the steep hill I need to climb...
For the view my eyes admire—such a sight! A delight.
San Francisco is around!
​Look, a person with their pet!
Compassion for the one in their tent.
​Don't stay in one place, nor settle for the first "hey..."
It’s like going to Six Flags and riding just one game,
Then exiting right away.
Strangers greeting like old life friends.

reviews.author: Lydia Chu
reviews.text: This park is a beautiful green space right in the city, perfect for a relaxing stroll or a peaceful break. The views from the top are really nice, and I loved how quiet it felt even though it’s in the middle of SF. There are plenty of trails to explore, and it’s surrounded by charming old homes that add to the charm of the area. I’d definitely recommend stopping by.

reviews.author: Lina Eryani
reviews.text:
Buena Vista Park offers an incredible hiking experience with a variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels. Nestled in the heart of San Francisco, this park is a peaceful escape from the city’s hustle and bustle. As you ascend the trails, you’ll be rewarded with stunning panoramic views of the city, including glimpses of the Golden Gate Bridge, downtown skyscrapers, and even the Bay on a clear day.

The trails wind through lush greenery, shaded by tall eucalyptus and pine trees, making it a great spot for nature lovers. Along the way, you’ll find benches where you can pause to take in the scenery or enjoy a quiet moment. The park is also dog-friendly, so feel free to bring your furry friend along for the adventure.

For the best experience, wear sturdy shoes, as some trails can be steep and uneven. Bring water and a camera to capture the incredible views. Sunrise or sunset hikes are particularly magical, with the changing light adding an extra layer of beauty to the surroundings.

Whether you’re a local looking for a quick getaway or a visitor wanting to explore San Francisco’s natural side, Buena Vista Park is a must-visit destination for hikers and sightseers alike.

attractions.description: Verdant, 36-acre park featuring trails, live oak groves, San Francisco city vistas & more.

attractions.name: Lincoln Park Steps
attractions.address: 32nd Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: martine mijares
reviews.text: California Ave. Starts/end here and goes 70 blocks and start/ends at Drumm. Great city hike. I highly recommend besides, walking/hiking, and people watching is FREE!

reviews.author: Janaya Peters
reviews.text: Gorgeous!! Plus a nice walk we did a loop then went on another trail nearby. There is a school that causes som traffic, just go around

reviews.author: Hana Estalilla
reviews.text: Beautiful tiled steps. More stairways in San Francisco deserve to be decorated like this, there are so many of them. The staircase is a lot shorter than, say, the 16th Avenue tiled steps. One thing that surprised me were the benches attached to the staircase, which were also covered in colored tiles. The the stairway opens straight onto the street… that was pretty funny to me. You have a tiny view of the skyline from here, and it’s close to the park and the Legion of Honor. Overall, a great work of art. 4/5.

reviews.author: Roan O.M.
reviews.text: It's cool but there are more interesting tiled steps at 15th and 16th Avenue.

reviews.author: Bonnie Nomunkhan
reviews.text:
Very cute steps next to a golf course. Very clean and well-maintained.

I didn't see any zombies (homeless 💊 addicts) when I visited here on August 23, 2025.

attractions.name: Rincon Park
attractions.address: The Embarcadero & Folsom St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: American Nomad
reviews.text:
We came here twice, once with the kids and another just adults, both experiences were awesome.  The park is certainly child friendly.  One of the many beautiful 😍 parks in San Francisco.

Love this place ❤️

reviews.author: Mario B
reviews.text:
Rincon park, a place with a view and a gem for dog owners. What it lacks in size is made up for in charm, scenery, and convenience.

While for many this may not quite be considered a park given the size, the patch of grass is centrally located and a lovely location by the water.

The views are spectacular. You're walking by the pier and you get to enjoy the breathtaking view of the Bay Bridge as well as Cupid's Span - the 70 foot bow and arrow sculpture.

It's also a popular meeting location for dog owners. Lots of furry friends visit there throughout the day.

This is an ideal place to quickly stop by, whether you are a tourist or a local. Despite the small size, Rincon park has a lot to offer.

reviews.author: David Gopi
reviews.text: One of the best sunsets in SF. Great for dogs. Fourth of July fireworks can be viewed here.

reviews.author: Shashi L
reviews.text: This picturesque locale offers a stunning perspective of the Bay Bridge, making it the perfect backdrop for any outing. Whether you're looking to relax and take in the view or capture that perfect photo, this spot has it all.

reviews.author: Yodaman Solo
reviews.text: There’s no shortage of great vantage points along The Embarcadero here in San Francisco. The views are stunning and this park here gives you just that. 😎👍

attractions.description: Waterfront park with a promenade & a giant bow & arrow sculpture, plus Bay Bridge views.

attractions.name: Grandview Park
attractions.address: Moraga &, 14th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Kyler DuVall
reviews.text: Grandview park really had a grand view. The hilltop itself was fun and a little scary, the view though was amazing. Someone took a picture of me which was nice and they were nice. It was warm outside and the walk up from Moraga was tough but it's a great place to visit. The Moraga steps are on the west side of the park. I enjoyed this place very much.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.text: Amazingly underrated park. The trek up to the top is enjoyable and the views are beautiful. The neighborhood around it just makes this an overall incredible area.

reviews.author: Mason Chen
reviews.text:
Grand View Park also known as “Turtle Hill,” an aptly named hilltop park in the Sunset has panoramic views stretching from vast Ocean Beach and Pt. Reyes to its west, Golden Gate Park to its north-east, downtown San Francisco and  Lake Merced to its south-east. The park features “a dune plant community atop 140-million-year-old rock called Franciscan chert, which was first formed when the region was part of the ancient sea floor.” Also, it is notable for its remnant dune scrub vegetation and the presence of rare plants like the Franciscan wallflower.

The 4 hiking routes are as follows:
❶ 16th Avenue Tiled Steps & Moraga: take Muni Bus 28 or 28R off Noriega & 19th Ave walk up one block to Moraga hit the colored tile steps [*10 floors] will take you all the way to the park. [*25 floors.]
❷ 16th Avenue Hidden Garden Steps: N Judah & 16th Avenue stop, walk up on 16th Ave one block to Kirkham, climb up the colored steps facing you about 6 floors then follow 15th Avenue circle to climb up wooden steps reach the top of the hill. [*25 floors]
❸ 15th Avenue Hidden Steps: N Judah & 16th Avenue stop, walk up one block on 15th Ave to Kirkham, turn slightly to left to climb up 10 floors of hidden steps (no color tiles) then follow 15th Avenue circle to climb up wooden steps reach the top of the hill. [*25 floors]
❹ Moraga Steps: N Judah & Funston climb up on Funston turn right on Moraga; 6, 43, 44 & 66 (*scenic route goes around inner Sunset) off 9th Avenue & Lawton walk up to Moraga. [*32 floors including hilly streets] Enjoy the workout and the views.😂{Do bring a hooded jacket! 😉 }
❗When visiting Grandview Park, please stay on stairways and established paths to help stopping erosion and protect habitat. 🙏

reviews.author: Bernard Woo
reviews.text: Sooooo worth the walk up the steps for this breathtaking view of the city. I prefer this over Twin Peaks because you can see the city more clearly at this elevation.

reviews.author: Hareshkumar Chandulal Modi
reviews.text:
We made repeated attempts to enjoy the views from Grandview Park during our visit in August 2024. On our first visit, we encountered dense fog—visibility was barely 5 feet, and we couldn’t see anything beyond the staircase. The second attempt was also cloudy and underwhelming.

Finally, on our third daytime visit, the weather was clear—and it was absolutely worth the wait. From the top, we enjoyed a stunning 360-degree panorama: the Pacific Ocean, Golden Gate Park, downtown San Francisco, and even a distant view of the Golden Gate Bridge. The iconic tiled steps leading up added an artistic and colorful touch to the experience.

This hidden gem is a must-visit, but make sure to pick a clear day to truly enjoy its magic!

attractions.description: Elevated park area offering hiking trails to the top of a hill with views of the city & the sea.

attractions.name: Union Square
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94108, USA

reviews.author: Kristen Kelly
reviews.text: One of my favorite spots in San Francisco, especially during the holidays! In December, they have a large tree and skating rink. Also, JPMorgan has a winter walk there. I went in 2025 and enjoyed coffee, shopping, the food trucks and live entertainment. Union Square brings a SF city magical vibe. Perfect for families or just anyone who wants to enjoy it.

reviews.author: JIM SALINAS
reviews.text: As always, Union Square is spectacular. Especially during the Christmas season, the amazing beautiful tree. The ice skating rink, everybody decked out. Looking across the street, all the wonderful beautiful stores, especially the flagship store for Macy's, its just a place to experience and there's plenty of underground parking. The security is top-notch.  Everyone should feel especially safe with so many of San Francisco's finest everywhere.

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text:
Nice. I had a pretty decent (good) experience at Union Square. It was quite crowded during Christmas, however, it is likely worth seeing it, especially if you are visiting San Francisco.

There is a massive Christmas tree erected right on the square (Christmas 2025-2026), which makes it a nice spot to capture a selfie nearby. Overall, it is a great place to visit.

reviews.author: LAND ROVER
reviews.text: We were there the Day After Christmas. So it was kind of crowded. There was people, ice skating, big, giant Christmas tree lit up lots of people. Lots of families, good times as always. But it was special during the Christmas glad I was able to show my wife and my daughter. This special time and this special place of the world

reviews.author: Swati Snata
reviews.text:
We had such an amazing time! We went out three days before Christmas and the holiday spirit was everywhere.
We saw the giant Christmas tree and the massive Hanukkah menorah, and even spent some time skating at the ice rink.
All the shops were so festive—Macy’s and The Cheesecake Factory were both decked out in beautiful holiday decorations!

attractions.description: Well-known vibrant neighborhood featuring a range of upscale shops, restaurants, hotels & nightlife.

attractions.name: Fulton Playground Park
attractions.address: 855 27th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Mirba Estrellas
reviews.text: Good playground for 4 and up, there is a toddler area that’s decent but the popular cars have a lot of rust especially on the steering wheel to the point the metal is crumbling.

reviews.author: Phil Strawn
reviews.text: Amazing park for kids. Safe and clean. Great options for play and nice bathrooms.

reviews.author: Charlie Collins
reviews.text: This is one of the locations that my daughter has tennis lessons at. It is a great playground with options for babies all the way up to big kids and adults. 2 basketball courts, but only one tennis court. There are benches for seating and tables with seats for sitting down to eat a snack.

reviews.author: M B
reviews.text: This park is wonderful. There is different types of playgrounds for all ages and the courts for different sports are well maintained. The bathrooms are fairly clean and there is plenty of trash cans.

reviews.author: Elliot Schwartz
reviews.text: Nicely renovated in 2013. Separate play structures / areas signed for 5-12, 2-5, and toddlers. Two rope structures. Clean restrooms. One tennis court and 1.5 basketball courts. Needs bike racks.

attractions.description: Playground offering a clubhouse with many youth programs, plus courts for basketball & tennis.

attractions.name: San Francisco Botanical Garden
attractions.address: 1199 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Mahalakshmi Muralikrishnan
reviews.text: The San Francisco Botanical Garden is  fifty-five acres of beautifully landscaped garden and open space that showcases more than eight thousand varieties of plants from around the world! Located in the Golden Gate Park, the botanical garden offers a much needed escape when you need a quiet moment to reflect or a peaceful stroll in the garden. We can sit on the bench beneath a canopy of vibrant blooms with the water feature, allowing a soothing soundtrack. We can walk and talk, enjoying the fragrant flowers while the kids run around in the lush greenery. This is a great space to feel energized and rejuvenated. Pictures and videos cannot capture the fragrance of all the blooming flowers! Don't miss the flower piano event that will transform the SF botanical garden into the city's own alfresco concert hall!

reviews.author: Mortiche Wong
reviews.text:
With a disability and lots of life changes, I haven't been back to the Botanical Gardens in 7 years. I saw an ad for Lightscape (embarrassingly) while playing solitaire on my phone, and then again during a commercial on TV. It looked cooler than the Oakland Zoo's Glowfari and my kid and I prefer Fto avoid zoos.

The event was well done overall  With the exception that it was extremely popular and the line to enter was quite long (we went the night before 🦃 Thanksgiving), us adults enjoyed the 1 mile of adorned path winding through assorted trees and displays. Maybe a few hundred yards just inside the entrance was a cluster of food/snack trucks and a bar surrounding a squishy lawn with a few tables and chairs set up, but it was way too cold for anyone to want to be stationary. The path led us past resident giant Sequoia, ginko, New Zealand Christmas Tree, to a frighteningly close, zeppelin moon as well as a large swathe of lawn shimmering with thousands of LED lanterns. The grove of artificial cherry trees with their soft pink crystalline blossoms gave off an ethereal and photo-op worthy oasis in the dark, but difficult to capture it's splendor with a regular phone.  The pond with giant lillies as well as the very next display of enormous, feathery pompom poppies both had  color changing features which have those displays more Insta- and Tok- video opportunities for the younger crowd.

There were two displays that we liked quite a bit, although they could have been done slightly better. The laser light and fog machine show through the oaks (?) was fun and made us moms feel like we were in a nightclub reliving our 20s again, and the other display with the giant Sequoias made their branches move in a seemingly preternatural way, but the repetitive songs (especially the sequoias section - the same Lindsey Stirling song over and over again) was just a bit annoying. I'm sure they wanted people to just walk through and take things in at a relatively decent clip and not linger.  I rarely go out though, and being able to stop, enjoy nature at night (safely!) with my kid and friends, and with a cool lighted display, I really wanted to take my time.

If you've never been, it's definitely worth seeing. I'd be happy to go again; I do wish they did it during the summer season as well, being able to enjoy all that when it's warmer and not 48°F out would draw me back for sure (but armed with a can of mosquito deterrent).

reviews.author: Abigail Figueroa
reviews.text:
​We really enjoyed our leisurely visit. A great tip: admission is free daily from 7:30–9 am, or check for the free 2nd Tuesday of the month.
​The garden is huge, with so many diverse plants to see from different places. It was wonderful to take our time and explore. We loved seeing the fish and were even lucky enough to spot a couple of beautiful red-tailed hawks!

reviews.author: Sterio A
reviews.text: I spent Thanksgiving Day at the San Francisco Botanical Garden and loved every minute of it—free admission was a nice bonus! What struck me most was how natural the whole place feels. The designers clearly aimed for minimal impact, and the result is a landscape that feels wild but welcoming. With plants from all over the world arranged by region, the garden feels like a peaceful trip across continents. I’ll definitely be coming back!

reviews.author: Levent
reviews.text: I had an amazing experience here. I was really surprised by the lush nature that existed in San Francisco. The garden offers a display of hundreds of plant species originating from/native to tens of different countries from different continents, hosting a diverse ecosystem in the park. Overall, I think it is a great place to visit.

attractions.description: 55 acres of serene gardens showcasing diverse plants from around the world & a magnolia collection.

attractions.name: Golden Gate National Recreation Area
attractions.address: California, USA

reviews.author: G L
reviews.text: Great experience to see.  Parking sucks, restrooms aren't much better.  The views however are gorgeous!!

reviews.author: Mind Set On Travel
reviews.text: A little foggy on our walk across the Golden Gate Bridge to tour the Golden Gate National Recreation Area. Views started to be better and better as the morning went on.  We ventured under the Golden Gate Bridge to the visitors center.

reviews.author: Steve Castillo
reviews.text: It's so beautiful area to visit was trying to get to lock fence but the parking is crazy packed so if you gonna visit please visit Early

reviews.author: Sagitario
reviews.text: One of my bucket list achievements. People don't realize that 82 tons of steel were produced and manufactured here in Steelton, Pennsylvania at the Bethlehem Steel plant. The view at night is as beautiful as it is in the day.

reviews.author: Audra Connors
reviews.text: Beautiful sunrises and sunsets over the beach to watch the half marathon!!

attractions.description: Coastal urban park with historic sites & activities from bicycling to hiking to water sports.

attractions.name: Lafayette Park
attractions.address: Gough St &, Washington St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Agnes Yi (Agnessy)
reviews.text: Lafayette Park is absolutely heavenly — peaceful, well-kept, and offering a lovely panoramic view of the city. As a birder, I was delighted by the diversity of birds here. It’s such a calming place to slow down and simply observe.

reviews.author: Lydia Chu
reviews.text: I’m visiting SF and found Lafayette Park. It’s such a lovely and peaceful spot! The park is very clean and well taken care of. I loved the dog park and the playground for kids. There are also tennis courts if you want to play. It wasn’t too crowded, so I could just relax and enjoy the fresh air. It’s also in a nice neighborhood in the city.

reviews.author: Jessica Heintz
reviews.text: Gorgeous park to visit! We went for the massive playground while visiting San Francisco. I was able to walk around and it seemed like a great place to walk a dog and take them to the dog park. Also in the park is a beautiful lookout with a view of the city at various points. The highlight was the great playground with multiple different structures and slides for the kids to play on. It was very well maintained. Near the playground was a public restroom which was very well kept as well.

reviews.author: Oliver Pe
reviews.text: December 2025 Beautiful green space.  Well maintained and a much needed question spot in the city.

reviews.author: Miranda Rider
reviews.text: Been coming here since I was a toddler, and still absolutely love relaxing in the sun on the grass at Lafayette! Beautiful green spaces like this are what make this city so one-of-a-kind :) and if you love dogs, there is a non-stop flow of cute pups to see and meet

attractions.description: Puppies & picnickers pack this hilltop park, which includes a playground & tennis courts.

attractions.name: Lands End Labyrinth
attractions.address: Lands End Trail, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: rachana karri
reviews.text: Lands End was absolutely beautiful when we visited. We went on a great day, and the views were stunning, coastal cliffs, ocean, and open sky all around. Such a refreshing place to walk and just take everything in. One of the best scenic spots in San Francisco

reviews.author: Sweta Basundhara
reviews.text: Beautiful spot to catch sunset. It's a short hike to the labyrinth. The labyrinth is not there as it was destroyed earlier in 2021. The trail has stairs so be prepared to climb and the view is worth the climb. This trail gets crowded during the weekends, if you want the place to yourself I suggest go during the weekdays. Dogs are allowed to use this trail.

reviews.author: Arthur Weaver
reviews.text: .Definitely make the trek out here, its a beautiful site to see. On a good summer day the Golden Gate Is a sight. On the other side is the view of Seal rock, again views of the ocean and sail boats are spectacular. Be warned, wear good shoes and be prepared to hike back up. Lots of stairs, not good for strollers and the like, big dogs

reviews.author: B. Li
reviews.text:
Heads up, the art installment has been gone for some time. If you're coming for some rocks placed in concentric circles, well, you're just gonna have to get in the sandbox and do it yourself.

Other than that, this is an amazing spot for sunset viewing. There's a peak point that's quite easy to get to. Do expect a crowd when the day is nice and sunny. The hardest part of the hike to it is the return trip uphill.

Other than that, it's a great place to enjoy the local landscape with the sun setting to your left and the Golden Gate bridge to your right. Might wanna bring a blanket since it's a dirt surface at the peak.

reviews.author: Bruce Liu
reviews.text: The Labyrinth is gone. Don’t care much about it anyway. The view is breathtaking. Definitely one of the best places to see Golden Gate Bridge. If you like pelicans, must visit this place. You can park at the visitor center or at Legion of Honor Museum.

attractions.description: Artist Eduardo Aguilera created this labyrinth with views over the Golden Gate Bridge in 2004.

attractions.name: Ina Coolbrith Park
attractions.address: Vallejo & Taylor, Vallejo St, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA

reviews.author: Hannah Koopman
reviews.text:
This park is one of my favorites in the city 💚 It’s a steep hike up, but you’re rewarded with incredible views of the bay, the Bay Bridge, the Transamerica Pyramid, Salesforce Tower, Coit Tower, and Chinatown 🌉🏙️.

The nature in this little park is so beautiful: trumpet trees, lots of hummingbirds 🐦, colorful flowers 🌸, and little benches where you can sit and just take in the city views. On the other side of the park, you can see Alcatraz and the bay!

Yes, the climb is steep, but it is so, so worth it. And if you want to keep going, you can cross the street (just be careful, there’s no real crosswalk and cars come up fast). Keep climbing for even more views all the way to the top of Russian Hill. Or walk down one block and walk the Macondray lane (another favorite!).

One of those places that really reminds you how special this city is ✨❤️

reviews.author: Molly Farey-Jones
reviews.text: Stunning views of the bay bridge and transamerican pyramid. Only warning is it seems to be a popular stop on a SF tour. I went around lunch and it wasn’t to busy! Highly recommend even for locals it really makes you appreciate our little city by the bay ❤️🌉

reviews.author: Mason Chen
reviews.text:
Ina Coolbrith Park is a forgotten hidden jewel of The City attached with a touching story of legendary American pioneers🫡 and it also has awesome views from Coit Tower, Alcatraz to the Bay Bridge and landmark downtown skyscrapers. The park is situated on 1715 Taylor Street what was once a steep and impassable section of Vallejo Street running downhill towards Chinatown in Russian Hill neighborhood.[*A few houses north from Coolbrith’s original home located at 1604 Taylor street that was destroyed in the 1906 earthquake and fire. It is said that although Coolbrith who is also a “cat lady” 😉escaped alive with her two white Persian cats and a female companion her personal library of over 3000 volumes of valuable books, correspondence, and her nearly finished manuscript on the history of early California literature were lost.😔]
The park is named after Ina Donna Coolbrith (1841-1928)(born Josephine Donna Smith, who was the niece of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints founder Joseph Smith🫡to my friends “Elder Murray” et al. from Utah whom I befriended at Chinatown church on Washington Street), a prominent San Francisco poet who became California's first Poet Laureate in 1915 during the Panama–Pacific International Exposition in San Francisco, who left the Mormon community with her mother (Née Coolbrith) as a child in 1851 after death of both her father and legendary uncle in the midst of prejudice, violence and persecution on a wagon train to Los Angeles, California, where she began to learn and write poetry. Although her personal life is sadly entangled with tragedies and poverty, Coolbrith managed to have published hundreds of works over a span of 50 years. More importantly, in order to support her family (her mother and sister who were sick then died in late 1870s) Coolbrith who was known to (if not admired by) many “big shots” figures including Mark Twain, Bret Harte, Joaquin Miller and John Muir had to work as Oakland’s first public librarian (and female imagine that) who mentored Jack London and Isadora Duncan who also lived or otherwise had ties there when they were teens. [* Note: Ira became an honorary member of the Bohemian Club, a rare feat in the history of that exclusive all-male San Francisco secretive private club that is also located on Taylor Street @ Post Street. "Weaving spiders come not here…"]
The park was dedicated to Ira in 1911 and a memorial plaque was placed in 1947. It's terraces and wooden staircases were carved from the hillside, creating a hidden garden effect similar to Greenwich or Gilbert steps (aka “The mother and daughter steps.”) Suggestion for following walking/hiking routes:❶Muni T-Chinatown, 30 (Stockton), 45 (Union/Stockton), 12 (Folsom/Pacific), 8 (Bayshore), 39 (Coit) walk up on Vallejo Street passing Central police station reach Mason Street climb up stairs to top of the park about 15 floors. {*The OA house (season 2 on Netflix) another City’s historic landmark is situated across the park on 1001 Vallejo Street.} ❷ Muni 30 (Stockton), 28 (19th Avenue) walk/climb up on steep Taylor street alone from North Point cutting through Columbus or Jones and Leavenworth about 25-30 floors. ❸ Muni 19 (Polk/Lombard), 49 (Van Ness/Mission), 30 (Stockton), 28 (19th Avenue) walk/climb up on Lombard then turn on Taylor about 40-45 floors. {If you choose to continue on Lombard it will take you all the way up to Coit Tower through Pioneer Park which would add on an additional 17 to 20 floors. You can then go back to the top of the Coolbrith park southbound on Taylor or via Vallejo at Mason from the bottom.}
Enjoy views of The City while appreciating our history and doing a little workout! 😉
“To its great City of the Seven Hills
And lo! The city bore Saint Francis’ name!”

reviews.author: sid srivastava
reviews.text:
One of the best viewpoints in the city. Super nice, great view, can see the entire city. If you like parrots, there’s always a bunch of them around at 4PM screaming away.

They filmed part of The OA around here too, and it’s a popular tourist spot and for people to make videos. I go here almost every day. 10/10.

reviews.author: Steven
reviews.text:
This park is a quiet escape with beautiful views, especially since it sits a little higher and overlooks the city.

It’s calm and peaceful, perfect for reading, thinking, or just relaxing. Also a great spot for photos with both nature and city backdrops. Beautiful sights of San Francisco

attractions.description: This pint-sized, unassuming tract features a steep path & views of the city & bay.

attractions.name: Alamo Square Park
attractions.address: Hayes St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Shawn .H Leathers
reviews.text: This hilltop park is an absolute gem, offering stunning views that capture both the city skyline and the iconic Painted Ladies. It’s an ideal spot for a relaxed picnic or a photography session, thanks to the wide grassy area that’s perfect for spreading out a blanket and soaking in the scenery. The atmosphere is peaceful yet inspiring, making it easy to linger and enjoy the surroundings. Whether you’re snapping photos, relaxing with friends, or simply taking in the views, this place is well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Olga Nemesh
reviews.text: Alamo Square Park is one of the most beautiful spots in San Francisco. The view of the Painted Ladies against the city skyline is iconic, and the park itself is clean, spacious, and well maintained. It’s perfect for relaxing, taking photos, or enjoying a picnic. Great atmosphere, family-friendly, and one of the best places to feel the charm of San Francisco.

reviews.author: Vikas Mathur
reviews.text:
Beautiful place in the heart of San Francisco. The part is just opposite to the amazing houses with typical San Francisco design and colours known as painted ladies.
Lot of people bring their pets here but they are under supervision so no worries. I saw a caution board for Coyotes, so avoid going in dark hours.
You can also see San Francisco skyline from there.
Go there and enjoy

reviews.author: Pezhman Raeisian
reviews.text:
Alamo Square Park is a peaceful, scenic spot that offers one of the most iconic views in San Francisco—the famous Painted Ladies with the city skyline in the background. We visited as a family and really enjoyed sitting on the grassy slope, taking photos, and letting our daughter explore the playground.

✅ Pros:
•	Stunning, postcard-worthy views
•	Clean and well-maintained green space
•	Playground for kids and plenty of seating
•	Dog-friendly and perfect for picnics
•	Great spot for photography and relaxing

❌ Cons:
•	Limited street parking nearby—can be tough during peak hours
•	Can get crowded on weekends, especially with tour buses
•	Not much shade in certain areas—bring a hat or sunscreen

Tip: Visit in the late afternoon for the best photo lighting and a quieter vibe. It’s a must-see for first-time visitors to San Francisco!

reviews.author: Roberta
reviews.text: Alamo Square park is - in my view - even more beautiful on a foggy slightly rainy day! Also, don’t just stick with the five painted ladies do the entire perimeter and you’ll see some even more interesting views! Enjoy!!

attractions.description: Green space with a playground, tennis court & views of the city's iconic painted Victorian houses.

attractions.name: Richmond Playground Park
attractions.address: 18th Ave & Lake St, Lake St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Staly Chin
reviews.text: They just finished remodeling this playground and it now looks SO AMAZING AND FUN. There's a sand pit! Brand new restrooms! A giant climbable bear! Ignore all the old reviews, this playground is one of the best in the city now.

reviews.author: GodzillaSF
reviews.text: Suuuuuper sweet local playground great for toddlers and anyone who wants to shoot some hoops.  It has been recently renovated including a very fun large bear that kids can climb in and slide down

reviews.author: Peter Moore
reviews.text: Lots of recreation options for such a small neighborhood park. There's a sign advisory for construction starting Nov 2023.

reviews.author: Rose Martin
reviews.text: They seriously need to change this kids area. It's just a giant sandbox at this point because they removed the play equipment and put in some log stumps. Now neighborhood dogs use it as a toilet because no one pays attention to the 'no dogs' signs. So I really hate bringing my kids here even though I live around the corner. You're better off at Mountain Lake or Rochambeau unless you just want to play basketball or have your kids ride their bike or scoot on the basketball courts. Clean bathrooms though.

reviews.author: Gaines Coleman
reviews.text: There is a tennis court, a basketball court, and a sandy place for kids to play. Very popular with the stroller set - they really cry when they have to go home. In the summer there can be a line of cars waiting to pick kids up. I think a bike riding group for older kids meets here. One of my friends said there were Pokémon there too, but I cannot verify this statement. Our 3 year old neighbor loves this place.

attractions.name: Crissy Field
attractions.address: San Francisco, CA 94129, USA

reviews.author: Brian Choi
reviews.text: Chrissy Fields has always been a pretty nice place to walk, especially if you want to walk your dog and in the recent years they’ve expanded and renovated a lot of it to make it even more walkable and kid friendly as well with some play areas. It’s pretty nice cause they also teach you about preservation in nature and the views are really nice especially on clear days where you can see both the Golden Gate Bridge, Alcatraz, Berkeley, Oakland, and many other places throughout the Bay Area.

reviews.author: SK HD
reviews.text:
Crissy Field is one of those places that never gets old. The views here are absolutely iconic you get a perfect look at the Golden Gate Bridge, Alcatraz, and the Bay all in one spot.

There’s easy beach access where you can walk along the sand, dip your feet in the water, or just relax and watch the waves.

On windy days, you’ll often see surfers and kiteboarders out near Fort Point, which adds such a cool energy to the area. The paved paths make it great for walking, biking, or just strolling with a coffee in hand while taking in the views. It can get busy, but it still feels peaceful and open, especially around sunset when the bridge glows in that golden light.

Overall, Crissy Field captures everything that makes San Francisco so special beautiful, breezy, and endlessly scenic. I’d definitely visit again!

reviews.author: vasileia “Billia” vespa
reviews.text:
San Francisco has so many parks to choose from! Yet another one, close to the Golden Gate Point View, in the one called Crissy Field.
Crissy Field is a former military airstrip that was converted into a public park in 2001. It is located on San Francisco Bay, and offers great views of the Golden Gate Bridge and Alcatraz. The park itself features beaches and areas for picnic.
Tip: if you are in a mood for walking, take the path from the Golden Gate Bridge to the Palace of Fine Arts. Very enjoyable.

reviews.author: Edward Yango
reviews.text: You can get a very nice view of Golden Gate Bridge, as well as Alcatraz Island. Parking may be challenging on weekends. If you have a couple hours, you can walk around the beach and marsh.

reviews.author: Marcos Maldonado
reviews.text: Wonderful views of San Francisco Golden Gate Bridge and of San Francisco. Great place for walking, running, riding a bike, looking at birds, flying a kite.

attractions.description: Grass field & sandy beach offering trails, a restored marsh & picturesque views of the Golden Gate.

attractions.name: Crane Cove Park
attractions.address: 18th street and, Illinois St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA

reviews.author: Neal
reviews.text:
One of the nicest and most unique parks in San Francisco.

There are plenty of spots to sit, there's a grassy area if you'd like to have a picnic, and there are porta-potties if you need to use one. There are great views of the bay and a platform so you can see everything. Also, the area is very clean and you could probably swim in the cove if you wanted.

Not only is it a park, but also a historical site. There are many signs with information about people building ships and some remnants of the equipment. Overall, I highly recommend this park. You could easily spend a whole afternoon here.

reviews.author: Yü Wu
reviews.text:
Sunny and clean city park with a nice lawn, dog area and a small beach! I passed by the park during a run along the coast and enjoyed a little stop in this beautiful park. There are some picnic tables in the park which is awesome but otherwise there aren't a lot of facilities in the park so I think it has some potentials to be more interesting.
The neighborhood feels modern and urban, has plenty of restaurants and cafes within a short walk.

reviews.author: Thushan Amarasiriwardena
reviews.text: Absolutely stellar, playful and uniquely situated park in Mission Bay / Dogpatch.  Love the historical structures still standing and the playful silhouette paintings of boats that were build here.  The beach looks like a stellar paddle board / wading area.

reviews.author: J N
reviews.text: Wonderful little park in Dogpatch. Family-friendly and well-loved by locals! Clean public restrooms.

reviews.author: Vanessa Ross
reviews.text: It’s a beach in Dogpatch Neighborhood. Bring your kayak, picnic basket or not as there are picnic tables with a grill! Super dog friendly, kids can ride their bikes, scooters, fly a kite, and have fun.

attractions.description: Reclaimed area in a former shipyard, with a small beach for water sports & views of passing boats.

services.name: The Culture SF
services.address: 2255 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Shane Rose
reviews.text: My partner & I have been going to this gym for 6 months now. It’s within walking distance, has everything you need for a workout, flexible hours and easy check-in. Not to mention staff are always welcoming and friendly. On top of that, we do personal training with Simon and I can’t recommend him enough. We look forward to every Monday power session with Simon. Each session the care is felt and the progress is seen. Glad we found this gem in the Richmond.

reviews.author: DJ Culture Club
reviews.text: Newly renovated gym in San Francisco’s Richmond District. Offering memberships & personal training. All new equipment, great staff, pleasant experience all around!

reviews.author: Brittany Buckmire
reviews.text: Had such a great experience at The Culture!! The equipment and facility was clean and the staff were all super friendly and encouraging throughout my workout. I’m looking forward to more sessions and excited for the community growing here 🖤  IG: @fitbritdaily

reviews.author: Kevin Lu
reviews.text: One of the best gyms I've ever been to! The staff are all super welcoming and the equipment is always kept clean and tidy. It's also great because it's one of the few gyms that's open late even on weekends.

reviews.author: Parvathy Nair
reviews.text: A great neighborhood gym! Has everything you need, all equipment is new/in good shape, and the gym is in a very central location for people who live in the Richmond. I highly recommend :)

services.name: FITNESS SF - Fillmore
services.address: 1455 Fillmore St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Brittany Moreira
reviews.text:
I have been a member of this gym for about a year. The condition of the gym has decreased especially for the women’s side.
This is everything that is broken today. 😭 Plus they increased the price for this type of service. Also they make us grab towels in the front but is very inconvenient in case you are showering or are in the steam room.

reviews.author: Jon A.
reviews.text:
Gym is okay, it's the people that use it that's where the issue lies, and with no negative feedback or repercussions from management, you'll often see the following:

People hogging equipment for 15+ minutes while supersetting and scrolling through Instagram.

I've once seen a guy supersetting four different equipment at a time.

Weights all around in whatever they feel like should be, if not at the machine altogether. You're not strong enough to take some 45s from some equipment? Well, you're out of luck.

The lack of repercussions from management makes the experience, in general, terrible. Everyone's paying the same price, so why do some of the gym goers seem to own the gym more than others?

reviews.author: Maher Sinjary
reviews.text:
My favorite gym and favorite location! Super clean and always up-to-date. Love the pool here and I swim there often. The staff are very friendly and helpful from Haifa at the front desk to the pool staff, and especially Kayla has been amazing and very accommodating.

I would highly recommend signing up to this pool.

reviews.author: Wes Ho
reviews.text: Most comprehensive equipment and amenities that includes cardio, free weights, muscle and relaxation equipment.    This location has Sauna, IR Salt room, Whirlpool, Swimming Pool and Cold Plunge.

reviews.author: jewls r
reviews.text: Besides the fact that they only have 1 or MAYBE 2 sets of 5 and 8 pound weights in the mat area, I am a dedicated fan of the saunas, showers, layout, vibe, etc….. I will say I hate when it’s super busy and I’m fighting for breathing room, but the staff is great, the culture is mostly great (just being a girl in a gym can be weird). Edit - minus 2 stars because I left some things in a locker and called them as soon as I got home to make sure it wouldn’t get stolen or thrown away and three days later it’s all gone but there is dirty undergarments in the lost in found

services.name: Local's Fitness
services.address: 2940 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Vinh Trinh
reviews.text:
10/10, best gym in SF.

GYM EXPERIENCE:
The gym is always clean, never too busy, and there’s pretty much always space to do whatever workout you want. The staff are super friendly and actually know you by name, and the members are respectful. I always see members and staff chatting at the front desk which shows how welcoming and friendly everyone is.

PERSONAL TRAINING:
I started coming here after dealing with knee pain for over a year, and training with Paul has been a game changer. He’s super knowledgeable, always checks in, and makes sure everything feels safe and comfortable. Biggest take away was learning how to perform certain exercises that wouldn't impact my knee pain but still allow me to do lower body exercises.

reviews.author: Linda Cano
reviews.text:
Local’s Fitness is such a gem! The space is always spotless, with a great variety of equipment to fit any workout style. The members are respectful, which makes for a really comfortable and motivating environment. I immediately felt a sense of community here.

It’s also super convenient to get to, and parking is always open around the area, huge plus. A big thank you to Daniel and Paul for always being so kind and welcoming. It’s clear they love what they do and take real pride in their gym.

Whether you live in the neighborhood or not, Local’s Fitness is absolutely worth signing up for!

reviews.author: Monica Chan
reviews.text: I love this gym. It’s always clean, and the machines and equipment are all new and easy to use. I have been going for months because Daniel, Matt, and Paul are extremely kind and make you feel so welcome there. I love that there are only a few people at a time whenever I go in, so I don’t have to wait to use the machines that I want to. Conveniently, there’s also 2 showers, 2 bathrooms, a water bottle refill station, and free lockers available for use. It’s a great humble gym that has all of the essentials.

reviews.author: Eric Armstrong
reviews.text: I love this gym. I spent the summer in San Francisco for a summer job and joined on the flexible month-to-month plan. The gym is brand new, has excellent and well-maintained equipment, and is never too crowded. The clientele are all good and respectful people. All the gym staff, especially the owner, Daniel, are really great and make an effort to greet you by name. The gym has solid hours too (closing at 11pm on most days) which is great if you work out late at night like I often do. Overall would highly recommend this gym if you live nearby!

reviews.author: Mathias Caetano
reviews.text:
Daniel and his team have set up a wonderful gym experience and culture.

High quality equipment that is always in the best operational state and openly taking feedback, upgrading to meet patron needs and asks, social activities of the community, clean lockers and changing rooms, filtered water and free disinfectant wipes.

services.name: Sunset Gym
services.address: 1247 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA

reviews.author: Brijhette Farmer
reviews.text: Great spot. The vibe is very community-oriented, which was amazing. Got the week pass. Love the opportunity to support homegrown places. Super friendly staff and patrons. A little cramped during crowded times but a massive variety of equipment. As another reviewer said, I felt like one of the few folks wiping items after use, so a stronger culture of cleanliness would be appreciated personally. Definitely recommend this gym!

reviews.author: Hammad Umman
reviews.text: Good service overall and a solid setup. There is definitely potential for much stronger online visibility and lead generation. With proper social media management and digital campaigns, this business could attract significantly more customers. If the management is open to ideas on improving online presence, I would be happy to connect

reviews.author: MaxPower1337
reviews.text:
Great place to train, loved it.
I was visiting San Francisco and got a day pass. The staff is very friendly.

The equipment is also very good with focus on powerlifting/free weights. But they also have many machines so you’ll find everything you need.

Absolutely recommended!

reviews.author: Ad Ba
reviews.text:
It gets the job done. I do basic aerobic exercises (i.e., jogging and cycling) and lifting.

pros: reasonable price, no startup fees, has everything you probably need as a casual (non-professional) gym-goer, they cram a lot into their small space

cons: It's a little cramped, as others have stated; doesn't seem to really have a culture of cleanliness, many people do not clean their machines after use; heavily male-dominated, i'm usually the only woman or only one of 2-3 women among 20+ men, which is the worst women-to-men ratio I've ever personally experienced; some of the machines are a little old, particularly the spin bikes upstairs

reviews.author: Dan Carey
reviews.text: I've been going to the sunset gym for about 3 years now, and I absolutely love it. It's a great gym with super friendly staff and plenty of equipment. It's also a good crowd of people that are members there. Highly recommend!

services.name: 24 Hour Fitness
services.address: 1200 Van Ness Ave, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Jessica McClellan
reviews.text: FILTHY. SHOULD BE SHUT DOWN BY THE BOARD OF HEALTH. OWNERS DO NOT RESPOND WHEN YOU GIVE THEM FEEDBACK STAFF STANDS AT THE FRONT DESK ON THEIR PHONE AND NEVER DOES CLEANING.  ALWAYS MANY MANY BROKEN MACHINES. OFTEN THERE IS A BROKEN TOILET WITH POOP IN IT. THE CEILING IS CRUMBLING AND LEAKING AND THEY SIMPLY SET OUT GARBAGE CAN DAILY AS A WAY TO ADDRESS IT. OVER TWO YEARS I HAVE GONE HERE I HAVE SEEN ZERO IMPROVEMENT EVEN WHEN TALKING TO OWNWERS.  I DON'T THE WINDOWS HAVE EVER BEEN WASHED.  THEY AREN'T EVEN OPEN 24 HOURS NOW SO THEY ARE MAKING MORE PROFIT AND THEY STILL DON'T FIT OR CLEAN THINGS.

reviews.author: Frank Padilla
reviews.text: There are many cables that are broken and take a long time to fix. I asked the girl who worked tonight, Sunday, if I could use the trainers' area and she said no. I always see people training there without a trainer; she was very rude.

reviews.author: Vincent
reviews.text:
I visited this gym today and had a great first experience. CAM was incredibly welcoming and took the time to show me around—especially the strength/weight training area and the sauna/steam room. Everything felt clean, well-organized, and the overall vibe was really motivating.

What I appreciated most was how patient and friendly he was, answering my questions and making me feel comfortable as a newcomer. I ended up signing up for a membership on the spot because the facility and the experience were that good.

Highly recommend this place if you’re looking for solid equipment, great amenities, and a genuinely friendly staff.

What I appreciated most was how patient and friendly he was, answering my questions and making me feel comfortable as a newcomer. I ended up downloading Meetup right away because I’m genuinely excited to come back and join some sessions.

Highly recommend this place if you’re looking for a gym with solid facilities and a great community feel.

reviews.author: Maxwell Markus lll
reviews.text: I came in last night. Not a bad gym but what makes it is the staff. Superb young people, kind and very helpful and understanding of my situation. Isaiah was so amazing and I would recommend anyone who comes in. He’s new bout he does his absolute best and served as a us marine. His view on fitness was spot on, Oorah marine. I can tell this location will far😁

reviews.author: Ryan R
reviews.text:
I went to the 24 Hour Fitness on Van Ness today and received OUTSTANDING customer service from Laura G. She was personable, patient and very helpful. She took her time showing me around the facility and went above and beyond to ensure I understood all of my options.

Because of her quality customer service, I signed up for a year membership, well worth it for the value.

As someone who works in sales, I know quality when I experience it, and Laura is an asset.

services.name: Fíor Fitness
services.address: 5509 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: yadhunand srinivasan
reviews.text: Fior Fitness CrossFit is an amazing place to work out, with coaches who push you to challenge yourself while helping you master the techniques. The community is supportive, friendly, and engaging, with regular events that make every workout even more fun

reviews.author: Luke Peretti
reviews.text:
Before joining Fior Fitness in September 2023, I had only done a small amount of one-on-one personalized training. I had heard of CrossFit but had never actually done a CrossFit workout. On my very first day, I was welcomed by Rory—and I won’t lie, that first workout had me digging deep in the trenches. It was challenging, and I was incredibly sore afterward.

There are moments in life that change your trajectory, not just physically but mentally as well. Walking into Fior for the first time was one of those moments for me. Since then, I’m in the best shape of my life, and I genuinely look forward to showing up every day—not only to improve my own fitness, but to motivate and grow alongside the people around me.

I’ve never been part of a gym where the community is so welcoming and supportive. From day one, Fior felt like a family. Rory and Alana dedicate an incredible amount of time and energy to ensuring every member is set up for success, both in achieving their fitness goals and being part of a community that constantly strives to improve.

After taking eight months away to figure out my life, I came back more motivated than ever and have grown tremendously over the past couple of months since I returned. Fior Fitness has truly changed my life. Hands down, the best gym in all of San Francisco.

Forever grateful for Rory and Alana

reviews.author: Tuuli & Patrick Almgren
reviews.text:
I’ve been doing CrossFit for a 10 years and have dropped into close to 100 boxes around the world. You always feel welcome in this community, but Fior CrossFit.

From the first moment, my family and I were welcomed like we’d been part of the crew for years. The atmosphere, the energy, and the way people support each other create something rare.

The coaching is sharp and the culture in the room makes you want to show up and get better. Whether you age new to CrossFit or fitness in general, or you’re a seasoned veteran, this place will meet you where you are and lift you up.

A real gem.

reviews.author: roxanna Fernandez
reviews.text:
I walked into Fíor gym 7 months ago with absolutely no CrossFit experience or idea about what to expect. Now, I can confidently say that it was one of the best decisions I've ever made. The supportive and friendly environment here allowed me to have the confidence to do my first ever CrossFit competition!!!

The classes are always different, so it never gets boring if anything it makes you want to come back for more.  The facility is well-equipped and clean. The Fíor app is easy to use, and there’s plenty of class times.

If you are thinking about joining Fíor gym, just do it!! You will not regret it. If I was not  moving to another city, I will still be apart of the Fíor fit fam.

By the way, I’m going to miss Dusty’s sweet good morning greetings.
🐶🐾😭

Thank you Rory and Alana for making my first CrossFit experience amazing! Fíor gym will always have a special place in my ♥️.

reviews.author: BAR Lisa
reviews.text:
2 years ago I joined Fior. It is the best party of day. Alana and Rory have thought me so much and basically held my hand through workouts. The community within Fior is one like I've never had before. If you are looking for a kick ass class and the best coaches try one class and you will be addicted.
Thank you Fior 🍀♥️

services.name: FITNESS SF - SoMa
services.address: 1001 Brannan St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: A Sharon
reviews.text:
I love this gym!! The staff is extremely friendly and helpful. The equipment is well maintained and updated on a consistent bases. If anything breaks they fix it in a timely manner.(no more than a week)  If you like to do squats or deadlifts, the gym has a total of 9 squat racks with weightlifting platforms.  They have a spacious area for aerobics,  stretching, and calisthenic exercises.

The gym is excessively clean. During business hours there is someone on the floor maintaining a clean environment. The locker rooms are always clean with plenty of fresh towels. If you love cardio  they have a plethora of treadmills, bikes, stair climbs, and rowing machines. There is no shortage of dumbbells and kettlebells. They even have free zero gravity massage chairs!

reviews.author: Vladimir Vukicevic
reviews.text:
I've been working with Max Rosenfeld as my trainer for almost a year now, and he's been amazing. I've seen tremendous progress, and look forward to my sessions with him. He's on the secret menu though, you won't see him in the list of trainers because he's usually booked solid. Worth it if you can get a slot!

The gym itself is great. Large, clean, well maintained and with friendly staff and members.

reviews.author: Zach Hoisington
reviews.text: This is the greatest gym of all time. Elite equipment selection (who else u know got 1lb eleiko plates? Also we got the new hammer strength pendulum X squat like the month it dropped), super nice staff, ton of space, amenities, anything you could ask for. Reasonably priced too. If you’re serious about fitness GET IN HERE‼️‼️

reviews.author: Joy Sheppard
reviews.text:
The Best Kept Secret at Fitness SF (Soma)- A Review for Max Rosenfeld.

Listen, if you want results, Max is your guy, assuming you can actually snag a spot on his calendar! He’s currently booked out with a full-blown cult following, and after training with him, I totally get why people are obsessed.

As an IFBB Pro-Athlete who has been competing on and off for a decade, I’m what you’d call "particular" about who I trust with my health. I’ve had a 1:1 trainer since I was 18, and now at 44, I don’t have time for fluff or BS. I need deep knowledge in anatomy, hypertrophy, form, punctuality, and someone who is actually a cool human to be around.
