When I first met Max, I was in a very dark place. Between navigating a separation and the single-mom life, I was a malnourished 105 lbs and seriously atrophied. I didn’t just need a trainer; I needed an intervention!

Max didn’t just help me find my baseline; he helped me find my fire again. He has this "wealth of experience" thing down to a science and he knows exactly how to push me to the limit without actually breaking me (because at 44, "recovery" hits a little different).

He’s basically a life coach and high level personal trainer in one. On the days when my inner critic is screaming louder than my pre-workout, Max is there to remind me of my potential.

I never thought I’d be stepping back on stage after the hurdles life threw at me, but here we are: 7 weeks out and feeling like a machine again. Max is the best-kept secret at Fitness SF. Thank you Max!

reviews.author: David Munoz
reviews.text: Great facility with even better coaching. Working with Albert has been a game changer for my recovery. After a back injury, I was worried about lifting again but his tailored approach allowed me to progress at the right pace. I’ve seen significant results while staying pain free. I've gained confidence in my lifting and my back is 100% while I've gotten stronger. Five stars for Albert and the team!

services.name: MuscleBeachSF Gym San Francisco fka FitClubSF
services.address: 710 La Playa St, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: elena
reviews.text: I used this gym for two months while I was in town and cancelled my membership over email July 21st because I was moving away. They responded confirming they cancelled it back in July but never actually cancelled and keep charging me monthly. So far they’ve taken 750$ and now they won’t pick up my calls or respond to my emails. Can’t go in person since I don’t live there anymore so I’m not sure if they’re trying to scam or completely forgot to cancel my subscription.

reviews.author: Tepeva Tang
reviews.text:
Great gym in SF you have everything you want, good equipment, nice people and staff. A/C, cleaning towel and towel available.
You can book on the website, for a day pass,  a week or a mouth.
Open 24h all day, parking is just outside the gym, you can find a place easily and safely.

reviews.author: Bernice Fan
reviews.text:
Great gym that fits all your needs without feeling overly crowded compared to the typical commercial gyms.

Highlights:
- less crowded
- in a walkable/runnable area for a pre-lift run/warm up
- open 24/7 via access code aka healthcare workers who work odd hours (ive been here at 4:30am)
- relatively safe and residential neighborhood
- owners actually cancel membership when asked haha

reviews.author: Danielle Repetti
reviews.text: I moved to the outer sunset in 2019 and was surprised by the lack of lifting gyms in this area. So glad i found them because fit club is the best!!! i go there often to powerlift and everyone is chill, the gym is never too crowded, and it's open 24/7. Thank you Derek & Team!

reviews.author: Henry Lyford
reviews.text: Great neighborhood gym. New equipment and friendly staff. Price is a little steep but having no enrollment fee (join and cancel whenever) and not being crowded makes it worth it.

services.name: Equinox Sports Club San Francisco
services.address: 747 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: AGOKCIN MUSIC
reviews.text:
The pool is either not cleaned frequently enough or has a malfunctioning filtration system, as it repeatedly becomes dirty and cloudy. I personally witnessed this issue five months ago and again yesterday (see photos below).

Although it was cleaned after my initial complaint, the problem has recurred and should not have happened again.

This is not what Equinox promises to its members, and not a good experience for those who joined to train in the pool.

reviews.author: maria
reviews.text: The parking situation is insane. We pay so much for membership and they charge $5 for three hours and after that it’s like $26 if you go over three hours. The LA sports club it’s $2 for three hours and $1 after every hour added which is a way better parking situation than this SF location. On top of that the cleaning lady closed the sauna and steam room early when they are supposed to close it at 9:30pm. I went in at 9:14pm ready to go into the sauna and steam room for that last bit of time and she was already cleaning it. I was confused thinking maybe it was because of holiday hours but after I was done showering I heard the announcement that the steam closes at 9:30pm so it’s really inconvenient that she closed it early. Other than that it is a nice clean gym with nice equipments. If they fix those issues it will be 5 stars for me.

reviews.author: Kevin Burton
reviews.text:
This gym is very mediocre.

That's really the best I could say about it.

My $29.95/month membership at 24 Hour Fitness in Boulder, Colorado was a lot better than this gym.

And I pay $500/month because I have an executive membership.

The only reason I pay for the executive membership is because it comes with free parking and is roughly the same as just paying for parking.

Other than that, this gym is very mediocre.

They have one leg extension machine.

ONE!

Meanwhile, they have like 75 treadmills.

They don't care at all about the quality of this gym.

All they would have to do is interview people, spend one day interviewing members to find out what needs to be changed.

And maybe spend $10,000 on new equipment.

For example, I am constantly waiting for a bench throughout the day.

There are literally 100% of the benches occupied at all times.

Because there's only like seven of them.

Meanwhile, they have 75 treadmills.

What's frustrating is that this is about status quo for gyms in San Francisco.

They don't need to change because there is no competition.

It's really a joke.

Come on, do better, Equinox.

reviews.author: Babak Farahmand
reviews.text:
Extremely terrible experience-
My girlfriend and I walked up to get a tour. The reception was extremely rude and short with us. Two different people. The incident was at Saturday 1/3/2026, noon.
Thank you for paying attention to this matter management. I used to be a member at Beale Street. It was kind of sad to see this type of behavior. Made me extremely uncomfortable. Won't be signing up.

reviews.author: Igor Nikonenko
reviews.text:
Unexpectedly poor customer service at this location.

Recently, I attempted to make a purchase at the Equinox Shop at the Equinox Sports Club SF and intended to use my points balance, which is due to expire quite soon. Unfortunately, no one on duty at the time had access to the system needed to process a points redemption, so only card payments were available.

After speaking with the club manager, Erica, she kindly suggested a workaround: I would complete the purchase with my credit card, and she would refund the charge the next day and re-process the transaction using my points. We also agreed that I could call her if anything didn’t go as expected.

Since then, no refund has been issued. I called the club the following day - and several times afterward - and each time was assured that Erica would get back to me. Unfortunately, no one has followed up.

While the amount is modest, the experience has been surprisingly disappointing, especially given the consistently exceptional service I’ve received from Equinox in the past. I would gladly forget this minor incident, but the lack of follow-through and repeated unreturned calls leave me feeling unheard as a member.

services.name: Live Fit Gym • Hayes Valley
services.address: 301 Fell St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA

reviews.author: Bobby G
reviews.text: I moved from SF to LA and had to give this gym up and I miss it! Amazing facilities, super clean and modern - it's small but crowd is minimal except for 6-7PM weekdays. What makes this place shine is the amazing and extremely helpful and personable staff! Really makes it feel like a community. Big shoutout to Andrew, Ryan, and Dan (who signed me up when I moved to SF many years ago) - you guys are the best and make the gym what it is.

reviews.author: Giesa Español
reviews.text:
I am so grateful I manifested @livefitgymsf this Season, it is a Wellness Portal of powerful healers. Today, I experienced Magick! Toshiro is one of their amazing massage therapists and he sees total darkness, yet radiates and shares his powerful healing through his massage therapy. My wish is for the world to experience more of his healing because each and everyone’s healing leads to the healing of the world🙏🌏🤲✨

My journey to this wellness portal is Divine Timing, waking in I met Dollie at Mission with her beautiful Purple Lipsticks, her energy was giving, this morning we shared deep convo and I was like Wow! The magick starts the moment I walked in. Then I meet Cassandra and Jen at Castro for my first class and signed up and was super excited that they have Chiropractic Alignment, Acupuncture, and Massage.

Meeting Dr. Katy and experiencing her alignment for the first time literally lit up my Third Eye Chakra, it’s a whole new level of knowing that this is a space that is TRUE ti their name LIVE. FIT. WELLNESS.

I am excited to experience their Acupuncture next week. If you’re vibe is a Retreat Life For Life, this space is a place to be in the heart of the city. 🙏🤲✨🌎🥰

reviews.author: Gabriel Acorda
reviews.text: Excellent experience at Live Fit! Great machines and the staff are super helpful. Really recommend it if your popping in or long term membership.

reviews.author: Ali Rohde
reviews.text: Incredible experience with Live Fit Gym in Hayes Valley so far. Beautiful, clean space with all the equipment you need. Not too crowded. Manager Andrew is super kind. The kind of place you look forward to visiting!

reviews.author: Pierre Godard
reviews.text:
A Home Away From Home - Live Fit Gym is the Real Deal

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Finding a gym that resonates with your spirit and aspirations can be a challenge, but Live Fit Gym in Hayes Valley has exceeded all my expectations and completely redefined the gym-going experience for me.

My journey with Live Fit Gym began quite unexpectedly. I was passing by, casting curious glances at the facilities, when Andrew, one of the staff members, spotted me from across the road. With an infectious energy and a big, welcoming smile, he waved at me and shouted, "Wanna have a workout?" That moment was the game-changer. Encouraged by his warm gesture, I walked in, took an appointment for two days later, and I've been training here consistently for the past two months.

Andrew and Simon, the coaches, have been exceptional in their roles, fostering a vibrant and energetic environment that inspires you to give your best. Their affable nature, coupled with their professional expertise, ensures that every workout session is not just physically rewarding, but also emotionally uplifting.

I've had the chance to compare Live Fit Gym with four other gyms in the area, some priced twice as high, yet none of them could match the kind of personalized service and care that I've received here. The staff at Live Fit Gym is not just there to assist; they genuinely care about your fitness journey and go out of their way to make you feel comfortable and motivated.

If I could, I would give Live Fit Gym an unprecedented 6 out of 5 stars! It’s not just a gym; it's a community, a hub of positivity, and a testament to the fact that a great workout environment is shaped by the people who run it.

If you're in the Hayes Valley area and in search of a place that offers a top-notch workout experience and genuinely values its members, look no further than Live Fit Gym. Trust me, you won't be disappointed.

services.description: Industrial-chic gym with traditional equipment & group classes, plus massage treatments & facials.

services.name: The Yard Strength Training
services.address: 2863 California St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Katee Orr
reviews.text:
I love going to The Yard for my strength workouts. It’s easy and convenient to simply book a squat rack and takes out all the anxiety and annoyance of having to wait for a rack at a traditional gym. The Yard has most of the equipment I would use at any regular gym (squat rack, dumbbells, ski erg, assault bike, kettlebells..etc.) and the new machines are great enhancements.

I’m a runner, cyclist and also do yoga so when looking for a gym I was looking for a place I could do simple strength workouts and The Yard is perfect.

The Yard is clean, and the front desk staff is friendly and welcoming. I would recommend the Yard to anyone looking for a great small gym that has all the basics, some extras and a nice community feel.

reviews.author: Juan Caceres
reviews.text:
The Yard is the best! physically, mentally, and with my confidence in the gym. The community here is real, the people are welcoming, and the energy is always on point.

The staff is supportive, the atmosphere is motivating, and the owner clearly cares about creating a space that feels unique and positive. Every class and training session pushes you to be better without ever feeling intimidating.

This gym is honestly one of the most unique spots in all of San Francisco. If you’re looking for great people, great vibes, and a place that truly helps you level up, The Yard is the one.

reviews.author: Kamelle Mills
reviews.text:
The Yard SF on California is hands down one of my favorite workout spaces in the city. The staff is genuinely welcoming, knowledgeable, and always brings great energy—it makes such a difference. The app is incredibly convenient and easy to use, which makes booking and planning workouts seamless.

The equipment is top-tier, the space is always spotless, and the overall vibe is motivating without feeling intimidating. It’s clear that a lot of care goes into maintaining both the facility and the community. If you’re looking for a clean, well-equipped gym with an amazing staff and a great atmosphere, this is the place. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Anderson Castillo
reviews.text:
I’ve trained here a few times and this is easily one of the best gyms in San Francisco. The whole concept is super clean and well thought out — the pod system keeps everything organized without feeling crowded, and the new turf area looks amazing.

Every session here feels intentional and motivating. The layout, equipment, and overall vibe are top tier and make you actually want to push your workouts instead of just going through the motions. Big shoutout to Juan — he’s great, super knowledgeable, and makes training here even better.

The Yard is definitely one of the best gyms in SF to call your place to train.

reviews.author: Shamara Carney
reviews.text: I've been running my personal training business out of the Yard SF for over 2 years and it's been a great experience. Being able to book a pod that is all for you and your client helps avoid the gym anxiety that comes with beginning a new fitness journey. Also, tsince starting there they've added many upgrades continuously improving the experience. Highly recommend if you're trying to build your training business in the city.

services.name: Live Fit Gym • Inner Richmond
services.address: 403 Arguello Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Shawn Kresal
reviews.text: Especially for San Francisco, the weight lifting area has plenty of room and a variety of machines and free weights including a Smith Deck. The cardio machine area is a bit cozy and has windows for fresh air and is well lit which is nice. I appreciate their hours and automated access so I can go almost any time of day or night, even on holidays when other gyms are closed.

reviews.author: Kate Li
reviews.text: I have an online coach and have been training with him for a long time. Today, the administrator at the gym came up to me in the middle of my workout and asked me to leave, allegedly because it’s against the rules. However, I couldn’t find this point in the rules. She refused to give me her name, was rude, and even claimed that I was the one being rude. As a result, I lost my training session, my money, and had to cancel my membership with the club. Terrible service.

reviews.author: Sarah Herring
reviews.text: Live Fit Gym ROCKS! The location of the gym is very convenient with good street parking and bike parking nearby, plus if you go on the weekends you can literally walk down and visit the Clement Farmer’s Market after your workout! Or if you’re feeling up for a sweet treat, they have a famous French pastry shop that is right next door! The gym itself has all the amenities/equipment you could need PLUS they have some super cool classes that you can take through their app (which is super easy and self-explanatory to use), I went to an “Athlete training” class and it absolutely kicked my ass (in a good way). But the reason I’m even writing a review at all in the first place is this location has AMAZING staff. Not only have they been incredibly transparent on pricing/membership options, but they are so incredibly friendly and welcoming each time I come to the gym. As someone who is new to the area, having a familiar and smiling face to be greeted by each day truly makes the world of a difference! One time I even accidentally dropped my water bottle into an un-retrievable crack in the wall (long story) and they did everything they could and actually was able to rescue my beloved water bottle! And compared to what I have heard about other gyms in the area, I feel SO lucky to go to a gym like this one! Definitely consider signing up if you’re considering a gym in the neighborhood. They even have a free day pass so you can see if this gym feels like a good fit!! 10/10 would recommend.

reviews.author: Timothy Tran
reviews.text: Few months back I had a free day pass, never tried a gym before, and confused how to get in because the door was closed. Approached a guy (possibly staff) and asked about the free day pass. He asked for money to be allowed in. I'm assuming because I am a middle school student I'm not taken seriously, not a very good introduction of gyms but I found a different gym that I go to regularly.

reviews.author: Cody Jaffe
reviews.text:
I decided to get a membership at Live Fit Gym Inner Richmond after checking out other gyms in the area. In addition to having a convenient check-in (using their app/QR code on your phone) which is available 5am - 11pm 365 days a year, I love how they kept the atmosphere of the vintage building. As a treadmill runner, I was also immediately impressed with their cardio room of 10+ (not sure exact number) treadmills, as I am not the biggest fan of having to wait during busy times. They also have a great selection of free weights and weight machines.

The real reason I felt inclined enough to write a review is because of the friendly staff, especially Christian. Christian has been incredibly friendly and welcoming since I joined, and clearly cares about engaging and getting to know everyone. I recently signed up for the fitness assessment with Christian and was very impressed. He is super knowledgeable and a great coach, and his insight and relaxed demeanor made me feel super comfortable.

He has even taken the time to give me extra pointers while I'm on the floor, which have been very helpful in improving my form. I will definitely be signing up with Christian for more training sessions, as I have already benefitted a lot from the feedback he has provided.

services.name: FITNESS SF - Transbay
services.address: 425 Mission St Suite 212, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

reviews.author: Tamir Bermann
reviews.text:
Fitness SF Transbay is an excellent gym — very well equipped, spacious, and always super clean. The staff is friendly and welcoming, and the atmosphere is respectful with a great crowd of people. Free towels and very nice showers are a big plus and make workouts even more convenient.
For the price, this gym offers outstanding value. The location in the Financial District is very central and easy to access, which is perfect for fitting workouts into a busy schedule. I genuinely enjoy training here.
If they had a sauna, it would be absolutely perfect, but even without it, this is already a top-quality gym that I highly recommend.

reviews.author: Selena Kiu
reviews.text:
The best gym in the area! Equipment is extensive and clean and the community is so friendly and inclusive. I especially enjoy my yoga classes with David - always the highlight of my week. He teaches with such gentle, encouraging energy, and I’ve really seen progress in my flexibility and confidence over time.

Thank you to Chad, Asad, Ken, Mariana, and everyone else who helps make Fitness SF Transbay such a wonderful place to be!

reviews.author: Ivan Martinez
reviews.text: Fitness SF is conveniently located near Salesforce park. The gym is clean, spacious and filled with different types of equipment. Members get access to free classes including yoga. Their recovery room makes your workout even better. The cold-heat massage chairs do wonders to your body. Personal trainers are also available on-site to improve your technique.

reviews.author: Eduardo Sánchez-Ubanell
reviews.text:
Fitness SF is hands down the best gym chain in San Francisco. It’s a fantastic value for the price point, especially with their flexible month-to-month membership option that you can cancel anytime.

They have locations throughout the city but their Transbay gym is the newest and most modern of them all. You can tell a lot of thought and care went into the space. It’s a huge gym, with multiple rooms, a spacious open floor plan, and large windows for great light throughout.

Even during the busiest hours after work, the space never feels too cramped like some other gyms. I especially like the room with turf which has 10+ squat racks. A perfect space for leg day.

They also have a nice lounge/lobby area at the entrance. You’ll always see people hanging out or doing work in there. It makes the club feel more social and a place you can spend time in. The staff are also nice and the front desk person is always friendly and welcoming.

The locker rooms also feel quite premium and are always very clean. They have private bathroom stalls with those fancy heated toilet seats with bidets. The showers use glass doors and a marble-like tile that looks elegant. Definitely a big step up from a Planet Fitness or 24 Hour locker room.

One minor gripe I have with Fitness SF in general is their toiletries. All their products are from a brand called EO but I really don’t like the smell of any of them. I always bring my own body wash/shampoo.

Overall though, Fitness SF Transbay is a fantastic gym that feels modern, spacious and social. It’s definitely one of the best gyms I’ve ever seen for its price point. 💪🏼🏋️‍♂️

reviews.author: Rory Davis
reviews.text:
Do yourself a favor and take a class with Paul Hoyos and Vince Mendoza at this location. Great teachers, so fun and they kick your @ss in the best possible way.
The biggest and most well laid-out SF Fitness location.

services.name: Richmond District YMCA
services.address: 360 18th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Marissa Krogue
reviews.text:
There’s nothing about this Y that my family doesn’t absolutely adore. The workout experience is probably not the best you can get- it’s crowded, there aren’t tons of machines, and the free weights are lacking. Not to mention the studio downstairs is often busy. BUT! What they lack in that, they gain in experience. They are so friendly. They know my name and my kid’s name. He doesn’t go to preschool largely in part because he goes to the Y. They are so good with the kids.

The classes are all great. Especially Jenny’s! We love her. Another great perk is how good they are to the community. They do community activities often. It’s clean, easy to get to, and well maintained. There is limited parking in the back but you can park at the Ross down the block and get up to two hours validated (stamp your parking ticket at the front desk.) This Y is so great!

reviews.author: Anna S
reviews.text: The management stinks. This gym is pretty tiny, but they do what they can with the space, and its unfortunately one of the better Y’s in the area. They’ve gotten better about letting members know when events are happening in some of the bigger spaces, like workout rooms, but still have a long way to go with communications on closures. The biggest perk is the classes available — the class coordinator is awesome.

reviews.author: Castle
reviews.text: Beware. YMCA puts up massive barriers to cancel membership. I've been trying to cancel for the last 2 months because I simply can't afford it. No one responds when I email the membership address - which is how they instruct people to pursue membership cancellation. I tried simply removing my payment method from my account but they are still somehow charging me. 24 HR fitness here I come.

reviews.author: liz wilburn
reviews.text: The Richmond District YMCA is the first gym I've actually enjoyed going to. I feel completely comfortable working out here. I haven't had the chance to try out other locations, but this particular branch puts on great classes and the instructors are all very talented. I attend many of Jenny's classes (TRX, BodyPump, Cycle, Basic Core) and have often brought friends along with me and everyone loves them. Being a former athlete, I could immediately tell Jenny had been a sports coach in the past... and a great one at that. She sets the pace to motivate and push you, while still bringing a fun fiery spirit to the class. She knows her stuff and she will work alongside you every step of the way... and she's funny.  I truly can't say enough great things about the woman or her classes. If you haven't joined yet, get a guest pass and try out a few classes!

reviews.author: Cris Marincat
reviews.text: Good place to go away from home. Welcoming community, clean, and courteous. Has services that one would need for exercise, filtered water, wifi, and staff that keep the place tidy, orderly while making time to make guests feel welcome. They make the effort to grant access to those that have served their country.

services.name: Advanced Wellness
services.address: 523 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Shawn .H Leathers
reviews.text: Walking into AW for the first time, I had zero background in structured training and felt unsure of myself. The coaching team immediately put me at ease, guiding me step by step in a way that felt encouraging and personalized. This gym stood out because it truly supports beginners while helping them grow at their own pace. Over the years, I’ve gained strength, improved technique, and built real confidence in how I move and train. The coaches are skilled, patient, and genuinely invested in their members, creating a motivating environment that feels more like a community than just a gym.

reviews.author: Brita Spano Villanueva
reviews.text:
I joined Advanced Wellness after my second baby, when I was feeling discouraged and not bouncing back like before. Robbi told me to give it four months of consistency, and she was right! I’m now stronger, down a size, sleeping better, and most importantly - feeling like myself again!

The workouts are specific and effective, mixing strength and conditioning for maximum results, while training movements that support long-term health. Even in the group classes, the coaches tailor everything to your goals and abilities and give great cues for proper form and mechanics. The workout variety keeps it fun and challenging, and I love the encouraging community with people of all ages and levels. I always leave feeling better than when I walked in.

Bonus: After seeing my great results for bone density and muscle mass on a DEXA scan, my husband signed up the very next day!

reviews.author: Gordon Rothstein
reviews.text:
I’ve been going to Advanced Wellness (AW) for the last 3 years.  I’ve had several of my friends join and we all love it! I love it for a number of reasons.

Before going to AW, I was intimidated going to a gym.  My family signed up for a gym membership when I was a kid and that gym was acquired by 24hour.  I have a $30/year membership but I rarely use it.  On the rare occasions when I went to 24hour, I would wander from machine to machine not getting much out of it.  Or I would struggle on the bench press by myself.

Leo and Robbi have built a completely different experience.  They started by building community at AW where everyone working out in a time slot knows each other’s name.  The staff push you to reach your potential but in a fun way.  They make sure your technique is correct before encouraging you to push it to the next level.  Leo and Robbi are so knowledgeable with body movement and mechanics and they pass this knowledge down to their staff.  The staff keep a smile on your face during the workout.  The workouts are varied and challenging but you can choose how much you want to push yourself.  I started out using a large band to assist with my pull-ups three years ago.  Now I can do pull-up’s with a 45lb weight hanging from my waist!  I’ve seen the workouts translate to my life in many ways.  I’ve protected a lower back issue by strengthening my core and legs.  I have much better posture.  I’m a much stronger skier (I’m skiing like I did in the 90’s when I lived in Vail and skied 90 days a year).  I took the confidence and strength gained at AW and used it to swim Alcatraz without being a very good swimmer ;-)

Come check it out.  Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Natalie Tondelli-Walsh
reviews.text: Love this gym! I have been coming here for a few years and I can’t believe it has taken me this long to write a review! Robbi and the AW Trainers are amazing. They are motivational, fun and knowledgeable. The workouts are never the same and are consistently challenging. AW also communicates the ever changing COVID a guidelines to its members and focuses on making sure everyone is staying healthy and safe. Highly, highly recommend this local gym.

reviews.author: Brad DeJean
reviews.text:
AW is a great gym, and it's unlike any other I've ever joined. Here, you can choose the level of intensity of your workout, and your trainers and fellow clients provide support and encouragement! It provides the structure you need to succeed and reach your goals. Most gyms stop working for you when you sign the contract. But for Leo, Robbi and the rest of the team, that is just the beginning. They provide a couture workout program that will get even the most reluctant person to become an enthusiastic workout warrior.

AW finds a way for you to get the most out of your workouts and be the best, fit person you can be. In my 3+ years there, I have transformed my physique and improved my health tremendously.

services.description: Members-only gym offering private & group fitness training in a casual, colorful space.

services.name: Eden Fitness Studio
services.address: 1640 and 1650 Bush St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA

reviews.author: Basak Dogan
reviews.text: Had my first session (1-hr appointment at one of the workout stations) at Eden Fitness Studio this weekend. It was a great experience. Having to compete with others for workout stations at other gyms in the past, I really enjoyed having "my station" reserved straight for an hour at Eden's. I was able to use the whole fitness space with the station reserved only for me. Having privacy and convenience during a workout session is another level of comfort and joy. Very excited to go back with a fun workout routine

reviews.author: Amelia Sheppard
reviews.text: I no longer live in the area, but visit occasionally for 1-2 week time periods. I needed a place to work out that worked with this schedule and Eden has been perfect! Tammy, the owner, has created a beautiful and safe space for women to work out. She herself is incredibly welcoming and kind, and made it easy to get started. The Eden app is easy to use to purchase credits and schedule visits. The gym itself is equipped with everything you need for a great session. Eden has made it incredibly easy to stay consistent while traveling, and I can’t wait for my next trip!

reviews.author: Carolyn Estebez
reviews.text: I have been training with Tammy and at Eden for years! It’s a truly special place, now even better with the new part that she has added.  Nowhere else is there a gym just for women, with exceptional trainers, equipment, and a clean and safe space like this.  Tammy has always trained her clients with workout plans tailored to their bodies and what they need, and her trainers do the same.  I’ve trained with her for my wedding, through 2 pregnancies, and after.  The new part of the gym has all new equipment set up in stations to create space for clients to use alone or with trainers.  Tammy has thought through every aspect of the space to make it suit her clients’ needs.  Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Shruok Hassan-Radwan
reviews.text:
Eden Studio is such a beautiful, peaceful space! It’s always spotless and so well maintained — you can really tell that care goes into keeping it clean and welcoming. I love that it’s never too crowded, which makes it easy to relax and enjoy the experience.
Tammy, the owner, is absolutely wonderful — warm, friendly, and genuinely cares about everyone who comes in. She makes you feel right at home from the moment you walk through the door. You can tell she’s passionate about what she does and about creating a positive environment for her clients.
If you’re looking for a clean, calm, and friendly studio where you can unwind and feel taken care of, I can’t recommend Eden Studio enough!

reviews.author: Stephany Ruiz
reviews.text:
Hey everyone!

Eden Studio is seriously my happy place! It's not your average gym – think more luxurious spa than sweaty weights room. I just love the vibe; it’s beautiful, calm, and I always feel safe and welcomed. Seriously, if you're someone who gets a little nervous or self-conscious in big gyms, Eden studio is a game-changer. It's got this amazing relaxed atmosphere and plenty of space to feel comfortable and get your workout on without feeling like you’re on display. Highly recommend checking it out! I even still come here throughout out my pregnancy-it’s that comfortable!

services.name: Live Fit Gym - Mission
services.address: 780 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA

reviews.author: Michelle Oh
reviews.text: The bench press here is made for only very tall people. This is crazy because I think the average height in sf is like 5’5. Luckily this isn’t a problem for me because I’m 6’3 (I’m also really good looking). Still I think they should look into getting a bench press that is more suitable to the local population

reviews.author: Brianna L
reviews.text:
Pros: The staff are all nice and the regular gym goers are respectful. They also have towels which is nice as I am hella sweaty.

Cons: Not enough airflow, so gets super hot and stifling even with normal mild SF weather. Having been to other locations, the Mission one is unusually sucky in this regard. I will revise the review if ownership ever fixes this 🙏 there are some other more common cons like it's small, can get crowded, but honestly the heat is the big difference between this place and other gyms in the city.

reviews.author: spiderwebs peace out
reviews.text: Live Fit Gym- Mission is small, but mighty. It's got a modern, semi upscale appeal, with all the equipment to fulfill your basic gym needs. Live Fit offers massage, chiropractic and acupuncture. It also offer personal training, with a staff of about 6 personal trainers, all with different skillsets. It's fairly chill until around 4pm. From 4pm u til around 8, it'll be the busiest. Come get strong. P.s. they really opened the personal training studio a block away. And are now offering semi private group training sessions there M-Th & Sat!

reviews.author: Ryan kirk
reviews.text: Solid gym, not too crowded, but not empty. It has all the basics you need. I don’t usually have to wait long for anything. Haven’t talked to many of the people there, but they seem friendly enough.

reviews.author: Fer Mondrian
reviews.text: This is my local gym and I wouldn’t change it for anything. The staff are consistently friendly, always greeting you with a smile and creating a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

services.name: Koret Health and Recreation Center
services.address: 2130 Fulton St, San Francisco, CA 94117, USA

reviews.author: Wes Ho
reviews.text:
Well kept facility in middle of SF.   Passed available for USF alumni, Seniors and Diabled people.

I come mainly for the Olympic swimming pool    It’s great for practice swimming 25yard laps. Has deep ends to 13ft for practicing deep dives.

The gym has all other facilities for working out, stretching, combative and martial arts, basketball and volleyball courts.

reviews.author: Khalil Ben Gamra
reviews.text: Awful experience. The gym is often closed during student holidays, and in the summer it closes at 7 p.m., which is not mentioned when you purchase an annual membership. I would avoid this place and go to a regular gym with normal opening hours.

reviews.author: Dan Flanery
reviews.text: If you’re 55 or over you can get a 15 visit pass for $4 a visit. Unless you plan on going to the gym more than 4 days a week, there isn’t a better deal in the city. They have a good selection of weight machines, free weights, cardio equipment and the biggest indoor pool in the area that’s rarely even close to full. Only limitation is you have to visit before 2PM M-F, and when school isn’t in session they’re closed Sundays.

reviews.author: Kyle Windham
reviews.text: Overall it’s a well equipped gym, but some machines seem to break down all the time; I’m looking at you StairMaster. The biggest issue for this gym is the weird hours and long breaks that occur in conjunction with the school schedule. If you’re not a student, the inconsistency really messes up your momentum and progress. Unfortunately, there aren’t many options in the area for gyms, so that’s why I continue to go. It’s an expensive gym for sure and it often closes early or is just closed all together. I wouldn’t recommend for that reason.

reviews.author: Jack Hutton
reviews.text: The pool is beautiful. Its managed terribly. You never know when it'll be open or closed. They close 1/2 the pool on a whim. There's a schedule online that is updated *whenever* .. There's always a "lifeguard emergency".  You cannot call them. So you cannot plan whether or not to swim. It wasn't always like this. It used to be managed well. But, if you want to be consistent with swimming. They are not reliable. Its such a disappointment. Again, the best pool in the city.. they show utter disdain and disrespect for their patrons.

services.name: Richmond Recreation Center
services.address: 251 18th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94121, USA

reviews.author: Neal
reviews.text:
Well-maintained recreation center with frequent events, basketball court open to the public, and ping-pong on Fridays.

This place was very clean when I visited, and had plenty of people, but wasn't crowded. The hallway has flyers and some art, and a calendar with the month's events. There are water fountains and restrooms. Outside, there are some concrete benches with words inscribed in them. Masks and vaccination cards are no longer required.

reviews.author: Mich A
reviews.text: Center leaves me with mixed feelings.  I went to play at the "Drop-in" pickle ball court and the director was very nice, as I had never played pickle ball before and he directed me to the beginners' court.  The players were nice, but a few minutes into the game a coordinator came by and told me that I couldn't play there, and that I had to go learn the game somewhere else before playing at the center.  I was shocked and bewildered as this is a "Drop-in" activity and not a reserved or private court activity.  Anyway, I'm not likely to return where I was so unwelcomed.

reviews.author: wyatt
reviews.text: Great facilities, the locals at the gym are all super cool. Great pickup basketball there are always pickup games going on. The schedule is great and only has two off days. If you are looking for a good gym to play basketball indoors this is the place.

reviews.author: John Kelly
reviews.text: Lovely gym still on the newish side.  There are cooking classes that are in great demand.  Never was able to get in one though.  The Chinese elder community love to play ping pong there, or hang out and watch others play.  They look pretty good, too.  There are dancing classes, kung fu, basketball.  I am sure there are way more things I don't even know about.  Nice little parking lot, with beautiful landscaping and an art installation just outside the front doors.

reviews.author: Allen G
reviews.text: First time playing pickleball w a friend. Nice indoor area; people are friendly. only issue, need to get use to all the different lines on court as same area used for basketball, volleyball and badminton

services.name: TC Strength & Fitness: Group Class & Personal Training Gym
services.address: 3545 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

photos.attribution: TC Strength & Fitness: Group Class & Personal Training Gym
TC Strength & Fitness: Group Class & Personal Training Gym
TC Strength & Fitness: Group Class & Personal Training Gym

reviews.author: John Mohammadioun
reviews.text: Really big fan of this gym.  It is a veritable playground for exercise enthusiast.  I felt like a kid in a candy shop with all of the specialized, unique, and quality equipment they have.  I was in town for a while and needed a place to train.  All the members and coaches were very kind and welcoming.  If you’re looking for a quality gym with even better people to train with.  I’d highly recommend checking out TC Strength & Fitness.

reviews.author: Ramuel Holgado
reviews.text: I had a real great time working out at this gym! The trainers are super knowledgeable and are willing to help you, no matter your experience level. There's also a great community vibe here that made classes feel welcoming. I would definitely recommend checking out TC for a good workout!

reviews.author: Lesley Ray
reviews.text: I love working out here! The instructors are fantastic and the community is so welcoming. Everyone is friendly, supportive, and kind. I came in with absolutely no experience, and the coaches were patient and helpful as I learned the techniques. I can't recommend Telegraph enough to anyone looking for an incredible workout and a truly supportive community.

reviews.author: Juriy
reviews.text: I had a pleasure to make this box my home gym for a month while visiting. Such good vibes. Kevin and Angel were super accommodating. I had an unlimited access to the box pretty much the entire time for a reasonable price. It’s not a big place but it’s got everything you need. Highly recommend checking it out!

reviews.author: BK W
reviews.text:
I’ve been coming to this gym now for nearly a decade.  I found this gym during my initial move to SF. This gym is walkable from my house so the convenience helps out a lot.

Initially, it was a bit daunting coming in as I had come from another CF Box in LA and had to travel a lot for work so I was not very consistent with my fitness.  I opted in on private training and slowly switched to taking classes.  During my time here, I have grown much stronger both physically and mentally.

Having both taken private training and classes, I can attest that this gym has something for everyone.

The coaches care about form and understand that there is no one size fits all definition so they will work to ensure exercises are tailored to help fit your overall needs.  They do encourage you to always prioritize your health and fitness even when you may not feel at your best.  The coaches have been easily approachable and offered support even outside of classes to help work on technique, strength and stability.

Whether you are looking for classes to join and work up a sweat or feel like you need individual personalized guidance, you can achieve that here.

Another seller is the overall gym vibe.  This definitely has a very “local” feel and there’s a lot of camaraderie so you are able to find yourself in good hands with the many members.

I recommend this gym to anyone looking to really focus on health and needing a place to sweat it out while having a good laugh in between.

services.description: CrossFit group classes are the specialty at this upbeat gym, which also provides personal training.

services.name: Kabuki Springs & Spa
services.address: 1750 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Matty Easton
reviews.text:
Possibly the best bathhouse in the Bay Area.

Kabuki Springs is tucked away off a busy street in Japantown and feels like a portal to another dimension, one full of warm water, a quiet atmosphere, and plenty of lemon ginger tea.

I was instantly drawn to how intentional everything feels here, from the dark wood of the sauna to the sleek tile in the showers. It feels carefully crafted to be both modern and timeless, with the Japanese motif flowing through all of the design.

Personally, I love the dim lighting in the baths; with no windows, it feels like time is suspended. Is it midday, or midnight? Sunny, stormy? Early, late? You can’t really tell when you’re there, and that feels like the point. It’s an escape in its truest, most relaxing sense.

Attendants are constant in the baths, which makes it feel far more luxurious than what the price would suggest.

Cucumber, lemon, or plain water are all on tap, as are iced hand towels, salt scrubs for the steam room, and hot lemon ginger tea. I loved how cups are encouraged to be reused (I hate seeing the giant pile of single-use cups in the trash at other spas). I’m holding out hope, however, that traditional green tea may make an appearance in the future!

I’d recommend attending on one of the single-gender days, as I found that lent itself best to the fully immersive experience. Shower sandals are available, though I found them a bit uncomfortable. Next time I’ll be sure to bring my own.

As a bathhouse enthusiast, I cannot recommend this place enough. I can’t wait to bring my friends here to learn for themselves just how relaxing and rejuvenating the sauna, seam, soak, and plunge rotation can be.

reviews.author: Melissa Stapf
reviews.text: The communal baths were a marvelous experience from beginning to end. The staff at the front desk was helpful and thorough with explaining the locations, materials, and process. It was oh so lovely to move from wet warm to cold wet to dry hot to steamy hot to ambient temperature. Rinse and repeat. The cold washcloths & ice water were appreciated. I’ve never felt so relaxed AND alert from a bathing experience before. I am still feeling the pleasant effects of the bathing after a night of rest. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Rabbit Hole
reviews.text: A little gem in the heart of SF! I absolutely loved everything about my experience. As a seasonal traveler, I always book a sauna visit in cities I go to. Growing up in Europe (where spas are fully nude as it is so gross to bathe and sweat in synthetic bathing suits), I am glad we can still find places like this in the US. I love Swedish saunas, French hydrotherapy spas, Germany mixed genders spas, Italians bathing houses, Turkish Hammams, Korean Crystal saunas... I visited Japan and this place is even better than an onsen for me because of the low light and the various saunas/pools there. The cold plunge is amazing, right in the center of the room, and there are wet and dry saunas, a jacuzzi, relaxing lounge chairs and towels and lockers available for everyone. I soaked for 2h in the quiet atmosphere (thank you for the absolutely no phone and silence!!) before getting a facial by Devin. I HIGHLY recommend the experience. Devin, a super professional and fun esthetician with 20 years of experience, was able to quickly deliver a diagnosis on my skin and assess its needs. He used Naturopathica products (including the Plant Stem Cell Booster Serum) and I ended up purchasing some once I was back. This was truly an amazing experience! I can't wait to go back! It is certainly worth the splurge. Make sure to reserve and treat yourself to a full experience at Kabuki Springs and Spa, it's magical. Thank you so much! Doe

reviews.author: CAITLIN MARIE CLIFT
reviews.text: Great price for the experience of the co ed spa night!!! 2.5 hrs was such a perfect amount of time. Loved the steam room, sauna, and all the other amenities. Also the complimentary tea was delicious!! Def recommend and I’ll be coming back

reviews.author: Andrew M
reviews.text: Solid spot. It’s a bit dungeony without windows. The communal cold plunge fits 5 people which is fun. It doesn’t say what the cold plunge temp is, maybe 50? I went with a friend to catch up but you’re not allowed to speak. They have tea and water. Nice spot but I like Alchemy springs better because it’s social.

services.description: This relaxed day spa offers both familiar & Asian treatments, plus Japanese-style communal baths.

services.name: Pearl Spa and Sauna
services.address: 1656 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Wendy Yoo
reviews.text: This is definitely more a fusion of Western and Korean because of the emphasis on spa service. Unlike LA Korean spa, SF Korea spas limit your time (2 hours). I would say it's not a very traditional Korean spa based on the limited room options and time limit but the cleanliness and quality of toiletries is what I appreciate. The owner shared with my mom they clean heavily. There is throughfulness of providing free snacks, a tea and latte machine, and your lwn robe. They also gift you with a facial mask.

reviews.author: ala myr
reviews.text:
This is the best spa I have ever tried.

The spa area is amazing — they have a jacuzzi, a cold plunge, a dry sauna, and a steam sauna. There is also a clay room and a salt room, which makes the experience feel very special and unique.

The staff is incredibly friendly and welcoming. They guide you everywhere and explain everything. They even have Dyson hair dryers and a wide selection of hair and skincare products — including different curling irons for styling. Everything is designed for women and their comfort. There is also delicious herbal tea, and I love that the shampoos and shower gels smell herbal and look natural.

I want to give special thanks for the service. I booked a 90-minute massage with a body scrub, and it was beyond my expectations. They carefully washed me, used hot towels during the massage, and placed pillows under my legs and head for extra comfort. You can really feel that this spa was created by someone who truly cares about people and their comfort.

They also included a facial massage and face masks. I even received a body wrap treatment.

This is the best spa experience I have ever had. I always dreamed of a service like this — where you receive a full body massage, are gently washed, and get a facial massage and masks all in one place, all in one treatment.

10 out of 10.

This is what a real spa should be like. 💆‍♀️✨

reviews.author: Barbra Calma
reviews.text: Where have you been all my life? Been wanting to try a Korean spa treatment since forever and finally got to visit this spa recently. Loved this place so much that I want to visit at least once a quarter. The spa provides you with a robe and slippers and plenty of towels around. In addition to the hot tub, cold plunge, dry sauna, and steam room, they also have a clay and Himalayan salt rooms. My friend and I did the 60-minute Silver Bell Scrub + Massage treatment (body scrub, silver oil body massage, hair wash), enjoyed all of it even the "aggressive scrub". They also provide post-treatment toiletries, including hair dryer and hair products. All the staff are also very friendly and welcoming. I look forward to my next visit to Pearl Spa & Sauna!

reviews.author: Archie Agrawal
reviews.text:
Had the most relaxing 3 hours here!
My boyfriend gifted me a scrub+massage here and everything was amazing!

The ambiance is beautiful, their services were great. They have a hot-tub and multiple saunas where you can relax before and after your session.
The scrub and massage were amazing!! Highly recommend this place.

They had really good Aveda hair, face and body products to use and get ready at the end.
I will be back!

reviews.author: Ksenia Se
reviews.text:
Fantastic place. Spacious, with every sauna you might need, with every detail taken care of (you should see their assortment for skin care and hair care after massages!). Scrub was great, massage was right on point. And - and - if you don’t see availability on their website, call! They were able to accommodate me which I’m grateful for.

They also gave me a little gift when I was leaving. That’s so nice of them.

Update: Came here the second time: this place is the best!

services.name: SenSpa
services.address: 1161 Gorgas Ave, San Francisco, CA 94129, USA

reviews.author: Ashia Bibi
reviews.text: My sun-day spent so beautifully well at senspa. This spa is my must go to Everytime I am in the city. The ambiance is filled with tranquil vibes and their service is always upto my standards. As an artist mediating is always on the menu and senspa caters to my soul. My spa treatments and infared sauna along with grounding in their meditation room was a perfect long weekend gift to myself. Coming back soon =]

reviews.author: Terri Baldocchi
reviews.text: I was so impressed with this spa.  The atmosphere was intimate, serene, calm and beautiful.  The aesthetician, Sarah was truly amazing because she was so thorough and informative with the current skin products on the market.  She explained why certain serums, cleansers, moisturizers and retinol are essential for healthy skin. She performed magic on my face!   I enjoyed my time with her and she is as asset to the spa.  I will be back with my daughters.  With much thanks!

reviews.author: Moorissa Tjokro
reviews.text:
Tulaxu is hands down the best masseuse! I’ve been going every month since earlier this year, and my body consistently felt much lighter after. No food a couple hours before the massage is highly recommended otherwise you’d feel bloated

Loved taking 1-2 hours before the massage sesh to wind down and enjoy their relaxation lounge. The place is absolutely zen, and what a beautifully warming way to have anonymous journals that we can all read and write on.

reviews.author: Laura De La Torre
reviews.text: This place is amazing! This has been my best spa experience so far. Maki’s deep tissue, dry brushing, and feet scrub were very relaxing. Meeting Sarah was absolutely wonderful. She is very knowledgeable and made great suggestions that I can’t wait to try. I will be back soon!

reviews.author: Stewart Cezar
reviews.text: I am healed! Michelle did an amazing job - I was pretty banged up. The entire experience was sublime, the best spa experience!

services.name: Serenity Wellness Spa
services.address: 2087 Union St #2, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA

reviews.author: Bianca Elena Iorga
reviews.text: I've been getting facials from Thida regularly and can highly recommend her! She makes the whole experience feel very relaxing and she gives great advice on products and treatments that would suit me. My skin has never looked better and I get compliments all the time now! I always look forward to my next facial :)

reviews.author: cordikat
reviews.text: i visited for a back facial and it was a lovely experience. i did the microdermabrasion add on and the treatments that took place were interesting and enjoyable. the various products used had different sensations and i loved the peppermint, smelled and felt great. staff was lovely and the vibes are comfortable, clean, and relaxing. would definitely visit again.

reviews.author: Alex Stewart
reviews.text: Incredible massage - one of the best I've ever had! So relaxing and truly helped work out so many of the knots and so much tension that I hold in my shoulders and back. I felt like it was the perfect combination of relaxation and progress in working out muscle knots. Could not recommend Joana enough, I will definitely be back!

reviews.author: shanay mallory
reviews.text: Ynes was very professional and explained everything to me. She was a very kind person and very gentle. This was my first ever facial and she made the experience well worth it. The establishment is very clean and welcoming. I felt so relaxed the products used smelled amazing and my face feels and looks great. I will be definitely adding facials into my monthly self care routines and Serenity Wellness Spa is where I am going every time

reviews.author: Brenda Kam
reviews.text: I have to leave a glowing review for Debra. She did an amazing custom facial for my first time and my skin feels incredible, not to mention it was incredibly relaxing and just what I needed. Debra described each step and educated me on all the products and procedures used. She was extremely meticulous and thoughtful - I cannot wait to go back. The spa is clean and super cute and the receptionist was wonderful as well.

services.name: Imperial Spa
services.address: 1875 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA

reviews.author: Karen Bayle
reviews.text:
I have been here many times and love it. I always get the same treatment! It’s called the  “HOT MILK BODY SCRUB   & SWEDISH OIL MASSAGE with fresh cucumber mask for face”.
Get there early to enjoy the showers, sauna, hot tub prior to your treatment. The scrubbing can be a bit intense, but your skin feels great afterwards. The massage is nice and includes all sorts of lotions, oils, warm buckets of water rinses and hot towels. It ends with a fresh cucumber face mask and hair wash. Lasts 70 minutes and costs $130, well worth it.

reviews.author: Stefanie Hiser
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to try this facility my friend recommended because of the recent negative reviews, but I ended up being pleasantly surprised. The inside is really nice—there are bathrooms, shoe lockers, separate lockers for your belongings, three standing open showers, three seated washing stations, a hot tub, a sauna, massage rooms, and scrubbing tables.

They give you a bracelet with a number that matches your locker, and that’s what they call you by. On the women’s side, everyone is nude. I felt shy at first, but I quickly got comfortable because everyone is respectful and no one is paying attention to you.

The scrub itself was amazing. It was a little rough at times, but never enough for me to ask her to stop or lighten up. Between each stage of the scrub, she poured warm water over me, which felt incredible. With my permission, she also washed my hair and scalp. The massage afterward was very good as well.

Next time I’m in San Francisco, I’ll definitely return and plan to spend even more time there. The only downside is parking—it was very difficult to find, and after circling several times, I got lucky when someone pulled out on a side street. I recommend going early.

One more tip: they don’t provide flip-flops, so bring a pair you don’t mind getting wet.

reviews.author: Noa
reviews.text:
Exactly what I was looking for. I am someone who enjoys the sauna -> cold-plunge cycle, with some hot tub time too. Thats what I'm looking for in a spa.

The spa was clean, had plenty of room for all the patrons, and had what I needed. The dry sauna was a good temperature and the cold plunge was PERFECT for me. It was very deep and spacious so more than 1 person could use it at a time, and it was cold enough to give me the benefits I needed but not so cold that I wasn't able to use it (like some places).

If I could change anything I'd raise the hot tub temp a couple degrees, and maybe add a lay-flat option for the chairs. But other than that it was perfect. The price was fair, and the length of stay allowed was 3 hours, which is an hour longer than other spas I've been to.

I've been to the other spas in the area and I'd probably pick this one.
Kabuki: Nice facilities, but they let a man into the bathouse on women's only day. They let people just identify into the private areas, which makes women unsafe. Also, they asked me to be quiet when I was breathing loudly in the cold plunge for 30 seconds, despite the showers being 4x the sound lol.

Pearl: Nice facilities, but hard to get in as a spa-only use patron. I like that they are actually women only. They don't have a men's section though, so can't bring my male friends.

Imperial: Best of both worlds. They have a male section so male friends and family can join, and they also keep the male and female section actually separated and protect women. The facilities were exactly what I needed, lockerroom is clean, they have dyson blow dryers and a sink in the mirror room to freshen up before a date or going out. Showers have great pressure and temp. Sauna and cold plunge were perfect. Hot tub is large and nice. Price is better than the other two, and length of stay is better than the other two, and availability is also better than the other two.

reviews.author: Ersyla Nellajoy
reviews.text:
this is a nice place. i would have given more stars if they  had a strong jacuzzi jet in their hot tub. the steam room and dry sauna are very nice as well.
If you can afford the services, the body work is gr8

reviews.author: Alexis Max
reviews.text: The cold plunge is broken. It hasn’t worked for 2 weeks and they don’t know when it will be fixed. They do not provide sandals anymore, but do not tell you in the prep email. You have to bring your own. Service doesn’t feel as great as it used to.

services.name: Cocoon Bay Area Day Spa - Noe Valley
services.address: 3910 24th St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

photos.attribution: Cocoon Bay Area Day Spa - Noe Valley
Cocoon Bay Area Day Spa - Noe Valley
Cocoon Bay Area Day Spa - Noe Valley

reviews.author: eliana salmeron
reviews.text:
Lia 10/10 my esthi-BESTIE! I have been booking with her since her last spa location, she gives the best most relaxing facials and my go to for lash lifts and tint! It’s always such a great experience. She gives the best consultations and recommendations. I’ve been to other places before, but Lia at Cocoon definitely has a different touch.

The lift is always perfect, she has the most accommodating and welcoming energy. The space itself is beautiful and calming, and the entire experience felt thoughtful from start to finish. Love Lia!!! You gotta book with HER.

reviews.author: Kathryn Ma
reviews.text: I wanted to share some positivity for a change. My experience at the Noe Valley Cocoon Day Spa was so friendly and relaxing. I especially appreciated the superior one- on -one interaction with their aesthetician, Ellie. She’s so professional and gentle with her facials. My skin feels very fresh and more youthful. I’m planning to return soon for a spray tan.

reviews.author: Meagan Ryan
reviews.text: I’ve been going to Cocoon and Kristina B for a few years and she is truly exceptional! Her ability to blend relaxation with targeted muscle work always sort me out. She introduced me to cupping therapy for my chronic knots, and it has made a huge difference. Every session leaves me feeling completely refreshed, highly recommend her! Also, the gals at the front desk are so nice!

reviews.author: Anna Melnyk
reviews.text: Today, I had the best massage of my life. I’ve never felt so relaxed before! Chanra is truly a magician! I highly recommend this as a gift. 🎁

reviews.author: William Toon
reviews.text: My wife and I visited Cocoon for a pre-wedding treatment from Ellie. It was our first time ever pampering ourselves like this, and Ellie was incredibly patient with us and my wife thoroughly enjoyed the facial services from Ellie. Cannot stress enough how professional and caring Ellie was during our entire session. We will be back soon!

services.name: Archimedes Banya
services.address: 748 Innes Ave, San Francisco, CA 94124, USA

reviews.author: P S
reviews.text: I have been here many times and have always appreciated the facilities, relaxing experience, and respectful energy. I went again recently and it was a nightmare! There were way too many people in this place here on a Friday night! This isn't supposed to feel like a club. One of the hot tubs was closed so everyone was squishing together into the only open one when there is a sign that clearly says "5 people max" but really there would be like 10 people in there and water spilling out of the sides from being too full and creating a hazardous wet floor! The steam room was so packed that it seemed dangerous since there was literally 0 room for anyone and the steam was too hot since the temperature kept getting messed up from people opening the door too often. The regular saunas had lines and people awkwardly standing around because of the limited space. People were VERY loud and would yell to each other across the facility or just be like laughing/screaming in the pool. Someone took my sandals that they assign you so I had to wear my street sandals in there. There was a guy trying to get into the hot tubs with his street clothes on and one of the employees had to stop him. Also, I don't want to soak in people's endocrine distributing laundry detergent, just soak naked! Nobody cares what you look like! Please get over your fears of being naked and leave your swim suits at home!! With this many people allowed in at the same time, even the overall cleanliness of the place could have used a solid refresh. Like the pool water was very murky, maybe due to the oak leaves that are used in the banya that people don't wash off before entering. Overall, I think the problem is 50/50. 50% this place should limit how many people are allowed in, especially if they have facilities that are not even open, and 50% people that are just ignorant and stupid that ruin the experience for everyone else by not following the rules. I ended up getting a cold from this place from being around too many people in a small, wet, environment. I still love and appreciate this place and hope they do well and keep providing a safe, clean, communal environment for everyone to enjoy.

reviews.author: Rosemary Elizabeth Earl
reviews.text:
ugh, I really did not want to make this review because I loved this place the last time I went. but something has to be said… for the cost of $67 per person, you’d think cleanliness wouldn’t be an afterthought.

The women’s changing room had ants trailing ALL over it. the jacuzzis had a disturbing clump of hair, and a weird slimy film at the edges.

When I kindly informed the staff of these issues, they just said “oh” and charged me. didn’t even ask where or say sorry. In this economy, I cannot afford to spend my money where businesses don’t even respect it. The price of the spa makes no sense with the lack of cleanliness, and the service was just a slap in the face.

I really don’t even like to make negative reviews for small businesses but this is just unacceptable.

reviews.author: Jane
reviews.text: excellent place! its not exactly luxurious, but it has everything you need for a pretty reasonable price!! variety of sauna, banya, pools, aromatherapy was AMAZING (definitely need to try!), venik plaza is awesome, the rooftop view is great to chill at even in winter (pic attached)! and the cafe has a variety of cuisines to offer! (my fav was borscht!) definitely will be back!

reviews.author: massagequeenofsucculents _
reviews.text:
I am very disappointed with the front desk experience at Archimedes Banya Spa San Francisco.
We came in to inquire about booking a group of eight women, fully prepared to spend money and plan a visit. Instead, we were met with a cold, dismissive attitude and a complete lack of professionalism.
One staff member was eating snacks with elbows on the counter and addressed us with, “what do you want,” which felt disrespectful and inappropriate in a customer-facing role. He also blurted out that "naked or clothed" which sound creepy the way he said it. Two other staff members remained seated and disengaged, making no effort to genuinely assist.

We were told:
There were no business cards
No brochures or printed materials
We were not allowed a tour of the facility

We were simply told in a cold tone to “go online.”
This was incredibly frustrating, especially since we were standing right in front of multiple staff members who could have easily answered questions or helped us plan a group visit. Turning away potential customers instead of offering basic hospitality or assistance is unacceptable.
This experience felt unwelcoming and left a very poor impression. Customer service and professionalism matter, especially in a business centered around wellness and hospitality.
Unfortunately, we will be taking our group elsewhere. Management definitely needs to retain their front end staff because they just lost some business.

reviews.author: Sergey Egorov
reviews.text:
We visited Archimedes Banya on January 21, 2026. It’s a fully equipped Russian-style bath complex with ice-cold plunge pools, a Finnish sauna, a steam room, a warm pool, and a jacuzzi. They also offer massages.
Even though it was a weekday, the place was busy. Most men and women were completely naked — very authentic banya culture.
After several hot–cold cycles, we stopped by the café for borsch with black bread and a cheburek — both were excellent.
They also have some art on the walls; I wish they would add a reproduction of Boris Kustodiev’s 1926 painting “Russian Venus” to match the atmosphere.
One thing I really appreciated was their attention to safety. Staff watched carefully for anyone who looked overheated. One of the workers even helped me walk and rest after my first session in the hot room. He mentioned that this is the hottest banya in the U.S.
Overall, a great experience — highly recommended.

services.name: Nature’s Edge Spa
services.address: 404 Balboa St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA

reviews.author: Noreen Curran
reviews.text:
Have had two massages here and I plan to return. I really enjoy the small, quiet, clean, relaxing environment and friendly staff. Mia at the front desk is really friendly and is very quick in terms of confirming bookings or informing of any scheduling changes.

When you arrive you sit in a small lobby with hot tea and candies. There’s relaxing music and it’s nice and quiet. You highlight on a laminated print-out where on your body you’d like the massage to be focused, as well as choosing between soft, medium, or hard pressure. They’re also quick to ask during the massage “is this okay?” “how’s that?” “are you alright?”  Very sweet ladies.

My first time here I got the 30 minute foot massage plus the 60 minute deep tissue massage. I wish I remembered the woman’s name who gave me the massage a few months back. She was very sweet though.

My most recent experience I booked in for the 60 minute deep tissue massage and was paired with Coco — I’m going to request her every time I go back! I was so impressed with how strong she is, as I prefer a very deep massage. She was so thorough and I overall had a wonderful experience.

Both treatments ended in hot rocks being used on my back (so nice!) and a scalp/gentle face massage.

I plan to return many times to Nature’s Edge. Support this business! I hope they are here to stay!!

reviews.author: Alona DAVIS
reviews.text: I had a very thorough 90 minute massage and I wish I would have asked the name of the therapist because she was strong and had some great strokes in just the right places. Minus one star because it was very difficult for me to breath - the hole that you put your face into was covered with so many layers of sheets and covers and smelled very strongly of laundry detergent - i was getting claustrophobic and had to keep lifting my head up to get a fresh breath of air. I have never experienced that during a massage before! The therapist tried to help and eventually we just removed all of the various coverings, but it did disrupt my comfort and it was very hard to relax. I am not sure I will return for a full body massage because of that despite the massage itself being excellent.

reviews.author: 江山稳慧
reviews.text: Nature’s Edge Spa is such a gem. The space is cozy, peaceful, and super clean. Everyone there is friendly and welcoming, which made me feel comfortable right away. I got a massage and it was honestly one of the best I’ve had—relaxing but also worked out all the tension in my shoulders. You can tell they really care about what they do. I left feeling totally relaxed and taken care of. Definitely coming back!

reviews.author: Hannah
reviews.text:
I like that the masseuses do not smell like cigarettes, are on time, and have good pressure when you book for a deep tissue massage. I bring my husband Andrew here and he also loves it. We prefer separate rooms and they honor that.

They also always end with a head massage, which I appreciate. But I need to remember that unless I note that I would also like my foot massaged on their indication card, they don’t focus on it as much. They will prioritize what you put on the card which is awesome 👏 they now have a male masseuse, who I think is new to the team.

I did ask for no hot stones today (twice) but I don’t think they understood. I didn’t end up caring in the end because it was so good 😊

I am so excited to fill up my return card coupon book with them.

reviews.author: Anushah Hossain
reviews.text: Got a body + foot reflexology massage. I felt like the masseuse did strokes and presses in an indiscriminate way for an hour? No attention to pressure points or physiology... The masseuse kept deep sighing. After the halfway point I too was just waiting for it to end. It was absolutely not a reflexology massage when she got to my feet, just scrubbed my calf over and over on one leg. After I asked her to focus on my sole for the other foot, she just scrubbed that over and over again. I've gotten much better massages elsewhere (at higher and lower price points) - would not return here.

services.name: Wela Thai Massage and Spa
services.address: 2343 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA

reviews.author: Tati Cantar
reviews.text:
I’m so happy I found this place. I got a Thai deep tissue massage with Otter and it was one of the best massages I’ve had in a long time. He was very attentive, listened to my concerns and truly helped release tension.

From the moment you walk in, it feels welcoming: there’s a cozy lounge area, they offer tea while you wait, and the whole space is clean and relaxing. Even the bathroom is beautifully decorated with Thai touches. Highly recommend, and definitely ask for Otter!

reviews.author: Lisa Douglass
reviews.text: Incredible!  The atmosphere is relaxing and everyone is so kind and attentive.  I got the triple s, Swedish, scrub and sauna and it was fabulous.   Tom really worked out all my knots.  Wela is now top on my massage venues by far.

reviews.author: Michael Smith
reviews.text: Had my first ever massage from Sean today, went with a simple hour Swedish massage that focused on my back with my known tension there. Wow. Put me totally at ease and relaxed and released several tension points in my shoulders and back I didn’t even know I had. Will be back soon for another absolutely!

reviews.author: Sarah M
reviews.text: Amanda was amazing: she flowed through the massage effortlessly.  I needed to book this after intense travel. Will come back!

reviews.author: R. Brandon Talanehzar
reviews.text: What an amazing experience! Real authentic Thai massage.  I go surfing and it was the ultimate surf recovery. The place is really nice and the people are so friendly. Very professional massage! Thank you! 🙏 I highly recommend especially for surf recovery therapy!

services.name: QUA SPA at SOMA GRAND
services.address: 1162 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

reviews.author: diana tran
reviews.text: Initially discovered through Groupon, the exceptional quality of my first visit prompted a return. Today’s deep tissue massage was, without exaggeration, perfect. I particularly appreciate her unique technique; unlike many therapists who rely heavily on elbows and forearms, she uses a more refined approach that is highly effective. Furthermore, her transitions are seamless, avoiding the common issue of over-focusing on a single area. She followed my requests precisely, balancing the time spent on my back and arms beautifully. Her passion for her craft and dedication to client satisfaction are evident. I left feeling incredibly restored and have already decided to make this a monthly priority. I highly recommend her services.

reviews.author: Kathryn
reviews.text: Had the best experience at Qua Spa. My daughter and I went for facials while on vacation. The spa has all the modern equipment to improve your skin - oxygen, red and blue light, etc. my facial was only supposed to last an hour and she went an extra 30 minutes to make sure my skin was properly taken care of. My skin looks amazing - fine lines much less noticeable and my dry, red skin gone. My daughter got the anti acne facial and all her blackheads are gone. I can’t wait to come back.

reviews.author: Dušan
reviews.text:
3rd Update 🌟
Joey was absolutely above and beyond,  I realized she is an expert on facial massage, and she has been doing it for 30 years!   which I haven't done before since I don't trust anyone with my face. She was very vocal of each step and explained every step, devices,  and products she used. I will definitely come back, and not only that, I'll recommend her  to my friends and business partners. I will promote her SPA on my social platforms and social media as well. It is also important to mention how everything was clean and maintained very well!

非常感謝
再見

2nd Update
Correction: Joey introduced herself as an SPA director, reached out to me, and offered a facial treatment in order to compensate for the first experience. I do appreciate how quickly she responded and how honest she was.

1st review.
I mentioned several times that I wanted a softer massage due to my back pain before even starting. The lady that did my massage ignored that and said it was supposed to hurt, in a tone like I never had a massage before. I complained several times during the session as well. Now, I am experiencing even worse back pain.  Avoid this place, in my opinion.

reviews.author: Hayward T
reviews.text:
I treat myself to a spa day every year on my birthday, but only at Qua Spa with Joey have I ever felt truly renewed. After the massage, my whole body felt lighter and more relaxed. The facial left my skin soft, fresh, and glowing—I honestly felt younger! The space is clean and cozy, and Joey is both friendly and incredibly professional.
Thank you, Joey! See you again next year!

reviews.author: Leo L
reviews.text: My shoulders and back were in pain, and a friend recommended Qua Spa. Joey suggested a 30-minute Pain Relief Massage, and I felt so much better—no more pain! Highly recommend. Plus, the place is clean, comfortable, and has a great atmosphere.

services.name: Cocoon Day Spa San Francisco - Soma
services.address: 330 1st St 2nd Floor, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA

photos.attribution: Cocoon Day Spa San Francisco - Soma
Cocoon Day Spa San Francisco - Soma

reviews.author: Jennifer Stuart Prichard
reviews.text: Hands down the best massage I’ve ever had!
